en: Everybody loves a fat pay rise. (6 words)
cn: 人人都喜欢丰厚的加薪。

en: Yet pleasure at your own can vanish if you learn that a colleague has been given a bigger one. (19 words)
cn: 但如果得知一个同事加薪更多，自己那份喜悦感就会消失得无影无踪。

en: Indeed, if he has a reputation for slacking, you might even be outraged. (13 words)
cn: 实际上，如果他还是出了名的懒惰，你甚至可能会怒不可遏。

en: Such behaviour is regarded as “all too human”, with the underlying assumption that other animals would not be capable of this finely developed sense of grievance. (26 words)
cn: 有人把这种行为看作是“人之常情”，其潜在的假设是其他动物不可能具有如此高度发达的不满意识。

en: But a study by Sarah Brosnan and Frans de Waal of Emory University in Atlanta, Georgia, which has just been published in Nature, suggests that it is all too monkey, as well. (32 words)
cn: 但是由佐治亚州亚特兰大埃默里大学的萨拉·布鲁斯南和弗朗斯·德瓦尔进行的一项研究表明，这也是“猴之常情”，这项研究成果刚刚发表在《自然》杂志上。

en: The researchers studied the behaviour of female brown capuchin monkeys. (10 words)
cn: 研究人员研究了雌性棕色卷尾猴的行为。

en: They look cute. (3 words)
cn: 这些猴子看起来可爱。

en: They are good-natured, co-operative creatures, and they share their food readily. (11 words)
cn: 性格温顺，相互合作，乐于分享食物。

en: Above all, like their female human counterparts, they tend to pay much closer attention to the value of “goods and services” than males. (23 words)
cn: 最重要的是，跟人类中女性一样，它们往往比雄性更关注“商品和服务”价值。

en: Such characteristics make them perfect candidates for Dr. Brosnan’s and Dr. de Waal’s study. (14 words)
cn: 这些特性使它们成为布鲁斯南博士和德瓦尔博士理想的研究对象。

en: The researchers spent two years teaching their monkeys to exchange tokens for food. (13 words)
cn: 研究人员花了两年时间教这些猴子用代币换食物。

en: Normally, the monkeys were happy enough to exchange pieces of rock for slices of cucumber. (15 words)
cn: 通常，猴子很愿意用石块来换黄瓜片。

en: However, when two monkeys were placed in separate but adjoining chambers, so that each could observe what the other was getting in return for its rock, their behaviour became markedly different. (31 words)
cn: 但是，当把两个猴子分别放在紧挨着的房间里，好让他们都能看见各自用石头交换来的东西，其行为就会截然不同。

en: In the world of capuchins, grapes are luxury goods (and much preferable to cucumbers). (14 words)
cn: 在卷尾猴的世界，葡萄是奢侈品（比黄瓜受欢迎得多）。

en: So when one monkey was handed a grape in exchange for her token, the second was reluctant to hand hers over for a mere piece of cucumber. (27 words)
cn: 所以当一只猴子用代币能换回一颗葡萄时，要第二只猴子仅为一片黄瓜给出自己的代币，她就不情愿了。

en: And if one received a grape without having to provide her token in exchange at all, the other either tossed her own token at the researcher or out of the chamber, or refused to accept the slice of cucumber. (39 words)
cn: 要是一只猴子完全不需要代币就能换一颗葡萄，那么另外一只就会将代币扔向研究人员或者扔出房间外，或者拒绝接受那片黄瓜。

en: Indeed, the mere presence of a grape in the other chamber (without an actual monkey to eat it) was enough to induce resentment in a female capuchin. (27 words)
cn: 事实上，只要另一个房间出现了葡萄（实际猴子并没有吃），都足以让雌卷尾猴产生怨恨。

en: The researchers suggest that capuchin monkeys, like humans, are guided by social emotions. (13 words)
cn: 研究人员指出，卷尾猴，和人类一样，也受社会情感的影响。

en: In the wild, they are a cooperative, group-living species. (9 words)
cn: 在野外，它们是相互合作的群居动物。

en: Such cooperation is likely to be stable only when each animal feels it is not being cheated. (17 words)
cn: 这种合作，只有在每只猴子都觉得自己没有受到欺骗时，才可能稳定。

en: Feelings of righteous indignation, it seems, are not the preserve of people alone. (13 words)
cn: 似乎不仅仅只有人类才会产生这种义愤填膺的感情。

en: Refusing a lesser reward completely makes these feelings abundantly clear to other members of the group. (16 words)
cn: 酬劳太少，那就彻底拒绝，可以让这种情绪准确无误地传达给其它成员。

en: However, whether such a sense of fairness evolved independently in capuchins and humans, or whether it stems from the common ancestor that the species had 35 million years ago, is, as yet, an unanswered question. (35 words)
cn: 但是这种公平感是在卷尾猴和人类身上各自独立演化而成，还是来自三千五百万前他们共同的祖先，这还是一个悬而未决的问题。

en: Do you remember all those years when scientists argued that smoking would kill us but the doubters insisted that we didn’t know for sure? (24 words)
cn: 还记得那些年吗？当时，科学家认为吸烟会致命，而怀疑者却坚持认为我们并不能确定。

en: That the evidence was inconclusive, the science uncertain? (8 words)
cn: 怀疑者坚持认为证据并不确凿，科学也有未知。

en: That the antismoking lobby was out to destroy our way of life and the government should stay out of the way? (21 words)
cn: 他们坚持认为，向政府游说禁烟的人群试图毁掉我们的生活，政府就不应该干预此事。

en: Lots of Americans bought that nonsense, and over three decades, some 10 million smokers went to early graves. (18 words)
cn: 他们这些废话，竟然有许多美国人相信，结果三十多年来，大约有一千万抽烟的人早早就进了坟墓。

en: There are upsetting parallels today, as scientists in one wave after another try to awaken us to the growing threat of global warming. (23 words)
cn: 现在出现了与吸烟类似的令人不安的事情，科学家们前仆后继，试图唤醒我们，让我们意识到全球气候变暖所带来的日益严重的威胁。

en: The latest was a panel from the National Academy of Sciences, enlisted by the White House, to tell us that the Earth’s atmosphere is definitely warming and that the problem is largely man-made. (33 words)
cn: 最近的一次是国家科学院的专家小组，得到了白宫的支持，他们告诉我们，地球的大气层肯定正在变暖，而这个问题主要是人为的。

en: The clear message is that we should get moving to protect ourselves. (12 words)
cn: 传达的信息很明确：我们应该立刻着手保护自己。

en: The president of the National Academy, Bruce Alberts, added this key point in the preface to the panel’s report: “Science never has all the answers. (25 words)
cn: 国家科学院院长布鲁斯·阿尔伯茨在专家小组报告的前言中加入了一个要点：“科学，回答不了所有问题。”

en: But science does provide us with the best available guide to the future, and it is critical that our nation and the world base important policies on the best judgments that science can provide concerning the future consequences of present actions.” (41 words)
cn: 但是科学的确提供了最为可靠的指导，引领我们走向未来，在现在的行为将会对未来产生什么影响的这个问题上，我们的国家和全世界应该用科学所能提供的最佳判断作为制定重要政策的根据，这一点至关重要。

en: Just as on smoking, voices now come from many quarters insisting that the science about global warming is incomplete, that it’s OK to keep pouring fumes into the air until we know for sure. (34 words)
cn: 正如讨论吸烟问题一样，谈论全球变暖的声音来自四面八方，他们坚持认为有关全球变暖的科学资料并不完善；在我们没有确定之前，一直往大气中排放有害气体也没事。

en: This is a dangerous game: by the time 100 percent of the evidence is in, it may be too late. (20 words)
cn: 这是一个危险的游戏：到了有百分之百的证据的时候，可能就太晚了。

en: With the risks obvious and growing, a prudent people would take out an insurance policy now. (16 words)
cn: 风险越来越明显，并且不断增加，一个谨慎的民族现在就应该去买一份保单了。

en: Fortunately, the White House is starting to pay attention. (9 words)
cn: 幸运的是，白宫开始关注这件事了。

en: But it’s obvious that a majority of the president’s advisers still don’t take global warming seriously. (16 words)
cn: 但是显然总统的大多数顾问并没有认真看待全球气候变暖这个问题。

en: Instead of a plan of action, they continue to press for more research—a classic case of “paralysis by analysis”. (20 words)
cn: 他们不拿出一套行动计划，却继续尽力想要做更多的研究——一个经典的“分析导致瘫痪”的案例。

en: To serve as responsible stewards of the planet, we must press forward on deeper atmospheric and oceanic research. (18 words)
cn: 作为这个星球负责任的管家，我们必须积极推进，深入研究大气和海洋。

en: But research alone is inadequate. (5 words)
cn: 但仅靠研究是不够的。

en: If the Administration won’t take the legislative initiative, Congress should help to begin fashioning conservation measures. (16 words)
cn: 如果政府不主动采取立法行动，国会就应该帮助政府，着手制定保护措施。

en: A bill by Democratic Senator Robert Byrd of West Virginia, which would offer financial incentives for private industry, is a promising start. (22 words)
cn: 西弗吉尼亚民主党议员罗伯特·勃德提出一项议案，从经济上激励私企，就是一个良好的开端。

en: Many see that the country is getting ready to build lots of new power plants to meet our energy needs. (20 words)
cn: 许多人看到，美国要建很多新的发电厂，以满足我们的能源需求。

en: If we are ever going to protect the atmosphere, it is crucial that those new plants be environmentally sound. (19 words)
cn: 如果我们想要一直保护大气层，新的电厂要对环境无害，这至关重要。

en: Of all the components of a good night’s sleep, dreams seem to be least within our control. (17 words)
cn: 要想晚上睡好觉的所有因素中，梦，似乎是最无法控制的一个。

en: In dreams, a window opens into a world where logic is suspended and dead people speak. (16 words)
cn: 梦，打开了一扇窗，通向的一个世界：逻辑暂时停摆，死人开口说话。

en: A century ago, Freud formulated his revolutionary theory that dreams were the disguised shadows of our unconscious desires and fears; by the late 1970s, neurologists had switched to thinking of them as just “mental noise”—the random byproducts of the neural-repair work that goes on during sleep. (47 words)
cn: 一个世纪前，弗洛伊德阐述了他革命性的理论：梦，在人们潜意识中，是欲望和恐惧看不见的影子；到了20世纪70年代末，神经病学家们转而认为梦是“精神噪音”，即睡眠中神经修复活动随机产生的副带影响。

en: Now researchers suspect that dreams are part of the mind’s emotional thermostat, regulating moods while the brain is “off-line.” (19 words)
cn: 目前，有些研究人员猜想，梦是大脑情感自动调节系统的组成部分，在大脑处于“离线”状态时，可以调整情绪。

en: And one leading authority says that these intensely powerful mental events can be not only harnessed but actually brought under conscious control, to help us sleep and feel better. (29 words)
cn: 一名最重要的权威人士说，梦这种异常强烈的精神活动不仅能可以驾驭，事实上还可以有意识地加以控制，以帮助我们睡得更好，感觉更棒。

en: “It’s your dream,” says Rosalind Cartwright, chair of psychology at Chicago’s Medical Center. (13 words)
cn: “梦是你的”，芝加哥医疗中心心理科主任罗莎琳·卡莱特说道。

en: “If you don’t like it, change it.” (7 words)
cn: “不喜欢，就改变它。”

en: Evidence from brain imaging supports this view. (7 words)
cn: 大脑成像提供的证据支持了以上观点。

en: The brain is as active during REM (rapid eye movement) sleep—when most vivid dreams occur—as it is when fully awake, says Dr. Eric Nofzinger at the University of Pittsburgh. (31 words)
cn: 匹兹堡大学的埃里克·诺夫辛格博士说，大多数鲜活的梦境都出现在快速动眼（REM）睡眠中，此时大脑很活跃，跟我们完全醒着一样。

en: But not all parts of the brain are equally involved; the limbic system (the “emotional brain”) is especially active, while the prefrontal cortex (the center of intellect and reasoning) is relatively quiet. (32 words)
cn: 但并非牵涉到了大脑的所有部分；大脑边缘系统（“情绪大脑”）尤其活跃，而前额叶皮质（思维和推理中心）则相对平静。

en: “We wake up from dreams happy or depressed, and those feelings can stay with us all day.” says Stanford sleep researcher Dr. William Dement. (24 words)
cn: 斯坦福睡眠研究员威廉·迪蒙特博士说：“我们从梦中醒来，或开心或沮丧，这些情绪会伴随我们一整天。”

en: The link between dreams and emotions shows up among the patients in Cartwright’s clinic. (14 words)
cn: 做梦和情绪之间的联系在卡特赖特诊所的病人身上就表现很明显。

en: Most people seem to have more bad dreams early in the night, progressing toward happier ones before awakening, suggesting that they are working through negative feelings generated during the day. (30 words)
cn: 多数人似乎会在前半夜做更多噩梦，在醒来之前会逐渐变得更快乐，这说明，他们再克服白天产生的负面情绪。

en: Because our conscious mind is occupied with daily life we don’t always think about the emotional significance of the day’s events—until, it appears, we begin to dream. (28 words)
cn: 因为日常生活占满了我们有意识的思维活动，所以我们不一定会去思考白天的事情对情绪的影响——似乎是，直到，我们开始做梦。

en: And this process need not be left to the unconscious. (10 words)
cn: 并且，没有必要让这个过程无意识地发生。

en: Cartwright believes one can exercise conscious control over recurring bad dreams. (11 words)
cn: 卡特赖特认为，人们可以练习有意识地控制噩梦重演。

en: As soon as you awaken, identify what is upsetting about the dream. (12 words)
cn: 一醒来，你就要弄明白，梦中困扰你的是什么。

en: Visualize how you would like it to end instead; the next time it occurs, try to wake up just enough to control its course. (24 words)
cn: 设想一下，你期待梦该怎么结束；下次再做梦，尽量正好在那个时间点醒过来，这样就能控制做梦的过程。

en: With much practice people can learn to, literally, do it in their sleep. (13 words)
cn: 多加练习，人们完全能够学会一边睡觉，一边控制做梦。

en: At the end of the day, there’s probably little reason to pay attention to our dreams at all unless they keep us from sleeping or “we wake up in a panic,” Cartwright says. (33 words)
cn: 说到底，我们或许几乎没有任何道理会去关注自己的梦，直到做梦让我们夜不能寐或者“在梦中惊醒”，卡特赖特这样说道。

en: Terrorism, economic uncertainties and general feelings of insecurity have increased people’s anxiety. (12 words)
cn: 恐怖主义、经济波动、还有普遍感觉到不安全，这些都加剧了人们的焦虑。

en: Those suffering from persistent nightmares should seek help from a therapist. (11 words)
cn: 长期噩梦不断、痛苦不堪的人应该寻求专家的帮助。

en: For the rest of us, the brain has its ways of working through bad feelings. (15 words)
cn: 而对其他人来说，大脑有自己的方式，可以消除不良情绪。

en: Sleep—or rather dream—on it and you’ll feel better in the morning. (13 words)
cn: 安然入睡，或最好是一夜好梦，早上醒来，你会感觉棒多了。

en: Americans no longer expect public figures, whether in speech or in writing, to command the English language with skill and gift. (21 words)
cn: 美国人对于公众人物熟练驾驭英语语言的才能，无论是口头的还是书面的，都没有任何期待。

en: Nor do they aspire to such command themselves. (8 words)
cn: 他们自己也不想去这么掌握语言。

en: In his latest book, Doing Our Own Thing: The Degradation of language and Music and Why We Should Like, Care, John McWhorter, a linguist and controversialist of mixed liberal and conservative views, sees the triumph of 1960s counter-culture as responsible for the decline of formal English. (46 words)
cn: 语言学家约翰·麦克沃特喜好争论，他的观点混杂着自由派与保守派的看法，在他最近的书《自行其是：语言和音乐的退化，为什么我们应该，呃，喜欢》中，这位学者认为 60 年代反文化运动的胜利要对正式英语的退化负责。

en: Blaming the permissive 1960s is nothing new, but this is not yet another criticism against the decline in education. (19 words)
cn: 责备放纵的六十年代毫无新意，但这还算不上是对教育滑坡的又一场批判。

en: Mr. McWhorter’s academic specialty is language history and change, and he sees the gradual disappearance of “whom”, for example, to be natural and no more regrettable than the loss of the case-endings of Old English. (35 words)
cn: 麦克沃特先生专治语言史和语言演变，他认为诸如“whom”这种单词的逐渐消失是自然的，跟古英语中词格尾缀的消失一样，都不是什么值得惋惜的事情。

en: But the cult of the authentic and the personal, “doing our own thing”, has spelt the death of formal speech, writing, poetry and music. (24 words)
cn: 但是，人们狂热地追求那种真我风采，想要特立独行，“自行其是”，导致了规范的口头语、书面语、诗歌和音乐的消亡。

en: While even the modestly educated sought an elevated tone when they put pen to paper before the 1960s, even the most well regarded writing since then has sought to capture spoken English on the page. (35 words)
cn: 在20世纪60年代以前，但凡受过一点教育的人在付诸笔墨时都会寻求一种高雅的格调；但从那之后，即使是最受关注的文章也都会试图把口语表现在纸张之上。

en: Equally, in poetry, the highly personal, performative genre is the only form that could claim real liveliness. (17 words)
cn: 同样，在诗歌中，高度个性化，极具表现力的风格成了表达真实生动的唯一形式。

en: In both oral and written English, talking is triumphing over speaking, spontaneity over craft. (14 words)
cn: 英语，无论是在口头语还是书面语中，说甚于讲，临场发挥甚于仔细推敲。

en: Illustrated with an entertaining array of examples from both high and low culture, the trend that Mr. McWhorter documents is unmistakable. (21 words)
cn: 麦克沃特用大量上流文化和通俗文化中有趣的例子来佐证，他记录的这种趋势是准确无误的。

en: But it is less clear, to take the question of his subtitle, why we should, like, care. (17 words)
cn: 但从其著作副标题中的疑问《为什么我们应该、呃，喜欢》来看，这种趋势似乎又不那么明确。

en: As a linguist, he acknowledges that all varieties of human language, including non-standard ones like Black English, can be powerfully expressive—there exists no language or dialect in the world that cannot convey complex ideas. (35 words)
cn: 作为语言学家，麦克沃特承认，所有的人类语言，包括像黑人英语这样的非标准语言，都具有强大的表达力 —— 世上任何语言或方言都能传达复杂的思想。

en: He is not arguing, as many do, that we can no longer think straight because we do not talk proper. (20 words)
cn: 他认为，与大多数人的看法不同，我们并不会因为表达不明确就导致思考不正确。

en: Russians have a deep love for their own language and carry large chunks of memorized poetry in their heads, while Italian politicians tend to elaborate speech that would seem old-fashioned to most English-speakers. (33 words)
cn: 俄罗斯人深爱自己的语言，脑海里有大量背诵过的诗歌；而意大利的政客往往会精心准备演讲，这种做法在大多数讲英语的人看来有点老派。

en: Mr. McWhorter acknowledges that formal language is not strictly necessary, and proposes no radical education reforms—he is really grieving over the loss of something beautiful more than useful. (29 words)
cn: 麦克沃特先生承认正式语言并不是一定需要，他还提议不要进行任何彻底的教育改革——他其实在为失去了美好的东西感到悲哀，而不会在意这些东西是否有用。

en: We now take our English “on paper plates instead of china”. (11 words)
cn: 现在，我们装英语的是“纸盘而非瓷盘”。

en: A shame, perhaps, but probably an inevitable one. (8 words)
cn: 或许，是遗憾，但可能已无法避免。

en: In spite of “endless talk of difference,”American society is an amazing machine for homogenizing people. (16 words)
cn: 尽管我们会“没完没了地讨论差异”，但美国社会却是一台惊人的机器，能使人们变得统一。

en: There is “the democratizing uniformity of dress and discourse, and the casualness and absence of deference” characteristic of popular culture. (20 words)
cn: 美国人衣着统一，言谈一致，都显得休闲随意且不拘礼节，这是美国流行文化的特征，美国人民自愿趋于这种统一。

en: People are absorbed into “a culture of consumption” launched by the 19th-century department stores that offered “vast arrays of goods in an elegant atmosphere. (24 words)
cn: 19 世纪开始百货商店倡导的“消费文化”深深地吸引着人们，这些商店“环境舒适、商品琳琅满目”。

en: Instead of intimate shops catering to a knowledgeable elite” these were stores “anyone could enter, regardless of class or background. (20 words)
cn: 不做精品小店，不迎合知识渊博的精英阶层，这些商店“人人都可以光临，不管其阶层和背景”。

en: This turned shopping into a public and democratic act.”(9 words)
cn: 这使得购物成为了一种公共且民主的行为。

en: The mass media, advertising and sports are other forces for homogenization. (11 words)
cn: 大众传媒、广告和体育是其他促成同化的力量。

en: Immigrants are quickly fitting into this common culture, which may not be altogether elevating but is hardly poisonous. (18 words)
cn: 移民很快地融入到这种大众文化中，这种情况也许不会对美国大众文化有所提升，但也不会对其产生不利影响。

en: Writing for the National Immigration Forum, Gregory Rodriguez reports that today’s immigration is neither at unprecedented levels nor resistant to assimilation. (21 words)
cn: 格雷戈里·罗德里格斯在为《国家移民论坛》撰稿时指出，今天的移民既没有达到空前的水平，也不会抵制同化。

en: In 1998 immigrants were 9.8 percent of population; in 1900, 13.6 percent. (12 words)
In the 10 years prior to 1990, 3.1 immigrants arrived for every 1,000 residents; in the 10 years prior to 1890, 9.2 for every 1,000. (25 words)
cn: 在1990年以前的十年之中，每千位居民中，新到的移民人口是3.1；而在1890年以前的十年之中，每千位居民中，是9.2。

en: Now, consider three indices of assimilation—language, home ownership and intermarriage. (11 words)
cn: 现在，我们来看看同化的三个指标——语言、房产和通婚。

en: The 1990 Census revealed that “a majority of immigrants from each of the fifteen most common countries of origin spoke English ‘well’ or ‘very well’ after ten years of residence.” (30 words)
cn: 1990 年人口普查显示，“从 15 个最常见的原属国中任何一个国家来的大多数移民在居住 10 年以后，英语都说得‘好’或‘很好’。”

en: The children of immigrants tend to be bilingual and proficient in English. (12 words)
cn: 移民的子女往往会说两种语言，精通英语。

en: “By the third generation, the original language is lost in the majority of immigrant families.” (15 words)
cn: “到了第三代，原来的语言在大多数移民家庭中都消失了。”

en: Hence the description of America as a “graveyard” for languages. (10 words)
cn: 因此，有人说美国是“语言的坟墓”。

en: By 1996 foreign-born immigrants who had arrived before 1970 had a home ownership rate of 75.6 percent, higher than the 69.8 percent rate among native-born Americans. (26 words)
cn: 到 1996 年，出生于国外的、并在 1970 年前到达美国的移民中，75.6%有房产，高于土生土长的美国人只有 69.8%的这个比率。

en: Foreign-born Asians and Hispanics “have higher rates of intermarriage than do U.S.-born whites and blacks.” (15 words)
cn: 国外出生的亚裔和拉美裔美国人“与异族通婚的比率比美国出生的白人和黑人要更高”。

en: By the third generation, one third of Hispanic women are married to non-Hispanics, and 41 percent of Asian-American women are married to non-Asians. (23 words)
cn: 到了第三代，有三分之一的拉美裔女性与非拉美裔男性结婚，有 41%亚裔美国女性与非亚裔男性结婚。

en: Rodriguez notes that children in remote villages around the world are fans of superstars like Arnold Schwarzenegger and Garth Brooks, yet “some Americans fear that immigrants living within the United States remain somehow immune to the nation’s assimilative power.” (39 words)
cn: 罗德里格斯指出，即使是世界各地偏远村庄的孩子都是阿诺·施瓦辛格和加思·布鲁克斯等超级明星的粉丝，但是“一些美国人却担心，生活在美国的移民不知何故，依然没有受到这个国家同化力的影响”。

en: Are there divisive issues and pockets of seething anger in America? (11 words)
cn: 美国有社会分裂的问题和愤愤不平的群体吗？

en: Indeed. (1 words)
cn: 当然。

en: It is big enough to have a bit of everything. (10 words)
cn: 这个国家如此之大，无奇不有。

en: But particularly when viewed against America’s turbulent past, today’s social indices hardly suggest a dark and deteriorating social environment. (19 words)
cn: 但是，特别是与美国动荡的过去相比，今天社会的各项指标几乎看不出任何迹象说明社会环境黑暗且不断恶化。

en: Stratford-on-Avon, as we all know, has only one industry—William Shakespeare—but there are two distinctly separate and increasingly hostile branches. (21 words)
cn: 文河畔斯特拉福，众所周知，只有一个产业：威廉·莎士比亚，但这儿却有两个泾渭分明且日益对立的分支。

en: There is the Royal Shakespeare Company (RSC), which presents superb productions of the plays at the Shakespeare Memorial Theatre on the Avon. (22 words)
cn: 一个是皇家莎士比亚公司（简称 RSC），它在埃文河畔的莎士比亚纪念剧院里演出精彩的莎剧作品。

en: And there are the townsfolk who largely live off the tourists who come, not to see the plays, but to look at Anne Hathaway’s Cottage, Shakespeare’s birthplace and the other sights. (31 words)
cn: 而另一个分支是小镇上的居民，他们主要靠游客营生；这些游客来这里不是看剧，而是来参观安妮·海瑟薇的小屋、莎士比亚的出生地和其他景点。（附：安妮是莎士比亚的妻子。译者注）

en: The worthy residents of Stratford doubt that the theatre adds a penny to their revenue. (15 words)
cn: 斯特拉福镇上贤达的居民认为剧院不会给他们收入多增加一个子儿。

en: They frankly dislike the RSC’s actors, them with their long hair and beards and sandals and noisiness. (17 words)
cn: 他们直言讨厌皇家莎士比亚公司的演员，不喜欢他们长头发，大胡子，拖着凉鞋，吵吵嚷嚷。

en: It’s all deliciously ironic when you consider that Shakespeare, who earns their living, was himself an actor (with a beard) and did his share of noise-making. (26 words)
cn: 但当你想到作为他们摇钱树的莎士比亚本人当时也是个演员，留大胡子，也一起大吵大闹，这无疑是一种绝妙的讽刺。

en: The tourist streams are not entirely separate. (7 words)
cn: 游客客流并不是完全分开的。

en: The sightseers who come by bus—and often take in Warwick Castle and Blenheim Palace on the side—don’t usually see the plays, and some of them are even surprised to find a theatre in Stratford. (36 words)
cn: 坐巴士来的观光客常常会去游览华威城堡和布莱海姆宫，但一般不会去看莎剧演出，其中一些人甚至会对在斯特拉福能找到剧院感到惊讶。

en: However, the playgoers do manage a little sight-seeing along with their playgoing. (12 words)
cn: 但是，看剧的人除了看剧，还会抽点时间观光游览。

en: It is the playgoers, the RSC contends, who bring in much of the town’s revenue because they spend the night (some of them four or five nights) pouring cash into the hotels and restaurants. (34 words)
cn: 皇家莎士比亚公司坚持认为，正式这些看剧的，给市镇带来大量收入，因为他们会住宿，有的住四五个晚上，撒钱给宾馆和饭店。

en: The sightseers can take in everything and get out of town by nightfall. (13 words)
cn: 然而观光客会在天黑前参观完毕一走了之。

en: The townsfolk don’t see it this way and local council does not contribute directly to the subsidy of the Royal Shakespeare Company. (22 words)
cn: 当地居民可不这么看，当地政府也没有直接给皇家莎士比亚公司补贴。

en: Stratford cries poor traditionally. (4 words)
cn: 斯特拉福向来都爱哭穷。

en: Nevertheless every hotel in town seems to be adding a new wing or cocktail lounge. (15 words)
cn: 不过，镇上每一个酒店似乎都在新增客房或者酒廊。

en: Hilton is building its own hotel there, which you may be sure will be decorated with Hamlet Hamburger Bars, the Lear Lounge, the Banquo Banqueting Room, and so forth, and will be very expensive. (34 words)
cn: 希尔顿也在那里建自己的酒店，可以肯定，酒店装修有哈姆雷特汉堡酒吧、李尔休息室、班柯宴会等等，并且入住价格会非常昂贵。

en: Anyway, the townsfolk can’t understand why the Royal Shakespeare Company needs a subsidy. (13 words)
cn: 反正居民就是不能理解为啥皇家莎士比亚公司还需要补贴。

en: (The theatre has broken attendance records for three years in a row. (12 words)
cn: 剧院上座率连续三年打破记录。

en: Last year its 1,431 seats were 94 percent occupied all year long and this year they’ll do better.) (18 words)
cn: 去年一整年剧院1431个座位的上座率达到94%，今年还会更高。

en: The reason, of course, is that costs have rocketed and ticket prices have stayed low. (15 words)
cn: 当然，剧院的理由是，成本飙升，票价太低。

en: It would be a shame to raise prices too much because it would drive away the young people who are Stratford’s most attractive clientele. (24 words)
cn: 票价提高太多可能会有点遗憾，因为这样会把年轻人们赶走，他们是斯特拉福最有魅力的顾客。

en: They come entirely for the plays, not the sights. (9 words)
cn: 他们完全是来看剧的，而不是观光。

en: They all seem to look alike (though they come from all over)—lean, pointed, dedicated faces, wearing jeans and sandals, eating their buns and bedding down for the night on the flagstones outside the theatre to buy the 20 seats and 80 standing-room tickets held for the sleepers and sold to them when the box office opens at 10:30 a.m. (60 words)
cn: 他们个个看起来都一样（尽管来自四面八方）——面容清瘦、棱角分明、神情执着，穿着牛仔、拖着凉鞋，啃着面包，晚上睡在剧院外石板上，等上午10 点半售票处开门，购买专门为这些露宿剧院外的人预留的 20 张坐票和 80 张站票。

en: When prehistoric man arrived in new parts of the world, something strange happened to the large animals. (17 words)
cn: 史前人类每到一个新地方，那里的大型动物就会发生奇怪的事情。

en: They suddenly became extinct. (4 words)
cn: 它们突然灭绝。

en: Smaller species survived. (3 words)
cn: 小型动物却存活了下来。

en: The large, slow-growing animals were easy game, and were quickly hunted to extinction. (13 words)
cn: 体型庞大、生长缓慢的动物易于猎取，很快猎杀殆尽。

en: Now something similar could be happening in the oceans. (9 words)
cn: 现在，海洋中可能发生了类似的事情。

en: That the seas are being overfished has been known for years. (11 words)
cn: 多年来，海洋过度捕捞，人尽皆知。

en: What researchers such as Ransom Myers and Boris Worm have shown is just how fast things are changing. (18 words)
cn: 兰塞姆·梅耶斯和鲍里斯·沃尔姆等之类研究人员已经表明，沧海桑田，事情正在迅速变化。

en: They have looked at half a century of data from fisheries around the world. (14 words)
cn: 他们查阅了世界各地渔场的 50 年来数据。

en: Their methods do not attempt to estimate the actual biomass (the amount of living biological matter) of fish species in particular parts of the ocean, but rather changes in that biomass over time. (33 words)
cn: 他们的研究方法并不是要试图估算海洋中特定区域鱼类的实际生物量（活着生物的总数），而是要估算这些生物量随着时间而产生的变化。

en: According to their latest paper published in Nature, the biomass of large predators (animals that kill and eat other animals) in a new fishery is reduced on average by 80% within 15 years of the start of exploitation. (38 words)
cn: 他们最近在《自然》杂志上发表的文章表明，一个新渔场的大型食肉动物（杀死并吃掉其它动物的动物）总量在开始捕捞后的 15 年中平均减少了 80%。

en: In some long-fished areas, it has halved again since then. (10 words)
cn: 在一些长期捕捞的区域，食肉动物总量在此之后又进一步减半。

en: Dr. Worm acknowledges that these figures are conservative. (8 words)
cn: 沃尔姆博士承认，这些数字还是保守数字。

en: One reason for this is that fishing technology has improved. (10 words)
cn: 其中一个原因在于捕鱼技术进步了。

en: Today’s vessels can find their prey using satellites and sonar, which were not available 50 years ago. (17 words)
cn: 现在的轮船可以使用卫星和声呐来找到猎物，而在50年前还没有这些技术。

en: That means a higher proportion of what is in the sea is being caught, so the real difference between present and past is likely to be worse than the one recorded by changes in catch sizes. (36 words)
cn: 这意味着人们正在捕捞更多的海洋生物，所以今昔之间真正差距可能会比捕捞量不同所反映出的差距还要更糟糕。

en: In the early days, too, longlines would have been more saturated with fish. (13 words)
cn: 早期，多钩长线本可以挂满更多的鱼。

en: Some individuals would therefore not have been caught, since no baited hooks would have been available to trap them, leading to an underestimate of fish stocks in the past. (29 words)
cn: 因为没有足够带饵的鱼钩来捕鱼，因此总有一些漏网之鱼，这就导致低估了过去的鱼类资源。

en: Furthermore, in the early days of longline fishing, a lot of fish were lost to sharks after they had been hooked. (21 words)
cn: 此外，早期用多钩长线捕鱼的时候，很多鱼上钩了又被鲨鱼吃掉了。

en: That is no longer a problem, because there are fewer sharks around now. (13 words)
cn: 这根本不再是问题，因为现在附近的鲨鱼更少了。

en: Dr. Myers and Dr. Worm argue that their work gives a correct baseline, which future management efforts must take into account. (21 words)
cn: Myers 博士和 Worm 博士声称，他们的工作已经确定了一个正确的底线，这个方针是未来管理中必须要使用的。

en: They believe the data support an idea current among marine biologists, that of the “shifting baseline.” (16 words)
cn: 他们相信自己的数据支持一个目前在海洋生物学家中非常流行的观点，那就是“变化的底线”。

en: The notion is that people have failed to detect the massive changes which have happened in the ocean because they have been looking back only a relatively short time into the past. (32 words)
cn: 这个概念就是说人们没有观察到海洋中发生的巨大变化，因为他们只是回顾从过去以来相对较短的一段时间。

en: That matters because theory suggests that the maximum sustainable yield that can be cropped from a fishery comes when the biomass of a target species is about 50% of its original levels. (32 words)
cn: 这一点很重要，因为从理论上看，当一种目标生物的生物量大约达到原始水平的50%时，就可以从一个渔场收获到最大程度的可持续发展的产量。

en: Most fisheries are well below that, which is a bad way to do business. (14 words)
cn: 然而大多数渔场远远低于这个数字，这样做生意是不对的。

en: Many things make people think artists are weird. (8 words)
cn: 许多事情让人们觉得艺术家都是奇葩。

en: But the weirdest may be this: artists’ only job is to explore emotions, and yet they choose to focus on the ones that feel bad. (25 words)
cn: 可最奇葩的也许是：艺术家的唯一工作应该是探索情感，而他们则喜欢去关注那些令人不快的情感。

en: This wasn’t always so. (4 words)
cn: 情况并非总是如此。

en: The earliest forms of art, like painting and music, are those best suited for expressing joy. (16 words)
cn: 艺术的最早形式，像绘画和音乐，都最适合表达快乐。

en: But somewhere from the 19th century onward, more artists began seeing happiness as meaningless, phony or, worst of all, boring, as we went from Wordsworth’s daffodils to Baudelaire’s flowers of evil. (31 words)
cn: 可是从 19 世纪的某个时期开始，越来越多的艺术家开始把快乐看成是毫无意义、虚伪矫饰、甚至是无聊至极，我们从华兹华斯的《水仙》到波德莱尔的《恶之花》就可以看出这种变化。

en: You could argue that art became more skeptical of happiness because modern times have seen so much misery. (18 words)
cn: 你可能会争辩说，艺术之所以越来越怀疑幸福的存在，是因为现代社会目睹了太多的苦难。

en: But it’s not as if earlier times didn’t know perpetual war, disaster and the massacre of innocents. (17 words)
cn: 这说得好像早期没有经历过战争连连、灾难频频、滥杀无辜。

en: The reason, in fact, may be just the opposite: there is too much damn happiness in the world today. (19 words)
cn: 事实上，原因可能恰好相反：当今世上有太多该死的幸福。

en: After all, what is the one modern form of expression almost completely dedicated to depicting happiness? (16 words)
cn: 归根结底，究竟是哪种现代表现形式几乎完全只描绘幸福呢？

en: Advertising. (1 words)
cn: 广告。

en: The rise of anti-happy art almost exactly tracks the emergence of mass media, and with it, a commercial culture in which happiness is not just an ideal but an ideology. (30 words)
cn: 大众传媒的出现，几乎同时带来了反幸福艺术的兴起；随之兴起还有一种商业文化，在这种文化中，幸福不仅是一个理想，而且还是一种观念。

en: People in earlier eras were surrounded by reminders of misery. (10 words)
cn: 早期的人们总萦绕在悲痛之中。

en: They worked until exhausted, lived with few protections and died young. (11 words)
cn: 他们工作到筋疲力尽，生活没有什么保障，年纪轻轻就命丧黄泉。

en: In the West, before mass communication and literacy, the most powerful mass medium was the church, which reminded worshippers that their souls were in danger and that they would someday be meat for worms. (34 words)
cn: 在西方，在大众传播和读写能力普及之前，最强有力的大众媒体是教会，它提醒信徒他们的灵魂处于危险之中，身体早晚要喂蛆。

en: Given all this, they did not exactly need their art to be a bummer too. (15 words)
cn: 有鉴于此，他们并不需要让自己的艺术也这样扫兴。

en: Today the messages the average Westerner is surrounded with are not religious but commercial, and forever happy. (17 words)
cn: 现在，普通西方人身边充斥着的信息不是宗教信息，而是商业信息，并且永远都是让人快乐的。

en: Fast-food eaters, news anchors, text messengers, all smiling, smiling, smiling. (10 words)
cn: 吃快餐的、新闻主持人、还有发信息的，都在笑、笑、笑。

en: Our magazines feature beaming celebrities and happy families in perfect homes. (11 words)
cn: 杂志会突出刊登满面春风的名人和他们美满幸福的家庭。

en: And since these messages have an agenda—to lure us to open our wallets—they make the very idea of happiness seem unreliable. (23 words)
cn: 这些信息都有一个目标——即引诱我们打开钱包——这使得幸福这个概念看起来不那么可靠。

en: “Celebrate!” commanded the ads for the arthritis drug Celebrex, before we found out it could increase the risk of heart attacks. (21 words)
cn: “普天同庆！”抗关节炎药西乐葆的广告发出了这样号召，随后我们却发现这种药物能增加患心脏病的风险。

en: But what we forget—what our economy depends on us forgetting—is that happiness is more than pleasure without pain. (20 words)
cn: 但我们忘记了——我们的经济依赖于我们忘记——幸福绝不仅仅是没有痛苦的快乐。

en: The things that bring the greatest joy carry the greatest potential for loss and disappointment. (15 words)
cn: 带给我们最大快乐的东西，也最有可能带给我们失望和损失。

en: Today, surrounded by promises of easy happiness, we need art to tell us, as religion once did, Memento mori: remember that you will die, that everything ends, and that happiness comes not in denying this but in living with it. (40 words)
cn: 现在，幸福唾手可得的诺言包围着我们，我们需要艺术来告诉我们，正如宗教曾经做过的那样，死亡无可避免：记住人终有一死，一切终会结束，否认不如接受，幸福终会到来。

en: It’s a message even more bitter than a clove cigarette, yet, somehow, a breath of fresh air. (17 words)
cn: 这样说，感觉比丁香烟更苦涩，但不知何故，却是一缕清风。

en: If you were to examine the birth certificates of every soccer player in 2006’s World Cup tournament, you would most likely find a noteworthy quirk: elite soccer players are more likely to have been born in the earlier months of the year than in the later months. (47 words)
cn: 如果你调查一下 2006 年世界杯上每一个足球运动员的出生证明，你很有可能发现一个有趣的怪现象：优秀足球运动员更可能出生在某年的头几个月而不是后几个月。

en: If you then examined the European national youth teams that feed the World Cup and professional ranks, you would find this strange phenomenon to be even more pronounced. (28 words)
cn: 如果你再调查一下那些为世界杯和各大职业联赛提供球员的欧洲国家青年队，你会发现这一奇怪的现象更为明显。

en: What might account for this strange phenomenon? (7 words)
cn: 导致这一奇怪现象的原因何在？

en: Here are a few guesses: a) certain astrological signs confer superior soccer skills; b) winter-born babies tend to have higher oxygen capacity, which increases soccer stamina; c) soccer-mad parents are more likely to conceive children in springtime, at the annual peak of soccer mania; d) none of the above. (49 words)
cn: 猜测如下：a）某种星象学的征兆使人具备高超的足球技术；b）冬季出生的婴儿可能会血氧含量更高，这在踢足球时耐力会更好；c）春季是每年足球狂热的鼎盛时期，迷恋足球的父母更可能在此时怀孕；d）以上各项都不对。

en: Anders Ericsson, a 58-year-old psychology professor at Florida State University, says he believes strongly in “none of the above.” (19 words)
cn: 58岁的安德斯·埃里克森是佛罗里达州立大学的一名心理学教授，他说，他坚信“以上各项都不对”。

en: Ericsson grew up in Sweden, and studied nuclear engineering until he realized he would have more opportunity to conduct his own research if he switched to psychology. (27 words)
cn: 埃里克森在瑞典长大，做核工程研究，后来他认识到如果转向心理学，就会有更多机会从事自己的研究。

en: His first experiment, nearly 30 years ago, involved memory: training a person to hear and then repeat a random series of numbers. (22 words)
cn: 他的首次试验是大约30年前进行的，就涉及到记忆：训练一个人先听一组随机的数字，然后复述出来。

en: “With the first subject, after about 20 hours of training, his digit span had risen from 7 to 20,” Ericsson recalls. (21 words)
cn: “第一个实验对象，大约经过20个小时的训练，他能记忆的数字广度从7个上升到20个，”埃利克森回忆说。

en: “He kept improving, and after about 200 hours of training he had risen to over 80 numbers.” (17 words)
cn: “第一个实验对象，大约经过20个小时的训练，他能记忆的数字广度从7个上升到20个，”埃利克森回忆说。“他不断进步，大约200个小时的训练后，数字提高到80多个了。”

en: This success, coupled with later research showing that memory itself is not genetically determined, led Ericsson to conclude that the act of memorizing is more of a cognitive exercise than an intuitive one. (33 words)
cn: 这一实验的成功，加上后来那些表明记忆本身不是遗传决定的研究，使得埃里克森得出结论：记忆是一种认知训练，而不是一种直觉。

en: In other words, whatever inborn differences two people may exhibit in their abilities to memorize, those differences are swamped by how well each person “encodes” the information. (27 words)
cn: 换言之，不管两个人的记忆力表现出有多大的先天差异，每个人“编码”信息的能力都会消除这些差异。

en: And the best way to learn how to encode information meaningfully, Ericsson determined, was a process known as deliberate practice. (20 words)
cn: 埃里克森确信，学习如何有目的地解读信息的最佳方法，众所周知，是刻意练习。

en: Deliberate practice entails more than simply repeating a task. (9 words)
cn: 刻意练习需要的不仅仅是简单地重复一项任务。

en: Rather, it involves setting specific goals, obtaining immediate feedback and concentrating as much on technique as on outcome. (18 words)
cn: 更确切地说，它包括设定明确的目标，获得即时的反馈，重视技巧，同样重视结果。

en: Ericsson and his colleagues have thus taken to studying expert performers in a wide range of pursuits, including soccer. (19 words)
cn: 因此，埃里克森和他的同事开始研究各种职业中的杰出表现者，包括足球。

en: They gather all the data they can, not just performance statistics and biographical details but also the results of their own laboratory experiments with high achievers. (26 words)
cn: 他们收集一切可能得到的资料，不仅包括性能统计数据和详细传记资料，还包括他们自己对成功人士进行的实验室实验结果。

en: Their work makes a rather startling assertion: the trait we commonly call talent is highly overrated. (16 words)
cn: 他们的研究得出了一个惊人的论断：我们过分高估了所谓的天赋这种品质。

en: Or, put another way, expert performers—whether in memory or surgery, ballet or computer programming—are nearly always made, not born. (21 words)
cn: 或者，换一种说法，杰出表现者——无论是在记忆方面还是外科方面，无论是跳芭蕾的，还是计算机编程的——几乎都是后天练就，而非天生的。

en: For the past several years, the Sunday newspaper supplement Parade has featured a column called “Ask Marilyn.” (17 words)
cn: 近几年来，《星期日报》的增刊《大观》重点推出了一个名为“玛丽莲答问”答问的专栏。

en: People are invited to query Marilyn vos Savant, who at age 10 had tested at a mental level of someone about 23 years old; that gave her an IQ of 228—the highest score ever recorded. (36 words)
cn: 邀请人们向玛丽莲·沃斯·莎凡特提问，她10岁时测定的脑力水平相当于一个23岁的人，智商高达228——有史以来最高水平。

en: IQ tests ask you to complete verbal and visual analogies, to envision paper after it has been folded and cut, and to deduce numerical sequences, among other similar tasks. (29 words)
cn: 智商测试要求人们完成一些文字和图像的类比分析，想象纸张折叠裁剪后的形状，推算数列，及其他类似的题目。

en: So it is a bit confusing when vos Savant fields such queries from the average Joe (whose IQ is 100) as, What’s the difference between love and fondness? (28 words)
cn: 所以，当沃斯·莎凡特要回答普通人（智商为100）类似“爱与喜欢之间的区别是什么？”或者“幸运与巧合的本质是什么？”

en: Or what is the nature of luck and coincidence? (9 words)
cn: 这样的问题时，大家就感觉有点莫名其妙。

en: It’s not obvious how the capacity to visualize objects and to figure out numerical patterns suits one to answer questions that have eluded some of the best poets and philosophers. (30 words)
cn: 一个人具备物体空间想象力和计算数字模型的能力，又如何能够回答连一些最优秀的诗人和哲学家都无法理解的问题，这一点我们无从得知。

en: Clearly, intelligence encompasses more than a score on a test. (10 words)
cn: 显然，智力包含的不仅仅是一次测试所得的分数。

en: Just what does it mean to be smart? (8 words)
cn: 只是，聪明究竟意味着什么？

en: How much of intelligence can be specified, and how much can we learn about it from neurology, genetics, computer science and other fields? (23 words)
cn: 智力，有多少是我们能说得清道得明的？又能有多少，是我们可以从神经学、遗传学、计算机科学或者其他领域中去了解到的？

en: The defining term of intelligence in humans still seems to be the IQ score, even though IQ tests are not given as often as they used to be. (28 words)
cn: 界定人类智力的术语似乎仍然是智商分数，即使人们并不像以前那样经常进行智商测试。

en: The test comes primarily in two forms: the Stanford-Binet Intelligence Scale and the Wechsler Intelligence Scales (both come in adult and children’s version). (23 words)
cn: 智商测试主要有为两种形式：斯坦福-比奈智力量表和维克斯勒智力量表（两者都包含成人版和儿童版）。

en: Generally costing several hundred dollars, they are usually given only by psychologists, although variations of them populate bookstores and the World Wide Web. (23 words)
cn: 一般花费要几百美元，这两种测试通常由心理学家来进行，尽管书店和互联网上到处充斥着各种这样的测试。

en: Superhigh scores like vos Savant’s are no longer possible, because scoring is now based on a statistical population distribution among age peers, rather than simply dividing the mental age by the chronological age and multiplying by 100. (37 words)
cn: 像沃斯·莎凡特那样的超高分数已经不在可能出现了，因为现在的分数依据的是同龄群体的统计人口分布，而不是简单地用心理年龄除以实际年龄再乘以100。

en: Other standardized tests, such as the Scholastic Assessment Test (SAT) and the Graduate Record Exam (GRE), capture the main aspects of IQ tests. (23 words)
cn: 其他 标准化测试，比如赛达（SAT）以及研究生入学考试（GRE），都充分体现了智商测试的主要特点。

en: Such standardized tests may not assess all the important elements necessary to succeed in school and in life, argues Robert J. Sternberg. (22 words)
cn: 罗伯特·J·斯腾伯格认为，这样的标准化测试或许并非能够评估要获得学业成功和生活成功必须的所有重要因素。

en: In his article “How Intelligent Is Intelligence Testing?”, Sternberg notes that traditional tests best assess analytical and verbal skills but fail to measure creativity and practical knowledge, components also critical to problem solving and life success. (36 words)
cn: 在其名为《智商测试有多明智？》的文章中，斯腾伯格指出，传统测试能很好地评估分析能力和言语能力，但却无法评估创造能力和实践知识，而这两者是解决问题和在生活中取得成功的关键因素。

en: Moreover, IQ tests do not necessarily predict so well once populations or situations change. (14 words)
cn: 而且，一旦人口或环境发生变化，智商测试就未必能够准确预测。

en: Research has found that IQ predicted leadership skills when the tests were given under low-stress conditions, but under high-stress conditions, IQ was negatively correlated with leadership—that is, it predicted the opposite. (32 words)
cn: 研究发现，如果在压力较小进行测试，智商就可以预测出领导才能，但如果压力大，智商测试所得的结果与领导才能是负相关的，也就是说，预测的结果与实际是相反的。

en: Anyone who has toiled through SAT will testify that test-taking skill also matters, whether it’s knowing when to guess or what questions to skip. (24 words)
cn: 任何苦战过过赛达（SAT）的人都会认为，应试能力也很重要，比如知道何时该蒙，或者知道哪些问题该放弃。

en: During the past generation, the American middle-class family that once could count on hard work and fair play to keep itself financially secure has been transformed by economic risk and new realities. (32 words)
cn: 在上一代人中，美国中产阶级家庭原本可以靠辛勤劳动和公平竞争就能保持稳定的经济状况，如今因经济风险和刚出现的实际情况改变了。

en: Now a pink slip, a bad diagnosis, or a disappearing spouse can reduce a family from solidly middle class to newly poor in a few months. (26 words)
cn: 如今，一张解雇小粉条、一份恶性诊断书或者配偶去世，这些都可以使稳固的中产阶级在几个月之内就沦为新的贫困户。

en: In just one generation, millions of mothers have gone to work, transforming basic family economics. (15 words)
cn: 只隔了一代人，数百万母亲出去工作，从而转变了家庭的基本经济状况。

en: Scholars, policymakers, and critics of all stripes have debated the social implications of these changes, but few have looked at the side effect: family risk has risen as well. (29 words)
cn: 学者、政策制定者、还有各类批评人士都在讨论这些变化可能带来的社会效应，但很少有人看到这些变化的副作用：家庭风险也增加了。

en: Today’s families have budgeted to the limits of their new two-paycheck status. (12 words)
cn: 现在各个家庭的开支预算已经达到了双薪收入这种新经济状况的最大极限。

en: As a result, they have lost the parachute they once had in times of financial setback—a back-up earner (usually Mom) who could go into the workforce if the primary earner got laid off or fell sick. (37 words)
cn: 这样一来，他们失去了在经济困难时期一度拥有的保护伞——一个可以挣钱的后备力量，通常是妈妈，她可以在家里主要挣钱的人下岗或者生病的时候出去工作。

en: This “added-worker effect” could support the safety net offered by unemployment insurance or disability insurance to help families weather bad times. (21 words)
cn: 这种“额外劳动者效应”可以加强由失业保险或伤残保险提供的安全网，以帮助家庭渡过难关。

en: But today, a disruption to family fortunes can no longer be made up with extra income from an otherwise-stay-at-home partner. (20 words)
cn: 但现在，家庭财产的损失再也不可能通过原本该呆在家里的另一半的额外收入来弥补了。

en: During the same period, families have been asked to absorb much more risk in their retirement income. (17 words)
cn: 与此同时，家庭还需在其退休收入上承担更多风险。

en: Steelworkers, airline employees, and now those in the auto industry are joining millions of families who must worry about interest rates, stock market fluctuation, and the harsh reality that they may outlive their retirement money. (35 words)
cn: 钢铁工人、航空公司职员、现在还有汽车行业的人都正在加入到数以百万计的家庭行列，这些家庭必须担心利率、股市波动以及退休金不够养老的严峻现实。

en: For much of the past year, President Bush campaigned to move Social Security to a savings-account model, with retirees trading much or all of their guaranteed payments for payments depending on investment returns. (33 words)
cn: 在去年的大部分时间里，小布什总统一直致力于将社保变成一种储蓄账户模式，让退休人员将其大部分或全部有保障的退休金用来投资，靠投资回报获得收益。

en: For younger families, the picture is not any better. (9 words)
cn: 对于更年轻的家庭来说，情况也不好。

en: Both the absolute cost of healthcare and the share of it borne by families have risen—and newly fashionable health-savings plans are spreading from legislative halls to Wal-Mart workers, with much higher deductibles and a large new dose of investment risk for families’ future healthcare. (45 words)
cn: 医保的绝对成本以及家庭需要承担的部分都提高——而且，最近流行的健康储蓄计划正在从立法机关推广到沃尔玛员工，这意味着医保免赔额度更高，还家庭未来的医保带来许多新的投资风险。

en: Even demographics are working against the middle class family, as the odds of having a weak elderly parent—and all the attendant need for physical and financial assistance—have jumped eightfold in just one generation. (35 words)
cn: 即使是人口统计也对中产阶层家庭不利，因为父母双亲有一个可能会年迈体弱的几率——以及随之而来需要付出的物质和经济援助的几率——在仅仅一代人的时间里就飞快增长为原来的8倍。

en: From the middle-class family perspective, much of this, understandably, looks far less like an opportunity to exercise more financial responsibility, and a good deal more like a frightening acceleration of the wholesale shift of financial risk onto their already overburdened shoulders. (41 words)
cn: 可以理解，这在中产阶级家庭眼里，很大程度上看上去远不像是有一个机会去履行更多的经济责任，而更像是加速把大部分的经济风险转移到本来就不堪重负的肩膀上，这种情况令人发指。

en: The financial fallout has begun, and the political fallout may not be far behind. (14 words)
cn: 经济方面的后果已经显现，政治影响可能也不远了。

en: It never rains but it pours. (6 words)
cn: 祸不单行。

en: Just as bosses and boards have finally sorted out their worst accounting and compliance troubles, and improved their feeble corporation governance, a new problem threatens to earn them—especially in America—the sort of nasty headlines that inevitably lead to heads rolling in the executive suite: data insecurity. (48 words)
cn: 就在老板和董事会终于解决了最棘手的财务和规章问题，改善了公司脆弱的管理之后，又有一个新的问题产生了威胁，即：数据不安全；这个问题，尤其是在美国，恐怕要让他们出现在那种恶心人的新闻头条上，必然会导致管理人员受到处罚。

en: Left, until now, to odd, low-level IT staff to put right, and seen as a concern only of data-rich industries such as banking, telecoms and air travel, information protection is now high on the boss’s agenda in businesses of every variety. (41 words)
cn: 迄今为止，数据保护一直是留给那些临时的低级别信息技术方面的员工来做，并且仅仅是那些像银行、电信以及航空等拥有大量数据资源的产业才会担心的一件事情，如今则成为各行各业老板议事日程中头等大事。

en: Several massive leakages of customer and employee data this year—from organizations as diverse as Time Warner, the American defense contractor Science Applications International Corp and even the University of California, Berkeley—have left managers hurriedly peering into their intricate IT systems and business processes in search of potential vulnerabilities. (50 words)
cn: 今年几起客户和员工数据的重大泄密事件发生在像时代华纳、美国国防部合同商科学应用国际公司、甚至是加州大学伯克利分校等不同的机构。这些事件使得管理人员匆忙去检查其复杂的信息技术系统和业务流程，以便寻找潜在的隐患。

en: “Data is becoming an asset which needs to be guarded as much as any other asset,” says Haim Mendelson of Stanford University’s business School. (24 words)
cn: “数据俨然成为了财富，需要多加保护，跟其他财富无异，”斯坦福大学商学院的海姆·曼德尔松如是说道。

en: “The ability to guard customer data is the key to market value, which the board is responsible for on behalf of shareholders”. (22 words)
cn: “保护客户数据的能力是确保市场价值的关键，董事会代表股东利益对市场价值负责”。

en: Indeed, just as there is the concept of Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP), perhaps it is time for GASP, Generally Accepted Security Practices, suggested Eli Noam of New York’s Columbia Business School. (32 words)
cn: 纽约哥伦比亚商学院的伊利·诺姆建议说，事实上，我们有公认会计准则（GAAP）这一概念，一样，或许现在是时候该建立公认安全准则（GASP）了。

en: “Setting the proper investment level for security, redundancy, and recovery is a management issue, not a technical one,” he says. (20 words)
cn: “为数据的安全、备份以及恢复确定适当的投资标准是一个管理问题，不是技术问题，”他说道。

en: The mystery is that this should come as a surprise to any boss. (13 words)
cn: 难以理解的是，所有老板对此都觉得诧异。

en: Surely it should be obvious to the dimmest executive that trust, that most valuable of economic assets, is easily destroyed and hugely expensive to restore—and that few things are more likely to destroy trust than a company letting sensitive personal data get into the wrong hands. (47 words)
cn: 即使是最笨的管理人员必定明白，信任是最有价值的经济资产，容易受损，一旦受损，恢复代价高昂；他们也明白，一个公司让敏感的个人数据落入恶人手中，对信任的损害之大，少有其他能与之匹敌。

en: The current state of affairs may have been encouraged—though not justified—by the lack of legal penalty (in America, but not Europe) for data leakage. (26 words)
cn: 目前事态可能会激化——尽管还没有得到证实——是因为（在美国而不是欧洲）缺乏对数据泄露的法律处罚。

en: Until California recently passed a law, American firms did not have to tell anyone, even the victim, when data went astray. (21 words)
cn: 在最近加州通过一项法律之前，美国公司在数据丢失时不必告知任何人，甚至是受害人。

en: That may change fast: lots of proposed data-security legislation is now doing the rounds in Washington, D.C. (17 words)
cn: 这种情况可能很快就会转变：要对数据安全立法的很多议案现正在华盛顿特区进行讨论。

en: Meanwhile, the theft of information about some 40 million credit-card accounts in America, disclosed on June 17th, overshadowed a hugely important decision a day earlier by America’s Federal Trade Commission (FTC) that puts corporate America on notice that regulators will act if firms fail to provide adequate data security. (49 words)
cn: 同时，6月17日曝光的在美国约4000万信用卡账号信息失窃事件给此前一天美国联邦商业委员会做出的重要决定蒙上了阴影，该决定向美国商界发出公告，如果公司不能充分保障数据安全，监管人员就会采取行动。

en: While still catching up to men in some spheres of modern life, women appear to be way ahead in at least one undesirable category. (24 words)
cn: 尽管在现代生活的诸多方面女性仍然在追赶男性，但是至少有一个不太好的方面，她们却似乎遥遥领先。

en: “Women are particularly susceptible to developing depression and anxiety disorders in response to stress compared to men,” according to Dr. Yehuda, chief psychiatrist at New York’s Veteran’s Administration Hospital. (29 words)
cn: “与男性相比，在承受压力时，女性特别容易患抑郁症或焦虑症，”纽约退伍军人管理局医院的精神科主治医生耶胡达说道。

en: Studies of both animals and humans have shown that sex hormones somehow affect the stress response, causing females under stress to produce more of the trigger chemicals than do males under the same conditions. (34 words)
cn: 对人与动物的研究都表明，性激素会以某种方式影响压力反应，使处于压力下的雌性比同等条件下的雄性分泌出更多诱发性化学物质。

en: In several of the studies, when stressed-out female rats had their ovaries (the female reproductive organs) removed, their chemical responses became equal to those of the males. (27 words)
cn: 其中几项研究表明，如果把极度焦躁不安的雌鼠卵巢（雌性生殖器官）切除，其体内化学反应与雄鼠相当。

en: Adding to a woman’s increased dose of stress chemicals, are her increased “opportunities” for stress. (15 words)
cn: 女性面临压力的“机会”增多使其体内压力化学物质分泌量增加。

en: “It’s not necessarily that women don’t cope as well. (9 words)
cn: “女性并非不能应对自如。

en: It’s just that they have so much more to cope with,” says Dr. Yehuda. (14 words)
cn: 只是他们需要应对的太多。”耶胡达医生说。

en: “Their capacity for tolerating stress may even be greater than men’s,” she observes, “it’s just that they’re dealing with so many more things that they become worn out from it more visibly and sooner.” (34 words)
cn: “她们忍受压力的能力甚至可能比男性还强，”她评论说，“只不过是她们处理的事情太多，才使得她们更快就显得疲惫不堪，满脸倦容。”

en: Dr. Yehuda notes another difference between the sexes. (8 words)
cn: 耶胡达医生还指出了两性之间的另一区别。

en: “I think that the kinds of things that women are exposed to tend to be in more of a chronic or repeated nature. (23 words)
cn: “我认为女性应对的事情经常是重复难改的积习。”

en: Men go to war and are exposed to combat stress. (10 words)
cn: 男人出征作战，承受的是战斗压力。

en: Men are exposed to more acts of random physical violence. (10 words)
cn: 他们面对的更多的是偶发的身体暴力行为。

en: The kinds of interpersonal violence that women are exposed to tend to be in domestic situations, by, unfortunately, parents or other family members, and they tend not to be one-shot deals. (31 words)
cn: 女性遭受的人与人之间暴力通常发生在家庭环境中，不幸的是，往往来自于父母或其他的家庭成员实，而且还通常不止一次。

en: The wear-and-tear that comes from these longer relationships can be quite devastating.”(12 words)
cn: 这些更长久的人际关系带来的折磨可能会相当致命。

en: Adeline Alvarez married at 18 and gave birth to a son, but was determined to finish college. (29 words)
cn: 艾德琳·阿尔瓦雷兹十八岁结婚并生有一子，但她决意要读完大学。

en: “I struggled a lot to get the college degree. (9 words)
cn: “我努力拼搏，得到大学学位。

en: I was living in so much frustration that that was my escape, to go to school, and get ahead and do better.” (22 words)
cn: 我生活一团糟，上学、奋进、做得更好成为了我逃避的方式。”

en: Later, her marriage ended and she became a single mother. (10 words)
cn: 后来，她的婚姻结束，成为了一个单身母亲。

en: “It’s the hardest thing to take care of a teenager, have a job, pay the rent, pay the car payment, and pay the debt. (24 words)
cn: “最艰难的是我要照顾一个十来岁的孩子、找工作、交房租、养车、还债。

en: I lived from paycheck to paycheck.” (6 words)
cn: 工资全花光，我得不停赚钱。”

en: Not everyone experiences the kinds of severe chronic stresses Alvarez describes. (11 words)
cn: 并不是每个人都会经受像阿尔瓦雷兹那样长期严重的压力。

en: But most women today are coping with a lot of obligations, with few breaks, and feeling the strain. (18 words)
cn: 但是大多数女性都承担着太多的责任，少有放松，压力巨大。

en: Alvarez’s experience demonstrates the importance of finding ways to diffuse stress before it threatens your health and your ability to function. (21 words)
cn: 阿尔瓦雷兹的遭遇说明，在压力威胁到健康和生活能力之前，找到解压的方式非常重要的。

en: It used to be so straightforward. (6 words)
cn: 原本应该很简单。

en: A team of researchers working together in the laboratory would submit the results of their research to a journal. (19 words)
cn: 在实验室里一起工作的研究团队将其研究成果提交给某刊物。

en: A journal editor would then remove the authors’ names and affiliations from the paper and send it to their peers for review. (22 words)
cn: 该刊编辑把论文作者姓名及相关信息隐去，然后提交给同行审阅。

en: Depending on the comments received, the editor would accept the paper for publication or decline it. (16 words)
cn: 看收到的评论如何，编辑会决定是否采纳并发表这篇论文。

en: Copyright rested with the journal publisher, and researchers seeking knowledge of the results would have to subscribe to the journal. (20 words)
cn: 因此，版权属于刊物出版商，想知道研究结果的研究人员必须花钱订阅该刊物才行。

en: No longer. (2 words)
cn: 不再如此了。

en: The Internet—and pressure from funding agencies, who are questioning why commercial publishers are making money from government-funded research by restricting access to it—is making access to scientific results a reality. (32 words)
cn: 商业刊物的出版商靠限制人们查阅政府资助的研究的权利来牟利，科研机构的拨款单位对此表示质疑——这种压力，加之互联网的发展使人们有权查阅科研结果成为现实。

en: The Organization for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) has just issued a report describing the far-reaching consequences of this. (19 words)
cn: 经济合作与发展组织(OECD)近日发布一项报告，描述了此举的深远影响。

en: The report, by John Houghton of Victoria University in Australia and Graham Vickery of the OECD, makes heavy reading for publishers who have, so far, made handsome profits. (28 words)
cn: 这份由澳大利亚维多利亚大学的约翰·霍顿和经合组织的格拉汉姆·维克利共同撰写的报告让那些迄今为止获得巨大利润的出版商读起来心情沉重。

en: But it goes further than that. (6 words)
cn: 但事情还不仅仅止于此。

en: It signals a change in what has, until now, been a key element of scientific endeavor. (16 words)
cn: 它表明当前科学研究中一个重要因素即将发生改变。

en: The value of knowledge and the return on the public investment in research depends, in part, upon wide distribution and ready access. (22 words)
cn: 知识的价值以及公众投资研究所取得的汇报，部分取决于知识能够广泛传播，以及公众随时能够查阅。

en: It is big business. (4 words)
cn: 这是门大生意。

en: In America, the core scientific publishing market is estimated at between $7 billion and $11 billion. (16 words)
cn: 美国核心科研出版市场估值在70亿美元到110亿美元左右。

en: The International Association of Scientific, Technical and Medical Publishers says that there are more than 2,000 publishers worldwide specializing in these subjects. (22 words)
cn: 国际科技与医学出版商协会称，全世界有2000多家出版公司专注于这些领域。

en: They publish more than 1.2 million articles each year in some 16,000 journals. (13 words)
cn: 他们每年在约16000种期刊上发表的文章超过120万篇。

en: This is now changing. (4 words)
cn: 现在变了。

en: According to the OECD report, some 75% of scholarly journals are now online. (13 words)
cn: 根据经合组织的报告，约75%的学术刊物现在可以在线阅读。

en: Entirely new business models are emerging; three main ones were identified by the report’s authors. (15 words)
cn: 出现了全新的商业模式；报告作者指出了其中主要的三种。

en: There is the so-called big deal, where institutional subscribers pay for access to a collection of online journal titles through site-licensing agreements. (22 words)
cn: 第一种是所谓的大订单模式，即机构订阅者通过网站许可协议付费阅读一批在线刊物。

en: There is open-access publishing, typically supported by asking the author (or his employer) to pay for the paper to be published. (21 words)
cn: 第二种是开放出版模式，通常靠要求作者（或其雇主）付费发表文章来维持。

en: Finally, there are open-access archives, where organizations such as universities or international laboratories support institutional repositories. (16 words)
cn: 最后，还有开放档案模式，即一些大学或者国际实验室这样的组织来支持机构典藏。

en: Other models exist that are hybrids of these three, such as delayed open-access, where journals allow only subscribers to read a paper for the first six months, before making it freely available to everyone who wishes to see it. (39 words)
cn: 还有其他的模式存在，都是对这上述三种模式的不同组合，比如延期开放模式是指一些期刊在论文发表最初六个月只允许订阅者阅读，然后再免费公开给任何想要查阅的人。

en: All this could change the traditional form of the peer-review process, at least for the publication of papers. (18 words)
cn: 所有这一切都将颠覆传统的同行审阅模式，至少对发表论文来说是如此。

en: In the early 1960s Wilt Chamberlain was one of only three players in the National Basketball Association (NBA) listed at over seven feet. (23 words)
cn: 20世纪60年代初，威尔特·张伯伦是职业篮球联赛（NBA）中仅有的身高超高7英尺的三个球员之一。

en: If he had played last season, however, he would have been one of 42. (14 words)
cn: 然而，要是他上个赛季还参赛的话，他就成了42个人中的一个了。

en: The bodies playing major professional sports have changed dramatically over the years, and managers have been more than willing to adjust team uniforms to fit the growing numbers of bigger, longer frames. (32 words)
cn: 几年来，参加主要职业赛事的球员身材发生了很大的改变，经理人非常乐意调整队服，以适应越来越多身形更高大的队员。

en: The trend in sports, though, may be obscuring an unrecognized reality: Americans have generally stopped growing. (16 words)
cn: 然而，运动方面的这种趋势可能蒙蔽了一个尚未认识到的事实：美国人身高增长已基本停止了。

en: Though typically about two inches taller now than 140 years ago, today’s people—especially those born to families who have lived in the U.S. for many generations—apparently reached their limit in the early 1960s. (35 words)
cn: 尽管现在与140年前相比通常要高两英寸，但现代人——尤其是那些父辈好几代都在美国生活的人——明显在20世纪60年代就达到了身高极限。

en: And they aren’t likely to get any taller. (8 words)
cn: 他们不太可能再长高了。

en: “In the general population today, at this genetic, environmental level, we’ve pretty much gone as far as we can go,” says anthropologist William Cameron Chumlea of Wright State University. (29 words)
cn: 莱特州立大学的人类学家威廉·卡梅隆·查姆利说：“就现在的总体人口而言，在目前的基因、环境水平下，我们已经达到能达到的程度。”

en: In the case of NBA players, their increase in height appears to result from the increasingly common practice of recruiting players from all over the world. (26 words)
cn: 就 NBA 球员而言，他们身高增长似乎是因为从世界各地招募球员这种日益普遍的做法造成的。

en: Growth, which rarely continues beyond the age of 20, demands calories and nutrients—notably, protein—to feed expanding tissues. (19 words)
cn: 人一过20岁就很少再长，长高需要热量和营养物质，尤其是蛋白质，以供给身体组织生长。

en: At the start of the 20th century, under-nutrition and childhood infections got in the way. (15 words)
cn: 在20世纪初期，营养不良和儿童疾病影响了身高增长。

en: But as diet and health improved, children and adolescents have, on average, increased in height by about an inch and a half every 20 years, a pattern known as the secular trend in height. (34 words)
cn: 但是随着饮食和健康状况的改进，儿童和青少年每20年平均长高约1.5英寸，这一模式就是众所周知的身高长期趋势。

en: Yet according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, average height—5′9″ for men, 5′4″ for women—hasn’t really changed since 1960. (23 words)
cn: 但是疾病控制和预防中心认为，人的平均身高（男性5英尺9英寸，女性5英尺4英寸）自 1960 年以来实际上并没有发生变化。

en: Genetically speaking, there are advantages to avoiding substantial height. (9 words)
cn: 从遗传的角度讲，避免长得太高是有好处的。

en: During childbirth, larger babies have more difficulty passing through the birth canal. (12 words)
cn: 分娩过程中，较大的婴儿更难通过产道。

en: Moreover, even though humans have been upright for millions of years, our feet and back continue to struggle with bipedal posture and cannot easily withstand repeated strain imposed by oversize limbs. (31 words)
cn: 此外，尽管人类直立行走已百万年之久，但脚部和背部得一直费力去保持双足行走的姿势，不太经得起肢体过长带来的重复压力。

en: “There are some real constraints that are set by the genetic architecture of the individual organism,” says anthropologist William Leonard of Northwestern University. (23 words)
cn: “的确有一些限制是由每种生物的基因结构决定的，”西北大学的人类学家威廉·李奥纳德这样说道。

en: Genetic maximums can change, but don’t expect this to happen soon. (11 words)
cn: 基因的最大值可以改变，但是不要期望它们会很快地改变。

en: Claire C. Gordon, senior anthropologist at the Army Research Center in Natick, Mass., ensures that 90 percent of the uniforms and workstations fit recruits without alteration. (26 words)
cn: 马萨诸塞州内蒂克的美国陆军研究中心人类学高级研究员克莱尔·C·戈登保证，90%的制服和工作间适合新兵，不需改动。

en: She says that, unlike those for basketball, the length of military uniforms has not changed for some time. (18 words)
cn: 她说，不像篮球队队服，军装的尺寸一段时间以来都没有改动。

en: And if you need to predict human height in the near future to design a piece of equipment, Gordon says that by and large, “you could use today’s data and feel fairly confident.” (33 words)
cn: 如果你需要预测人类在近期的身高变化，以便设计一款装备，戈登认为，基本上“你可以用现在的数据，相当有把握。”

en: In 1784, five years before he became president of the United States, George Washington, 52, was nearly toothless. (18 words)
cn: 1748年，在成为美国总统的5年前，乔治华盛顿，52岁，牙齿几乎掉光了。

en: So he hired a dentist to transplant nine teeth into his jaw—having extracted them from the mouths of his slaves. (21 words)
cn: 所以他请了一个牙医给他种了9颗牙——这些牙齿都是从他奴隶口中拔出来的。

en: That’s a far different image from the cherry-tree-chopping George most people remember from their history books. (16 words)
cn: 这跟很多人从历史书上读到的那个砍樱桃树的华盛顿的形象有点大相径庭。

en: But recently, many historians have begun to focus on the roles slavery played in the lives of the founding generation. (20 words)
cn: 但最近，很多历史学家都开始关注奴隶制在开国元勋的生活中所起的作用。

en: They have been spurred in part by DNA evidence made available in 1998, which almost certainly proved Thomas Jefferson had fathered at least one child with his slave Sally Hemings. (30 words)
cn: 1998 年获得的 DNA 证据从某种程度上刺激了这些历史学家，证据几乎可以确认托马斯·杰斐逊曾与其奴隶萨利·海明斯有过至少一个孩子。

en: And only over the past 30 years have scholars examined history from the bottom up. (15 words)
cn: 只是在最近30年来，学者才开始自下而上的研究历史。

en: Works of several historians reveal the moral compromises made by the nation’s early leaders and the fragile nature of the country’s infancy. (22 words)
cn: 有几位历史学家的著作揭示了这个国家早期的一些领导人做出的道德妥协，以及国家初期的脆弱本质。

en: More significantly, they argue that many of the Founding Fathers knew slavery was wrong—and yet most did little to fight it. (22 words)
cn: 更意味深长的是，他们认为很多开国元勋知道奴隶制是错误的，但大部分人却对此无动于衷

en: More than anything, the historians say, the founders were hampered by the culture of their time. (16 words)
cn: 历史学家认为，最重要的原因是开国元勋受到他们所处时代文化的束缚。

en: While Washington and Jefferson privately expressed distaste for slavery, they also understood that it was part of the political and economic bedrock of the country they helped to create. (29 words)
cn: 尽管华盛顿和杰斐逊私下都表达过他们对于奴隶制的反感，但他们也知道奴隶制是他们曾帮助创建的这个国家政治经济基础的一部分。

en: For one thing, the South could not afford to part with its slaves. (13 words)
cn: 一方面，南方不能没有奴隶。

en: Owning slaves was “like having a large bank account”, says Wiencek, author of An Imperfect God: George Washington, His Slaves, and the Creation of America. (25 words)
cn: 拥有奴隶就像“就像拥有一笔巨额存款”，《不完美的上帝：乔治·华盛顿、他的奴隶、和美国的建立》一书作者温塞克说道。

en: The southern states would not have signed the Constitution without protections for the “peculiar institution”, including a clause that counted a slave as three fifths of a man for purposes of congressional representation. (33 words)
cn: 如果没有对“特殊制度”的保护条款，南方各州是不会签署宪法的，其中一项是一个奴隶按五分之三人口计算国会代表权。

en: And the statesmen’s political lives depended on slavery. (8 words)
cn: 并且这些政治家的政治生命也取决于奴隶制。

en: The three-fifths formula handed Jefferson his narrow victory in the presidential election of 1800 by inflating the votes of the southern states in the Electoral College. (26 words)
cn: 这个五分之三方案扩大了选举人团中南方各州的选票，使杰弗逊在1800年的总统选举中险胜。

en: Once in office, Jefferson extended slavery with the Louisiana Purchase in 1803; the new land was carved into 13 states, including three slave states. (24 words)
cn: 一上任，杰弗逊就在 1803 年通过“路易斯安娜购地案”扩大了奴隶制；这片新土地被分为 13 个州，其中有三个实行奴隶制。

en: Still, Jefferson freed Hemings’s children—though not Hemings herself or his approximately 150 other slaves. (15 words)
cn: 但杰裴逊还是解放了赫明丝的孩子 —— 尽管没有解放赫明丝本人和其他的约150名奴隶。

en: Washington, who had begun to believe that all men were created equal after observing the bravery of the black soldiers during the Revolutionary War, overcame the strong opposition of his relatives to grant his slaves their freedom in his will. (40 words)
cn: 华盛顿在美国独立战争时期目睹了黑人士兵的勇敢之后，开始相信人人生而平等，后来在其遗嘱中，他不顾亲戚的强烈反对，给予奴隶自由。

en: Only a decade earlier, such an act would have required legislative approval in Virginia. (14 words)
cn: 要是再早仅仅10年，这种做法在弗吉尼亚还要得到立法批准才行。

en: Habits are a funny thing. (5 words)
cn: 习惯是有件有趣的事情。

en: We reach for them mindlessly, setting our brains on auto-pilot and relaxing into the unconscious comfort of familiar routine. (19 words)
cn: 我们不注意就成了习惯，使大脑自动运转，一切轻车熟路，放松进入到潜意识的舒适状态。

en: “Not choice, but habit rules the unreflecting herd,” William Wordsworth said in the 19th century. (15 words)
cn: 威廉·华兹华斯（William Wordsworth）19世纪曾说：“不是选择，而是习惯控制着那些没有思想的人”。

en: In the ever-changing 21st century, even the word “habit” carries a negative implication. (13 words)
cn: 21世纪日新月异，即使“习惯”这个词也含有贬义。

en: So it seems paradoxical to talk about habits in the same context as creativity and innovation. (16 words)
cn: 所以，把习惯和创造、革新放在同样的语境下讨论，似乎有点自相矛盾。

en: But brain researchers have discovered that when we consciously develop new habits, we create parallel paths, and even entirely new brain cells, that can jump our trains of thought onto new, innovative tracks. (33 words)
cn: 但大脑研究人员发现，当我们有意识地培养新习惯的时候，我们的大脑会产生平行的路径，甚至是全新的脑细胞，可以使我们的思路跳转到新的创新轨道。

en: Rather than dismissing ourselves as unchangeable creatures of habit, we can instead direct our own change by consciously developing new habits. (21 words)
cn: 我们不要轻把自己当成是一成不变的习惯动物就打发了，相反，我们可以有意识培养新习惯来引导自己做出改变。

en: In fact, the more new things we try—the more we step outside our comfort zone—the more inherently creative we become, both in the workplace and in our personal lives. (31 words)
cn: 事实上，我们对尝试的新事物越多，走出自己的舒适带越远，我们在工作场所和个人生活中的内在创造力就越强，

en: But don’t bother trying to kill off old habits; once those ruts of procedure are worn into the brain, they’re there to stay. (23 words)
cn: 但是没有必要费力去抛弃旧习惯；一旦旧习惯的套路印在大脑中，就会一直存在。

en: Instead, the new habits we deliberately press into ourselves create parallel pathways that can bypass those old roads. (18 words)
cn: 相反，我们刻意培养的新习惯能在大脑中产生平行路径，避开原来那些老路。

en: “The first thing needed for innovation is a fascination with wonder,” says Dawna Markova, author of The Open Mind. (19 words)
cn: 《开明的思想》一书的作者道娜·马尔科娃说：“创新，首先要充满好奇。”

en: “But we are taught instead to ‘decide’, just as our president calls himself ‘the Decider’.” (15 words)
cn: “然而我们却受到教育去做‘决定’，就像我们的总裁称自己是‘决策者’。”

en: She adds, however, that “to decide is to kill off all possibilities but one. (14 words)
cn: 她补充道，但“但是，做决定就意味着否决所有可能性而只保留一种。

en: A good innovational thinker is always exploring the many other possibilities.”(11 words)
cn: 一个优秀的具有革新精神的思想者总是在探寻许多其它的可能。”

en: All of us work through problems in ways of which we’re unaware, she says. (14 words)
cn: 她说，我们都不会去留心自己解决问题的方法。

en: Researchers in the late 1960s discovered that humans are born with the capacity to approach challenges in four primary ways: analytically, procedurally, relationally (or collaboratively) and innovatively. (27 words)
cn: 20世纪60年代晚期的研究人员发现，人类与生俱来的应对挑战的方法主要有

en: At the end of adolescence, however, the brain shuts down half of that capacity, preserving only those modes of thought that have seemed most valuable during the first decade or so of life. (33 words)
cn: 而到青春期结束时，大脑关闭一半的能力，仅仅保留了那些大约在生命最开始的十几年时间里似乎是最有价值的思维方式。

en: The current emphasis on standardized testing highlights analysis and procedure, meaning that few of us inherently use our innovative and collaborative modes of thought. (24 words)
cn: 目前重视标准化测试就是鼓励分析和计划能力，也就意味着我们中很少有人会本能地使用创新和合作的思维方式。

en: “This breaks the major rule in the American belief system—that anyone can do anything,” explains M. J. Ryan, author of the 2006 book This Year I Will... and Ms. Markova’s business partner. (33 words)
cn: M. J. 瑞安是 2006 年出版的著作《今年我将......》一书的作者以及马尔科娃女士的商业合作伙伴，她解释说：“这打破了美国信仰体系里的重要准则——任何人都可以做任何事。”

en: “That’s a lie that we have perpetuated, and it fosters commonness. (11 words)
cn: 这是一个谎言，我们竟然一直都信，它造就了平庸。

en: Knowing what you’re good at and doing even more of it creates excellence.” (13 words)
cn: 了解自己擅长什么，多多实践，就会成就卓越。

en: This is where developing new habits comes in. (8 words)
cn: 这样才能培养出新习惯。

en: It is a wise father that knows his own child, but today a man can boost his paternal (fatherly) wisdom—or at least confirm that he’s the kid’s dad. (29 words)
cn: 再聪明的父亲也未必了解自己的孩子，但是如今男人可以提升其为人之父的智慧，或者，至少可以确认他是孩子的爹。

en: All he needs to do is shell out $30 for a paternity testing kit (PTK) at his local drugstore—and another $120 to get the results. (26 words)
cn: 他要做的仅仅就是在当地药房花30美元买一盒亲子鉴定工具包（PTK），再花120美元就可以获得检测结果。

en: More than 60,000 people have purchased the PTKs since they first became available without prescriptions last year, according to Doug Fogg, chief operating officer of Identigene, which makes the over-the-counter kits. (31 words)
cn: 识别基因公司（Identigene）生产了这种工具包，使得顾客可以直接在柜台购买。其首席运营官道格福格说，自从去年首次不需要处方就能购买PTK以来，购买者已经超过6万。

en: More than two dozen companies sell DNA tests directly to the public, ranging in price from a few hundred dollars to more than $2,500. (24 words)
cn: 直接向公众出售DNA检测的公司有超过24家，价格从几百美元到2500多美元不等。

en: Among the most popular: paternity and kinship testing, which adopted children can use to find their biological relatives and families can use to track down kids put up for adoption. (30 words)
cn: 其中最受欢迎的是父子关系鉴定和血缘关系鉴定，被收养的孩子可以利用它找到有血缘关系的亲属，而家庭也可以用它来追查被人收养的孩子。

en: DNA testing is also the latest rage among passionate genealogists—and supports businesses that offer to search for a family’s geographic roots. (22 words)
cn: DNA检测最近也是风靡一时，很多热心的家谱学专家大力推崇，它还为帮忙寻找某个家族地域根源的公司提供支持。

en: Most tests require collecting cells by swabbing saliva in the mouth and sending it to the company for testing. (19 words)
cn: 大多数检测都需要通过棉签擦拭口腔唾液来采集细胞，并将唾液送至公司进行检测。

en: All tests require a potential candidate with whom to compare DNA. (11 words)
cn: 所有的测试都需要一个潜在的对照者进行DNA对比。

en: But some observers are skeptical. (5 words)
cn: 但是，一些观察家则表示怀疑。

en: “There’s a kind of false precision being hawked by people claiming they are doing ancestry testing,” says Troy Duster, a New York University sociologist. (24 words)
cn: 纽约大学的社会学家特洛伊·达斯特说：“那些声称自己在做血统检测的人所在兜售精确信息是虚假的。”

en: He notes that each individual has many ancestors— numbering in the hundreds just a few centuries back. (17 words)
cn: 他指出，每个人祖先人数都不少，仅上溯几百年，数量就达好几百。

en: Yet most ancestry testing only considers a single lineage, either the Y chromosome inherited through men in a father’s line or mitochondrial DNA, which is passed down only from mothers. (30 words)
cn: 但是大多数血统检测只考虑单一的族系，要么是从父系一方男性遗传而来的Y染色体，要么是仅通过母系一方遗传的线粒体DNA。

en: This DNA can reveal genetic information about only one or two ancestors, even though, for example, just three generations back people also have six other great-grandparents or, four generations back, 14 other great-great-grandparents. (33 words)
cn: 这种DNA检测只能检测出一两位祖先的基因信息，尽管，比如说，仅上溯三代，每个人还有另外六位曾祖父母，若追溯到第四代，还有另外的14个高祖父母。

en: Critics also argue that commercial genetic testing is only as good as the reference collections to which a sample is compared. (21 words)
cn: 批评家们还认为，商业性基因检测的过程基本上就是收集参考数据，然后用样本进行比对。

en: Databases used by some companies don’t rely on data collected systematically but rather lump together information from different research projects. (20 words)
cn: 一些公司使用的数据库里的数据并非系统收集所得，而是整合不同研究项目的信息而得。

en: This means that a DNA database may have a lot of data from some regions and not others, so a person’s test results may differ depending on the company that processes the results. (33 words)
cn: 这意味某个DNA数据库可能会有很多信息来自于某些地区，而缺乏别的地区，所以一个人的检测结果可能会因处理结果的公司不同而存在差异。

en: In addition, the computer programs a company uses to estimate relationships may be patented and not subject to peer review or outside evaluation. (23 words)
cn: 此外，一个公司用来判断血缘关系的电脑程序可能是其专利，所以这些软件可能缺乏同行评审或外界评估。

en: The relationship between formal education and economic growth in poor countries is widely misunderstood by economists and politicians alike. (19 words)
cn: 经济学家和政治家都普遍误解了贫穷国家的正规教育与经济增长之间的关系。

en: Progress in both areas is undoubtedly necessary for the social, political and intellectual development of these and all other societies; however, the conventional view that education should be one of the very highest priorities for promoting rapid economic development in poor countries is wrong. (44 words)
cn: 毫无疑问，在这两个方面都有所进步对于这些贫穷国家和他国家的社会、政治和智力发展而言是必要的；传统观点认为：教育是促进贫穷国家经济快速发展的头等大事，然而这种观点是错误的。

en: We are fortunate that it is, because building new educational systems there and putting enough people through them to improve economic performance would require two or three generations. (28 words)
cn: 我们庆幸这种传统观点是错误的，因为在这些国家创立新的教育体制，并通过体制来培养足够的人才以提高经济效益，需要两三代人来完成。

en: The findings of a research institution have consistently shown that workers in all countries can be trained on the job to achieve radically higher productivity and, as a result, radically higher standards of living. (34 words)
cn: 一家研究机构的多次研究发现都一再表明，所有国家的工人都可以靠岗前培训来从根本上提高生产率，进而从根本上提高生活水平。

en: Ironically, the first evidence for this idea appeared in the United States. (12 words)
cn: 讽刺的是，这一观点最先在美国得到证实。

en: Not long ago, with the country entering a recession and Japan at its pre-bubble peak, the U.S. workforce was derided as poorly educated and one of the primary causes of the poor U.S. economic performance. (35 words)
cn: 不久前，美国经济开始衰退，日本则处于泡沫破灭前的高峰期，美国工人被嘲讽教育水平低，是美国经济表现不佳的主要原因之一。

en: Japan was, and remains, the global leader in automotive-assembly productivity. (10 words)
cn: 日本，在过去是，现在依然是，在汽车装配生产能力方面，保持全球领先地位。

en: Yet the research revealed that the U.S. factories of Honda, Nissan, and Toyota achieved about 95 percent of the productivity of their Japanese counterparts—a result of the training that U.S. workers received on the job. (36 words)
cn: 然而，研究表明，本田、日产和丰田在美国工厂的生产能力达到日本同行的95%左右，这是美国工人接受在职培训的结果。

en: More recently, while examining housing construction, the researchers discovered that illiterate, non-English-speaking Mexican workers in Houston, Texas, consistently met best-practice labor productivity standards despite the complexity of the building industry’s work. (31 words)
cn: 最近，研究人员在调查住房建设时发现，尽管建筑行业工作复杂，但在德克萨斯休斯顿，不识字、不会说英语的墨西哥工人总能够达到劳动生产率最佳标准。

en: What is the real relationship between education and economic development? (10 words)
cn: 教育与经济发展之间，到底是什么关系？

en: We have to suspect that continuing economic growth promotes the development of education even when governments don’t force it. (19 words)
cn: 我们不得不怀疑，即使政府不强制发展教育，经济持续增长也会促进教育发展。

en: After all, that’s how education got started. (7 words)
cn: 毕竟，教育就是这样产生的。

en: When our ancestors were hunters and gatherers 10,000 years ago, they didn’t have time to wonder much about anything besides finding food. (22 words)
cn: 一万年前当我们的祖先还在过着采集狩猎的生活之时，他们根本没有时间去思考发现食物以外的其他任何事情。

en: Only when humanity began to get its food in a more productive way was there time for other things. (19 words)
cn: 只有当人类能够更高效地获取食物，他们才有时间做其它的事情。

en: As education improved, humanity’s productivity potential increased as well. (9 words)
cn: 教育一发展，人类生产潜力也会提高。

en: When the competitive environment pushed our ancestors to achieve that potential, they could in turn afford more education. (18 words)
cn: 当竞争环境促使我们的祖先实现这一生产潜力，他们反过来又有能力获得更多的教育机会。

en: This increasingly high level of education is probably a necessary, but not a sufficient, condition for the complex political systems required by advanced economic performance. (25 words)
cn: 先进的经济水平要求复杂的政治制度，越来越高的教育水平可能是这种复杂政治制度必要的，但不是充分的条件。

en: Thus poor countries might not be able to escape their poverty traps without political changes that may be possible only with broader formal education. (24 words)
cn: 因此，政治改革才可以使贫穷国家摆脱贫困陷阱，而政治改革则只能靠更广泛的正规教育实现。

en: A lack of formal education, however, doesn’t constrain the ability of the developing world’s workforce to substantially improve productivity for the foreseeable future. (23 words)
cn: 但是， 发展中国家的劳动力在可预见的未来充分提高生产力的能力没有因缺乏正规教育而受到限制。

en: On the contrary, constraints on improving productivity explain why education isn’t developing more quickly there than it is. (18 words)
cn: 相反，正是因为生产力的提高受到了各种限制，所以这些国家的教育没能更快地发展。

en: The most thoroughly studied intellectuals in the history of the New World are the ministers and political leaders of seventeenth-century New England. (22 words)
cn: 新大陆史上被研究得最彻底的知识分子是17世纪新英格兰的牧师和政治领袖。

en: According to the standard history of American philosophy, nowhere else in colonial America was “so much importance attached to intellectual pursuits.” (21 words)
cn: 根据美国标准哲学史的记载，在美洲殖民地中，没有任何地方能“如此重视知识的追求。”

en: According to many books and articles, New England’s leaders established the basic themes and preoccupations of an unfolding, dominant Puritan tradition in American intellectual life. (25 words)
cn: 据大量书籍和文献记载，清教传统正日益发展，逐渐占据统治地位，新英格兰的领袖确立了美国精神生活中清教传统的基本主旨和要点。

en: To take this approach to the New Englanders normally means to start with the Puritans’ theological innovations and their distinctive ideas about the church—important subjects that we may not neglect. (31 words)
cn: 通过这条途径来了解新英格兰人，通常意味着要首先研究清教徒的神学创新及其对于教会的独特看法——这些是我们不可忽略的重要课题。

en: But in keeping with our examination of southern intellectual life, we may consider the original Puritans as carriers of European culture, adjusting to New World circumstances. (26 words)
cn: 但是为了与我们对南部精神生活的考察保持一致，我们可以认为最初的清教徒们带来了欧洲文化，他们根据新大陆的情况进行了调整。

en: The New England colonies were the scenes of important episodes in the pursuit of widely understood ideals of civility and virtuosity. (21 words)
cn: 在追求公认的文明礼节和精湛技能的理想过程当中，新英格兰殖民地发生了许多重要的事情。

en: The early settlers of Massachusetts Bay included men of impressive education and influence in England. (15 words)
cn: 马萨诸塞湾的早期移民就包括了那些在英国接受过良好的教育并深具影响力的人。

en: Besides the ninety or so learned ministers who came to Massachusetts churches in the decade after 1629, there were political leaders like John Winthrop, an educated gentleman, lawyer, and official of the Crown before he journeyed to Boston. (38 words)
cn: 在1629年之后的十年间，除了来到马萨诸塞教会的约90位博学的牧师，还有像约翰·温斯罗普（John Winthrop）这样的政治领袖，他是一位绅士、律师，受过良好教育，到波士顿之前曾是皇室官员。

en: These men wrote and published extensively, reaching both New World and Old World audiences, and giving New England an atmosphere of intellectual earnestness. (23 words)
cn: 这些人著作等身，读者遍及新旧大陆，这给新英格兰营造了一种求知若渴的氛围。

en: We should not forget, however, that most New Englanders were less well educated. (13 words)
cn: 但是，我们不该忘记，大多数新英格兰人并未受过良好的教育。

en: While few craftsmen or farmers, let alone dependents and servants, left literary compositions to be analyzed, it is obvious that their views were less fully intellectualized. (26 words)
cn: 虽然鲜有工匠或农民，更别提其子女和仆人，留下只言片语可供分析；但是很明显他们的观点并不具有太大的思想性。

en: Their thinking often had a traditional superstitious quality. (8 words)
cn: 他们的思想中往往带有一种传统的迷信色彩。

en: A tailor named John Dane, who emigrated in the late 1630s, left an account of his reasons for leaving England that is filled with signs. (25 words)
cn: 有个裁缝，叫约翰·戴恩（John Dane），于17世纪30年代末期移居新大陆，他留下一个记录，陈述了离开英国的理由，其内容充满了各种符号征兆。

en: Sexual confusion, economic frustrations, and religious hope—all came together in a decisive moment when he opened the Bible, told his father that the first line he saw would settle his fate, and read the magical words: “Come out from among them, touch no unclean thing, and I will be your God and you shall be my people.” (58 words)
cn: 他翻开《圣经》，告诉父亲，他所见的第一行字会决定他的命运，然后就读到了以下神奇的文字：“从他们中间走出来，不要沾不洁净的物，我将成为你们的神，你将是我的子民。”在这一决定性的时刻，性方面的困感、经济上的挫败、还有宗教上期待——所有的一切都一股脑儿涌现出来。

en: One wonders what Dane thought of the careful sermons explaining the Bible that he heard in Puritan churches. (18 words)
cn: 人们会纳闷，戴恩在清教徒教堂里听到那些精心诠释圣经的布道后会做何感想。

en: Meanwhile, many settlers had slighter religious commitments than Dane’s, as one clergyman learned in confronting folk along the coast who mocked that they had not come to the New World for religion. (32 words)
cn: 同时，许多移民并没有戴恩那般虔诚的宗教信仰，就像一位牧师在海边遇到渔夫听到他们说的那样，那些人嘲弄说自己来新大陆可不是为了宗教。

en: “Our main end was to catch fish.” (7 words)
cn: “ 我们最终目的是来捕鱼。”

en: Of all the changes that have taken place in English-language newspapers during the past quarter-century, perhaps the most far-reaching has been the inexorable decline in the scope and seriousness of their arts coverage. (33 words)
cn: 过去的25年，英文报纸发生的所有变化中，影响最深远的就是其艺术报道范围和严肃性呈现不可遏止的衰落之势。

en: It is difficult to the point of impossibility for the average reader under the age of forty to imagine a time when high-quality arts criticism could be found in most big-city newspapers. (32 words)
cn: 对于40岁以下的普通读者而言，他们很难甚至不可能想象得到，当年还可以在许多大城市报纸上读到高质量的艺术评论。

en: Yet a considerable number of the most significant collections of criticism published in the 20th century consisted in large part of newspaper reviews. (23 words)
cn: 然而在20世纪出版的大量最重要的评论集中，很大部分都是报刊评论。

en: To read such books today is to marvel at the fact that their learned contents were once deemed suitable for publication in general-circulation dailies. (24 words)
cn: 若今天再读这些书，定会大为震惊：文集中这些精深的内容竟然当成是适合发表在大众日报中的文章。

en: We are even farther removed from the unfocused newspaper reviews published in England between the turn of the 20th century and the eve of World War Ⅱ, at a time when newsprint was dirt-cheap and stylish arts criticism was considered an ornament to the publications in which it appeared. (49 words)
cn: 甚至更让我们不解的还有，从20世纪伊始到二战前夕英国报纸上评论文章主题广泛，包罗万象。当时新闻纸相当便宜，人们认为时髦的艺术评论是其刊载出版物的一个点缀。

en: In those far-off days, it was taken for granted that the critics of major papers would write in detail and at length about the events they covered. (27 words)
cn: 在那些遥远的年代，各大报刊的评论家都会做长篇大论详细评论他们所报道的事情，这在当时看做是理所当然的。

en: Theirs was a serious business, and even those reviewers who wore their learning lightly, like George Bernard Shaw and Ernest Newman, could be trusted to know what they were about. (30 words)
cn: 他们干的是正事，人们相信：即使是萧伯纳和欧内斯特·纽曼这样不掉书袋博学的评论家，他们也知道自己要干啥。

en: These men believed in journalism as a calling, and were proud to be published in the daily press. (18 words)
cn: 这帮人奉新闻业为天职，并且对自己的文章能见诸于报刊颇为自豪。

en: “So few authors have brains enough or literary gift enough to keep their own end up in journalism,” Newman wrote, “that I am tempted to define ‘journalism’ as ‘a term of contempt applied by writers who are not read to writers who are’.” (43 words)
cn: “能拥有足够智慧或文学天赋在新闻写作中坚持不懈的人少之又少”，纽曼曾写道,“以至于我很想把‘新闻写作’定义为不受读者欢迎的作家用来嘲讽受读者欢迎的作家的一个 ‘轻蔑之词’ ”。

en: Unfortunately, these critics are virtually forgotten. (6 words)
