en: While Europe’s monarchies will no doubt be smart enough to survive for some time to come, it is the British royals who have most to fear from the Spanish example. (30 words)
cn: 欧洲的王室无疑都足够聪明，能在未来一段时间继续存留，但从这次西班牙的例子当中感到最多担忧的，当属英国王室。

en: It is only the Queen who has preserved the monarchy’s reputation with her rather ordinary (if well-heeled) granny style. (19 words)
cn: 女王，普通老奶奶一样的形象，尽管挺有钱的，只有她，才保全了英国王室的名声。

en: The danger will come with Charles, who has both an expensive taste of lifestyle and a pretty hierarchical view of the world. (22 words)
cn: 危险可能在于查尔斯，因为他不仅喜欢奢侈的生活，还认为这个世界等级就是相当分明。

en: He has failed to understand that monarchies have largely survived because they provide a service—as non-controversial and non-political heads of state. (22 words)
cn: 他没能明白到，王室家族之所以能留存下来，很大程度上是因为他们提供了一种服务——作为国家元首，不会引起争议，没有政治倾向。

en: Charles ought to know that as English history shows, it is kings, not republicans, who are the monarchy’s worst enemies. (20 words)
cn: 查尔斯应该知道：国王，而非共和主义者，才是君主制的死敌，英国历史也表明了这一点。

en: Just how much does the Constitution protect your digital data? (10 words)
cn: 宪法到底能在多大程度上保护你的数字资料呢？

en: The Supreme Court will now consider whether police can search the contents of a mobile phone without a warrant if the phone is on or around a person during an arrest. (31 words)
cn: 目前，最高法院正在考虑，警察在抓捕嫌疑人过程中，没有搜查令，是否可以查看嫌疑人身上或者身旁的手机的内容。/ 警察没有搜查令是否可以查看一部手机的内容，如果在抓捕一个人的过程中，他的手机在手上或者在近旁。

en: California has asked the justices to refrain from a sweeping ruling, particularly one that upsets the old assumption that authorities may search through the possessions of suspects at the time of their arrest. (33 words)
cn: 加州要求法官废止一项重大裁决，尤其是这项裁决会推翻旧有的共识——警方在逮捕嫌疑人时可以对其财务进行搜查。

en: It is hard, the state argues, for judges to assess the implications of new and rapidly changing technologies. (18 words)
cn: 加州认为，法官很难评估快速发展的新技术可能带来的影响。

en: The court would be recklessly modest if it followed California’s advice. (11 words)
cn: 要是最高法院听从了加州的建议，谦虚有余，谨慎不足。

en: Enough of the implications are discernable, even obvious, so that the justices can and should provide updated guidelines to police, lawyers and defendants. (23 words)
cn: 新技术产生的影响之大，足以让人看清，甚至相当明显；因此法官能够而且也应该为警方、律师和被告提供与时俱进的指导原则。

en: They should start by discarding California’s lame argument that exploring the contents of a smartphone—a vast storehouse of digital information—is similar to, say, going through a suspect’s purse. (30 words)
cn: 他们应该首先抛弃加州那种蹩脚的观点：智能手机是一个庞大的数据信息库，翻手机类似于，比如说，翻嫌疑人的钱包。

en: The court has ruled that police don’t violate the Fourth Amendment when they go through the wallet or pocketbook of an arrestee without a warrant. (25 words)
cn: 最高法院已经裁定，警方若没有搜查令搜查被捕人的钱包或钱夹并不违反宪法第四修正案。

en: But exploring one’s smartphone is more like entering his or her home. (12 words)
cn: 但翻人手机更像是进人家里。

en: A smartphone may contain an arrestee’s reading history, financial history, medical history and comprehensive records of recent correspondence. (18 words)
cn: 被捕人的智能手机里可能有其阅读记录，财务记录，病历和近期通信往来的详细记录。

en: The development of “cloud computing”, meanwhile, has made that exploration so much the easier. (14 words)
cn: 同时，“云计算”的发展，使得这样搜查手机甚至变得更容易。

en: Americans should take steps to protect their digital privacy. (9 words)
cn: 美国人应该采取措施保护其数字隐私。

en: But keeping sensitive information on these devices is increasingly a requirement of normal life. (14 words)
cn: 但把敏感信息保存在这些设备上，逐渐成为日常生活的需要。

en: Citizens still have a right to expect private documents to remain private and protected by the Constitution’s prohibition on unreasonable searches. (21 words)
cn: 公民仍有权要求其私人文件应保持隐私状态，并且受宪法保护以避免受到无理的搜查。

en: As so often is the case, stating that principle doesn’t ease the challenge of line-drawing. (15 words)
cn: 情况往往都是如此，规定了做事情的原则，也很难划定实际的界限。

en: In many cases, it would not be overly burdensome for authorities to obtain a warrant to search through phone contents. (20 words)
cn: 很多情况下，警方要获得搜查手机内容的搜查令并不太费力。

en: They could still invalidate Fourth Amendment protections when facing severe, urgent circumstances, and they could take reasonable measures to ensure that phone data are not erased or altered while waiting for a warrant. (33 words)
cn: 一旦碰到严重的紧急状况，他们仍会越过第四修正案的保护条例；他们还会在等待搜查令的时候，采取合理的措施以确保手机数据不会被删除或者篡改。

en: The court, though, may want to allow room for police to cite situations where they are entitled to more freedom. (20 words)
cn: 尽管如此，或许最高法院还想给警方留点余地，好让他们列举出一些可以享有更多行动自由的情况。

en: But the justices should not swallow California’s argument whole. (9 words)
cn: 但对于加州的意见，法官不应该生吞活剥。

en: New, disruptive technology sometimes demands novel applications of the Constitution’s protections. (11 words)
cn: 全新的，颠覆性的技术有时候需要以新的方式运用宪法保护条例。

en: Orin Kerr, a law professor, compares the explosion and accessibility of digital information in the 21st century with the establishment of automobile use as a virtual necessity of life in the 20th: The justices had to specify novel rules for the new personal domain of the passenger car then; they must sort out how the Fourth Amendment applies to digital information now. (62 words)
cn: 21世纪数字信息爆炸式增长，很容易就可以获取，法学教授奥林·科尔将此与20世纪把汽车确立为日常生活必需品作比较：当时法官不得不针对私家车这个新的领域制定新的规定；现在他们也不得不解决如何把宪法第四修正案适用于数字信息这一问题。

en: The journal Science is adding an extra round of statistical checks to its peer-review process, editor-in-chief Marcia McNutt announced today. (20 words)
cn: 《科学》杂志主编玛西娅·麦克纳特今天宣布，该杂志拟对同行评审程序额外增加一轮统计数据审查。

en: The policy follows similar efforts from other journals, after widespread concern that basic mistakes in data analysis are contributing to the irreproducibility of many published research findings. (27 words)
cn: 这一规定是在效仿其他杂志的类似举措，之所以这样规定，是因为人们普遍担心数据分析中的基本错误导致许多已经发表的研究成果无法复制。

en: “Readers must have confidence in the conclusions published in our journal,” writes McNutt in an editorial. (16 words)
cn: “读者必须对发表在我们杂志上的研究结论有信心，”麦克纳特在一篇社论中写到。

en: Working with the American Statistical Association, the journal has appointed seven experts to a statistics board of reviewing editors (SBoRE). (20 words)
cn: 《科学》杂志与美国统计协会合作，任命了七名专家组成了一个统计数据核查编辑委员会（SBoRE）。

en: Manuscripts will be flagged up for additional scrutiny by the journal’s internal editors, or by its existing Board of Reviewing Editors or by outside peer reviewers. (26 words)
cn: 杂志内部编辑、现有评审编委会、或者外部同行评审会对原稿做出标示，以接受额外的彻底审查。

en: The SBoRE panel will then find external statisticians to review these manuscripts. (12 words)
cn: 统计数据核查编辑委员会专家小组会找外面的统计学家来审查这些原稿。

en: Asked whether any particular papers had impelled the change, McNutt said: “The creation of the ‘statistics board’ was motivated by concerns broadly with the application of statistics and data analysis in scientific research and is part of Science’s overall drive to increase reproducibility in the research we publish.” (48 words)
cn: 问及是否有什么特定的论文促成了这次变化，麦克纳特说：“成立‘统计数据检查委员会’的动机，开始是因为我们广泛关注统计数据和数据分析在科学研究领域的应用，现在则是因为《科学》杂志致力于全面提高本刊发表论文的可复制性，成立‘统计数据检查委员会’就是其中的一环。”

en: Giovanni Parmigiani, a biostatistician at the Harvard School of Public Health, a member of the SBoRE group, says he expects the board to “play primarily an advisory role.” (28 words)
cn: 哈佛大学公共卫生学院的生物统计学家乔瓦尼·帕尔米加尼是统计数据核查编辑委员会的成员。他说，他期望该委员会“主要起到咨询的作用”。

en: He agreed to join because he “found the foresight behind the establishment of the SBoRE to be novel, unique and likely to have a lasting impact. (26 words)
cn: 他之所以同意加入，是因为他“觉得成立这个编辑委员会有先见之明，新颖独特，并可能带来长期的影响”。

en: This impact will not only be through the publications in Science itself, but hopefully through a larger group of publishing places that may want to model their approach after Science.” (30 words)
cn: 这种影响不仅会遍及《科学》自己的文章，还会波及一大批想效法《科学》杂志的其他出版单位。

en: John Ioannidis, a physician who studies research methodology, says that the policy is “a most welcome step forward” and “long overdue”. (21 words)
cn: 约翰·约阿尼迪斯医生专攻研究方法论，他说，该项规定是“一种进步，令人欣慰”，且“早该如此”。

en: “Most journals are weak in statistical review, and this damages the quality of what they publish. (16 words)
cn: “大多数期刊在统计数据审核方面都很薄弱，这有导致其发表论文质量下降。

en: I think that, for the majority of scientific papers nowadays, statistical review is more essential than expert review,” he says. (20 words)
cn: 我认为，对于目前大多数科研论文而言，统计数据审查比专家审查更重要。”他这样说道。

en: But he noted that biomedical journals such as Annals of Internal Medicine, the Journal of the American Medical Association and The Lancet pay strong attention to statistical review. (28 words)
cn: 不过他也指出，生物医学类期刊，如《内科学年鉴》、《美国医学协会杂志》、和《柳叶刀》等，都格外重视统计数据审核。

en: Professional scientists are expected to know how to analyze data, but statistical errors are alarmingly common in published research, according to David Vaux, a cell biologist. (26 words)
cn: 专业的科学家应该知道如何分析数据，但根据细胞生物学家大卫·沃克斯的看法，统计错误在已发表的研究中相当普遍，令人担忧。

en: Researchers should improve their standards, he wrote in 2012, but journals should also take a tougher line, “engaging reviewers who are statistically literate and editors who can verify the process”. (30 words)
cn: 研究人员应该提高自己的研究水平，他在2012年写道，但是杂志社的立场也应该更坚定，“聘请通晓统计学的评审和能够核查整个评审过程的编辑”。

en: Vaux says that Science’s idea to pass some papers to statisticians “has some merit, but a weakness is that it relies on the board of reviewing editors to identify ‘the papers that need scrutiny’ in the first place”. (38 words)
cn: 沃克斯说，《科学》想把部分论文交给统计学家审核，“有可取之处，但不足之处在于它首先得靠评审编辑委员会来确定‘哪些论文需要仔细审查’”。

en: Two years ago, Rupert Murdoch’s daughter, Elisabeth, spoke of the “unsettling dearth of integrity across so many of our institutions.” (20 words)
cn: 两年前，鲁伯特·默多克之女伊丽莎白曾说到过，“众多机构都缺乏诚信，令人担忧”。

en: Integrity had collapsed, she argued, because of a collective acceptance that the only “sorting mechanism” in society should be profit and the market. (23 words)
cn: 诚信的价值观依然瓦解，她争辩说，因为大家都认为，社会唯一的“分选机制”应该是利润和市场。

en: But “it’s us, human beings, we the people who create the society we want, not profit.” (16 words)
cn: 但“正是我们，人类，我们人民大众创造了我们想要的社会，而不是利润”。

en: Driving her point home, she continued: “It’s increasingly apparent that the absence of purpose, of a moral language within government, media or business could become one of the most dangerous goals for capitalism and freedom.” (35 words)
cn: 为阐明自己的观点，她接着说道：“政府、媒体、或企业等内部价值的缺失，道德语言的缺失，或将成为资本主义和自由最危险的目标之一；这一点日益明显。”

en: This same absence of moral purpose was wounding companies such as News International, she thought, making it more likely that it would lose its way as it had with widespread illegal telephone hacking. (33 words)
cn: 她认为，同样的道德价值缺失问题正在危害新闻国际这样的公司，使其更有可能迷失方向，正如它此前在大规模电话窃听事件中一样。

en: As the hacking trial concludes—finding guilty one ex-editor of the News of the World, Andy Coulson, for conspiring to hack phones, and finding his predecessor, Rebekah Brooks, innocent of the same charge—the wider issue of dearth of integrity still stands. (42 words)
cn: 电话窃听案件审判结果出炉：裁定《世界新闻报》前编辑 安迪·库尔森因合谋窃听电话有罪，其前任丽贝卡·布鲁克斯同诉无罪；如该案结论表明，更大的问题依然存在：诚信缺失。

en: Journalists are known to have hacked the phones of up to 5,500 people. (13 words)
cn: 据悉，记者曾窃听多达5500人的电话。

en: This is hacking on an industrial scale, as was acknowledged by Glenn Mulcaire, the man hired by the News of the World in 2001 to be the point person for phone hacking. (32 words)
cn: 这是一场工业规模的窃听行为，格伦·穆凯尔承认，他 2001 年受雇于《世界新闻报》，是电话窃听的关键人物。

en: Others await trial. (3 words)
cn: 其他人还在候审。

en: This long story still unfolds. (5 words)
cn: 这一连串事件还在发酵。

en: In many respects, the dearth of moral purpose frames not only the fact of such widespread phone hacking but the terms on which the trial took place. (27 words)
cn: 我们可以从很多方面看出，道德价值缺失，不仅体现在如此大规模的电话窃听的事实上，还体现在审判这一案件所使用的条款上。

en: One of the astonishing revelations was how little Rebekah Brooks knew of what went on in her newsroom, how little she thought to ask and the fact that she never inquired how the stories arrived. (35 words)
cn: 其中揭露出的惊人真相是，丽贝卡·布鲁克斯对发生在其新闻编辑室的情况几乎一无所知，她也几乎没有想到要去过问，而且事实上是她从来没有询问过这些报道的来源。

en: The core of her successful defence was that she knew nothing. (11 words)
cn: 她辩护成功的关键，就是她一无所知。

en: In today’s world, it has become normal that well-paid executives should not be accountable for what happens in the organizations that they run. (23 words)
cn: 在当今社会，待遇优厚的高管对自己经营的公司所发生的事情不负责任，成了常态。

en: Perhaps we should not be so surprised. (7 words)
cn: 或许我们不该如此大惊小怪。

en: For a generation, the collective doctrine has been that the sorting mechanism of society should be profit. (17 words)
cn: 一代人了，他们集体的信条就是，社会的分选机制就应该是利益。

en: The words that have mattered are efficiency, flexibility, shareholder value, business–friendly, wealth generation, sales, impact and, in newspapers, circulation. (20 words)
cn: 有点分量的字眼是：效率、弹性、股东价值、商业友好、创造财富、销售额、影响力，还有报纸的发行量。

en: Words degraded to the margin have been justice, fairness, tolerance, proportionality and accountability. (13 words)
cn: 而正义、公平、宽容、均衡和责任成了边缘词汇。

en: The purpose of editing the News of the World was not to promote reader understanding, to be fair in what was written or to betray any common humanity. (28 words)
cn: 编辑《世界新闻报》的目的，不是为了加强读者的理解认知能力，不是为了在报道中追求正义，也根本不是为了彰显共同的人性。

en: It was to ruin lives in the quest for circulation and impact. (12 words)
cn: 而是为了追求发行量和影响力，要破坏人们的生活。

en: Ms Brooks may or may not have had suspicions about how her journalists got their stories, but she asked no questions, gave no instructions—nor received traceable, recorded answers. (29 words)
cn: 布鲁克斯女士可能曾经，也或许未曾怀疑过她的记者是怎样获得新闻题材的，但是她从不过问，从不指示——当然也从来没有得到过有线索有记录的回答。

en: France, which prides itself as the global innovator of fashion, has decided its fashion industry has lost an absolute right to define physical beauty for women. (26 words)
cn: 法国，是全球时尚创新者，并引以为傲，如今已经裁定其时尚业不再拥有定义女性形体美的绝对权。

en: Its lawmakers gave preliminary approval last week to a law that would make it a crime to employ ultra-thin models on runways. (22 words)
cn: 其立法机构上周初步批准了一项法规：雇用超瘦 T 台模特可能会犯罪。

en: The parliament also agreed to ban websites that “incite excessive thinness” by promoting extreme dieting. (15 words)
cn: 议会还同意取缔那些靠提倡极端节食的方式来“鼓动人们暴瘦下来”的网站。

en: Such measures have a couple of uplifting motives. (8 words)
cn: 这些举措鼓舞人心，达到了如下几个目的。

en: They suggest beauty should not be defined by looks that end up with impinging on health. (16 words)
cn: 这些举措表明，美，不应该靠外表来定义，因为只求外表，最终会有损于健康。

en: That’s a start. (3 words)
cn: 这是其一。

en: And the ban on ultra-thin models seems to go beyond protecting models from starving themselves to death—as some have done. (21 words)
cn: 另外，针对超瘦模特的禁令似乎不仅仅是保护模特，以免他们节食过度致死——还真有这样的事。

en: It tells the fashion industry that it must take responsibility for the signal it sends women, especially teenage girls, about the social tape-measure they must use to determine their individual worth. (31 words)
cn: 女性，尤其是十几岁的少女，他们必须使用某种社会准确来决定其个人价值，对此，时尚行业能给他们发出相关信号予以指引，禁令要求时尚行业要对其发出的信号承担责任。

en: The bans, if fully enforced, would suggest to women (and many men) that they should not let others be arbiters of their beauty. (23 words)
cn: 这些禁令，如果彻底执行，将向女性（以及很多男性）表明，自己的美不应该由他人的裁决。

en: And perhaps faintly, they hint that people should look to intangible qualities like character and intellect rather than dieting their way to size zero or wasp-waist physiques. (27 words)
cn: 或许没那么明显，但他们向大家暗示的是，应该注重性格、才智等无形的品质，而不是一味节食，追求零码或者蜂腰。

en: The French measures, however, rely too much on severe punishment to change a culture that still regards beauty as skin-deep—and bone-showing. (22 words)
cn: 法国的这些措施，然而，太过于依赖于靠重罚来改变那种追求外在美、骨感美的文化。

en: Under the law, using a fashion model that does not meet a government-defined index of body mass could result in a $85,000 fine and six months in prison. (28 words)
cn: 根据该项法律，雇佣未达到政府规定体重指数的时尚模特将会导致85000美元罚款和六个月监禁。

en: The fashion industry knows it has an inherent problem in focusing on material adornment and idealized body types. (18 words)
cn: 时尚行业也知道，自身固有的问题是，太注重物质装饰和理想体型。

en: In Denmark, the United States, and a few other countries, it is trying to set voluntary standards for models and fashion images that rely more on peer pressure for enforcement. (30 words)
cn: 在丹麦、美国、以及其他几个国家，时尚行业正试图就模特和时尚形象制定一些非强制性的标准，标准的实施主要依靠同辈压力来起到作用。

en: In contrast to France’s actions, Denmark’s fashion industry agreed last month on rules and sanctions regarding age, health, and other characteristics of models. (23 words)
cn: 与法国的举措既然不同，上个月，丹麦的时尚行业就模特的年龄、健康及其他特征的规定和处罚达成了一致。

en: The newly revised Danish Fashion Ethical Charter clearly states: “We are aware of and take responsibility for the impact the fashion industry has on body ideals, especially on young people.” (30 words)
cn: 新修订的《丹麦时尚道德宪章》明确声明：“我们深知，时尚行业对人们理想身材的观念，尤其是对年轻人影响重大，我们以此为己任。”

en: The charter’s main tool of enforcement is to deny access for designers and modeling agencies to Copenhagen Fashion Week (CFW), which is run by the Danish Fashion Institute. (28 words)
cn: 该宪章的主要执行手段是，拒绝设计师和模特中介公司参加丹麦时装协会组织的“哥本哈根时装周（CFW）”。

en: But in general it relies on a name-and-shame method of compliance. (11 words)
cn: 但总体来说靠的是点评批评的方法让人遵守。

en: Relying on ethical persuasion rather than law to address the misuse of body ideals may be the best step. (19 words)
cn: 靠道德劝说而不是法律，来解决理想身材标准滥用问题，可能是最好的措施。

en: Even better would be to help elevate notions of beauty beyond the material standards of a particular industry. (18 words)
cn: 若要好上加好，就得帮助人们提高对美的认识，超越特定行业的有形标准。

en: For the first time in history more people live in towns than in the country. (15 words)
cn: 史上第一次，城里住的人比乡村多。

en: In Britain this has had a curious result. (8 words)
cn: 在英国，这一现象产生了一种奇怪的结果。

en: While polls show Britons rate “the countryside” alongside the royal family, Shakespeare and the National Health Service (NHS) as what makes them proudest of their country, this has limited political support. (31 words)
cn: 尽管民调显示，英国人认为“乡村”，与皇室、莎士比亚和国民保健制度一样，是自己国家最引以为傲的，但这得到的政治支持却比较有限。

en: A century ago Octavia Hill launched the National Trust not to rescue stylish houses but to save “the beauty of natural places for everyone forever.” (25 words)
cn: 一个世纪前，奥克塔维娅·希尔牵头成立了国民信托，她不是想要挽救哪些别具特色的住宅，而是想保护“属于每个人的永久自然美景”。

en: It was specifically to provide city dwellers with spaces for leisure where they could experience “a refreshing air.” (18 words)
cn: 尤其是要给城市居民提供一个休闲场所，好让他们能够享受“清新的空气”。

en: Hill’s pressure later led to the creation of national parks and green belts. (13 words)
cn: 希尔后来不断呼吁，终于建立了国家公园和绿化带。

en: They don’t make countryside any more, and every year concrete consumes more of it. (14 words)
cn: 他们不再建设乡村，每年混凝土都在不断蚕食乡村。

en: It needs constant guardianship. (4 words)
cn: 乡村，需要持久的保护

en: At the next election none of the big parties seem likely to endorse this sentiment. (15 words)
cn: 在下一届选举中，几大党派似乎没有一个赞成这种观点。

en: The Conservatives’ planning reform explicitly gives rural development priority over conservation, even authorising “off-plan” building where local people might object. (20 words)
cn: 保守党的改革计划明确支持农村开发优先于农村保护，甚至在当地居民反对的地方批准建期房。

en: The concept of sustainable development has been defined as profitable. (10 words)
cn: 有人认为，可持续发展的概念就是有利可图。

en: Labour likewise wants to discontinue local planning where councils oppose development. (11 words)
cn: 同样，工党也想在当地议会反对开发的地方终止地方规划。

en: The Liberal Democrats are silent. (5 words)
cn: 自由民主党则保持沉默。

en: Only Ukip, sensing its chance, has sided with those pleading for a more considered approach to using green land. (19 words)
cn: 只有独立党，察觉到了这个机会，与那些主张合理利用绿地的人站在了一起。

en: Its Campaign to Protect Rural England struck terror into many local Conservative parties. (13 words)
cn: 其“保护英国乡村运动”使得许多地方保守党胆战心惊。

en: The sensible place to build new houses, factories and offices is where people are, in cities and towns where infrastructure is in place. (23 words)
cn: 要建造新房、工厂和办公楼，合理的地方是有人的地方，比如在基础设施齐全的城镇。

en: The London agents Stirling Ackroyd recently identified enough sites for half a million houses in the London area alone, with no intrusion on green belt. (25 words)
cn: 伦敦地产代理公司斯特林-艾可罗伊德仅在伦敦地区就找到了足够开发50万套住宅的地皮，且完全不会侵占绿化带。

en: What is true of London is even truer of the provinces. (11 words)
cn: 伦敦能这样，其他地方更是如此。

en: The idea that “housing crisis” equals “concreted meadows” is pure lobby talk. (12 words)
cn: “住房危机”就等同于“绿地上建房”，这样的观点纯粹是游说团体的说辞。

en: The issue is not the need for more houses but, as always, where to put them. (16 words)
cn: 问题不是需要建更多的房子，而是，且一直都是，在哪里建房。

en: Under lobby pressure, George Osborne favours rural new-build against urban renovation and renewal. (13 words)
cn: 迫于游说团体的压力，乔治·奥斯本更倾向于在乡村新建楼房，而不是在城市改造翻新。

en: He favours out-of-town shopping sites against high streets. (8 words)
cn: 他支持在城外建购物中心，而不是发展商业街。

en: This is not a free market but a biased one. (10 words)
cn: 这不是一个自由市场，而是一个偏见市场。

en: Rural towns and villages have grown and will always grow. (10 words)
cn: 乡村小镇和村庄已经发展了起来，并且会一直发展。

en: They do so best where building sticks to their edges and respects their character. (14 words)
cn: 有些乡村，坚持优势，尊重特色，发展得最好。

en: We do not ruin urban conservation areas. (7 words)
cn: 我们没有破坏城里的保护区。

en: Why ruin rural ones? (4 words)
cn: 为什么要去破坏乡村的呢？

en: Development should be planned, not let rip. (7 words)
cn: 开发应该是有规划的，而不是任意妄为。

en: After the Netherlands, Britain is Europe’s most crowded country. (9 words)
cn: 英国是欧洲人口最密集的国家，仅次于荷兰。

en: Half a century of town and country planning has enabled it to retain an enviable rural coherence, while still permitting low-density urban living. (23 words)
cn: 半个世纪的城乡发展计划使英国能保持令人羡慕的乡村和谐；同时还能实现低密度的城市生活。

en: There is no doubt of the alternative—the corrupted landscapes of southern Portugal, Spain or Ireland. (16 words)
cn: 另一种结局是毫无疑问的——那就是葡萄牙南部、西班牙或爱尔兰那种遭到破坏的景观。

en: Avoiding this rather than promoting it should unite the left and right of the political spectrum. (16 words)
cn: 避免这种结局而非助长它，应当成为凝聚政治光谱左右两派的共识。

en: “There is one and only one social responsibility of business,” wrote Milton Friedman, a Nobel Prize-winning economist, “That is, to use its resources and engage in activities designed to increase its profits.” (32 words)
cn: “企业，有，且仅有一种社会责任，”诺贝尔经济学奖获得者米尔顿·弗里德曼写到，“那就是，利用资源，参与各种活动，增加利润”。

en: But even if you accept Friedman’s premise and regard corporate social responsibility (CSR) policies as a waste of shareholders’ money, things may not be absolutely clear-cut. (26 words)
cn: 但即使你接受弗里德曼的观点，并且还认为企业社会责任（CSR）政策是在浪费股东的钱，事情也可能并非是绝对的泾渭分明。

en: New research suggests that CSR may create monetary value for companies—at least when they are prosecuted for corruption. (19 words)
cn: 新的研究表明，企业社会责任（CSR）可以为公司创造货币价值——至少在其受到腐败指控时是如此。

en: The largest firms in America and Britain together spend more than $15 billion a year on CSR, according to an estimate by EPG, a consulting firm. (26 words)
cn: 根据EPG咨询公司的预测，英国两国最大型的公司每年总共在“企业社会责任”上的支出超过1500亿美元。

en: This could add value to their businesses in three ways. (10 words)
cn: 这能从三个方面增加公司的价值。

en: First, consumers may take CSR spending as a “signal” that a company’s products are of high quality. (17 words)
cn: 其一，消费者或许会认为“企业社会责任”上的花费就可以表明一个公司的产品质量高。

en: Second, customers may be willing to buy a company’s products as an indirect way to donate to the good causes it helps. (22 words)
cn: 其次，顾客可能更愿意购买某个公司的产品，认为这是一种间接的方式捐赠给公司扶持的善事。

en: And third, through a more diffuse “halo effect,” whereby its good deeds earn it greater consideration from consumers and others. (20 words)
cn: 第三，通过一种能影响到更多人的“光环效应”，其善举赢得了消费者和其他人更多的尊重。

en: Previous studies on CSR have had trouble differentiating these effects because consumers can be affected by all three. (18 words)
cn: 以前对“企业社会责任”的研究就没有区分这些效应，因为消费者可能同时会受到上述三个方面的影响。

en: A recent study attempts to separate them by looking at bribery prosecutions under America’s Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA). (19 words)
cn: 最近的一项研究分析了美国《反海外腐败法》下的行贿诉讼案件，试图区分这三种影响。

en: It argues that since prosecutors do not consume a company’s products as part of their investigations, they could be influenced only by the halo effect. (25 words)
cn: 这项研究认为，由于检察官不会去消费某一公司的产品作为其调查的一部分，他们可能只受到了光环效应的影响。

en: The study found that, among prosecuted firms, those with the most comprehensive CSR programmes tended to get more lenient penalties. (20 words)
cn: 研究发现，在被起诉的公司里，“企业社会责任”项目全面的企业往往会得到宽大处理。

en: Their analysis ruled out the possibility that it was firms’ political influence, rather than their CSR stand, that accounted for the leniency: Companies that contributed more to political campaigns did not receive lower fines. (34 words)
cn: 他们的分析排除了这样的可能性，即：是公司的政治影响力，而非公司在“企业社会责任”上的作为，使他们得到较轻的惩罚；因为那些在整治活动上投入更多的公司并没有获得轻罚。

en: In all, the study concludes that whereas prosecutors should only evaluate a case based on its merits, they do seem to be influenced by a company’s record in CSR. (29 words)
cn: 总之，该项研究得出的结论是，尽管检察官应该只根据案件本身的情况来评判，但他们似乎的确受到了公司“企业社会责任”记录的影响。

en: “We estimate that either eliminating a substantial labour-rights concern, such as child labour, or increasing corporate giving by about 20% results in fines that generally are 40% lower than the typical punishment for bribing foreign officials,” says one researcher. (39 words)
cn: 一位研究人员说：“我们估计，消除诸如雇佣童工等重大劳工问题，或是把企业捐赠数额提高20%，都能使企业受到较轻的惩罚，这比贿赂外国官员受到的常规惩罚要低40%。”

en: Researchers admit that their study does not answer the question of how much businesses ought to spend on CSR. (19 words)
cn: 研究人员承认，其研究并没有回答企业该在“企业社会责任”上花费多少这一问题。

en: Nor does it reveal how much companies are banking on the halo effect, rather than the other possible benefits, when they decide their do-gooding policies. (25 words)
cn: 该研究也没有表明，企业在制定慈善政策时，在多大程度上指望光环效应，而不是靠其他可能的慈善活动。

en: But at least they have demonstrated that when companies get into trouble with the law, evidence of good character can win them a less costly punishment. (26 words)
cn: 但他们至少证明了，一旦公司陷入法律纠纷，品行良好的证据会使其获得较轻的惩罚。

en: There will eventually come a day when The New York Times ceases to publish stories on newsprint. (17 words)
cn: 总有一天，《纽约时报》会停止刊发纸质媒体报道。

en: Exactly when that day will be is a matter of debate. (11 words)
cn: 确切是哪一天，还众说纷纭。

en: “Sometime in the future,” the paper’s publisher said back in 2010. (11 words)
cn: “会是将来某一天”，该报出版人早在2010年曾说过。

en: Nostalgia for ink on paper and the rustle of pages aside, there’s plenty of incentive to ditch print. (18 words)
cn: 撇开人们对纸上油墨和翻看报纸沙沙作响的怀旧之情不谈，有太多因素促使我们放弃印刷。

en: The infrastructure required to make a physical newspaper—printing presses, delivery trucks —isn’t just expensive; it’s excessive at a time when online-only competitors don’t have the same set of financial constraints. (31 words)
cn: 印刷纸版实体包子所需的基础设施，如印刷机、送货车，不仅仅是贵；而且在当下显得有点多余，因为那些只做在线报纸的竞争对手并不用承受这一系列同样的财务负担。

en: Readers are migrating away from print anyway. (7 words)
cn: 无论如何，纸质版报纸的读者正在流失。

en: And though print ad sales still dwarf their online and mobile counterparts, revenue from print is still declining. (18 words)
cn: 尽管纸质版的广告销售额远超网络版和手机版，但是纸版的收入还在不断下滑。

en: Overhead may be high and circulation lower, but rushing to eliminate its print edition would be a mistake, says BuzzFeed CEO Jonah Peretti. (23 words)
cn: 运营费用可能很高，发行量可能很低，但急于砍掉纸质版将会是个错误的决定，美国新闻网站百资得（BuzzFeed）的首席运营官乔纳·佩雷蒂这样说道。

en: Peretti says the Times shouldn’t waste time getting out of the print business, but only if they go about doing it the right way. (24 words)
cn: 佩雷蒂认为，《纽约时报》不应该在剥离纸质版业务上浪费时间，除非他们能恰当处理这件事情。

en: “Figuring out a way to accelerate that transition would make sense for them,” he said, “but if you discontinue it, you’re going to have your most loyal customers really upset with you.” (32 words)
cn: “他们得找到办法加速这一转型，才是明智的，”他说，“但如果停止纸版业务，真的会让最忠实的读者感到失望。”

en: Sometimes that’s worth making a change anyway. (7 words)
cn: 不管怎样，改变，是值得的。

en: Peretti gives the example of Netflix discontinuing its DVD-mailing service to focus on streaming. (14 words)
cn: 佩雷蒂拿网飞公司来举例说明，网飞公司终止其DVD邮寄服务，转而聚焦流媒体。

en: “It was seen as a blunder,” he said. (8 words)
cn: “当时认为是失误，”他说。

en: The move turned out to be foresighted. (7 words)
cn: 后来才发现此举有先见之明。

en: And if Peretti were in charge at the Times? (9 words)
cn: 那要是佩雷蒂执掌《纽约时报》呢？

en: “ I wouldn’t pick a year to end print,” he said. (11 words)
cn: “我不会选择某一年来结束纸质报纸”，他说。

en: “I would raise prices and make it into more of a legacy product.” (13 words)
cn: “我会提高价格，并将其打造成为更像是一种怀旧产品”。

en: The most loyal customers would still get the product they favor, the idea goes, and they’d feel like they were helping sustain the quality of something they believe in. (29 words)
cn: 佩雷蒂的理念是，最忠实的顾客依然会买他们钟爱的产品，而且他们会感觉像是在帮助维护自己信赖的产品的质量。

en: “So if you’re overpaying for print, you could feel like you were helping,” Peretti said. (15 words)
cn: “所以，如果你多付钱买纸质版报纸，你会感觉自己是在帮忙，”佩雷蒂这样说道。

en: “Then increase it at a higher rate each year and essentially try to generate additional revenue.” (16 words)
cn: “然后逐年涨价，实质上是想要创造额外的收入”。

en: In other words, if you’re going to make a print product, make it for the people who are already obsessed with it. (22 words)
cn: 换言之，如果你想做纸质版报纸，那就最好专门做给那些迷恋纸版的人。

en: Which may be what the Times is doing already. (9 words)
cn: 而《纽约时报》或许就是这么做的。

en: Getting the print edition seven days a week costs nearly $500 a year - more than twice as much as a digital-only subscription. (23 words)
cn: 一周七天订阅纸质版报纸，每年要花大概500美元，是纯电子版订阅费的两倍多。

en: “It’s a really hard thing to do and it’s a tremendous luxury that BuzzFeed doesn’t have a legacy business,” Peretti remarked. (21 words)
cn: “但这绝非易事，并且很庆幸百资得没有这样的传统产业”，佩雷蒂说。

en: “But we’re going to have questions like that where we have things we’re doing that don’t make sense when the market changes and the world changes. (26 words)
cn: “但是，市场在变，世界也在变，我们却还在做一些没有意义的事情，那我们也会遇到类似的事情。

en: In those situations, it’s better to be more aggressive than less aggressive.” (12 words)
cn: 若真如此，积极进取会更好一点。”

en: First two hours, now three hours—this is how far in advance authorities are recommending people show up to catch a domestic flight, at least at some major U.S. airports with increasingly massive security lines. (35 words)
cn: 开始是两个小时，现在三个小时——这就是官方建议我们赶国内航班须提前抵达的时间，至少在安检队伍越来越长的美国几大主要机场得如此。

en: Americans are willing to tolerate time-consuming security procedures in return for increased safety. (13 words)
cn: 美国人愿意忍受耗费时间的安检程序，以期获得更高的安全水平。

en: The crash of EgyptAir Flight 804, which terrorists may have downed over the Mediterranean Sea, provides another tragic reminder of why. (21 words)
cn: 埃及航空804航班坠毁，疑遭恐怖分子袭击沉没于地中海，这一悲剧再次提

en: But demanding too much of air travelers or providing too little security in return undermines public support for the process. (20 words)
cn: 但是，对航空旅客要求太多，提供相应的安全保障又太少，都会削减公众对安检程序的支持。

en: And it should: Wasted time is a drag on Americans’ economic and private lives, not to mention infuriating. (18 words)
cn: 会的。时间浪费了，拖累了美国经济，连累了私人生活，更别提有多让人恼火。

en: Last year, the Transportation Security Administration (TSA) found in a secret check that undercover investigators were able to sneak weapons—both fake and real—past airport security nearly every time they tried. (32 words)
cn: 去年，美国运输安全管理局（TSA）在一次秘密检查中发现，穿便衣的调查人员能偷偷携带武器，真的假的都能，通过机场安检，几乎屡试不爽。

en: Enhanced security measures since then, combined with a rise in airline travel due to the improving economy and low oil prices, have resulted in long waits at major airports such as Chicago’s O’Hare International. (34 words)
cn: 从那之后加强了安全措施，还有因经济好转、油价降低带来的航空出行增加，导致几大机场大排长龙，比如芝加哥奥黑尔国际机场。

en: It is not yet clear how much more effective airline security has become—but the lines are obvious. (18 words)
cn: 航线安全提升了多少，尚不明确，但是排队的长线显而易见。

en: Part of the issue is that the government did not anticipate the steep increase in airline travel, so the TSA is now rushing to get new screeners on the line. (30 words)
cn: 部分问题在于，政府并没有预料到航空出行会急剧增加，所以运管局现在正忙不迭地招募新的机场安检员。

en: Part of the issue is that airports have only so much room for screening lanes. (15 words)
cn: 部分问题在于，机场就只有那么点空间可以用作安检通道。

en: Another factor may be that more people are trying to overpack their carry-on bags to avoid checked-baggage fees, though the airlines strongly dispute this. (24 words)
cn: 另一个因素可能是更多人为了避免缴纳托运行李的费用，会尽量把自己的随身行李装到装不下了，尽管航空公司强烈反对这样做。

en: There is one step the TSA could take that would not require remodeling airports or rushing to hire: Enroll more people in the PreCheck program. (25 words)
cn: 有一项措施运管局可以采取，不需要改造机场，也不需要匆忙招聘安检：把更多的人纳入预检计划。

en: PreCheck is supposed to be a win-win for travelers and the TSA. (12 words)
cn: 预检计划，对于乘客和运管局来说，本该是一种双赢。

en: Passengers who pass a background check are eligible to use expedited screening lanes. (13 words)
cn: 通过背景调查的乘客，有资格使用快速安检通道。

en: This allows the TSA to focus on travelers who are higher risk, saving time for everyone involved. (17 words)
cn: 这可以使运管局专注于那些高危旅客，节约了所有相关人员的时间。

en: The TSA wants to enroll 25 million people in PreCheck. (10 words)
cn: 运管局计划把2500万人纳入预检。

en: It has not gotten anywhere close to that, and one big reason is sticker shock: Passengers must pay $85 every five years to process their background checks. (27 words)
cn: 实际情况差了十万八千里，一个原因是定价太高：乘客每5年必须缴纳85美金来支付其背景调查费用。

en: Since the beginning, this price tag has been PreCheck’s fatal flaw. (11 words)
cn: 从一开始，这个标价就是预检项目的致命缺点。

en: Upcoming reforms might bring the price to a more reasonable level. (11 words)
cn: 或许即将进行的改革会把价格定位在一个更合理的水平。

en: But Congress should look into doing so directly, by helping to finance PreCheck enrollment or to cut costs in other ways. (21 words)
cn: 但是国会应该直接调查一下定价问题，可以拨款给注册预检项目的人，或者用其他方式削减开支。

en: The TSA cannot continue diverting resources into underused PreCheck lanes while most of the traveling public suffers in unnecessary lines. (20 words)
cn: 大多数乘客都在毫无必要的排队中煎熬，预检通道没有充分发挥作用，运管局不能再继续把资源挪用至此。

en: It is long past time to make the program work. (10 words)
cn: 早就该好好运作这个预检计划了。

en: “The ancient Hawaiians were astronomers,” wrote Queen Liliuokalani, Hawaii’s last reigning monarch, in 1897. (14 words)
cn: 1897年，夏威夷最后一位执政君主利留卡拉妮女王写道：“古夏威夷人都是天文学家”。

en: Star watchers were among the most esteemed members of Hawaiian society. (11 words)
cn: 观星者曾是夏威夷社会上最受尊敬的人。

en: Sadly, all is not well with astronomy in Hawaii today. (10 words)
cn: 遗憾的是，现在，在夏威夷，天文学的处境却不尽如人意。

en: Protests have erupted over construction of the Thirty Meter Telescope (TMT), a giant observatory that promises to revolutionize humanity’s view of the cosmos. (23 words)
cn: 三十米级望远镜（TMT）是一座巨型的天文台，很可能会彻底改变人类的宇宙观。但因反对建设该望远镜，爆发了抗议活动。

en: At issue is the TMT’s planned location on Mauna Kea, a dormant volcano worshiped by some Hawaiians as the piko, that connects the Hawaiian Islands to the heavens. (28 words)
cn: 争议的问题在于，三十米级望远镜的规划选址位于莫纳克亚山：一座休眠火山，一些夏威夷人奉为神山，认为它是连接夏威夷群岛与天空的脐带。

en: But Mauna Kea is also home to some of the world’s most powerful telescopes. (14 words)
cn: 但莫纳克亚山山上也安装了多台世界上最强大望远镜。

en: Rested in the Pacific Ocean, Mauna Kea’s peak rises above the bulk of our planet’s dense atmosphere, where conditions allow telescopes to obtain images of unsurpassed clarity. (27 words)
cn: 莫纳克亚山坐落在太平洋上，其顶峰拔地而起，高耸入云，穿过浓密的大气层；这里的条件能让望远镜拍到无与伦比的清晰图像。

en: Opposition to telescopes on Mauna Kea is nothing new. (9 words)
cn: 反对在莫纳克亚山上建造望远镜并不是什么新鲜事情。

en: A small but vocal group of Hawaiians and environmentalists have long viewed their presence as disrespect for sacred land and a painful reminder of the occupation of what was once a sovereign nation. (33 words)
cn: 有少数敢于直言的夏威夷人和环保主义者长期以来一直都认为这些望远镜的存在亵渎了圣土，触到了他们的痛处，提醒他们想起曾经的主权国家已被占领。

en: Some blame for the current controversy belongs to astronomers. (9 words)
cn: 近期发生争执，部分责任归咎于天文学家。

en: In their eagerness to build bigger telescopes, they forgot that science is not the only way of understanding the world. (20 words)
cn: 他们急于建造望远镜，但忘记了科学并非是理解世界的唯一途径。

en: They did not always prioritize the protection of Mauna Kea’s fragile ecosystems or its holiness to the island’s inhabitants. (19 words)
cn: 莫纳克亚山的生态系统极其脆弱，但其对于岛上居民具有神圣地位，他们没有始终优先考虑到这些问题。

en: Hawaiian culture is not a relic of the past; it is a living culture undergoing a renaissance today. (18 words)
cn: 夏威夷文化不是古风遗俗；而是一种鲜活的文化，当前正在复兴。

en: Yet science has a cultural history, too, with roots going back to the dawn of civilization. (16 words)
cn: 然而科学也有其文化史，其根源可以追溯至文明肇始之初。

en: The same curiosity to find what lies beyond the horizon that first brought early Polynesians to Hawaii’s shores inspires astronomers today to explore the heavens. (25 words)
cn: 最初，探索地平线之外的好奇心曾把早期的波利尼西亚人带到夏威夷海岸，如今，同样的好奇心驱使现在的天文学家去探索天空。

en: Calls to disassemble all telescopes on Mauna Kea or to ban future development there ignore the reality that astronomy and Hawaiian culture both seek to answer big questions about who we are, where we come from and where we are going. (41 words)
cn: 呼吁拆除莫纳克亚山上所有望远镜或者禁止未来在山上再建，都是罔顾事实，即：天文学和夏威夷文化这两者都在试图回答我们是谁、从何而来、要去何处等几个重大的问题。

en: Perhaps that is why we explore the starry skies, as if answering a primal calling to know ourselves and our true ancestral homes. (23 words)
cn: 或许这就是我们探索星空的原因，好像在回应一个原始的呼唤，去了解我们自己，了解我们祖先的真正家园。

en: The astronomy community is making compromises to change its use of Mauna Kea. (13 words)
cn: 天文学界也在让步，改变其对莫纳克亚山的利用方式。

en: The TMT site was chosen to minimize the telescope’s visibility around the island and to avoid archaeological and environmental impact. (20 words)
cn: 三十米级望远镜的选址尽量缩小，避免在全岛都能看得见望远镜，同时避免对考古和环境造成影响。

en: To limit the number of telescopes on Mauna Kea, old ones will be removed at the end of their lifetimes and their sites returned to a natural state. (28 words)
cn: 为限制莫纳克亚山上的望远镜数量，旧的在达到使用年限后将被移除，其原址会恢复到自然状态。

en: There is no reason why everyone cannot be welcomed on Mauna Kea to embrace their cultural heritage and to study the stars. (22 words)
cn: 完全有理由欢迎所有人到莫纳克亚，拥抱他们的文化遗产，探索星空。

en: Robert F. Kennedy once said that a country’s GDP measures “everything except that which makes life worthwhile.” (17 words)
cn: 罗伯特·F·肯尼迪曾说，一个国家的GDP（国内生产总值）可以衡量“一切，但却衡量不出人生价值的所在”。

en: With Britain voting to leave the European Union, and GDP already predicted to slow as a result, it is now a timely moment to assess what he was referring to. (30 words)
cn: 英国公投脱欧，有人已经预测GDP会因此下滑，现在正是考量肯尼迪这句话意欲所指的最佳时机。

en: The question of GDP and its usefulness has annoyed policymakers for over half a century. (15 words)
cn: 国内生产总值（GDP）及其实用性问题，半个多世纪来一直困扰着政策制定者。

en: Many argue that it is a flawed concept. (8 words)
cn: 很多人认为，这个概念存在缺陷。

en: It measures things that do not matter and misses things that do. (12 words)
cn: 它衡量的是无关痛痒的事情，却忽略了至关重要的事情。

en: By most recent measures, the UK’s GDP has been the envy of the Western world, with record low unemployment and high growth figures. (23 words)
cn: 根据最近的评估，英国的国内生产总值一直都是西方国家羡慕的对象，增长数据有史以来最高，同时失业率最低。

en: If everything was going so well, then why did over 17 million people vote for Brexit, despite the warnings about what it could do to their country’s economic prospects? (29 words)
cn: 如果诸事都如此顺遂，那为什么尽管有很多人警告说脱欧会影响英国经济前景，还是有 1700多万人投票支持脱欧？

en: A recent annual study of countries and their ability to convert growth into well-being sheds some light on that question. (20 words)
cn: 一份最近的年度报告或许能拨开这个问题的迷雾，该报告分析了不同国家及其将数据增长转化为社会福利的能力。

en: Across the 163 countries measured, the UK is one of the poorest performers in ensuring that economic growth is translated into meaningful improvements for its citizens. (26 words)
cn: 在所有评估的163个国家中，英国在确保经济增长能促进民生重大改善方面，表现最差。

en: Rather than just focusing on GDP, over 40 different sets of criteria from health, education and civil society engagement have been measured to get a more rounded assessment of how countries are performing. (33 words)
cn: 该报告并没有只关注国内生产总值，而是从健康、教育、公民社会参与度等40 多套不同的标准来进行衡量，从而更加全面评估各国的表现。

en: While all of these countries face their own challenges, there are a number of consistent themes. (16 words)
cn: 尽管所有的这些国家都面临各自的挑战，但仍有很多主题得一致面对。

en: Yes, there has been a budding economic recovery since the 2008 global crash, but in key indicators in areas such as health and education, major economies have continued to decline. (30 words)
cn: 的确，自从2008年全球经济危机以来，已经有经济复苏迹象萌芽，但是在健康和教育等领域的关键指标上，几大经济体还在继续下滑。

en: Yet this isn’t the case with all countries. (8 words)
cn: 然而，并非所有国家的情况都如此。

en: Some relatively poor European countries have seen huge improvements across measures including civil society, income equality and the environment. (19 words)
cn: 欧洲一些相对贫穷的国家在公民社会、收入公平和环境等诸多方面都有巨大的改善。

en: This is a lesson that rich countries can learn: When GDP is no longer regarded as the sole measure of a country’s success, the world looks very different. (28 words)
cn: 这也是一些富裕国家需要学习的经验：当国内生产总值不再是衡量国家成功与否的唯一标准，这个世界会看起来就会大不相同。

en: So, what Kennedy was referring to was that while GDP has been the most common method for measuring the economic activity of nations, as a measure, it is no longer enough. (31 words)
cn: 因为，肯尼迪的意思是，尽管国内生产总值一直是衡量国家经济活动的最常用的方法，但作为一种衡量标准，它还远远不够。

en: It does not include important factors such as environmental quality or education outcomes—all things that contribute to a person’s sense of well-being. (23 words)
cn: 它并不包括环境质量和教育成果这样的重要因素——所有的这些因素才会给人们带来幸福感。

en: The sharp hit to growth predicted around the world and in the UK could lead to a decline in the everyday services we depend on for our well-being and for growth. (31 words)
cn: 英国及世界各国都预测经济增长会受到重创，这会导致维持我们幸福生活和经济增长所倚靠的日常服务水平下降。

en: But policymakers who refocus efforts on improving well-being rather than simply worrying about GDP figures could avoid the forecasted doom and may even see progress. (25 words)
cn: 但是如果政策制定者集中精力提高社会福利，而不只是担心国内生产总值的数据，有可能避免注定的悲剧，或许甚至还能看到进步。

en: In a rare unanimous ruling, the US Supreme Court has overturned the corruption conviction of a former Virginia governor, Robert McDonnell. (21 words)
cn: 美国最高法院罕见地一致裁决，推翻了对弗吉尼亚州前州长罗伯特·麦克唐纳的贪腐定罪。

en: But it did so while holding its nose at the ethics of his conduct, which included accepting gifts such as a Rolex watch and a Ferrari automobile from a company seeking access to government. (34 words)
cn: 但做出裁决的同时，对他的道德行为也嗤之以鼻：一家公司想找机会接近政府官员，他从那家公司那里捞了一块劳力士手表和一辆法拉利跑车等礼物。

en: The high court’s decision said the judge in Mr. McDonnell’s trial failed to tell a jury that it must look only at his “official acts,” or the former governor’s decisions on “specific” and “unsettled” issues related to his duties. (39 words)
cn: 高院的裁决认为，麦克唐纳德先生一案中，法官未能告诉陪审团只能审查其“公务”行为：即在涉及与其公务职责有关的“具体”、“待定”的问题上，这个前任州长是如何决策的。

en: Merely helping a gift-giver gain access to other officials, unless done with clear intent to pressure those officials, is not corruption, the justices found. (24 words)
cn: 法官裁决，只是帮助送礼的人接近其他政府官员，如果没有明确意图要给这些官员施压，不算受贿。

en: The court did suggest that accepting favors in return for opening doors is “distasteful” and “nasty.” (16 words)
cn: 法院表示，开后门，收礼物，的确是“卑鄙下流，令人恶心”。

en: But under anti-bribery laws, proof must be made of concrete benefits, such as approval of a contract or regulation. (19 words)
cn: 但是根据反贿赂法，证据必须是具体的利益，比如帮人批了一份合同或者法规。

en: Simply arranging a meeting, making a phone call, or hosting an event is not an “official act”. (17 words)
cn: 只是安排见了个面，打了个电话，搞了个活动，不算“公务行为”。

en: The court’s ruling is legally sound in defining a kind of favoritism that is not criminal. (16 words)
cn: 法院对此案的裁决，界定它是无罪偏袒行为，在法律上是合理的。

en: Elected leaders must be allowed to help supporters deal with bureaucratic problems without fear of prosecution for bribery. (18 words)
cn: 应该允许当选的领导去帮助其支持者解决官僚问题，而不必担心被控受贿。

en: “The basic compact underlying representative government,” wrote Chief Justice John Roberts for the court, “assumes that public officials will hear from their constituents and act on their concerns.” (28 words)
cn: 首席大法官约翰·罗伯茨在法庭报告中提到，“奠定代议制政府基础的基本契约认定，公职人员应该倾听选民心声，为他们排忧解难。”

en: But the ruling reinforces the need for citizens and their elected representatives, not the courts, to ensure equality of access to government. (22 words)
cn: 但是法院的裁决强调，有必要由市民和他们选出的代表，而不是法庭，来确保公平接触政府部门。

en: Officials must not be allowed to play favorites in providing information or in arranging meetings simply because an individual or group provides a campaign donation or a personal gift. (29 words)
cn: 决不能允许政府官员仅因为个人或者团体提供了竞选资助或者个人礼物，就在提供信息或者安排会面方面厚此薄彼。

en: This type of integrity requires well-enforced laws in government transparency, such as records of official meetings, rules on lobbying, and information about each elected leader’s source of wealth. (28 words)
cn: 这种廉正建设需要充分贯彻法律，实现政务透明，比如官方会谈记录、游说

en: Favoritism in official access can fan public perceptions of corruption. (10 words)
cn: 公务会面中的偏袒行为会煽动公众的情绪，认为是腐败。

en: But it is not always corruption. (6 words)
cn: 但并不总是腐败。

en: Rather officials must avoid double standards, or different types of access for average people and the wealthy. (17 words)
cn: 相反，公务员必须避免采用双重标准：用不同的方式接待老百姓和有钱人。

en: If connections can be bought, a basic premise of democratic society—that all are equal in treatment by government—is undermined. (21 words)
cn: 如果这种联系可以收买，民主社会的基本前提——在政府代遇上人人平等——就被破坏了。

en: Good governance rests on an understanding of the inherent worth of each individual. (13 words)
cn: 善政良治有赖于充分理解每一个人内在价值。

en: The court’s ruling is a step forward in the struggle against both corruption and official favoritism. (16 words)
cn: 在与贪污腐败、公务偏袒的斗争中，最高法院的裁决是一次进步。

en: Among the annoying challenges facing the middle class is one that will probably go unmentioned in the next presidential campaign: What happens when the robots come for their jobs? (29 words)
cn: 中产阶级面临的众多烦人的事情中，有一个可能在下一届总统竞选中并不会提及：如果机器人来抢工作了，会发生什么？

en: Don’t dismiss that possibility entirely. (5 words)
cn: 不要以为完全不可能。

en: About half of U.S. jobs are at high risk of being automated, according to a University of Oxford study, with the middle class disproportionately squeezed. (25 words)
cn: 哈佛大学的一项研究表明，美国大约有一半的工作都很可能实现自动化，中产阶级面临极大的风险，受到排挤。

en: Lower-income jobs like gardening or day care don’t appeal to robots. (11 words)
cn: 低收入的工作，像园艺或者日托等，机器人不感兴趣。

en: But many middle-class occupations—trucking, financial advice, software engineering—have aroused their interest, or soon will. (16 words)
cn: 但是很多中产阶级做的工作，比如火车驾驶、理财咨询、软件工程等，已经引起他们的关注了，或者很快就会。

en: The rich own the robots, so they will be fine. (10 words)
cn: 机器人是富人的，他们没事。

en: This isn’t to be alarmist. (5 words)
cn: 这不是危言耸听。

en: Optimists point out that technological upheaval has benefited workers in the past. (12 words)
cn: 乐观主义者认为，技术变革在过去就曾让劳动者受益。

en: The Industrial Revolution didn’t go so well for Luddites whose jobs were displaced by mechanized looms, but it eventually raised living standards and created more jobs than it destroyed. (29 words)
cn: 工业革命因为卢德之流发展并不太顺利，他们的工作完全被机械化织布机取代；但工业革命最终提高了生活水平；相比起曾毁掉的工作，创造的工作更多。

en: Likewise, automation should eventually boost productivity, stimulate demand by driving down prices, and free workers from hard, boring work. (19 words)
cn: 同样，自动化最终也会推动生产力，通过拉低价格以刺激需求，并将工人从繁重、枯燥的工作中解放出来。

en: But in the medium term, middle-class workers may need a lot of help adjusting. (14 words)
cn: 但是在自动化的发展中期，中产阶级可能需要很多帮助来做出调整。

en: The first step, as Erik Brynjolfsson and Andrew McAfee argue in The Second Machine Age, should be rethinking education and job training. (22 words)
cn: 第一步，正如埃里克·布伦乔尔森和安德鲁·迈卡菲在其《第二次机械革命》一书中论述的那样，应该是反思教育和职业培训。

en: Curriculums—from grammar school to college —should evolve to focus less on memorizing facts and more on creativity and complex communication. (21 words)
cn: 学校的课程，从小学到大学，都应该发展成为少注重死记硬背，多注重创造力和复杂的沟通能力。

en: Vocational schools should do a better job of fostering problem-solving skills and helping students work alongside robots. (17 words)
cn: 职业学校应该更好的培养学生解决问题的能力，更好的帮助他们与机器人协同工作。

en: Online education can supplement the traditional kind. (7 words)
cn: 在线教育是传统教育的补充。

en: It could make extra training and instruction affordable. (8 words)
cn: 它使大家都能负担得起额外的培训和辅导。

en: Professionals trying to acquire new skills will be able to do so without going into debt. (16 words)
cn: 想要掌握新技术的专业人员，无需负债也将得偿所愿。

en: The challenge of coping with automation underlines the need for the U.S. to revive its fading business dynamism: Starting new companies must be made easier. (25 words)
cn: 应对自动化带来的挑战凸显出美国有必要恢复其日益衰退的商业活力。

en: In previous eras of drastic technological change, entrepreneurs smoothed the transition by dreaming up ways to combine labor and machines. (20 words)
cn: 在以前的技术大变革时代，企业家就想到把劳动力和机器结合起来的方式，实现了平稳过度。

en: The best uses of 3D printers and virtual reality haven’t been invented yet. (13 words)
cn: 三维打印机和虚拟现实迄今为止还没有得到充分应用。

en: The U.S. needs the new companies that will invent them. (10 words)
cn: 美国需要新公司来实现这些技术。

en: Finally, because automation threatens to widen the gap between capital income and labor income, taxes and the safety net will have to be rethought. (24 words)
cn: 最后，因为自动化可能会加深资本收入和劳动收入之间的鸿沟，所以必须得重新考虑税收和安全保障体系等问题。

en: Taxes on low-wage labor need to be cut, and wage subsidies such as the earned income tax credit should be expanded: This would boost incomes, encourage work, reward companies for job creation, and reduce inequality. (35 words)
cn: 缩减低收入劳工的税收，扩大劳动所得税抵扣等工资补贴：这将会提高收入、鼓励工作、奖励公司创造就业，并减少不平等现象。

en: Technology will improve society in ways big and small over the next few years, yet this will be little comfort to those who find their lives and careers upended by automation. (31 words)
cn: 技术在未来几年多少都会促进社会发展，然而对于自动化已经颠覆了其生活和事业的人来说，这几乎不是什么安慰。

en: Destroying the machines that are coming for our jobs would be nuts. (12 words)
cn: 捣毁抢走我们工作的机器是疯狂之举。

en: But policies to help workers adapt will be indispensable. (9 words)
cn: 但帮助工人适应的政策则是不可或缺的。

en: A new survey by Harvard University finds more than two-thirds of young Americans disapprove of President Trump’s use of Twitter. (20 words)
cn: 哈佛大学一项最新的调查发现，超过三分之二的美国年轻人不赞成特朗普总统使用推特。

en: The implication is that Millennials prefer news from the White House to be filtered through other sources, not a president’s social media platform. (23 words)
cn: 这表明，千禧一代更希望白宫消息由其他途径发布，而不是总统的社交平台。

en: Most Americans rely on social media to check daily headlines. (10 words)
cn: 大多数美国人都靠社交媒体来查看每天的头条新闻。

en: Yet as distrust has risen toward all media, people may be starting to beef up their media literacy skills. (19 words)
cn: 然而对所有媒体都越来越不信任，人们可能正在开始提高自己对媒体鉴别能力。

en: Such a trend is badly needed. (6 words)
cn: 这样的情形，势在必行。

en: During the 2016 presidential campaign, nearly a quarter of web content shared by Twitter users in the politically critical state of Michigan was fake news, according to the University of Oxford. (31 words)
cn: 根据牛津大学的研究，2016年的总统竞选期间，在具有重要政治意义的密歇根州，推特用户分享的网页内容有大约四分之一是虚假新闻。

en: And a survey conducted for BuzzFeed News found 44 percent of Facebook users rarely or never trust news from the media giant. (22 words)
cn: 并且，百资得新闻公司进行的一项调查发现，有 44%的脸书用户对来自该媒体巨头的新闻几乎不信任，或者根本不信任。

en: Young people who are digital natives are indeed becoming more skillful at separating fact from fiction in cyberspace. (18 words)
cn: 年轻人是数字土著，他们的确是越来越善于在网络空间分辨真假。

en: A Knight Foundation focus-group survey of young people between ages 14 and 24 found they use “distributed trust” to verify stories. (21 words)
cn: 奈特基金会对14到24岁的年轻人做了一次专题调查，发现他们用“分布式

en: They cross-check sources and prefer news from different perspectives—especially those that are open about any bias. (17 words)
cn: 他们多方核对信息来源，更喜欢有观点的新闻，尤其是那种对任何偏见都能

en: “Many young people assume a great deal of personal responsibility for educating themselves and actively seeking out opposing viewpoints,” the survey concluded. (22 words)
cn: 调查得出结论，“很多年轻人把进行自我教育，积极寻求对立观点作为自己的主要责任”。

en: Such active research can have another effect. (7 words)
cn: 这种积极的探寻可能产生另一种效果。

en: A 2014 survey conducted in Australia, Britain, and the United States by the University of Wisconsin-Madison found that young people’s reliance on social media led to greater political engagement. (29 words)
cn: 威斯康星大学麦迪逊分校在2014年对澳大利亚、英国和美国进行了一次调查，发现年轻人对社交媒体的依赖会让他们更多的了解政治。

en: Social media allows users to experience news events more intimately and immediately while also permitting them to re-share news as a projection of their values and interests. (27 words)
cn: 社交媒体使用户更加及时深入地体验新闻事件，同时他们还能够转发分享，表达自己的价值观和兴趣。

en: This forces users to be more conscious of their role in passing along information. (14 words)
cn: 这会促使用户在其传递信息的时候要更注意自己的作用。

en: A survey by Barna research group found the top reason given by Americans for the fake news phenomenon is “reader error,” more so than made-up stories or factual mistakes in reporting. (31 words)
cn: 巴纳研究小组的一项调查发现，美国人认为出现虚假新闻这种现象的首要原因是“读者失误”，而不是报道中的乱编故事或者内容失实。

en: About a third say the problem of fake news lies in “misinterpretation or exaggeration of actual news” via social media. (20 words)
cn: 大约有三分之一的人认为，虚假新闻的问题在于通过社交媒体“误解或者夸大了新闻真相”。

en: In other words, the choice to share news on social media may be the heart of the issue. (18 words)
cn: 换言之，选择在社交媒体上转发分享新闻或许是问题的核心。

en: “This indicates there is a real personal responsibility in counteracting this problem,” says Roxanne Stone, editor in chief at Barna Group. (21 words)
cn: 巴纳集团总编罗克珊·斯通说：“这表明，在解决这一问题过程中确实存在一个个人的责任问题”。

en: So when young people are critical of an over-tweeting president, they reveal a mental discipline in thinking skills—and in their choices on when to share on social media. (29 words)
cn: 所以，年轻人在批判总统发推太多的时候，展现出他们在思维上，以及选择何时在社交媒体上分享的自制力。

en: Any fair-minded assessment of the dangers of the deal between Britain’s National Health Service (NHS) and DeepMind must start by acknowledging that both sides mean well. (26 words)
cn: 对于英国国家医疗服务局（NHS）和深度思维公司（DeepMind）之间协议的种种危害，任何公正的评价都得首先承认双方本义是好的。

en: DeepMind is one of the leading artificial intelligence (AI) companies in the world. (13 words)
cn: 深度思维是世界上顶尖的人工智能公司。

en: The potential of this work applied to healthcare is very great, but it could also lead to further concentration of power in the tech giants. (25 words)
cn: 这一成果用到医保上，潜力巨大；但也可能会导致权力进一步集中到科技巨头上面。

en: It is against that background that the information commissioner, Elizabeth Denham, has issued her damning verdict against the Royal Free hospital trust under the NHS, which handed over to DeepMind the records of 1.6 million patients in 2015 on the basis of a vague agreement which took far too little account of the patients’ rights and their expectations of privacy. (60 words)
cn: 事情发生的背景是，信息专员伊丽莎白·德纳姆对国家医疗服务局下属的皇家自由信托公司做出有罪裁决，因为该公司在2015年仅凭一份模糊的协议就把160万病人的档案交给了深度思维公司，这份协议严重忽视了患者的权利及其期望得到的隐私保护。

en: DeepMind has almost apologized. (4 words)
cn: 深度思维基本上已经道歉了。

en: The NHS trust has mended its ways. (7 words)
cn: 国家医疗服务局也已改过自新。

en: Further arrangements —and there may be many—between the NHS and DeepMind will be carefully scrutinised to ensure that all necessary permissions have been asked of patients and all unnecessary data has been cleaned. (34 words)
cn: 国民医疗服务局和深度思维今后的协议，或许还有很多，都会受到严格的审查，以确保获得患者所有必要的许可，并删除所有不必要的数据。

en: There are lessons about informed patient consent to learn. (9 words)
cn: 患者的知情同意权，我们要吸取教训。

en: But privacy is not the only angle in this case and not even the most important. (16 words)
cn: 但是在本案中，隐私不是唯一的着眼点，甚至都不是最重要的。

en: Ms Denham chose to concentrate the blame on the NHS trust, since under existing law it “controlled” the data and DeepMind merely “processed” it. (24 words)
cn: 德纳姆女士集中火力攻击国家医疗局的信托公司，因为根据现行法律，它“掌控”数据，深度思维只能“处理”数据。

en: But this distinction misses the point that it is processing and aggregation, not the mere possession of bits, that gives the data value. (23 words)
cn: 但是这种区分忽略了一点：正是处理并聚合信息赋予数据价值的，而非只是拥有信息。

en: The great question is who should benefit from the analysis of all the data that our lives now generate. (19 words)
cn: 关键问题在于，分析我们现在生活中出现的所有数据，谁该从中获利？

en: Privacy law builds on the concept of damage to an individual from identifiable knowledge about them. (16 words)
cn: 隐私法设立的依据在于，个人识别信息会对其造成伤害。

en: That misses the way the surveillance economy works. (8 words)
cn: 但它并没有理解监察经济的运作模式。

en: The data of an individual there gains its value only when it is compared with the data of countless millions more. (21 words)
cn: 在此，个人数据只有在与成千上万不计其数的其他人的信息相比较之下才能体现其价值。

en: The use of privacy law to curb the tech giants in this instance feels slightly maladapted. (16 words)
cn: 在这一案例中，用隐私法来约束科技巨头，感觉略有不妥。

en: This practice does not address the real worry. (8 words)
cn: 这样做，不能解决真正的担忧。

en: It is not enough to say that the algorithms DeepMind develops will benefit patients and save lives. (17 words)
cn: 深度思维公司开发的算法有利于患者，还能挽救生命，但是光讲这一点还不够。

en: What matters is that they will belong to a private monopoly which developed them using public resources. (17 words)
cn: 重要的是，这些算法属于私人垄断企业，但却用了公共资源开发出来的。

en: If software promises to save lives on the scale that drugs now can, big data may be expected to behave as a big pharma has done. (26 words)
cn: 如果软件有望能像药物现在那样治病救人，那么大数据或许有望能像制药公司那样发挥作用。

en: We are still at the beginning of this revolution and small choices now may turn out to have gigantic consequences later. (21 words)
cn: 我们还在这场革命的起始阶段，现在小小的选择或许在今后产生大大的影响。

en: A long struggle will be needed to avoid a future of digital feudalism. (13 words)
cn: 为避免未来出现数字封建主义，需要付出长期的努力。

en: Ms Denham’s report is a welcome start. (7 words)
cn: 德纳姆女士的报告是一个良好的开端。

en: The U.S. Postal Service (USPS) continues to bleed red ink. (10 words)
cn: 美国邮政署（USPS）还在亏钱。

en: It reported a net loss of $5.6 billion for fiscal 2016, the 10th straight year its expenses have exceeded revenue. (20 words)
cn: 2016财年，其公布的净亏损额高达56亿美元，已经连续10年入不敷出了。

en: Meanwhile, it has more than $120 billion in unfunded liabilities, mostly for employee health and retirement costs. (17 words)
cn: 同时，它还有超过1200亿美元的无资金准备债务，主要是员工的医疗费和退休金。

en: There are many reasons this formerly stable federal institution finds itself on the verge of bankruptcy. (16 words)
cn: Fundamentally, the USPS is in a historic squeeze between technological change that has permanently decreased demand for its bread-and-butter product, first-class mail, and a regulatory structure that denies management the flexibility to adjust its operations to the new reality. (39 words)

en: And interest groups ranging from postal unions to greeting-card makers exert self-interested pressure on the USPS’s ultimate overseer—Congress—insisting that whatever else happens to the Postal Service, aspects of the status quo they depend on get protected. (38 words)
cn: 从邮政工会到贺卡制造商的各个利益集团处于自己的私立，都在给美国邮政的最终监管机构——国会施压，他们坚持要求，不管邮政署其他方面发生了什么，他们利益所在的方方面面的现状都要得到保护。

en: This is why repeated attempts at reform legislation have failed in recent years, leaving the Postal Service unable to pay its bills except by deferring vital modernization. (27 words)
cn: 这也解释了为什么最近几年一再努力，改革立法，均以失败告终，只留下邮政署无力支付账单，不得不推迟其至关重要的现代化改革。

en: Now comes word that everyone involved—Democrats, Republicans, the Postal Service, the unions and the system’s heaviest users—has finally agreed on a plan to fix the system. (28 words)
cn: 现在传来的消息是，有关的各方，包括民主党、共和党、美国邮政、工会组织，还有系统的极重度用户，最终都已经达成计划，解决邮政系统的问题。

en: Legislation is moving through the House that would save USPS an estimated $28.6 billion over five years, which could help pay for new vehicles, among other survival measures. (28 words)
cn: 众议院正要进行立法，这项法案预估能使美国邮政署在五年内节省大约286亿美元，这些钱能帮助他们购买新的运输工具，还可以花在其他维持生存的措施上。

en: Most of the money would come from a penny-per-letter permanent rate increase and from shifting postal retirees into Medicare. (19 words)
cn: 这笔钱主要来自两个方面，一是把每封信的固定邮费上调一美分，二是把邮政退休员工转到医保系统。

en: The latter step would largely offset the financial burden of annually pre-funding retiree health care, thus addressing a long-standing complaint by the USPS and its unions. (26 words)
cn: 后一项举措将会大大抵消每年为退休人员预付医保而带来的财务负担，这样也解决了美国邮政署及其公会长期抱怨的问题。

en: If it clears the House, this measure would still have to get through the Senate—where someone is bound to point out that it amounts to the bare, bare minimum necessary to keep the Postal Service afloat, not comprehensive reform. (40 words)
cn: 如果这一措施在众议院通过，它还得参议院通过——在参议院肯定有人会说，这只是能让邮政署勉强维持下去的最低，最低的救助方案，而不是全面的改革。

en: There’s no change to collective bargaining at the USPS, a major omission considering that personnel accounts for 80 percent of the agency’s costs. (23 words)
cn: 美国邮政署内的集体谈判没有任何变化，考虑到员工支出占到公司成本的80%，这是一个重大遗漏。

en: Also missing is any discussion of eliminating Saturday letter delivery. (10 words)
cn: 也根本没有讨论是否要取消周六信件投递的问题。

en: That common-sense change enjoys wide public support and would save the USPS $2 billion per year. (16 words)
cn: 这种按照常理做出的改变，得到大众广泛的支持，并且将为美国邮政署每年节省20亿美元。

en: But postal special-interest groups seem to have killed it, at least in the House. (14 words)
cn: 但是邮政系统的特殊利益集团似乎已经将其扼杀，至少在众议院是如此。

en: The emerging consensus around the bill is a sign that legislators are getting frightened about a politically embarrassing short-term collapse at the USPS. (23 words)
cn: 围绕这一法案达成的初步共识表明，立法人员担心的是美国邮政署短期的崩溃会给他们带来政治上的难堪。

en: It is not, however, a sign that they’re getting serious about transforming the postal system for the 21st century. (19 words)
cn: 然而，这也表明，他们并没有认真对待邮政系统面向21世纪的转型问题。

en: Financial regulators in Britain have imposed a rather unusual rule on the bosses of big banks. (16 words)
cn: 英国金融监管机构给几大银行的老总强制推行了一项极不寻常的规定。

en: Starting next year, any guaranteed bonus of top executives could be delayed 10 years if their banks are under investigation for wrongdoing. (22 words)
cn: 明年起，如果银行因不当行为受到调查，其高管的所有固定将近都可能会推迟10年发放。

en: The main purpose of this “clawback” rule is to hold bankers accountable for harmful risk-taking and to restore public trust in financial institutions. (23 words)
cn: 这项“追款”规定的主要目的是要让银行家为有害的冒险行为负责，重塑公众对金融机构的信任。

en: Yet officials also hope for a much larger benefit: more long-term decision-making, not only by banks but by all corporations, to build a stronger economy for future generations. (28 words)
cn: 然而，官员们还期望得到更大的利益：无论是银行还是所有公司，都要做更长远的决策，为子孙后代打造更强的经济。

en: “Short-termism” or the desire for quick profits, has worsened in publicly traded companies, says the Bank of England’s top economist, Andrew Haldane. (22 words)
cn: “短期主义”，或者说渴望快速获利，在上市公司中愈演愈烈，英格兰银行首席经济学家安德鲁·霍尔丹这样说道。

en: He quotes a giant of classical economics, Alfred Marshall, in describing this financial impatience as acting like “children who pick the plums out of their pudding to eat them at once” rather than putting them aside to be eaten last. (40 words)
cn: 他援引古典经济学巨匠阿尔弗雷德·马歇尔的话，把这种金融上的急躁行为形容成是“孩子从布丁里挑出葡萄干马上就吃了”，而不是放在一边最后吃。

en: The average time for holding a stock in both the United States and Britain, he notes, has dropped from seven years to seven months in recent decades. (27 words)
cn: 在英美两国，持有一只股票的平均时长，他指出，在最近几十年从七年减少至七个月。

en: Transient investors, who demand high quarterly profits from companies, can hinder a firm’s efforts to invest in long-term research or to build up customer loyalty. (25 words)
cn: 短期投资者要求每个季度都能从公司获得高额利润，而公司要尽力投资长期的研发，要尽力增强客户的忠实度，他们可能会成为阻碍。

en: This has been dubbed “quarterly capitalism”. (6 words)
cn: 这就是人们所称的“季度资本主义”。

en: In addition, new digital technologies have allowed more rapid trading of equities, quicker use of information, and thus shorter attention spans in financial markets. (24 words)
cn: 此外，新的数字技术使得股票交易更快速，信息使用更高效，因此使得金融

en: “There seems to be a predominance of short-term thinking at the expense of long-term investing,” said Commissioner Daniel Gallagher of the US Securities and Exchange Commission in a speech this week. (31 words)
cn: “占主导地位的似乎是短线思维，其代价是损害长期投资”，美国证券交易委员会委员丹尼尔·盖伦格在本周的一场演讲中这样说道。

en: In the US, the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002 has pushed most public companies to defer performance bonuses for senior executives by about a year, slightly helping reduce “short-termism.” (28 words)
cn: 在美国，已经迫使大多数上市公司推迟一年左右发放高管的绩效津贴，略微有助于减少“短期主义”现象。

en: In its latest survey of CEO pay, The Wall Street Journal finds that “a substantial part” of executive pay is now tied to performance. (24 words)
cn: 《华尔街日报》最近调查了首席执行官的薪酬，发现高管薪酬的“大部分”现在都与业绩挂钩。

en: Much more could be done to encourage “long-termism,” such as changes in the tax code and quicker disclosure of stock acquisitions. (21 words)
cn: 要鼓励“长期主义”，我们要做的还有很多，比如修订税法，加快披露股票收购。

en: In France, shareholders who hold onto a company investment for at least two years can sometimes earn more voting rights in a company. (23 words)
cn: 在法国，持有某个公司股票两年以上的股东有时候在公司会有更大的投票权。

en: Within companies, the right compensation design can provide incentives for executives to think beyond their own time at the company and on behalf of all stakeholders. (26 words)
cn: 在公司内部，合理的薪酬设计能激励管理者，让他们能够深谋远虑，不局限与自己在公司的任期期限，还能为所有股东着想。

en: Britain’s new rule is a reminder to bankers that society has an interest in their performance, not just for the short term but for the long term. (27 words)
cn: 英国的这条新政是在提醒银行家，社会在关注他们的业绩，不只是短期业绩，还有长期业绩。

en: Grade inflation—the gradual increase in average GPAs (grade-point averages) over the past few decades—is often considered a product of a consumer era in higher education, in which students are treated like customers to be pleased. (37 words)
cn: 成绩通胀——近几十年学生的平均绩点（GPA）逐渐上升——常常看成是高等教育消费者时代的产物；在这样的时代下，学生被当做客户，是需要取悦的对象。

en: But another, related force—a policy often buried deep in course catalogs called “grade forgiveness”—is helping raise GPAs. (19 words)
cn: 但另一个相关因素——通常深藏在课程目录里叫做“成绩宽容”的政策——也在助长绩点的走高。

en: Grade forgiveness allows students to retake a course in which they received a low grade, and the most recent grade or the highest grade is the only one that counts in calculating a student’s overall GPA. (36 words)
cn: 成绩宽容政策可以让学生重修得分较低的课程，并且在计算某个学生总绩点的时候只统计最近的或者最高的分数。

en: The use of this little-known practice has accelerated in recent years, as colleges continue to do their utmost to keep students in school (and paying tuition) and improve their graduation rates. (31 words)
cn: 近年来，这一鲜为人知的做法得以加速应用，因为各大高校还在竭尽全力留住学生（收学费），提高毕业率。

en: When this practice first started decades ago, it was usually limited to freshmen, to give them a second chance to take a class in their first year if they struggled in their transition to college-level courses. (36 words)
cn: 数十年前该做法刚实行时，通常仅适用于大一新生，为的是在其向大学水平课程艰难过渡的过程中，给予他们一次在第一学年重修课程的机会。

en: But now most colleges, save for many selective campuses, allow all undergraduates, and even graduate students, to get their low grades forgiven. (22 words)
cn: 但现在，除许多知名院校外，多数大学允许所有本科生，甚至研究生得到低分"宽恕"。

en: College officials tend to emphasize that the goal of grade forgiveness is less about the grade itself and more about encouraging students to retake courses critical to their degree program and graduation without incurring a big penalty. (37 words)
cn: 高校管理者常常强调，成绩宽恕的目的并不在分数本身，更多是为了鼓励学生重修那些事关学位和毕业的课程而免于受到严重处分。

en: "Ultimately," said Jack Miner, Ohio State University's registrar, "we see students achieve more success because they retake a course and do better in subsequent courses or master the content that allows them to graduate on time." (36 words)
cn: "最终，"俄亥俄州立大学教务主任杰克·迈纳说，"我们看到学生获得更大的成功，因为他们重修了一门课程而在后续课程中表现更好，或掌握了让他们得以按时毕业的内容。"

en: That said, there is a way in which grade forgiveness satisfies colleges' own needs as well. (16 words)
cn: 即便如此，成绩宽恕也以某种方式满足了大学自身的需要。

en: For public institutions, state funds are sometimes tied partly to their success on metrics such as graduation rates and student retention—so better grades can, by boosting figures like those, mean more money. (33 words)
cn: 对公立机构而言，州政府拨款有时部分取决于其在毕业率、学生保留率等指标上的成功——因此成绩越好，就越能提升上述数据，从而带来更多拨款。

en: And anything that raises GPAs will likely make students—who, at the end of the day, are paying the bill—feel they've gotten a better value for their tuition dollars, which is another big concern for colleges. (37 words)
cn: 而且，任何能提高GPA的东西都可能让学生——说到底，是他们在埋单——觉得他们的学费花得更值了，这是大学极为关心的另一点。

en: Indeed, grade forgiveness is just another way that universities are responding to consumers' expectations for higher education. (17 words)
cn: 实际上，成绩宽恕不过是大学回应消费者对高等教育期望的又一种方式。

en: Since students and parents expect a college degree to lead to a job, it is in the best interest of a school to turn out graduates who are as qualified as possible—or at least appear to be. (38 words)
cn: 既然学生和家长都期望大学文凭能带来一份工作，那么产出尽可能合格的毕业生——或者至少看起来合格——就是最符合学校利益的做法。

en: On this, students' and colleges' incentives seem to be aligned. (10 words)
cn: 在这一点上，学生和大学的动机似乎是一致的。

en: This year marks exactly two centuries since the publication of Frankenstein; or, The Modern Prometheus, by Mary Shelley. (18 words)
cn: 今年恰好是玛丽·雪莱《弗兰肯斯坦》——又名《现代普罗米修斯》——出版两百周年。

en: Even before the invention of the electric light bulb, the author produced a remarkable work of speculative fiction that would foreshadow many ethical questions to be raised by technologies yet to come. (32 words)
cn: 甚至在电灯泡发明之前，这位作者就写出了一部非凡的科幻小说，预示了诸多尚未诞生的技术日后将会引发的伦理追问。

en: Today the rapid growth of artificial intelligence (AI) raises fundamental questions: “What is intelligence, identity, or consciousness? What makes humans humans?” (21 words)
cn: 如今，人工智能（AI）的迅猛发展引出了一些根本性的问题："什么是智能、身份认同或意识？是什么让人成为人？"

en: What is being called artificial general intelligence, machines that would imitate the way humans think, continues to evade scientists. (19 words)
cn: 所谓通用人工智能——能够模仿人类思维方式的机器——至今仍是科学家们难以攻克的难题。

en: Yet humans remain fascinated by the idea of robots that would look, move, and respond like humans, similar to those recently depicted on popular sci-fi TV series such as “Westworld” and “Humans.”(32 words)
cn: 然而，人类始终对外形、动作和反应都与真人无异的机器人痴迷不已，就像近来热播的科幻剧《西部世界》和《真实的人类》中所呈现的那样。

en: Just how people think is still far too complex to be understood, let alone reproduced, says David Eagleman, a Stanford University neuroscientist. (22 words)
cn: 斯坦福大学神经科学家大卫·伊格尔曼说，人类的思维方式究竟如何运作，至今仍复杂得难以理解，更遑论复制。

en: “We are just in a situation where there are no good theories explaining what consciousness actually is and how you could ever build a machine to get there.” (28 words)
cn: "我们目前的困境在于，没有任何成熟的理论能说清楚意识究竟是什么，也没有人知道怎样才能造出一台能够实现意识的机器。"

en: But that doesn’t mean crucial ethical issues involving AI aren’t at hand. (12 words)
cn: 但这并不意味着人工智能所涉及的重要伦理问题还远在天边。

en: The coming use of autonomous vehicles, for example, poses thorny ethical questions. (12 words)
cn: 以即将普及的自动驾驶汽车为例，它就带来了一系列棘手的伦理难题。

en: Human drivers sometimes must make split-second decisions. (7 words)
cn: 人类驾驶员有时不得不在电光火石之间做出判断。

en: Their reactions may be a complex combination of instant reflexes, input from past driving experiences, and what their eyes and ears tell them in that moment. (26 words)
cn: 他们的反应，可能是即时本能、过往驾驶经验的积累，以及当下视听感知三者交织而成的复杂产物。

en: AI “vision” today is not nearly as sophisticated as that of humans. (12 words)
cn: 如今人工智能的"视觉"，远不及人类的精密。

en: And to anticipate every imaginable driving situation is a difficult programming problem. (12 words)
cn: 而要预判每一种可能出现的驾驶情境，本身就是一道极难解决的编程难题。

en: Whenever decisions are based on masses of data, “you quickly get into a lot of ethical questions,” notes Tan Kiat How, chief executive of a Singapore-based agency that is helping the government develop a voluntary code for the ethical use of AI. (42 words)
cn: 每当决策依赖于海量数据，"你很快就会陷入大量伦理问题之中，"陈杰豪指出；他是一家新加坡机构的首席执行官，该机构正协助政府制定人工智能伦理使用的自愿性准则。

en: Along with Singapore, other governments and mega-corporations are beginning to establish their own guidelines. (14 words)
cn: 与新加坡一道，其他国家政府和大型企业也开始着手制定各自的规范准则。

en: Britain is setting up a data ethics center. (8 words)
cn: 英国正在筹建数据伦理中心。

en: India released its AI ethics strategy this spring. (8 words)
cn: 印度于今年春天发布了本国的人工智能伦理战略。

en: On June 7 Google pledged not to “design or deploy AI” that would cause “overall harm,” or to develop AI-directed weapons or use AI for surveillance that would violate international norms. (31 words)
cn: 6月7日，谷歌承诺不"设计或部署"会造成"整体伤害"的人工智能，不研发以人工智能为导向的武器，也不将人工智能用于违反国际准则的监控活动。

en: It also pledged not to deploy AI whose use would violate international laws or human rights. (16 words)
cn: 谷歌还承诺，不部署任何使用方式会违反国际法或侵害人权的人工智能。

en: While the statement is vague, it represents one starting point. (10 words)
cn: 这份声明措辞虽然模糊，却代表了一个起点。

en: So does the idea that decisions made by AI systems should be explainable, transparent, and fair. (16 words)
cn: 人工智能系统所做的决策应当可解释、透明且公正，这一理念同样如此。

en: To put it another way: How can we make sure that the thinking of intelligent machines reflects humanity’s highest values? (20 words)
cn: 换言之：我们如何确保智能机器的"思考"，能够体现人类最崇高的价值观？

en: (12 words) 唯有如此，它们才能成为称职的仆人，而非弗兰肯斯坦笔下那个失控的怪物

States will be able to force more people to pay sales tax when they make online purchases under a Supreme Court decision Thursday that will leave shoppers with lighter wallets but is a big financial win for states. (38 words)
cn: 根据最高法院周四的裁定，各州将有权强制更多的人为在线购物支付销售税，这会让消费者的钱包瘪下去，但对各州而言是财政上的一大胜利。

en: The Supreme Court's opinion Thursday overruled a pair of decades-old decisions that states said cost them billions of dollars in lost revenue annually. (23 words)
cn: 最高法院周四的这项意见推翻了数十年前的两起裁定，各州表示这两起裁定每年给自己的财政收入造成了数十亿美元的损失。

en: The decisions made it more difficult for states to collect sales tax on certain online purchases. (16 words)
cn: 这两起裁定加大了各州对某些网购商品的销售税征收难度。

en: The cases the court overturned said that if a business was shipping a customer's purchase to a state where the business didn't have a physical presence such as a warehouse or office, the business didn't have to collect sales tax for the state. (43 words)
cn: 最高法院所推翻的判例认为，如果企业将顾客购得的商品运往某个州，而该企业在本州并无实体存在（比如仓库或办公室），那么该企业就不需要为本州代收销售税。

en: Customers were generally responsible for paying the sales tax to the state themselves if they weren't charged it, but most didn't realize they owed it and few paid. (28 words)
cn: 一般来说，并未被收取销售税的顾客需要自行向所在州缴纳，但大多数人并不知道自己欠了税，而上缴税款的消费者也很少。

en: Justice Anthony Kennedy wrote that the previous decisions were flawed. (10 words)
cn: 大法官安东尼·肯尼迪写道，此前的裁定有疏漏之处。

en: "Each year the physical presence rule becomes further removed from economic reality and results in significant revenue losses to the states," he wrote in an opinion joined by four other justices. (31 words)
cn: "实体存在规则每年都愈发脱离经济现实，使各州蒙受巨额收入损失，"他连同其他四位大法官在联署的意见书中写道。

en: Kennedy wrote that the rule "limited states' ability to seek long-term prosperity and has prevented market participants from competing on an even playing field." (24 words)
cn: 肯尼迪还写道，这项规则"限制了各州寻求长久繁荣的能力，使市场参与者无法在公平的环境下展开竞争。"

en: The ruling is a victory for big chains with a presence in many states, since they usually collect sales tax on online purchases already. (24 words)
cn: 这一裁定对在多州设有实体存在的大型连锁店而言是一次胜利，因为他们通常已经对网购商品收取销售税了。

en: Now, rivals will be charging sales tax where they hadn't before. (11 words)
cn: 如今，竞争者将要对此前并未征税的消费者开始收取销售税。

en: Big chains have been collecting sales tax nationwide because they typically have physical stores in whatever state a purchase is being shipped to. (23 words)
cn: 大型连锁店已经在全国范围内收取销售税，这是因为无论商品运往哪个州，他们一般在当地都设有实体门店。

en: Amazon.com, with its network of warehouses, also collects sales tax in every state that charges it, though third-party sellers who use the site don't have to. (26 words)
cn: 亚马逊有遍布各地的仓储网络，同样在每个收取销售税的州执行代收，不过利用亚马逊网站的第三方卖家倒不必如此。

en: Until now, many sellers that have a physical presence in only a single state or a few states have been able to avoid charging sales taxes when they ship to addresses outside those states. (34 words)
cn: 在此之前，许多仅在一个或数个州有实体存在的商家，在向州外地址发货时都可以避免代收销售税。

en: Sellers that use eBay and Etsy, which provide platforms for smaller sellers, also haven't been collecting sales tax nationwide. (19 words)
cn: 那些使用eBay和Etsy的商家——这两个平台均为较小商家提供销售渠道——也没有在全国范围内代收销售税。

en: Under the ruling Thursday, states can pass laws requiring out-of-state sellers to collect the state's sales tax from customers and send it to the state. (25 words)
cn: 根据周四的裁定，各州可通过法律，要求州外销售商对本州顾客收取销售税，并上缴本州。

en: Retail trade groups praised the ruling, saying it levels the playing field for local and online businesses. (17 words)
cn: 零售贸易团体赞扬了这项裁定，称其为地方商家和线上商家创造了公平的竞争环境。

en: The losers, said retail analyst Neil Saunders, are online-only retailers, especially smaller ones. (13 words)
cn: 零售分析师尼尔·桑德斯表示，输家是纯线上零售商，尤其是小型纯线上零售商。

en: Those retailers may face headaches complying with various state sales tax laws. (12 words)
cn: 这些零售商可能要面临遵守各州不尽相同的销售税法的种种麻烦。

en: The Small Business & Entrepreneurship Council advocacy group said in a statement, "Small businesses and internet entrepreneurs are not well served at all by this decision." (26 words)
cn: 小企业与创业委员会这一倡议团体在声明中表示："小型企业和互联网企业家完全没有得到本次裁定的丝毫考虑。"

en: A group of labour MPs, among them Yvette Cooper, are bringing in the new year with a call to institute a UK “town of culture” award. (26 words)
cn: 包括伊薇特·库珀在内的一些工党议员在新年之际呼吁设立英国“文化小镇”奖。

en: The proposal is that it should sit alongside the existing city of culture title, which was held by Hull in 2017, and has been awarded to Coventry for 2021. (29 words)
cn: 该提议认为，这一奖项应与现有的“文化之城”称号并列设置，该称号曾由赫尔在2017年获得，并已授予考文垂作为2021年的获得者。

en: Cooper and her colleagues argue that the success of the crown for Hull, where it brought in £220m of investment and an avalanche of arts, ought not to be confined to cities. (32 words)
cn: 库珀及其同事认为，赫尔获得该称号的成功——它带来了2.2亿英镑的投资和大量艺术活动——不应仅局限于城市。

en: Britain’s towns, it is true, are not prevented from applying, but they generally lack the resources to put together a bid to beat their bigger competitors. (26 words)
cn: 诚然，英国的小镇并未被禁止申请，但它们通常缺乏整合资源提出申请以战胜更大竞争对手的能力。

en: A town of culture award could, it is argued, become an annual event, attracting funding and creating jobs. (18 words)
cn: 有人认为，“文化小镇”奖可以成为一项年度活动，从而吸引资金并创造就业。

en: Some might see the proposal as a booby prize for the fact that Britain is no longer able to apply for the much more prestigious title of European capital of culture, a sought-after award bagged by Glasgow in 1990 and Liverpool in 2008. (43 words)
cn: 有人可能会将这一提议视为一种安慰，因为英国已无法申请更具声望的“欧洲文化之都”称号，而该奖项曾分别于1990年授予格拉斯哥、2008年授予利物浦。

en: A cynic might speculate that the UK is on the verge of disappearing into an endless fever of self-celebration in its desperation to reinvent itself for the post-Brexit world: after town of culture, who knows what will follow—village of culture? Suburb of culture? Hamlet of culture? (47 words)
cn: 愤世嫉俗者或许会认为，英国在脱欧后急于重塑自身，正濒临陷入无休止的自我庆祝狂热之中：继“文化小镇”之后，谁知道接下来会是什么——“文化村庄”吗？“文化郊区”？还是“文化村落”？

en: It is also wise to recall that such titles are not a cure-all. (13 words)
cn: 同时也应当记住，这类称号并非灵丹妙药。

en: A badly run “year of culture” washes in and out of a place like the tide, bringing prominence for a spell but leaving no lasting benefits to the community. (29 words)
cn: 一个运作不善的“文化年”就像潮水般来去匆匆，虽能带来一时的关注，却不会给当地社区留下持久益处。

en: The really successful holders of such titles are those that do a great deal more than fill hotel bedrooms and bring in high-profile arts events and good press for a year. (31 words)
cn: 真正成功的获得者所做的远不止填满酒店客房、举办高规格艺术活动以及带来一年的媒体关注。

en: They transform the aspirations of the people who live there; they nudge the self-image of the city into a bolder and more optimistic light. (24 words)
cn: 它们改变当地居民的追求，并推动城市的自我形象变得更加大胆和乐观。

en: It is hard to get right, and requires a remarkable degree of vision, as well as cooperation between city authorities, the private sector, community groups and cultural organisations. (28 words)
cn: 这并不容易做到，需要非凡的远见，以及政府部门、私营部门、社区团体和文化机构之间的合作。

en: But it can be done: Glasgow’s year as European capital of culture can certainly be seen as one of a complex series of factors that have turned the city into the powerhouse of art, music and theatre that it remains today. (41 words)
cn: 但这是可以实现的：格拉斯哥作为“欧洲文化之都”的那一年，完全可以看作一系列复杂因素之一，这些因素使该城市发展成为如今仍然是艺术、音乐和戏剧重镇。

en: A “town of culture” could be not just about the arts but about honouring a town’s peculiarities—helping sustain its high street, supporting local facilities and above all celebrating its people. (31 words)
cn: “文化小镇”不仅关乎艺术，还在于彰显小镇的独特性——帮助维系商业街，支持本地设施，尤其是赞美当地居民。

en: Jeremy Wright, the culture secretary, should welcome this positive, hope-filled proposal, and turn it into action. (16 words)
cn: 文化大臣杰里米·赖特应当欢迎这一积极且充满希望的提议，并将其付诸行动。

en: Scientific publishing has long been a licence to print money. (10 words)
cn: 科学出版长期以来一直是一种“印钞许可证”。

en: Scientists need journals in which to publish their research, so they will supply the articles without monetary reward. (18 words)
cn: 科学家需要期刊来发表研究成果，因此他们会在没有报酬的情况下提供论文。

en: Other scientists perform the specialised work of peer review also for free, because it is a central element in the acquisition of status and the production of scientific knowledge. (29 words)
cn: 其他科学家也免费从事专业性的同行评审工作，因为这是获取学术地位和产出科学知识的核心环节。

en: With the content of papers secured for free, the publisher needs only to find a market for its journal. (19 words)
cn: 在论文内容免费获得的情况下，出版商只需为其期刊寻找市场。

en: Until this century, university libraries were not very price sensitive. (10 words)
cn: 直到本世纪，大学图书馆对价格并不十分敏感。

en: Scientific publishers routinely report profit margins approaching 40% on their operations, at a time when the rest of the publishing industry is in an existential crisis. (26 words)
cn: 在整个出版业面临生存危机之际，科学出版商却常常报告其业务利润率接近40%。

en: The Dutch giant Elsevier, which claims to publish 25% of the scientific papers produced in the world, made profits of more than £900m last year, while UK universities alone spent more than £210m in 2016 to enable researchers to access their own publicly funded research; both figures seem to rise unstoppably despite increasingly desperate efforts to change them. (58 words)
