cn: 荷兰出版巨头爱思唯尔声称其出版了全球25%的科研论文，去年其利润超过9亿英镑；与此同时，仅英国各大学在2016年就花费了超过2.1亿英镑，使研究人员能够获取他们自己由公共资金资助的研究成果；尽管人们越来越努力试图改变这种状况，这两个数字似乎仍在不断上升。

en: The most drastic, and thoroughly illegal, reaction has been the emergence of Sci-Hub, a kind of global photocopier for scientific papers, set up in 2012, which now claims to offer access to every paywalled article published since 2015. (38 words)
cn: 最激烈且完全非法的反应是Sci-Hub的出现，这是一种“全球科研论文复印机”，创建于2012年，如今声称可提供自2015年以来所有付费墙论文的访问。

en: The success of Sci-Hub, which relies on researchers passing on copies they have themselves legally accessed, shows the legal ecosystem has lost legitimacy among its users and must be transformed so that it works for all participants. (37 words)
cn: Sci-Hub的成功——它依赖研究人员共享他们合法获取的论文副本——表明现行的法律体系已在用户中失去正当性，必须加以改革，使其能够服务所有参与者。

en: In Britain the move towards open access publishing has been driven by funding bodies. (14 words)
cn: 在英国，向开放获取出版转变的推动力来自资助机构。

en: In some ways it has been very successful. (8 words)
cn: 从某些方面来看，这一转变是非常成功的。

en: More than half of all British scientific research is now published under open access terms: either freely available from the moment of publication, or paywalled for a year or more so that the publishers can make a profit before being placed on general release. (44 words)
cn: 如今，英国超过一半的科研成果以开放获取形式发表：要么自发表之日起即可免费获取，要么先设置一年的或更长时间的付费墙，以便出版商在公开发布之前获取利润。

en: Yet the new system has not worked out any cheaper for the universities. (13 words)
cn: 然而，这一新体系并未使大学的成本降低。

en: Publishers have responded to the demand that they make their product free to readers by charging their writers fees to cover the costs of preparing an article. (27 words)
cn: 出版商通过向作者收取费用来应对“向读者免费开放内容”的要求，以此弥补论文准备的成本。

en: These range from around ￡500 to $5,000. (7 words)
cn: 这些费用从约500英镑到5000美元不等。

en: A report last year pointed out that the costs both of subscriptions and of these “article preparation costs” had been steadily rising at a rate above inflation. (27 words)
cn: 去年的一份报告指出，订阅费用和这些“论文处理费用”都在持续上涨，涨幅超过通货膨胀率。

en: In some ways the scientific publishing model resembles the economy of the social Internet: labour is provided free in exchange for the hope of status, while huge profits are made by a few big firms who run the market places. (40 words)
cn: 在某种程度上，科学出版模式类似于社交互联网经济：人们免费提供劳动以换取地位的期望，而巨额利润则被少数运营平台的大公司获取。

en: In both cases, we need a rebalancing of power. (9 words)
cn: 在这两种情况下，我们都需要重新平衡权力。

en: Progressives often support diversity mandates as a path to equality and a way to level the playing field. (18 words)
cn: 进步派人士往往支持多元化配额政策，将其视为实现平等、营造公平竞争环境的一种途径。

en: But all too often such policies are an insincere form of virtue-signaling that benefits only the most privileged and does little to help average people. (25 words)
cn: 但这种政策往往只是一种并不真诚的“道德姿态展示”，只使最具特权者受益，而对普通人帮助甚微。

en: A pair of bills sponsored by Massachusetts state Senator Jason Lewis and House Speaker Pro Tempore Patricia Haddad, to ensure “gender parity” on boards and commissions, provide a case in point. (31 words)
cn: 马萨诸塞州参议员杰森·刘易斯和众议院临时议长帕特里夏·哈达德提出的两项旨在确保各类委员会实现“性别均衡”的法案，就是一个很好的例证。

en: Haddad and Lewis are concerned that more than half the state-government boards are less than 40 percent female. (18 words)
cn: 哈达德和刘易斯担心，该州超过一半的政府委员会中女性比例不足40%。

en: In order to ensure that elite women have more such opportunities, they have proposed imposing government quotas. (17 words)
cn: 为了确保精英女性能够获得更多此类机会，他们提出实行政府配额制度。

en: If the bills become law, state boards and commissions will be required to set aside 50 percent of board seats for women by 2022. (24 words)
cn: 如果这些法案成为法律，各州委员会将被要求在2022年前预留50%的席位给女性。

en: The bills are similar to a measure recently adopted in California, which last year became the first state to require gender quotas for private companies. (25 words)
cn: 这些法案类似于加利福尼亚州最近通过的一项措施，而该州去年成为首个要求私营企业实行性别配额的州。

en: In signing the measure, California Governor Jerry Brown admitted that the law, which明确按性别对人进行分类，可能是违宪的。 (24 words)
cn: 在签署该法案时，加利福尼亚州州长杰里·布朗承认，这项明确以性别对人进行分类的法律很可能是违宪的。

en: The US Supreme Court frowns on sex-based classifications unless they are designed to address an “important” policy interest. (18 words)
cn: 美国最高法院通常不赞成基于性别的分类，除非这种做法是为了解决某种“重要”的政策利益。

en: Because the California law applies to all boards, even where there is no history of prior discrimination, courts are likely to rule that the law violates the constitutional guarantee of “equal protection.” (32 words)
cn: 由于加利福尼亚州的这项法律适用于所有委员会，即便在不存在既往歧视历史的情况下也是如此，法院很可能会裁定该法律违反了宪法所保障的“平等保护”。

en: But are such government mandates even necessary? (7 words)
cn: 但这些政府强制措施真的有必要吗？

en: Female participation on corporate boards may not currently mirror the percentage of women in the general population, but so what? (20 words)
cn: 企业董事会中的女性比例目前或许未能反映总体人口中的女性比例，但这又有什么关系呢？

en: The number of women on corporate boards has been steadily increasing without government interference. (14 words)
cn: 在没有政府干预的情况下，企业董事会中的女性人数一直在稳步增加。

en: According to a study by Catalyst, between 2010 and 2015 the share of women on the boards of global corporations increased by 54 percent. (24 words)
cn: 根据Catalyst的一项研究，2010年至2015年间，全球企业董事会中的女性比例增长了54%。

en: Requiring companies to make gender the primary qualification for board membership will inevitably lead to less experienced private sector boards. (20 words)
cn: 要求企业将性别作为董事会成员的首要资格，必然会导致私营部门董事会经验不足。

en: That is exactly what happened when Norway adopted a nationwide corporate gender quota. (13 words)
cn: 这正是挪威实施全国性企业性别配额时所发生的情况。

en: Writing in The New Republic, Alice Lee notes that increasing the number of opportunities for board membership without increasing the pool of qualified women to serve on such boards has led to a “golden skirt” phenomenon, where the same elite women scoop up multiple seats on a variety of boards. (50 words)
cn: 爱丽丝·李在《新共和》撰文指出，在未扩大具备任职资格女性人选规模的情况下增加董事会席位机会，已导致一种“金裙子”现象，即同一批精英女性在不同董事会中同时占据多个席位。

en: Next time somebody pushes corporate quotas as a way to promote gender equity, remember that such policies are largely self-serving measures that make their sponsors feel good but do little to help average women. (34 words)
cn: 下次有人以企业配额作为促进性别平等的手段时，应当记住，这类政策在很大程度上是自利之举，它们使倡导者自我感觉良好，却对普通女性帮助甚微。

en: Last Thursday, the French Senate passed a digital services tax, which would impose an entirely new tax on large multinationals that provide digital services to consumers or users in France. (30 words)
cn: 上周四，法国参议院通过了一项数字服务税，将对向法国消费者或用户提供数字服务的大型跨国公司征收一种全新税种。

en: Digital services include everything from providing a platform for selling goods and services online to targeting advertising based on user data, and the tax applies to gross revenue from such services. (31 words)
cn: 数字服务涵盖的范围很广，从提供线上商品与服务销售平台，到基于用户数据投放定向广告，均在其列；该税针对此类服务的总收入征收。

en: Many French politicians and media outlets have referred to this as a “GAFA tax,” meaning that it is designed to apply primarily to companies such as Google, Apple, Facebook and Amazon—in other words, multinational tech companies based in the United States. (42 words)
cn: 许多法国政界人士和媒体将此税称为“GAFA税”，意指它主要针对谷歌、苹果、脸书和亚马逊这类公司——换句话说，就是总部设在美国的跨国科技公司。

en: The digital services tax now awaits the signature of President Emmanuel Macron, who has expressed support for the measure, and it could go into effect within the next few weeks. (30 words)
cn: 该数字服务税目前正等待总统马克龙签署，而马克龙此前已对此表示支持，因此法案很可能在未来几周内生效。

en: But it has already sparked significant controversy, with the United States trade representative opening an investigation into whether the tax discriminates against American companies, which in turn could lead to trade sanctions against France. (34 words)
cn: 但它已引发重大争议：美国贸易代表已展开调查，以确定该税是否构成对美国企业的歧视，而一旦认定歧视，则可能引发对法国的贸易制裁。

en: The French tax is not just a unilateral move by one country in need of revenue. (16 words)
cn: 法国这项税收并非只是一个急需财政收入的国家在单打独斗。

en: Instead, the digital services tax is part of a much larger trend, with countries over the past few years proposing or putting in place an alphabet soup of new international tax provisions. (32 words)
cn: 恰恰相反，数字服务税只是一场更大浪潮中的一朵浪花：过去几年间，多国纷纷提出或落地了名目繁多的国际税收新规。

en: These have included Britain's DPT (diverted profits tax), Australia's MAAL (multinational antiavoidance law), and India's SEP (significant economic presence) test, to name but a few. (25 words)
cn: 其中包括英国的转移利润税、澳大利亚的跨国反避税法，以及印度的“显著经济存在”测试等，不一而足。

en: At the same time, the European Union, Spain, Britain and several other countries have all seriously contemplated digital services taxes. (20 words)
cn: 与此同时，欧盟、西班牙、英国及其他多个国家也都已认真考虑过推出数字服务税。

en: These unilateral developments differ in their specifics, but they are all designed to tax multinationals on income and revenue that countries believe they should have a right to tax, even if international tax rules do not grant them that right. (40 words)
cn: 这些单边行动细节各异，但目标一致：对跨国公司的利润和收入征税，而各国认为这本就是他们应有的权利，即便现行国际税收规则并未赋予他们这一权利。

en: In other words, they all share a view that the international tax system has failed to keep up with the current economy. (22 words)
cn: 换言之，它们有一个共识：国际税收体系已落后于当今经济发展。

en: In response to these many unilateral measures, the Organization for Economic Cooperation and Development (OECD) is currently working with 131 countries to reach a consensus by the end of 2020 on an international solution. (34 words)
cn: 面对这诸多单边举措，经济合作与发展组织正与131个国家合作，争取在2020年底前就一项国际解决方案达成共识。

en: Both France and the United States are involved in the organization's work, but France's digital services tax and the American response raise questions about what the future holds for the international tax system. (33 words)
cn: 法国和美国均参与了经合组织的这项工作，但法国的数字服务税以及美方的回应，使国际税收体系的未来变得扑朔迷离。

en: France's planned tax is a clear warning: Unless a broad consensus can be reached on reforming the international tax system, other nations are likely to follow suit, and American companies will face a cascade of different taxes from dozens of nations that will prove burdensome and costly. (44 words)
cn: 法国拟征的这一税种是一个明确信号：如果无法就改革国际税收体系达成广泛共识，其他国家很可能会步其后尘，美国企业则将面对来自几十个国家、五花八门的税种接踵而至，最终不堪重负、代价高昂。

en: How can the train operators possibly justify yet another increase to rail passenger fares? (14 words)
cn: 火车运营商怎能证明这又一次的铁路客运涨价是正当合理的呢？

en: It has become a grimly reliable annual ritual: every January the cost of travelling by train rises, imposing a significant extra burden on those who have no option but to use the rail network to get to work or otherwise. (40 words)
cn: 这已经成了一种冷酷无情却又准时必到的年度仪式：每到一月，火车出行费用便会上涨，给那些别无选择、只能靠铁路通勤上下班或往返其他地方的人带来沉重的额外负担。

en: This year's rise, an average of 2.7 percent, may be a fraction lower than last year's, but it is still well above the official Consumer Price Index (CPI) measure of inflation. (31 words)
cn: 今年的涨幅平均为2.7%，也许比去年略低一点，但仍远高于官方消费者价格指数所衡量的通胀水平。

en: Successive governments have permitted such increases on the grounds that the cost of investing in and running the rail network should be borne by those who use it, rather than the general taxpayer. (33 words)
cn: 历届政府都批准了这样的涨价，理由是铁路网的投资与运营成本理应由使用者承担，而非由全体纳税人承担。

en: Why, the argument goes, should a car-driving pensioner from Lincolnshire have to subsidise the daily commute of a stockbroker from Surrey? (21 words)
cn: 这种论调的逻辑是：林肯郡一个开车的退休老人凭什么要补贴萨里郡一个股票经纪人的日常通勤？

en: Equally, there is a sense that the travails of commuters in the South East, many of whom will face among the biggest rises, have received too much attention compared to those who must endure the relatively poor infrastructure of the Midlands and the North. (44 words)
cn: 同样，还有一种看法认为，东南部通勤者的艰难处境——其中许多人还将面临幅度最大的涨价——受到了过多的关注，而那些不得不忍受中部和北部地区相对落后基础设施的通勤者却被忽视了。

en: However, over the past 12 months, those commuters have also experienced some of the worst rail strikes in years. (19 words)
cn: 然而，在过去12个月里，这些通勤者还经历了几年来最严重的一些铁路罢工。

en: It is all very well train operators trumpeting the improvements they are making to the network, but passengers should be able to expect a basic level of service for the substantial sums they are now paying to travel. (38 words)
cn: 火车运营商吹嘘自己正在改善铁路网，这倒也没什么，但乘客既然支付了高昂的出行费用，就理应能够要求最基本的服务水平。

en: The responsibility for the latest wave of strikes rests on the unions. (12 words)
cn: 最近这波罢工的责任在于工会。

en: However, there is a strong case that those who have been worst affected by industrial action should receive compensation for the disruption they have suffered. (25 words)
cn: 然而，有充分的理由认为，那些受劳工行动影响最严重的人应当为他们所遭受的混乱获得赔偿。

en: The Government has pledged to change the law to introduce a minimum service requirement so that, even when strikes occur, services can continue to operate. (25 words)
cn: 政府已承诺修改法律，引入最低服务要求，以便即使发生罢工，铁路服务仍能继续运行。

en: This should form part of a wider package of measures to address the long-running problems on Britain's railways. (18 words)
cn: 这应当成为解决英国铁路长期问题的一系列更广泛措施的一部分。

en: Yes, more investment is needed, but passengers will not be willing to pay more indefinitely if they must also endure cramped, unreliable services, interrupted by regular chaos when timetables are changed, or planned maintenance is managed incompetently. (37 words)
cn: 是的，更多的投资是必要的，但如果乘客还必须忍受拥挤、不准点的服务，且这些服务常常因时刻表变更或计划内养护执行不力而陷入惯常的混乱，那么他们不会愿意无限期地支付更高的票价。

en: The threat of nationalisation may have been seen off for now, but it will return with a vengeance if the justified anger of passengers is not addressed in short order. (30 words)
cn: 国有化的威胁眼下或许已被击退，但如果乘客合情合理的愤怒不能迅速得到解决，它必将以更猛烈的势头卷土重来。

en: Last year marked the third year in a row that Indonesia's bleak rate of deforestation has slowed in pace. (19 words)
cn: 去年是印度尼西亚不容乐观的森林砍伐率连续放缓的第三年。

en: One reason for the turnaround may be the country's antipoverty program. (11 words)
cn: 这一好转出现的原因之一可能是该国实施的反贫困计划。

en: In 2007, Indonesia started phasing in a program that gives money to its poorest residents under certain conditions, such as requiring people to keep kids in school or get regular medical care. (32 words)
cn: 2007年，印度尼西亚开始逐步实施一项重大计划：在特定条件下向最贫困的居民提供资金，比如要求人们让孩子接受教育或接受常规医疗保健。

en: Called conditional cash transfers or CCTs, these social assistance programs are designed to reduce inequality and break the cycle of poverty. (21 words)
cn: 这类被称为有条件现金转移（CCTs）的社会援助计划旨在减少不平等，打破贫困的恶性循环。

en: They're already used in dozens of countries worldwide. (8 words)
cn: 它们已经在全世界几十个国家得到应用。

en: In Indonesia, the program has provided enough food and medicine to substantially reduce severe growth problems among children. (18 words)
cn: 在印度尼西亚，这一扶贫计划提供了充足的食物和药品，大大减少了儿童严重发育问题的出现。

en: But CCT programs don't generally consider effects on the environment. (10 words)
cn: 但CCT计划一般不考虑对环境的影响。

en: In fact, poverty alleviation and environmental protection are often viewed as conflicting goals, says Paul Ferraro, an economist at Johns Hopkins University. (22 words)
cn: 事实上，扶贫和环保常常被视为两个相互矛盾的目标，约翰斯·霍普金斯大学经济学家保罗·费拉罗表示。

en: That's because economic growth can be correlated with environmental degradation, while protecting the environment is sometimes correlated with greater poverty. (20 words)
cn: 这是因为经济增长可能与环境恶化相关，而环境保护有时与贫困恶化相关。

en: However, those correlations don't prove cause and effect. (8 words)
cn: 然而，这些相关性并不能证明（它们）存在因果关系。

en: The only previous study analyzing causality, based on an area in Mexico that had instituted CCTs, supported the traditional view. (20 words)
cn: 此前唯一一项分析经济与环境间因果关系的研究（基于墨西哥一个实施CCT计划的地区）证实了传统看法。

en: There, as people got more money, some of them may have more cleared land for cattle to raise for meat, Ferraro says. (22 words)
cn: 在那里，当人们拿到的钱越多时，他们中有些人就有可能伐木垦出更多的土地来饲养肉牛，费拉罗指出。

en: Such programs do not have to negatively affect the environment, though. (11 words)
cn: 不过，这类扶贫计划不一定会对环境产生负面影响。

en: Ferraro wanted to see if Indonesia's poverty-alleviation program was affecting deforestation. (11 words)
cn: 费拉罗想了解印度尼西亚的扶贫计划是否会影响森林砍伐。

en: Indonesia has the third-largest area of tropical forest in the world and one of the highest deforestation rates. (18 words)
cn: 印度尼西亚拥有世界第三大热带森林面积，也是森林砍伐率最高的国家之一。

en: Ferraro analyzed satellite data showing annual forest loss from 2008 to 2012—including during Indonesia's phase-in of the antipoverty program—in 7,468 forested villages across 15 provinces. (27 words)
cn: 费拉罗分析了显示2008年至2012年（含印度尼西亚分阶段实施反贫困计划期间）15个省、7468个林村的年度林木减少情况的卫星数据。

en: "We see that the program is associated with a 30 percent reduction in deforestation," Ferraro says. (16 words)
cn: 费拉罗指出，"我们发现，这个反贫困计划与森林砍伐降低30%有关。"

en: That's likely because the rural poor are using the money as makeshift insurance policies against inclement weather, Ferraro says. (19 words)
cn: 费拉罗表示，这很可能是因为农村贫困人口将这笔钱用作应对恶劣天气的权宜之计。

en: Typically, if rains are delayed, people may clear land to plant more rice to supplement their harvests. (17 words)
cn: 通常情况下，如果雨季推迟，人们可能会伐木垦地种植更多水稻以补充收成。

en: With the CCTs, individuals instead can use the money to supplement their harvests. (13 words)
cn: 而有了CCT计划，人们则可以用这笔钱来补充收成。

en: Whether this research translates elsewhere is anybody's guess. (8 words)
cn: 这项研究是否适用于其他地区还不得而知。

en: Ferraro suggests the results may transfer to other parts of Asia, due to commonalities such as the importance of growing rice and market access. (24 words)
cn: 费拉罗认为，这些研究结果也许适用于亚洲其他地区，因为这些区域在水稻种植的重要性以及市场准入等方面跟印度尼西亚共通。

en: And regardless of transferability, the study shows that what's good for people may also be good for the environment. (19 words)
cn: 而且，不管研究结果共通与否，这项研究表明了对人们有益的东西也可以对环境有益。

en: Even if this program didn't reduce poverty, Ferraro says, "the value of the avoided deforestation just for carbon dioxide emissions alone is more than the program costs." (27 words)
cn: 费拉罗指出，即使这个反贫困计划未能减轻贫困，"由此避免的森林砍伐具有二氧化碳减排价值，仅这一方面的价值就超越了该反贫困计划的成本。"

en: As a historian who's always searching for the text or the image that makes us re-evaluate the past, I've become preoccupied with looking for photographs that show our Victorian ancestors smiling (what better way to shatter the image of 19th-century prudery?). (41 words)
cn: 作为一名总在搜寻能让我们重新审视过去的文字或图像的史学工作者，我一心专注于寻找那些展现我们维多利亚时代祖先微笑的照片——还有什么方法能比这更好地粉碎19世纪拘谨古板的形象呢？

en: I've found quite a few, and—since I started posting them on Twitter—they have been causing quite a stir. (20 words)
cn: 我已经找到了不少，而且——自从我把它们发到推特上之后——这些照片便一直引起不小的轰动。

en: People have been surprised to see evidence that Victorians had fun and could, and did, laugh. (16 words)
cn: 人们惊讶地看到，有证据表明维多利亚时代的人也有乐趣，也能笑，而且确实笑过。

en: They are noting that the Victorians suddenly seem to become more human as the hundred-or-so years that separate us fade away through our common experience of laughter. (27 words)
cn: 他们注意到，维多利亚时代的人突然显得更有人情味了，因为我们之间相隔的那百来年时光，经由我们共通的欢笑体验，渐渐消融了。

en: Of course, I need to concede that my collection of "Smiling Victorians" makes up only a tiny percentage of the vast catalogue of photographic portraiture created between 1840 and 1900, the majority of which show sitters posing miserably and stiffly in front of painted backdrops, or staring absently into the middle distance. (52 words)
cn: 当然，我必须承认，我收集的这些"微笑的维多利亚人"只占1840年至1900年间那海量肖像摄影作品中极小的一部分，其中大多数作品展示的是被摄者痛苦而僵硬地坐在彩绘背景前，或是茫然地凝视着不远处。

en: How do we explain this trend? (6 words)
cn: 我们如何解释这一现象？

en: During the 1840s and 1850s, in the early days of photography, exposure times were notoriously long: the daguerreotype photographic method (producing an image on a silvered copper plate) could take several minutes to complete, resulting in blurred images as sitters shifted position or adjusted their limbs. (46 words)
cn: 在19世纪40年代和50年代，摄影术的早期，曝光时间之长是出了名的：达盖尔银版摄影法（在镀银铜版上成像）可能需要好几分钟才能完成，一旦被摄者挪动姿势或调整四肢，就会导致影像模糊。

en: The thought of holding a fixed grin as the camera performed its magical duties was too much to contemplate, and so a non-committal blank stare became the norm. (28 words)
cn: 在相机履行其神奇职责时，保持一个固定不变的露齿笑容，这念头光是想想就让人受不了，于是，一种不置可否的茫然凝视便成了常态。

en: But exposure times were much quicker by the 1880s, and the introduction of the Box Brownie and other portable cameras meant that, though slow by today's digital standards, the exposure was almost instantaneous. (33 words)
cn: 但到了19世纪80年代，曝光时间已大大缩短，而盒式布朗尼相机及其他便携相机的问世意味着，尽管以今天的数码标准来看仍属缓慢，但曝光已几乎是瞬间完成了。

en: Spontaneous smiles were relatively easy to capture by the 1890s, so we must look elsewhere for an explanation of why Victorians still hesitated to smile. (25 words)
cn: 到19世纪90年代，自然的微笑已相对容易捕捉，因此我们必须另寻原因，来解释维多利亚时代的人为何仍不愿微笑。

en: One explanation might be the loss of dignity displayed through a cheesy grin. (13 words)
cn: 一种解释可能是，透过做作的露齿笑所展现出的，是尊严的丧失。

en: "Nature gave us lips to conceal our teeth," ran one popular Victorian maxim, alluding to the fact that before the birth of proper dentistry, mouths were often in a shocking state of hygiene. (33 words)
cn: "大自然予我们嘴唇，为的是遮住牙齿，"维多利亚时代一则流行的格言如此说道，暗指在严格意义上的牙科学诞生之前，口腔的卫生状况往往糟糕得惊人。

en: A flashing set of healthy and clean, regular "pearly whites" was a rare sight in Victorian society, the preserve of the super-rich (and even then, dental hygiene was not guaranteed). (30 words)
cn: 一口闪闪发亮、健康洁净、整整齐齐的"皓齿"，在维多利亚社会实属罕见，这是超级富豪的专属——而即便在超级富豪那里，牙齿卫生也未必有保障。

en: A toothy grin (especially when there were gaps or blackened teeth) lacked class: drunks, tramps and music hall performers might gurn and grin with a smile as wide as Lewis Carroll's gum-exposing Cheshire Cat, but it was not a becoming look for properly bred persons. (45 words)
cn: 露齿而笑——尤其是当牙齿豁口或发黑时——是有失身份的：酒鬼、流浪汉和音乐厅戏子或许会像刘易斯·卡罗尔笔下露着牙龈的柴郡猫那样扮鬼脸咧嘴大笑，但这对教养良好的人来说可不是体面的样子。

en: Even Mark Twain, a man who enjoyed a hearty laugh, said that when it came to photographic portraits there could be "nothing more damning than a silly, foolish smile fixed forever". (31 words)
cn: 就连马克·吐温这样一个爱开怀大笑的人，也说过在摄影肖像这件事上，没有"什么比一个傻乎乎、蠢兮兮的微笑被永久定格更糟糕的了"。

en: From the early days of broadband, advocates for consumers and web-based companies worried that the cable and phone companies selling broadband connections had the power and incentive to favor affiliated websites over their rivals'. (34 words)
cn: 自打宽带问世初期，消费者及互联网公司的权益倡导者们就在担心，销售宽带连接的有线电视和电话公司不仅有能力而且有动机去偏袒自己的关联网站、歧视竞争对手的网站。

en: That's why there has been such a strong demand for rules that would prevent broadband providers from picking winners and losers online, preserving the freedom and innovation that have been the lifeblood of the Internet. (35 words)
cn: 正是基于这一原因，人们一直以来都在强烈要求出台相关规则，避免由宽带运营商来决定网上谁是赢家谁是输家，从而维护自由与创新——互联网的命脉所在。

en: Yet that demand has been almost impossible to fill—in part because of pushback from broadband providers, anti-regulatory conservatives and the courts. (22 words)
cn: 然而这一需求一直都几乎不可能被满足——一部分原因就在于宽带运营商、反监管的保守派以及各级法院的反对。

en: A federal appeals court weighed in again Tuesday, but instead of providing a badly needed resolution, it only prolonged the fight. (21 words)
cn: 周二，联邦上诉法院再次介入，但它并未提供急需的决议，而只是延长了这场争论。

en: At issue before the U.S. Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit was the latest take of the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) on net neutrality, adopted on a party-line vote in 2017. (34 words)
cn: 摆在美国哥伦比亚特区巡回上诉法院面前的争议焦点是联邦通信委员会（FCC）就网络中立性于2017年经由一次政党路线投票通过的最新意见。

en: The Republican-penned order not only eliminated the strict net neutrality rules the FCC had adopted when it had a Democratic majority in 2015, but rejected the commission's authority to require broadband providers to do much of anything. (37 words)
cn: 共和党人起草的这一规定不仅废除了FCC于2015年（彼时大多数成员是民主党人）通过的严苛的网络中立性规则，而且否决了委员会对宽带运营商提出任何行为要求的权力。

en: The order also declared that state and local governments couldn't regulate broadband providers either. (14 words)
cn: 该规定同时声明，州政府及地方政府同样不得监管宽带运营商。

en: The commission argued that other agencies would protect against anti-competitive behavior, such as a broadband-providing conglomerate like AT&T favoring its own video-streaming service at the expense of Netflix and Apple TV. (31 words)
cn: FCC提出，自有其他部门对抗反竞争行为，比如，像AT&T这样的宽带服务集团偏袒自家流媒体服务而损害Netflix和Apple TV的利益。

en: Yet the FCC also ended the investigations of broadband providers that imposed data caps on their rivals' streaming services but not their own. (23 words)
cn: 然而，FCC也叫停了对某些宽带运营商的多项调查，这些运营商给对手的流媒体服务设置了数据限额，却没对自己的服务这么做。

en: On Tuesday, the appeals court unanimously upheld the 2017 order deregulating broadband providers, citing a Supreme Court ruling from 2005 that upheld a similarly deregulatory move. (26 words)
cn: 周二，上诉法院援引最高法院2005年一项裁决（该裁决认可了类似的去监管化举措），一致维持了2017年解除对宽带运营商监管的规定。

en: But Judge Patricia Millett rightly argued in a concurring opinion that "the result is unhinged from the realities of modern broadband service," and said Congress or the Supreme Court could intervene to "avoid trapping Internet regulation in technological anachronism." (39 words)
cn: 但帕特里夏·米利特法官在协同意见书中公正地指出"这一结果与现代宽带服务的现实脱节"，并表示国会或最高法院可进行干预，以"避免网络监管在技术发展面前沦为过时"。

en: In the meantime, the court threw out the FCC's attempt to block all state rules on net neutrality, while preserving the commission's power to preempt individual state laws that undermine its order. (32 words)
cn: 与此同时，法院驳回了FCC试图阻止所有州级网络中立法规的请求，但维护了FCC如下权力：凭借"联邦法优先"原则，否决动摇其现有规定的个别州法。

en: That means more battles like the one now going on between the Justice Department and California, which enacted a tough net neutrality law in the wake of the FCC's abdication. (30 words)
cn: 这意味着会有更多类似当前"司法部和加州之争"的较量，后者在FCC放弃监管后颁布了一项严厉的网络中立法。

en: The endless legal battles and back-and-forth at the FCC cry out for Congress to act. (15 words)
cn: 法律之争没完没了，FCC反复无常，这一切亟须国会采取行动。

en: It needs to give the commission explicit authority once and for all to bar broadband providers from meddling in the traffic on their network and to create clear rules protecting openness and innovation online. (34 words)
cn: 国会需要一劳永逸，直截了当授权FCC禁止宽带运营商干预网络流量，并制定明确法规来保护网络的开放性和创新性。

en: People often grumble that plastics are too durable. (8 words)
cn: 人们常常抱怨塑料过于耐用。

en: Water bottles, shopping bags, and other trash litter the planet, from Mount Everest to the Mariana Trench, because plastics are ubiquitous and don't break down easily. (26 words)
cn: 从珠穆朗玛峰到马里亚纳海沟，水瓶、购物袋及其他垃圾遍布全球，因为塑料无处不在且不易分解。

en: But some plastic materials change over time. (7 words)
cn: 但有些塑料材料会随着时间推移发生变化。

en: They crack and frizzle. (4 words)
cn: 它们会开裂、卷曲。

en: They "weep" out additives. (4 words)
cn: 它们会"渗出"添加剂。

en: They melt into sludge. (4 words)
cn: 它们会融化成泥浆。

en: All of which creates huge headaches for institutions, such as museums, trying to preserve culturally important objects. (17 words)
cn: 所有这些都给试图保护重要文物的机构，比如博物馆，带来了巨大的麻烦。

en: Until recently, museums only had to worry about traditional materials. (10 words)
cn: 直到不久以前，博物馆还只需为传统材料操心。

en: The variety of plastic objects at risk is dizzying: early radios, avant-garde sculptures, celluloid animation stills from Disney films, David Bowie costumes, the first artificial heart. (26 words)
cn: 面临风险的塑料物品种类之多令人眼花缭乱：早期收音机、前卫雕塑、迪士尼电影的赛璐珞动画剧照、大卫·鲍伊的演出服装、第一颗人造心脏。

en: Certain artifacts are especially vulnerable because some pioneers in plastic art didn't always know how to mix ingredients properly, says Thea van Oosten, a polymer chemist who, until retiring a few years ago, worked for decades at the Cultural Heritage Agency of the Netherlands (RCE). (45 words)
cn: 某些工艺品尤为脆弱，因为一些塑料艺术的先驱们并不总能知道如何恰当地混合各种成分，西娅·范·奥斯滕说道。她是一位高分子化学家，直到几年前退休前，曾在荷兰文化遗产局工作了几十年。

en: "It's like baking a cake: If you don't have exact amounts, it goes wrong," she says. (16 words)
cn: "这就好比烤蛋糕：如果用料分量不精确，就会出问题，"她说。

en: "The object you make is already a time bomb." (9 words)
cn: "你做出来的东西就已经是一枚定时炸弹了。"

en: And sometimes, it's not the artist's fault. (7 words)
cn: 而有时候，这并不是艺术家的错。

en: In the 1960s, the Italian artist Piero Gilardi began to create hundreds of bright, colorful foam pieces. (17 words)
cn: 20世纪60年代，意大利艺术家皮耶罗·吉拉尔迪开始创作数百件色彩明丽的泡沫塑料作品。

en: Those pieces included small beds of roses and other items as well as a few dozen "nature carpet" — large rectangles decorated with foam pumpkins, cabbages, and watermelons. (27 words)
cn: 这些作品包括小型玫瑰花圃及其他物件，还有几十块"自然地毯"——饰有泡沫南瓜、卷心菜和西瓜的大型矩形垫子。

en: He wanted viewers to walk around on the carpets — which meant they had to be durable. (16 words)
cn: 他希望观众能在地毯上走来走去——这意味着它们必须经久耐用。

en: Unfortunately, the polyurethane foam he used is inherently unstable. (9 words)
cn: 遗憾的是，他所用的聚氨酯泡沫本质上是不稳定的。

en: It's especially vulnerable to light damage, and by the mid-1990s, Gilardi's pumpkins, roses, and other figures were splitting and crumbling. (20 words)
cn: 它尤其容易受到光损伤，到了20世纪90年代中期，吉拉尔迪的南瓜、玫瑰和其他塑像纷纷开裂碎裂。

en: Museums locked some of them away in the dark. (9 words)
cn: 博物馆将其中一些锁进了暗处。

en: So van Oosten and colleagues at RCE began to study ways to protect polyurethane. (14 words)
cn: 于是，范·奥斯滕和她在荷兰文化遗产局的同事们开始研究保护聚氨酯的方法。

en: First, they took foam samples similar to the nature carpets and infused some with stabilizing and consolidating chemicals that modern manufacturers often use. (23 words)
cn: 首先，他们选取了与自然地毯相似的泡沫样本，向其中一些注入了现代制造商常用的稳定剂和加固剂。

en: Van Oosten calls those chemicals "sunscreens" because their goal was to prevent further light damage and rebuild worn polymer fibers. (20 words)
cn: 范·奥斯滕称这些化学物质为"防晒霜"，因为它们的作用是防止进一步的光损伤并修复受损的聚合物纤维。

en: Then the team used xenon lamps to artificially age both treated and untreated samples, and examined them under high-powered microscopes. (20 words)
cn: 然后，团队用氙灯对经过处理和未经处理的样品进行人工老化，并在高倍显微镜下对它们进行检测。

en: The results were encouraging. (4 words)
cn: 结果令人鼓舞。

en: Samples that lacked sunscreen had withered under the barrage of photons: The molecular "struts" shoring up the foam were 42% thinner and notably more brittle than before the lamp treatment. (30 words)
cn: 缺少"防晒霜"的样品在光子的轰击下已衰败：支撑泡沫的分子"支柱"比灯照处理前薄了42%，且明显更脆。

en: The struts in samples with sunscreen decreased by as little as 12.5%. (12 words)
cn: 而有"防晒霜"保护的样品，其支柱仅变薄了12.5%。

en: Armed with that knowledge, conservators working with RCE infused several Gilardi sculptures, including two nature carpets, with the sunscreen to stabilize them. (22 words)
cn: 凭借这一认识，与荷兰文化遗产局合作的修复人员为几件吉拉尔迪的雕塑——包括两块自然地毯——注入了"防晒霜"以稳定其状态。

en: Van Oosten is proud that several have even gone on display again, albeit sometimes beneath protective cases. (17 words)
cn: 令范·奥斯滕感到自豪的是，其中几件甚至重新得以展出，尽管有时需要置于保护罩之下。

en: Long called the "queen of plastics," in 2012, van Oosten was knighted in the Netherlands for her efforts to preserve plastic objects and spread knowledge to other institutes. (28 words)
cn: 长期以来被称为"塑料女王"的范·奥斯滕，于2012年在荷兰被授予骑士勋章，以表彰她在保护塑料物品以及向其他机构传播知识方面所做的努力。

en: Despite such success stories, preservation of plastics will likely get harder. (11 words)
cn: 尽管有这样的成功故事，塑料的保护工作很可能会变得更加困难。

en: Old objects continue to deteriorate. (5 words)
cn: 旧物件在持续老化。

en: Worse, biodegradable plastics, designed to disintegrate, are increasingly common. (9 words)
cn: 更糟的是，为可降解而设计的生物降解塑料正变得越来越普遍。

en: And more is at stake here than individual objects. (9 words)
cn: 而且，利害攸关的不仅仅是单个物件。

en: Ferreira notes that archaeologists first defined the great material ages of human history — Stone Age, Iron Age, and so on — after examining artifacts in museums. (25 words)
cn: 费雷拉指出，考古学家正是在考察了博物馆中的文物之后，才首次界定了人类历史上伟大的材料时代——石器时代、铁器时代等等。

en: We now live in an age of plastic, she says, "and what we decide to collect today, what we decide to preserve...will have a strong impact on how in the future we'll be seen." (34 words)
cn: 我们如今生活在一个塑料时代，她说，"而我们今天决定收藏什么，决定保护什么……将对未来人们如何看待我们产生深远影响。"

en: As the latest crop of students pen their UCAS form and weigh up their options, it may be worth considering just how the point, purpose and value of a degree has changed and what Gen Z (and their parents) need to consider as they start the third stage of their educational journey. (53 words)
cn: 当最新一批学生填写大学入学申请表、权衡自己的选择时，或许值得思考一下：学位的意义、目的和价值究竟发生了怎样的变化，以及Z世代（和他们的父母）在开启教育旅程第三阶段时需要思考些什么。

en: Millennials were told that if you did well in school, got a decent degree, you would be set up for life. (21 words)
cn: 千禧一代曾被告知，只要在学校表现优异、拿下一个像样的学位，这辈子就衣食无忧了。

en: But that promise has been found wanting. (7 words)
cn: 但事实证明，这一承诺并未兑现。

en: As degrees became universal, they became devalued (just as governments hiked up the cost of getting one). (17 words)
cn: 随着学位变得普及，它们也随之贬值（正如政府大幅抬高了获取学位的成本一样）。

en: Education was no longer a secure route of social mobility. (10 words)
cn: 教育不再是通往社会阶层流动的可靠路径。

en: Today, 28 per cent of graduates in the UK are in non-graduate roles; a percentage which is double the average amongst the OECD. (23 words)
cn: 如今，英国有28%的毕业生在从事无需本科学历的工作；这一比例是经合组织国家平均水平的两倍。

en: This is not to say that there is no point in getting a degree, but, rather stress that a degree is not for everyone, that the switch from classroom to lecture hall is not an inevitable one and that other options are available. (43 words)
cn: 这并不是说拿学位毫无意义，而是要强调：学位并非适合每一个人，从中学教室到大学讲堂的转变并非必然之选，还有其他道路可走。

en: Thankfully, there are signs that this is already happening, with Gen Z seeking to learn from their millennial predecessors, even if parents and teachers tend to be still set in the degree mindset. (33 words)
cn: 值得庆幸的是，已有迹象表明这一情形正在发生：尽管父母和老师们往往仍固守着学位至上的思维，Z世代已在努力从他们的千禧前辈身上汲取教训。

en: Employers have long seen the advantages of hiring school leavers who often prove themselves to be more committed and loyal employees than graduates. (23 words)
cn: 雇主们早就看到了招聘中学毕业生的好处——事实证明，比起大学毕业生，他们往往是更敬业、更忠诚的员工。

en: Many too are seeing the advantages of scrapping a degree requirement for certain roles—just as Penguin did in 2016. (20 words)
cn: 许多雇主也正在认识到取消某些职位的学位要求所带来的好处——正如企鹅出版社在2016年所做的那样。

en: For those for whom a degree is the desired route, consider that this may well be the first of many. (20 words)
cn: 对于那些认定学位是理想道路的人来说，不妨这样想：这很可能只是众多学位中的第一个。

en: In this age of generalists, it pays to have specific knowledge or skills. (13 words)
cn: 在这个通才时代，拥有专门的知识或技能才更有价值。

en: Postgraduates now earn 40 per cent more than graduates. (9 words)
cn: 如今，研究生的收入比本科毕业生高出40%。

en: When more and more of us have a degree, it makes sense (albeit expensive) to have two. (17 words)
cn: 当越来越多的人拥有一个学位时，拥有两个学位（尽管代价不菲）就成了明智之举。

en: It is unlikely that Gen Z will be done with education at 18 or 21; they will need to be constantly up-skilling throughout their career to stay agile, relevant and employable. (31 words)
cn: Z世代不太可能在18岁或21岁就彻底告别教育；他们将需要在整个职业生涯中不断学习新技能，以保持灵活应变、跟上时代并具备就业竞争力。

en: It has been estimated that this generation, due to the pressures of technology, the wish for personal fulfilment and desire for diversity, will work for 17 different employers over the course of their working life and have five different careers. (40 words)
cn: 据估计，由于技术带来的压力、自我实现的愿望以及对多样性的追求，这一代人一生中将在17个不同的雇主手下工作，从事五种不同的职业。

en: Education, and not just knowledge gained on campus, will be a core part of Generation Z's career trajectory. (18 words)
cn: 教育，而不仅仅是在校园里获得的知识，将成为Z世代职业轨迹的核心组成部分。

en: I have often heard older generations talk about their degree (even if it was gained decades ago) in the present and personal tense: "I am a geographer" or "I am a classist". (32 words)
cn: 我常听到老一辈人以现在时和指向个人身份的口吻谈论自己的学位（即使是几十年前获得的）："我是一名地理学家"或"我是一名古典学研究者"。

en: Their sons or daughters would never say such a thing; it's as if they already know that their degree won't define them in the same way. (26 words)
cn: 他们的子女绝不会说出这样的话来；仿佛他们已经知道，自己的学位不会以同样的方式定义自己。

en: Enlightening, stimulating, inspiring, fun. (4 words)
cn: 启迪心智，激发思考，赋予灵感，趣味盎然。

en: These were some of the words that Nature readers used to describe their experiences of art-science collaborations in a series of articles on partnerships between artists and researchers. (28 words)
cn: 这些是《自然》读者在关于艺术家与科研人员合作关系的一系列文章中，用以描述各自科艺合作体验的部分词汇。

en: Nearly 40% of the roughly 350 people who responded to an accompanying poll said they had collaborated with artists; and almost all said they would consider doing so in future. (30 words)
cn: 在回应随附问卷调查的大约350人中，近四成表示自己曾与艺术家合作过；而几乎所有人都表示将来会考虑这样做。

en: Such an encouraging result is not surprising. (7 words)
cn: 如此令人鼓舞的结果并不出人意料。

en: Public engagement has become essential to many research projects. (9 words)
cn: 公众参与对许多研究项目而言已变得不可或缺。

en: Scientists are increasingly seeking out visual artists and designers to help them to communicate their work to new audiences. (19 words)
cn: 科学家正日益物色视觉艺术家和设计师，以帮助自己将研究成果传达给新的受众。

en: "Artists help scientists reach a broader audience and make emotional connections that enhance learning," one respondent said. (17 words)
cn: "艺术家帮助科学家触达更广泛的受众，并建立起能增进学习的情感联结，"一位受访者说。

en: "The experience is very liberating for me, as a scientist," said another. (12 words)
cn: "作为一名科学家，这种体验让我感到非常解放，"另一位受访者说道。

en: "There's often a visual aspect to my science that generating and publishing data does not convey." (16 words)
cn: "我的科学研究往往含有一个视觉维度，这是生成和发表数据所无法传达的。"

en: One example of how artists and scientists have together rocked the senses came last month when the Sydney Symphony Orchestra in Australia performed a reworked version of Antonio Vivaldi's The Four Seasons. (32 words)
cn: 艺术家与科学家如何携手震撼感官，上月便有一例：澳大利亚悉尼交响乐团演奏了安东尼奥·维瓦尔第《四季》的改编版。

en: They reimagined the 300-year-old score by injecting the latest climate prediction data for each season — provided by Monash University's Climate Change Communication Research Hub in Melbourne. (26 words)
cn: 他们为这首300年历史的乐谱注入每个季节的最新气候预测数据——由墨尔本蒙纳士大学气候变化传播研究中心提供，由此重新构想了这部作品。

en: The work was entitled The (Uncertain) Four Seasons, and variations of the score containing local data were sent to every major orchestra in the world. (25 words)
cn: 这部作品被命名为《（不确定的）四季》，而融入当地数据的乐谱变体则寄往了全球各大主要管弦乐团。

en: The performance was a creative call to action ahead of November's United Nations. (13 words)
cn: 这场演出是一次富有创意的行动呼吁，赶在十一月联合国大会召开之前。

en: But a genuine partnership must be a two-way street. (9 words)
cn: 但真正的合作必须是双向奔赴的。

en: Fewer artists than scientists responded to the Nature poll; however, several respondents noted that artists do not simply assist scientists with their communication requirements. (24 words)
cn: 回应《自然》调查的艺术家少于科学家；然而，若干受访者指出，艺术家并非只是协助科学家满足其在传播方面的需求。

en: Nor should their work be considered only as an object of study — even if these are reasons why scientists seek opportunities to work with artists. (25 words)
cn: 他们的工作也不应只被当作研究对象来对待——即便这些确实是科学家寻求与艺术家合作机会的原因。

en: The alliances are most valuable when scientists and artists have a shared stake in a project, are able to jointly design it and can critique each other's work. (28 words)
cn: 唯有当科学家与艺术家在某一项目中有共同的利益关切，能够共同设计项目，并能相互评判彼此的工作时，这种联盟才最具价值。

en: Such an approach can both prompt new research as well as result in powerful art. (15 words)
cn: 这一方式既能催生新的研究，也能造就掷地有声的艺术。

en: More than half a century ago, the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) opened its Center for Advanced Visual Studies (CAVS) in Cambridge to explore the role of technology in culture. (30 words)
cn: 半个多世纪前，麻省理工学院在剑桥市设立了高级视觉研究中心，旨在探索技术在文化中的作用。

en: The centre was established during the Vietnam War, when many scientists in the United States were being criticized for working on defence contracts. (23 words)
cn: 该中心创立于越南战争期间，彼时美国许多科学家正因从事国防合同项目而饱受批评。

en: Its founders believed that artists and scientists could, together, create a vision for a more humane world. (17 words)
cn: 其创始人相信，艺术家与科学家能够一道，为创造一个更富人道精神的世界勾勒愿景。

en: They deliberately focused their projects around light — hence the 'visual studies' in the name. (14 words)
cn: 他们特意将项目聚焦于"光"——中心名称中的"视觉研究"便由此而来。

en: Light was a something that both artists and scientists had an interest in, and therefore could form the basis of collaboration, says Seth Riskin, a visual-arts researcher at the MIT Museum who previously worked at CAVS. (36 words)
cn: 光，是艺术家和科学家共同感兴趣的东西，因而能构成合作的基础，曾在CAVS工作过的麻省理工学院博物馆视觉艺术研究员塞思·里斯金这样说道。

en: The reach of art-science tie-ups needs to go beyond the necessary purpose of research communication, and participants must not fall into the trap of stereotyping each other. (27 words)
cn: 科艺合作的范围必须超越科研传播这一必要目的，参与者务必避免堕入以刻板印象看待彼此的陷阱。

en: Artists and scientists alike are immersed in discovery and invention, and challenge and critique are core to both, too. (19 words)
cn: 艺术家与科学家同样浸淫于发现与发明，而挑战与批判也同为两者的核心。

en: The personal grievance provisions of the Employment Relations Act 2000 (ERA) prevent an employer from firing an employee without good cause. (21 words)
cn: 《2000年雇佣关系法》的个人申诉条款禁止雇主无正当理由解雇员工。

en: Instead, dismissals must be justified. (5 words)
cn: 换言之，解雇必须具有正当理由。

en: Employers must both show cause and act in a procedurally fair way. (12 words)
cn: 雇主既须说明理由，也须在程序上做到公正。

en: Personal grievance procedures were designed to guard the jobs of ordinary workers from "unjustified dismissals". (15 words)
cn: 个人申诉程序旨在保护普通劳动者的工作岗位免遭"不正当解雇"。

en: The premise was that the common law of contract lacked sufficient safeguards for workers against arbitrary conduct by management. (19 words)
cn: 其前提是，普通合同判例法对劳动者缺乏足够的保障，使其免受管理层恣意行为的侵害。

en: Long gone are the days when a boss could simply give an employee contractual notice. (15 words)
cn: 老板只需发一纸合同通知就能将员工解雇的时代早已一去不返。

en: But these provisions create difficulties for businesses when applied to highly paid managers and executives. (15 words)
cn: 但当这些条款被应用于高薪经理和高管时，就会给企业带来难题。

en: As countless boards and business owners will attest, constraining firms from firing poorly performing, high-earning managers is a handbrake on boosting productivity and overall performance. (25 words)
cn: 正如无数董事会和企业主所能证实的那样，限制企业解雇表现不佳的高薪管理者，如同给生产率和整体业绩的提升拉上了手刹。

en: The difference between C-grade and A-grade managers may very well be the difference between business success or failure. (18 words)
cn: 三流经理与一流经理之间的差距，很可能就是企业成败之别。

en: Between preserving the jobs of ordinary workers or losing them. (10 words)
cn: 亦是保住还是丢掉普通劳动者饭碗之别。

en: Yet mediocrity is no longer enough to justify a dismissal. (10 words)
cn: 然而，仅凭工作平庸已不足以构成解雇的正当理由。

en: Consequently — and paradoxically — laws introduced to protect the jobs of ordinary workers may be placing those jobs at risk. (19 words)
cn: 因此——并且矛盾的是——旨在保护普通劳动者岗位的法律，反倒可能让这些岗位岌岌可危。

en: If not placing jobs at risk, to the extent employment protection laws constrain business owners from dismissing underperforming managers, those laws act as a constraint on firm productivity and therefore on workers' wages. (33 words)
cn: 即便未危及工作岗位，就业保护法在限制企业主解雇表现不佳的管理者方面，也对企业生产率构成了制约，进而制约了劳动者的工资。

en: Indeed, in "An International Perspective on New Zealand's Productivity Paradox" (2014), the Productivity Commission singled out the low quality of managerial capabilities as a cause of the country's poor productivity growth record. (32 words)
cn: 事实上，在《国际视角下的新西兰生产率悖论》（2014年）一文中，生产力委员会明确指出，管理能力低下是该国生产率增长记录不佳的原因之一。

en: Nor are highly paid managers themselves immune from the harm caused by the ERA's unjustified dismissal procedures. (17 words)
cn: 高薪管理者自身也未能免受ERA不正当解雇程序带来的伤害。

en: Because employment protection laws make it costlier to fire an employee, employers are more cautious about hiring new staff. (19 words)
cn: 由于就业保护法加大了辞退员工的成本，雇主在招聘新员工时便更加谨慎。

en: This makes it harder for the marginal manager to gain employment. (11 words)
cn: 这让能力平平的管理者更难找到工作。

en: And firms pay staff less because firms carry the burden of the employment arrangement going wrong. (16 words)
cn: 而企业支付给员工的薪酬也更低，因为企业背负着雇佣安排出错的负担。

en: Society also suffers from excessive employment protections. (7 words)
cn: 社会同样因过度的就业保护而深受其害。

en: Stringent job dismissal regulations adversely affect productivity growth and hamper both prosperity and overall wellbeing. (15 words)
cn: 严苛的解雇法规对生产率增长产生不利影响，并阻碍经济繁荣和整体福祉。

en: Across the Tasman, Australia deals with the unjustified dismissal paradox by excluding employees earning above a specified "high-income threshold" from the protection of its unfair dismissal laws. (27 words)
cn: 在塔斯曼海对岸，澳大利亚是这样应对不正当解雇悖论的：将收入超过某一特定"高收入门槛"的员工排除在其不公平解雇法的保护范围之外。

en: In New Zealand, a 2016 private members' Bill tried to permit firms and high-income employees to contract out of the unjustified dismissal regime. (23 words)
cn: 在新西兰，一项2016年的无公职议员法案曾试图允许企业和高收入员工通过订约退出不正当解雇制度的约束。

en: However, the mechanisms proposed were unwieldy and the Bill was voted down following the change in government later that year. (20 words)
cn: 然而，该法案所提议的机制难以操作，加之当年晚些时候政府更迭，法案最终遭到投票否决。

en: The weather in Texas may have cooled since the recent extreme heat, but the temperature will be high at the State Board of Education meeting in Austin this month as officials debate how climate change is taught in Texas schools. (40 words)
cn: 得克萨斯州的天气在最近极端高温之后或许已经凉下来了，但本月在奥斯汀举行的州教育委员会会议上，随着官员们就得州学校如何教授气候变化展开辩论，会场的温度将会很高。

en: Pat Hardy, who sympathises with the views of the energy sector, is resisting proposed changes to science standards for pre-teen pupils. (21 words)
cn: 对能源行业观点抱有同情的帕特·哈代，正在抵制一项针对小学高年级学生科学课程标准提出的修改方案。

en: "There are as many scientists working against all the panic of global climate change as there are those who are pushing it," she claims. (24 words)
cn: "反对这种全球气候变化恐慌的科学家，和推动这种恐慌的科学家，数量一样多，"她声称。

en: "Texas is an energy state and we need to recognise that. (11 words)
cn: "得克萨斯是一个能源州，我们必须认识到这一点。

en: You need to remember where your bread is buttered." (9 words)
cn: 你得明白自己的利益在哪里。"

en: Most scientists and experts sharply dispute Hardy's views. (8 words)
cn: 大多数科学家和专家对哈代的观点予以了尖锐反驳。

en: Board members like her "casually dismiss the career work of scholars and scientists as just another misguided opinion," says Dan Quinn, senior communications strategist at the Texas Freedom Network, a non-profit group that monitors public education. (36 words)
cn: 像她这样的委员会成员"轻率地将学者和科学家的毕生研究斥为不过是又一种被误导的观点，"得克萨斯自由网络——一个监督公共教育的非营利组织——的高级传播策略师丹·奎因表示。

en: Such debates reflect fierce discussions across the US, as researchers, policymakers, teachers and students step up demands for a greater focus on teaching about the facts of climate change in schools. (31 words)
cn: 这类辩论反映了全美范围内的激烈讨论，因为研究人员、政策制定者、教师和学生正日益强烈地要求在学校教育中更加注重气候变化事实的讲授。

en: A study last year by the National Center for Science Education, a non-profit group of scientists and teachers, looking at how state public schools across the country address climate change in science classes, gave barely half of US states a grade B+ or higher. (44 words)
cn: 由科学家和教师组成的非营利组织国家科学教育中心去年进行的一项研究，考察了全美各州公立学校在科学课上如何处理气候变化，结果仅给不到一半的州打了B+或更高的评分。

en: Among the 10 worst performers were some of the most populous states, including Texas, which was given the lowest grade (F) and has a disproportionate influence because its textbooks are widely sold elsewhere. (33 words)
cn: 在表现最差的十个州中，有几个人口最多的州，其中包括得克萨斯州，该州得了最低评级（F），而且由于该州的教科书在其他地方被广泛销售，其影响力大得不成比例。

en: Glenn Branch, the centre's deputy director, cautions that setting state-level science standards is only one limited benchmark in a country that decentralises decisions to local school boards. (27 words)
cn: 该中心副主任格伦·布兰奇提醒说，在一个将决策权下放给地方学校董事会的国家，制定州级科学课程标准只是一种有限的衡量标尺。

en: Even if a state is considered a high performer in its science standards, "that does not mean it will be taught", he says. (23 words)
cn: 即便一个州在科学课程标准方面被视为表现优异，"那也不意味着相关内容会被真正教授，"他说。

en: Another issue is that, while climate change is well integrated into some subjects and at some ages—such as earth and space sciences in high schools—it is not as well represented in curricula for younger children and in subjects that are more widely taught, such as biology and chemistry. (50 words)
cn: 另一个问题是，虽然气候变化在部分学科和某些年龄段——如高中的地球与空间科学——已得到较好的融入，但在面向更年幼儿童的课程中，以及在生物学和化学这类教授范围更广的学科中，它的呈现程度则远不及此。

en: It is also less prominent in many social studies courses. (10 words)
cn: 在许多社会研究课程中，气候变化也不那么突出。

en: Branch points out that, even if a growing number of official guidelines and textbooks reflect scientific consensus on climate change, unofficial educational materials that convey more biased perspectives are being distributed to teachers. (33 words)
cn: 布兰奇指出，即便越来越多的官方指导方针和教科书反映了气候变化方面的科学共识，那些传达更多偏见观点的非官方教学材料却仍在被分发给教师。

en: They include materials sponsored by energy industry associations. (8 words)
cn: 其中包括由能源行业协会赞助的材料。

en: Communities throughout New England have been attempting to regulate short-term rentals since sites like Airbnb took off in the 2010s. (20 words)
cn: 自爱彼迎等网站于2010年代兴起以来，新英格兰各地的社区便一直在试图对短期租赁加以监管。

en: Now, with record-high home prices and historically low inventory, there's an increased urgency in such regulation, particularly among those who worry that developers will come in and buy up swaths of housing to flip for a fortune on the short-term rental market. (42 words)
cn: 如今，面对创纪录的高房价和历史性的低库存，此类监管更显紧迫，尤其是对于那些担心开发商会大举涌入、成片收购住房，再放到短租市场上转手牟取暴利的人而言。

en: In New Hampshire, where the rental vacancy rate has dropped below 1 percent, housing advocates fear unchecked short-term rentals will put further pressure on an already strained market. (28 words)
cn: 在新罕布什尔州，出租房空置率已跌破1%，住房倡导者担心不加约束的短租将给本已捉襟见肘的市场施加更大的压力。

en: The state Legislature recently voted against a bill that would've made it illegal for towns to create legislation restricting short-term rentals. (21 words)
cn: 该州议会最近否决了一项法案，该法案原本会令各城镇制定限制短租的法规成为非法之举。

en: "We are at a crisis level on the supply of rental housing," said Nick Taylor, executive director of the Workforce Housing Coalition of the Greater Seacoast. (26 words)
cn: "我们在租赁住房供应上已处于危机水平，"大西海岸劳动力住房联盟执行主任尼克·泰勒表示。

en: Without enough affordable housing in southern New Hampshire towns, "employers are having a hard time attracting employees, and workers are having a hard time finding a place to live," Taylor said. (31 words)
cn: 新罕布什尔州南部城镇缺乏足够的经济适用房，"雇主难以招到员工，员工也难以找到住处，"泰勒说。

en: However, short-term rentals also provide housing for tourists, pointed out Ryan Castle, CEO of a local association of realtors. (19 words)
cn: 不过，当地一家房地产经纪人协会的首席执行官瑞安·卡斯尔指出，短租也为游客提供了住宿。

en: "A lot of workers are servicing the tourist industry, and the tourism industry is serviced by those people coming in short term," Castle said, "and so it's a cyclical effect." (30 words)
cn: "大量劳动者服务于旅游业，而旅游业又由那些短期前来的游客所支撑，"卡斯尔说，"所以这是一种循环效应。"

en: Short-term rentals themselves are not the crux of the issue, said Keren Horn, an expert on affordable housing policy. (19 words)
cn: 短租本身并非问题的症结，经济适用房政策专家凯伦·霍恩说。

en: "I think individuals being able to rent out their second home is a good thing. (15 words)
cn: "我认为个人能把自己的第二处住房出租是一件好事。

en: If it's their vacation home anyway, and it's just empty, why can't you make money off it?" Horn said. (19 words)
cn: 反正那本来就是他们的度假屋，就那么空置着，为什么不能拿来赚钱呢？"霍恩说。

en: Issues arise, however, when developers attempt to create large-scale short-term rental facilities—de facto hotels—to bypass taxes and regulations. (20 words)
cn: 然而，当开发商试图打造大规模短租设施——实质上的酒店——以绕开税收与监管时，问题就来了。

en: "I think the question is, shouldn't a developer who's really building a hotel, but disguising it as not a hotel, be treated and taxed and regulated like a hotel?" Horn said. (31 words)
cn: "我认为问题在于，一个实际上在建酒店、却把它伪装成非酒店的开发商，难道不应该被当作酒店来对待、征税和监管吗？"霍恩说。

en: At the end of 2018, Governor Charlie Baker of Massachusetts signed a bill to rein in those potential investor-buyers. (19 words)
cn: 2018年底，马萨诸塞州州长查理·贝克签署了一项法案，以遏制那些潜在的投资性购房者。

en: The bill requires every rental host to register with the state, mandates they carry insurance, and opens the potential for local taxes on top of a new state levy. (29 words)
cn: 该法案要求每位出租业主向州政府登记，强制其购买保险，并在州一级新增税费的基础上，为地方征税打开了空间。

en: Boston took things even further, requiring renters to register with the city's Inspectional Services Department. (15 words)
cn: 波士顿则更进一步，要求出租方到该市的检验服务部门进行登记。

en: Horn said similar registration requirements could benefit struggling cities and towns, but "if we want to make a change in the housing market, the main one is we have to build a lot more." (34 words)
cn: 霍恩表示，类似的登记要求可能有益于那些苦苦挣扎的城镇，但"如果我们想在住房市场上有所作为，首要的一条是我们必须建造更多的住房。"

en: If you're heading for your nearest branch of Waterstones, the biggest book retailer in the UK, in search of the Duchess of Sussex's new children's book The Bench, you might have to be prepared to hunt around a bit; the same may be true of The President's Daughter, the new thriller by Bill Clinton and James Patterson. (57 words)
cn: 如果你正前往最近的一家水石书店——英国最大的图书零售商——去寻找萨塞克斯公爵夫人新出的童书《长凳》，恐怕得准备多找一阵子了；比尔·克林顿与詹姆斯·帕特森合著的新惊悚小说《总统的女儿》可能也是如此。

en: Both of these books are published next week by Penguin Random House (PRH), a company currently involved in a stand-off with Waterstones. (22 words)
cn: 这两本书都将于下周由企鹅兰登书屋出版，而该公司目前正与水石书店处于对峙之中。

en: The problem began late last year, when PRH confirmed that it had introduced a credit limit with Waterstones "at a very significant level". (23 words)
cn: 问题始于去年年底，当时企鹅兰登书屋确认已向水石书店设置了一个"额度极高"的信用上限。

en: The trade magazine The Bookseller reported that Waterstones branch managers were being told to remove PRH books from prominent areas such as tables, display spaces and windows, and were "quietly retiring them to their relevant sections". (36 words)
cn: 行业杂志《书商》报道称，水石书店各分店经理接到指示，要将企鹅兰登书屋的图书从桌面、展示区和橱窗等显眼位置撤下，并"悄悄将其放回各自所属的书架区域"。

en: PRH declined to comment on the issue, but a spokesperson for Waterstones told me: "Waterstones are currently operating with reduced credit terms from PRH, the only publisher in the UK to place any limitations on our ability to trade. (39 words)
cn: 企鹅兰登书屋拒绝对此事发表评论，但水石书店的一位发言人告诉我："水石书店目前正以被企鹅兰登书屋缩减的信用条款来维持经营，而企鹅兰登书屋是英国唯一一家对我们的交易能力施加任何限制的出版商。

en: We are not boycotting PRH titles but we are doing our utmost to ensure that availability for customers remains good despite the lower overall levels of stock. (27 words)
cn: 我们并没有抵制企鹅兰登书屋的图书，不过尽管整体库存水平有所降低，我们仍在竭尽全力确保顾客仍能买到这些书。

en: We are hopeful with our shops now open again that normality will return and that we will be allowed to buy appropriately. (22 words)
cn: 随着我们的书店现已重新开业，我们抱持希望，期待一切回归常态，也期待我们能被允许进行合理的采购。

en: Certainly, our shops are exceptionally busy. (6 words)
cn: 毫无疑问，我们各家书店的生意异常忙碌。

en: The sales for our May Books of the Month surpassed any month since 2018." (14 words)
cn: 我们五月月度图书的销量超过了2018年以来的任何一个月。"

en: In the meantime, PRH authors have been the losers. (9 words)
cn: 在此期间，企鹅兰登书屋的作者成了输家。

en: Big-name PRH authors may suffer a bit, but it's those mid-list authors, who normally rely on Waterstones staff's passion for promoting books by lesser-known writers, who will be praying for an end to the dispute. (35 words)
cn: 企鹅兰登书屋的大牌作家或许会受些许影响，但损失最重的却是那些中等知名度的作者——他们通常要靠水石书店员工对推介小众作家作品的热情来带动销量——此刻他们正在苦苦祈祷这场争端早日结束。

en: It comes at a time when authors are already worried about the consequences of the proposed merger between PRH and another big publisher, Simon & Schuster—the reduction in the number of unaligned UK publishers is likely to lead to fewer bidding wars, lower advances, and more conformity in terms of what is published. (54 words)
cn: 而恰逢此时，作者们已然在担心企鹅兰登书屋与另一大型出版商西蒙与舒斯特拟议合并所带来的后果——英国独立出版商数量的减少，很可能导致竞标战变少、预付款降低，以及出版内容更趋同质化。

en: "This is all part of a wider change towards concentration of power," says literary agent Andrew Lownie. (17 words)
cn: "这都是权力日趋集中的更广泛变革的一部分，"文学经纪人安德鲁·劳尼表示。

en: "The publishing industry talks about diversity in terms of authors and staff but it also needs a plurality of ways of delivering intellectual contact, choice and different voices. (28 words)
cn: "出版业大谈作者和员工的多元化，但它同样需要多元的渠道来传递思想交流、提供选择、发出不同的声音。

en: After all, many of the most interesting books in recent years have come from small publishers." (16 words)
cn: 毕竟，近年来许多最有趣的书籍都出自小型出版商。"

en: We shall see whether that plurality is a casualty of the current need among publishers to be big enough to take on all-comers. (23 words)
cn: 我们拭目以待，看这种多元化是否会成为出版商当前追求规模、欲与天下群雄一争高下这一需求下的牺牲品。

en: Scientific papers are the recordkeepers of progress in research. (9 words)
cn: 科学论文是研究进展的记录者。

en: Each year researchers publish millions of papers in more than 30,000 journals. (12 words)
cn: 每年，研究人员在超过三万种期刊上发表数百万篇论文。

en: The scientific community measures the quality of those papers in a number of ways, including the perceived quality of the journal (as reflected by the title's impact factor) and the number of citations a specific paper accumulates. (37 words)
cn: 科学界以多种方式衡量这些论文的质量，包括期刊的公认质量（反映在期刊的影响因子上），以及单篇论文积累的引用次数。

en: The careers of scientists and the reputation of their institutions depend on the number and prestige of the papers they produce, but even more so on the citations attracted by these papers. (32 words)
cn: 科学家的职业生涯及其所在机构的声誉，取决于他们所发表论文的数量和声望，但更取决于这些论文所吸引的引用。

en: Citation cartels, where journals, authors, and institutions conspire to inflate citation numbers, have existed for a long time. (18 words)
cn: 引用卡特尔——即期刊、作者和机构合谋虚增引用量——早已有之。

en: In 2016, researchers developed an algorithm to recognize suspicious citation patterns, including groups of authors that disproportionately cite one another and groups of journals that cite each other frequently to increase the impact factors of their publications. (37 words)
cn: 2016年，研究人员开发出一种算法来识别可疑的引用模式，包括彼此之间引用比例畸高的作者群，以及为抬高自家出版物的影响因子而频繁相互引用的期刊群。

en: Recently, another expression of this predatory behavior has emerged: so-called support service consultancies that provide language and other editorial support to individual authors and to journals sometimes advise contributors to add a number of citations to their articles. (38 words)
cn: 最近，这种掠夺性行为的另一种表现形式出现了：那些向个人作者和期刊提供语言及其他编辑支持的所谓支持服务咨询公司，有时会建议投稿者在文章中添加若干引用。

en: The advent of electronic publishing and authors' need to find outlets for their papers resulted in thousands of new journals. (20 words)
cn: 电子出版的到来，加上作者为自己的论文寻找发表渠道的需求，催生了数以千计的新期刊。

en: The birth of predatory journals wasn't far behind. (8 words)
cn: 掠夺性期刊的诞生紧随其后。

en: These journals can act as milk cows where every single article in an issue may cite a specific paper or a series of papers. (24 words)
cn: 这些期刊可以充当摇钱树：某一期的每一篇文章可能都会引用某篇特定的论文或一系列论文。

en: In some instances, there is absolutely no relationship between the content of the article and the citations. (17 words)
cn: 在某些情况下，文章内容与所引文献之间毫无关联。

en: The peculiar part is that the journal that the editor is supposedly working for is not profiting at all—it is just providing citations to other journals. (27 words)
cn: 蹊跷之处在于，那位编辑名义上供职的期刊本身并没有从中获利——它不过是在为其他期刊提供引用。

en: Such practices can lead an article to accrue more than 150 citations in the same year that it was published. (20 words)
cn: 这种做法会让一篇文章在发表当年就累积超过150次引用。

en: How insidious is this type of citation manipulation? (8 words)
cn: 这种类型的引用操纵有多阴险？

en: In one example, an individual—acting as author, editor, and consultant—was able to use at least 15 journals as citation providers to articles published by five scientists at three universities. (31 words)
cn: 举一个例子：某人同时扮演作者、编辑和顾问的角色，竟能利用至少15种期刊作为引用提供方，为三所大学的五位科学家发表的文章提供引用。

en: The problem is rampant in Scopus, a citation database, which includes a high number of the new "international" journals. (19 words)
cn: 这一问题在引文数据库Scopus中十分猖獗，该数据库收录了大量新创办的“国际”期刊。

en: In fact, a listing in Scopus seems to be a criterion to be targeted in this type of citation manipulation. (20 words)
cn: 事实上，被Scopus收录似乎已成为此类引用操纵瞄准的一个标准。

en: Scopus itself has all the data necessary to detect this malpractice. (11 words)
cn: Scopus本身拥有检测这一舞弊行为所需的所有数据。

en: Red flags include a large number of citations to an article within the first year. (15 words)
cn: 危险信号之一便是一篇文章在发表后第一年内即获得大量引用。

en: And for authors who wish to steer clear of citation cartel activities: when an editor, a reviewer, or a support service asks you to add inappropriate references, do not oblige and do report the request to the journal. (38 words)
cn: 而对于希望远离引用卡特尔活动的作者：当有编辑、审稿人或支持服务机构要求你添加不当引用时，不要照办，而且务必向期刊举报这一请求。

en: Nearly 2000 years ago, as the Romans began to pull out of Scotland, they left behind a curious treasure: 10 tons of nails, nearly a million of the things. (28 words)
cn: 将近两千年前，当罗马人开始撤出苏格兰时，他们留下了一笔奇特的宝藏：十吨钉子，将近一百万枚。

en: The nail hoard was discovered in 1960 in a four-metre-deep pit covered by two metres of gravel. (17 words)
cn: 这批埋藏的钉子于1960年被发现，藏在一个四米深的坑中，上面覆盖着两米厚的砾石。

en: Why had the Romans buried a million nails? (8 words)
cn: 罗马人为什么要埋掉一百万枚钉子？

en: The likely explanation is that the withdrawal was rushed, and they didn't want the local Caledonians getting their hands on 10 tons of weapon-grade iron. (25 words)
cn: 最可能的解释是，撤军过程匆忙，他们不愿让当地的喀里多尼亚人得到这十吨可制造武器的铁。

en: The Romans buried the nails so deep that they would not be discovered for almost two millennia. (17 words)
cn: 罗马人把钉子埋得极深，以至于在将近两千年间都未被发现。

en: Later civilisations would value the skilled blacksmith's labour in a nail even more than the raw material. (17 words)
cn: 后世的文明对一枚钉子中所倾注的熟练铁匠的劳动，看得甚至比原材料本身还要重。

en: As Roma Agrawal explains in her new delightful book Nuts and Bolts, early 17th-century Virginians would sometimes burn down their homes if they were planning to relocate. (27 words)
cn: 正如罗玛·阿格拉瓦尔在她那本引人入胜的新书《螺母与螺栓》中所解释的，17世纪早期的弗吉尼亚人若打算迁居他处，有时竟会把自己的房屋烧掉。

en: This was an attempt to recover the valuable nails, which could be reused after sifting the ashes. (17 words)
cn: 这是为了回收那些宝贵的钉子，筛过灰烬之后便可重新使用。

en: The idea that one might burn down an entire house just to reclaim the nails underlines how scarce, costly and valuable the simple-seeming technology was. (25 words)
cn: 一个人为了收回钉子竟会烧掉整座房子，这种想法本身就凸显出这项看似简单的技术在当时是多么稀缺、昂贵和珍贵。

en: The price of nails fell by 90% between the late 1700s and mid-1900s, as economist Daniel Sichel points out in a research paper. (23 words)
cn: 正如经济学家丹尼尔·西克尔在一篇研究论文中所指出的，钉子的价格在18世纪末到20世纪中期下降了90%。

en: According to Sichel, although the falling price of nails was driven partly by cheaper iron and cheaper energy, most of the credit goes to nail manufacturers who simply found more efficient ways to turn steel into nails. (37 words)
cn: 据西克尔分析，尽管钉子价格下降的部分原因是更便宜的铁和更便宜的能源，但最主要的功劳归于钉子制造商们，他们不过是找到了将钢变成钉子的更高效的方法。

en: Nails themselves have changed over the years, but Sichel studied them because they haven't changed much. (16 words)
cn: 尽管钉子本身在岁月流转中已有所变化，但西克尔之所以研究它们，恰恰是因为它们变化不大。

en: Roman lamps and Roman chariots are very different from LED strips and sports cars, but Roman nails are still clearly nails. (21 words)
cn: 罗马油灯和罗马战车与LED灯带和跑车大相径庭，但罗马钉子至今看来仍然是毫无疑义的钉子。

en: It would be absurd to try to track the changing price of sports cars since 1695, but to ask the same question of nails makes perfect sense. (27 words)
cn: 试图追踪跑车自1695年以来的价格变化是荒谬的，但对钉子提出同样的问题则完全合乎情理。

en: I make no apology for being obsessed by a particular feature of these objects: their price. I am an economist, after all. (22 words)
cn: 我毫不为自己痴迷于这些物品的某个特定特征——它们的价格——而感到歉意。毕竟，我是一名经济学家。

en: After writing two books about the history of inventions, one thing I've learnt is that while it is the enchantingly sophisticated technologies that get all the hype, it's the cheap technologies that change the world. (35 words)
cn: 在写过两本关于发明史的著作之后，我领悟到一件事：备受追捧的总是那些精妙绝伦的技术，但真正改变世界的，却是那些廉价的技术。

en: The Gutenberg printing press transformed civilisation not by changing the nature of writing but by changing its cost—and it would have achieved little without a parallel collapse in the price of surfaces to write on, thanks to an often-overlooked technology called paper. (42 words)
cn: 古腾堡印刷机改变文明，不是靠改变书写的本质，而是靠改变其成本——而如果没有书写承载面价格的同时暴跌，它所取得的成就也将微乎其微，这得归功于一种常被忽视的技术，那就是纸。

en: Solar panels had few niche uses until they became cheap; now they are transforming the global energy system. (18 words)
cn: 太阳能电池板在变得廉价之前，用途寥寥无几；而今，它们正在改变全球能源体系。

en: Parenting tips obtained from hunter-gatherers in Africa may be the key to bringing up more contented children, researchers have suggested. (20 words)
cn: 研究人员指出，从非洲狩猎采集部落那里获取的育儿经验，或许是养育出更知足常乐的孩子的关键。

en: The idea is based on studies of communities such as the Kung of Botswana, where each child is cared for by many adults. (23 words)
cn: 这一观点基于对博茨瓦纳昆人部落等社群的研究，在这些社群中，每个孩子都由许多成年人共同照料。

en: Kung children as young as four will help to look after younger ones and “baby-wearing”, in which infants are carried in slings, is considered the norm. (26 words)
cn: 昆人部落的孩子年仅四岁就会帮忙照看更年幼的孩子，而用背带把婴儿挂在身上"抱抱婴儿"也被视为常态。

en: According to Dr Nikhil Chaudhary, an evolutionary anthropologist at Cambridge University, these practices, known as alloparenting, could lead to less anxiety for children and parents. (25 words)
cn: 剑桥大学进化人类学家尼基尔·乔杜里博士认为，这些被称为"异亲抚育"的做法，可能会让儿童和父母的焦虑感都有所减轻。

en: Dr Annie Swanepoel, a child psychiatrist, believes that there are ways to incorporate them into western life. (14 words)
cn: 儿童精神科医生安妮·斯瓦内普尔博士相信，有办法将这些做法融入西方生活。

en: In Germany, one scheme has paired an old people's home with a nursery. (14 words)
cn: 在德国，有一项计划将一家养老院与一所托儿所配对在一起。

en: The residents help to look after the children, an arrangement akin to alloparenting. (13 words)
cn: 养老院的住户帮忙照看孩子，这种安排与异亲抚育颇为相似。

en: Another measure could be encouraging friendships between children in different school years to mirror the unsupervised mixed-age playgroups in hunter-gatherer communities. (20 words)
cn: 另一项措施可以是鼓励不同年级的孩子之间建立友谊，以此再现狩猎采集部落中那种无需成人监管的混龄游戏群体的模式。

en: In a paper published in the Journal of Child Psychology and Psychiatry, researchers said that the western nuclear family was a recent invention which broke with evolutionary history. (29 words)
cn: 在《儿童心理学与精神病学杂志》上发表的一篇论文中，研究人员指出，西方核心家庭是较晚近才出现的发明，与人类的进化史背道而驰。

en: This abrupt shift to an "intensive mothering narrative", which suggests that mothers should manage childcare alone, was likely to have been harmful. (22 words)
cn: 这种向"密集型母职叙事"——即认为母亲应当独自承担育儿责任——的骤然转变，很可能是有害的。

en: "Such narratives can lead to maternal exhaustion and have dangerous consequences," they wrote. (13 words)
cn: "这类叙事可能导致母亲身心俱疲，并带来危险的后果，"他们写道。

en: By contrast, in hunter-gatherer societies adults other than the parents can provide almost half of a child's care. (18 words)
cn: 相比之下，在狩猎采集社会，除父母之外的其他成年人可以提供儿童将近一半的照料。

en: One previous study looked at the Efe people of the Democratic Republic of Congo. (14 words)
cn: 此前有一项研究考察了刚果民主共和国的埃费人。

en: It found that infants had an average of 14 alloparents a day by the time they were 18 weeks old and were passed between caregivers eight times an hour. (29 words)
cn: 研究发现，到18周大时，埃费婴儿平均每天有14位非父母照料者，每小时在不同照料者之间被传递八次。

en: Chaudhary said that parents now had less childcare support from family and social networks than during most of humans' evolutionary history, but introducing additional caregivers could reduce stress and maternal depression, which could have a "knock-on" benefit to a child's wellbeing. (41 words)
cn: 乔杜里表示，与人类进化史上的大部分时期相比，现在的父母从家庭和社交网络中获得育儿支持要少，但引入额外的照料者可以减轻压力和母亲抑郁，这对孩子的身心健康可能产生"连锁"益处。

en: An infant born to a hunter-gatherer society could have more than ten caregivers---this contrasts starkly to nursery settings in the UK where regulations call for a ratio of one carer to four children aged two to three. (39 words)
cn: 在狩猎采集社会出生的婴儿可能有十多位照料者——这与英国托儿所的情况形成鲜明对比，英国的规定要求一名保育员照料四名两到三岁的儿童。

en: While hunter-gatherer children learnt from observation and imitation in mixed-age playgroups, researchers said that western "instructive teaching", where pupils are asked to sit still, may contribute to conditions such as attention deficit hyperactivity disorder. (34 words)
cn: 狩猎采集部落的儿童在混龄游戏群体中通过观察和模仿来学习，而研究人员指出，西方那种要求学生端坐静听的"教导式教学"，可能是导致注意力缺陷多动障碍等病症的原因之一。

en: Chaudhary said that Britain should explore the possibility that older siblings helping their parents "might also enhance their own social development." (21 words)
cn: 乔杜里表示，英国应该探讨这样一种可能性：年长的兄弟姐妹在帮助父母的过程中，"或许也能促进自身的社交能力发展"。

en: A Polish digital artist who uses classical painting styles to create dreamy fantasy landscapes. (14 words)
cn: 一位波兰数字艺术家，以古典绘画风格创作梦幻般的奇幻风景画。

en: He has made illustrations for games such as Sony's Horizon Forbidden West, Ubisoft's Anno, Dungeons & Dragons, and Magic: The Gathering. (21 words)
cn: 他曾为索尼的《地平线：西之绝境》、育碧的《纪元》、《龙与地下城》以及《万智牌》等游戏绘制插画。

en: And he's become a sudden hit in the new world of text-to-image AI generation. (14 words)
cn: 而如今，他在文字生成图像这一人工智能新领域一夜爆红

en: His distinctive style is now one of the most commonly used prompts in the new AI art generator Stable Diffusion. (20 words)
cn: 他独特的风格，现已成为新型AI艺术生成器Stable Diffusion中最常用的提示词之一。

en: The tool, along with other popular image-generation AI models, allows anyone to create impressive images based on text prompts. (19 words)
cn: 这一工具，与市面上其他流行的图像生成AI模型一道，让任何人都能根据文字提示创作出令人印象深刻的图像。

en: For example, type in “Wizard with sword and a glowing orb of magic fire fights a fierce dragon Greg Rutkowski,” and the system will produce something that looks not a million miles away from works in Rutkowski's style. (38 words)
cn: 例如，输入“持剑的巫师手持一颗发光的魔法火球与猛龙搏斗，格雷格·鲁特科夫斯基风格”，系统便会生成一幅看上去与鲁特科夫斯基画风相差不远的作品。

en: But these open-source programs are built by scraping images from the internet, often without permission and proper attribution to artists. (20 words)
cn: 但这些开源程序是通过从互联网上抓取图像构建而成的，往往既未获得许可，也未给艺术家应有的署名。

en: As a result, they are raising tricky questions about ethics and copyright. (12 words)
cn: 由此，它们在伦理和版权方面引发了棘手的问题。

en: And artists like Rutkowski have had enough. (7 words)
cn: 而像鲁特科夫斯基这样的艺术家已经忍无可忍了。

en: According to the website Lexica, which tracks over 10 million images and prompts generated by Stable Diffusion, Rutkowski's name has been used as a prompt around 93,000 times. (28 words)
cn: 据Lexica网站统计——该网站追踪了Stable Diffusion所生成的逾千万张图像和提示词——鲁特科夫斯基的名字被用作提示词的次数已达约93,000次。

en: Rutkowski was initially surprised but thought it might be a good way to reach new audiences. (16 words)
cn: 鲁特科夫斯基起初感到惊讶，但转念一想，这或许是触达新受众的好办法。

en: Then he tried searching for his name to see if a piece he had worked on had been published. (19 words)
cn: 后来，他试着搜索自己的名字，想看看自己参与创作的作品是否已经发表。

en: The online search brought back work that had his name attached to it but wasn't his. (16 words)
cn: 网上搜索出来的结果是挂着他名字的作品，却并非出自他本人之手。

en: “It's been just a month. What about in a year? I probably won't be able to find my work out there because the internet will be flooded with AI art,” Rutkowski says. (32 words)
cn: "才过了一个月而已。一年以后呢？我很可能到时就找不到自己的作品了，因为互联网上将充斥着AI艺术，"鲁特科夫斯基说。

en: “That's concerning.” (2 words)
cn: "这令人担忧。"

en: Other artists besides Rutkowski have been surprised by the apparent popularity of their work in text-to-image generators—and some are now fighting back. (23 words)
cn: 除鲁特科夫斯基之外，其他艺术家也惊讶于自己的作品在文字生成图像工具中如此受到追捧——而其中一些人已开始反击。

en: Karla Ortiz, an illustrator based in San Francisco who found her work in Stable Diffusion's data set, has been raising awareness about the issues around AI art and copyright. (29 words)
cn: 旧金山的插画家卡拉·奥尔蒂斯在Stable Diffusion的数据集中发现了自己的作品，她一直在提高人们对AI艺术与版权问题的认识。

en: Artists say they risk losing income as people start using AI-generated images based on copyrighted material for commercial purposes. (19 words)
cn: 艺术家们表示，随着人们开始将基于受版权保护材料生成的AI图像用于商业目的，他们面临着收入流失的风险。

en: But it's also a lot more personal, Ortiz says, arguing that because art is so closely linked to a person, it could raise data protection and privacy problems. (37 words)
cn: 但奥尔蒂斯说，这也要私人化得多，她指出，正因为艺术与个人紧密相连，这便可能引发数据保护和隐私方面的问题。

en: “There is a coalition growing within artist industries to figure out how to tackle or mitigate this,” says Ortiz. (19 words)
cn: "艺术行业内部正在形成联盟，以寻求应对或缓解这一问题的方法，"奥尔蒂斯说。

en: The group is in its early days of mobilization, which could involve pushing for new policies or regulation. (18 words)
cn: 这一团体正处于动员的初期阶段，其行动可能包括推动出台新的政策或法规。

en: One suggestion is that AI models could be trained on images in the public domain, and AI companies could forge partnerships with museums and artists, Ortiz says. (27 words)
cn: 奥尔蒂斯表示，有一项建议是，AI模型可以用公共领域的图像进行训练，而AI公司则可以与博物馆和艺术家建立合作关系。

en: The miracle of the Chesapeake Bay lies not in its depths, but in the complexity of its natural construction, the interaction of fresh and saline waters, and the mix of land and water. (33 words)
cn: 切萨皮克湾的奇迹不在于其深度，而在于其自然构造的复杂性，在于淡水与咸水的交融，也在于陆地与水域的交错。

en: The shallows provide homes for hundreds of species while storing floodwaters, filtering pollutants from water, and protecting nearby communities from potentially destructive storm surges. (24 words)
cn: 浅滩为数百个物种提供了栖息地，同时还能蓄积洪水、过滤水体中的污染物，并保护附近社区免受潜在破坏性风暴潮的侵袭。

en: All this was put at great risk late last month, when the U.S. Supreme Court issued a ruling in an Idaho case that provides the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) far less authority to regulate wetlands and waterways. (38 words)
cn: 上月底，所有这一切被置于极大的风险之中：美国最高法院对爱达荷州的一起案件做出裁决，使得美国环境保护局在监管湿地和水道方面的权力大为削弱。

en: Specifically, a 5-4 majority decided that wetlands protected by the EPA under its Clean Water Act authority must have a "continuous surface connection" to bodies of water. (27 words)
cn: 具体而言，以五票赞成、四票反对的多数，大法官们裁定，受环保局依据《清洁水法》授权的湿地必须与水体之间存在"连续的地表连接"。

en: This narrowing of the regulatory scope was a victory for builders, mining operators and other commercial interests often at odds with environmental rules. (23 words)
cn: 这一监管范围的收窄，对于常常与环境法规相抵触的建筑商、矿业经营者及其他商业利益集团而言，是一场胜利。

en: And it carries "significant repercussions for water quality and flood control throughout the United States," as Justice Brett Kavanaugh observed. (20 words)
cn: 而正如大法官布雷特·卡瓦诺所指出的，这"将对整个美国的水质和防洪产生重大影响"。

en: In Maryland, the good news is that there are many state laws in place that provide wetlands protections. (18 words)
cn: 在马里兰州，好消息是，已有许多州法律在保护湿地。

en: But that's a very shortsighted view, particularly when it comes to the Chesapeake Bay. (14 words)
cn: 但那是一种非常短视的看法，尤其是当涉及切萨皮克湾的时候。

en: The reality is that water, and the pollutants that so often come with it, don't respect state boundaries. (18 words)
cn: 现实情况是，水，以及常常随之而来的污染物，可不会尊重州界。

en: The Chesapeake draws from a 64,000-square-mile watershed that reaches into Virginia, Pennsylvania, New York, West Virginia, the District of Columbia, and Delaware. (21 words)
cn: 切萨皮克湾的水来自一个面积达6.4万平方英里的流域，该流域延展至弗吉尼亚州、宾夕法尼亚州、纽约州、西弗吉尼亚州、哥伦比亚特区和特拉华州。

en: Will those jurisdictions extend the same protections now denied under Sackett v. EPA? (13 words)
cn: 这些司法管辖区，会提供在"萨克特诉环保局案"裁决下现已不再要求的同样保护吗？

en: Perhaps some, but all? (4 words)
cn: 也许有些会，但全部都会吗？

en: That seems unlikely. (3 words)
cn: 这似乎不太可能。

en: It is too easy, and misleading, to see such court rulings as merely standing up for the rights of land owners when the consequences can be so dire for their neighbors. (31 words)
cn: 当后果对左邻右舍可能如此严峻时，将此类法院裁决仅仅视为维护土地所有者的权利，未免太过轻率，且具有误导性。

en: And it's a reminder that the EPA's involvement in the Chesapeake Bay Program has long been crucial as the means to transcend the influence of deep-pocketed special interests in neighboring states. (31 words)
cn: 这同时也提醒我们，环保局在切萨皮克湾计划中的参与长期以来都至关重要，因为它是超越邻近各州财力雄厚的特殊利益集团影响的手段。

en: Pennsylvania farmers, to use one telling example, aren't thinking about next year's blue crab harvest in Maryland when they decide whether to spread animal waste on their fields, yet the runoff into nearby creeks can have enormous impact downstream. (39 words)
cn: 举一个很能说明问题的例子：宾夕法尼亚州的农民，在决定是否将牲畜粪便撒在田里时，并不会想到马里兰州明年的蓝蟹收成，然而，流入附近溪流的径流却会给下游带来巨大的影响。

en: And so we would call on state lawmakers from Richmond to Albany to consider reviewing their own wetlands protections and see for themselves the enormous stakes involved. (27 words)
cn: 因此，我们呼吁从里士满到奥尔巴尼的各州立法者，考虑重新审视各自的湿地保护措施，并亲自来看看其中所涉及的巨大利益攸关。

en: We can offer them a visit to Blackwater National Wildlife Refuge in Dorchester County where bald eagles fly over tidal marshes so shallow you could not paddle a boat across them but teeming with aquatic life. (36 words)
cn: 我们可以邀请他们去多尔切斯特县的黑水国家野生动物保护区，在那里，白头鹰在潮汐沼泽上空翱翔，那沼泽浅得连船都划不过去，却遍布着丰富的水生生物。

en: It's worth the scenic drive. (5 words)
cn: 这趟沿途风景优美的车程，绝对值得。

en: The grammar school boy from Stratford-upon-Avon has landed a scholarly punch after groundbreaking research showed that Shakespeare does benefit children's literacy and emotional development. (27 words)
cn: 这位来自埃文河畔斯特拉特福文法学校的男孩在学术界挥出了一记重拳：一项开创性研究表明，莎士比亚确实有益于儿童的读写能力和情感发展。

en: But only if you act him out. (8 words)
cn: 但前提是你得把他表演出来。

en: A study found that a "rehearsal room" approach to teaching Shakespeare broadened children's vocabulary and the complexity of their writing as well as their emotional literacy. (29 words)
cn: 一项研究发现，以"排练室"方式教授莎士比亚能扩大儿童的词汇量，提升他们写作的复杂性，并增强他们的情感素养。

en: "The research shows that the way actors work makes a big difference to the way children use language and also how they think about themselves," Jacqui O'Hanlon of the Royal Shakespeare Company (RSC), which commissioned the study, said. (41 words)
cn: "研究表明，演员的工作方式对儿童运用语言的方式以及他们如何看待自己都有着重大影响，"委托开展这项研究的皇家莎士比亚剧团（RSC）的杰奎·奥汉隆说道。

en: The randomised control trial involved hundreds of year 5 pupils—aged nine and ten—at 45 state primary schools that had not been "previously exposed to RSC pedagogy." (29 words)
cn: 这项随机对照试验涉及45所公立小学的数百名五年级学生，年龄为九至十岁，这些学校此前未曾"接触过RSC教学法"。

en: They were split into target and control groups and asked to write, for example, a message in a bottle as Ferdinand following the shipwreck in The Tempest. (28 words)
cn: 他们被分成目标组与对照组，并都被要求写作，例如以《暴风雨》中遭遇船难后的斐迪南的口吻写一封瓶中信。

en: The target group were given a 30-minute drama-based activity to accompany the passage. (14 words)
cn: 目标组被给予了一个30分钟的戏剧类活动来配合这段文段。

en: The peer-reviewed results showed that the target group of pupils drew on a wider vocabulary, used words "classed as more sophisticated or rarer", and wrote at greater length. (32 words)
cn: 同行评审的结果显示，目标组学生调用的词汇更广泛，使用了"被归类为更复杂或更罕见的"词语，写作篇幅也更长。

en: They also "appear to be more comfortable writing in role...while [control] pupils imagine how they themselves would react to being shipwrecked, [target] children put themselves in the shoes of a literary character and express that character's emotion". (42 words)
cn: 他们"在角色写作中也显得更自如……对照组学生想象的是自己遭遇船难会作何反应，而目标组的孩子则让自己代入文学角色的处境，并表达那个角色的情感"。

en: The Time to Act study, which is published by the RSC this week, also found that while control pupils relied on "desert island cliches" such as palm trees, target pupils were "more expansive [giving] a broader picture of the sky, the sea and the atmospheric conditions". (48 words)
cn: 由RSC本周发表的"行动时间"研究还发现，对照组学生依赖的是棕榈树这类"荒岛陈词滥调"，而目标组学生则"更为开阔，描绘出一幅涵盖天空、大海和气候状况的更广阔画面"。

en: O'Hanlon said she had been most surprised by the "emotional literacy that was evident in the [target] children's writing" and that they were "more resilient in their writing, more hopeful". (31 words)
cn: 奥汉隆说，最让她感到惊讶的是"目标组孩子写作中明显展现出的情感素养"，以及他们"在写作中更有韧性，更有希望感"。

en: She added "The emotional understanding was very evident and it is probably related to the [rehearsal room process] where you are used to trying to imagine your way through. (32 words)
cn: 她补充道："情感理解非常明显，这很可能与排练室过程有关，在这一过程中你会习惯于试着去想象自己该如何应对过来。

en: They were comfortable in describing different emotional states and part of what you do in drama is put yourself in different shoes." (25 words)
cn: 他们能从容地描述不同的情感状态，而你在戏剧中所做的其中一件事，就是将自己置于他人的处境中。"

en: The study showed the importance of embedding arts in education, she said. (13 words)
cn: 该研究表明了将艺术嵌入教育之中的重要性，她说。

en: But could the results be replicated with any old dramatist? (10 words)
cn: 但这些结果能在任何一位古老剧作家身上得到复制吗？

en: O'Hanlon said more research would be needed but suggested that Shakespeare's use of 20,000 words, compared with the everyday 2,000 words, gave a "massive expansion of language into children's lives", which was combined with children "using their whole bodies to bring words to life". (46 words)
cn: 奥汉隆表示，还需要更多研究，但她提出，与日常使用的两千个词汇相比，莎士比亚所用的两万个词汇"将语言极大地扩展到了孩子们的生活中"，再加之以孩子们"用整个身体去让词语活起来"。

en: I was shocked to learn recently that some scientists want to scale back their research in an effort to decrease carbon emissions. (22 words)
cn: 最近得知一些科学家为了减少碳排放而想要缩减他们的研究规模，我感到震惊。

en: The crisis is here, they said, and we need to cut back on our energy-intensive modelling. (17 words)
cn: 他们说，危机已经来临，我们必须削减能源密集型的建模工作。

en: At the very least, we need to make our energy use far more sustainable. (14 words)
cn: 至少，我们需要让能源使用变得远更具可持续性。

en: It is unarguable that our laboratories, scientific instruments, rockets and satellites—the tools we scientists need to measure the planet's pulse—demand significant amounts of energy both in their construction and operation. (36 words)
cn: 无可争辩的是，我们的实验室、科学仪器、火箭和卫星——这些我们科学家用来测量地球脉搏的工具——在其建造和运行过程中都需要消耗大量的能源。

en: And it is equally true that science's unrelenting appetite for information has caused a mushrooming of energy-intensive data centres around the world. (25 words)
cn: 同样确凿的是，科学对信息永不满足的渴求，已导致世界各地能源密集型数据中心如雨后春笋般涌现。

en: According to the International Energy Agency, these buildings now consume about 1 percent of the world's electricity. (18 words)
cn: 据国际能源署统计，这些建筑如今消耗着全球约1%的电力。

en: However, this is a price we must pay for understanding the world. (13 words)
cn: 然而，这是我们为了解世界而必须付出的代价。

en: How can we inform decision makers about the best ways to bring down carbon emissions if we can't track the amount of carbon dioxide in the atmosphere, where it's coming from and who's producing it? (35 words)
cn: 倘若我们无法追踪大气中二氧化碳的含量、来源和排放者，又怎能告知决策者减少碳排放的最佳路径？

en: The carbon emissions from technological research are well spent; ultimately this research will safeguard the future of our planet. (22 words)
cn: 技术研究产生的碳排放是值得的；归根结底，这类研究将守护我们星球的未来。
