en: It can be hard for scientists to make the case because our work is complex, often takes place behind closed doors and does not always lend itself to easy interpretation or explanation. (33 words)
cn: 科学家们可能很难为此据理力争，因为我们的工作复杂，常在幕后进行，且并非总能被简单解读或解释。

en: But demonstrating the efficacy of science will be crucial if we are to solve humanity's greatest challenges. (19 words)
cn: 但如果我们想要解决人类最重大的挑战，展示科学的效力便至关重要。

en: It is all too easy to feel paralysed in the face of daunting problems such as climate change and to do nothing. (22 words)
cn: 面对气候变化等令人生畏的问题，人们很容易陷入瘫痪、无所作为。

en: But then I think of a friend's daughter who turned her fears into action: she became a wind energy engineer and now thrives on delivering renewable energy, limiting emissions. (30 words)
cn: 但接着我想到一位朋友的女儿，她将恐惧化为行动：她成为一名风能工程师，如今在提供可再生能源、限制排放的领域里干得风生水起。

en: Recognising the hope that science and engineering can bring was the impetus behind the creation of the Millennium Technology Prize, which is now entering its 20th year as a celebration of human ingenuity. (37 words)
cn: 认识到科学与工程所能带来的希望，正是千禧年技术奖设立的初衷，这一奖项作为对人类聪明才智的礼赞，如今已进入第20个年头。

en: One of the past winners, Professor Martin Green from the University of New South Wales, Australia, is the inventor of the Passivated Emitter and Rear Cell technology which is now found in most of the world's solar panels. (38 words)
cn: 往届获奖者之一、澳大利亚新南威尔士大学的马丁·格林教授是钝化发射极与背面电池技术的发明者，这项技术如今已应用于全球大多数太阳能电池板中。

en: Thanks to his invention, we have a real chance to decrease the world's carbon emissions. (16 words)
cn: 多亏了他的发明，我们才真正有机会减少全球的碳排放。

en: Every day, scientists, technologists and engineers are discovering new ways to exploit renewable energy sources and develop techniques not just to use power more intelligently but to power our intelligence. (33 words)
cn: 每一天，科学家、技术专家和工程师们都在发掘利用可再生能源的新途径，并开发各种技术，不仅是为了更智能地使用能源，更是为了给我们的智能设备供能。

en: A great example of this is LUMI, Europe's largest supercomputer, which is astonishingly carbon-negative. (16 words)
cn: 一个绝佳的例子便是“露米”LUMI，欧洲最大的超级计算机，它令人惊叹地实现了碳负排放。

en: LUMI is housed in an old paper mill, powered by a nearby river, and its remote heat warms the people of the nearby town of Kajaani. (27 words)
cn: LUMI设在一座旧造纸厂内，由附近河流提供电力，其远程余热还为邻近的卡亚尼镇居民供暖。

en: If the world is to meet its net-zero ambitions, we must think hard about how we can deliver sustainable computing and deliver more LUMIs. (26 words)
cn: 如果世界要实现净零排放的雄心，我们就必须认真思考如何推动可持续计算，并造就更多像LUMI这样的范例。

en: Ever since taking on Netflix Inc. at its own game, old Hollywood has struggled to turn a profit in streaming, with the likes of Disney+, Peacock and Paramount+ losing billions of dollars each year, sparking concerns that the services will never be as profitable as cable once was. (48 words)
cn: 自从在奈飞公司的主场与其正面交锋以来，传统好莱坞在流媒体领域一直挣扎着难以盈利，迪士尼+、孔雀和派拉蒙+等平台每年亏损数十亿美元，这让人们担忧，这些流媒体服务可能永远无法像从前的有线电视那样赚钱。

en: But the age of streaming has been a boon for some unintended winners: pirates that use software to rip a film or television show in seconds from legitimate online video platforms and host the titles on their own, illegitimate services, which rake in about $2 billion annually from ads and subscriptions. (50 words)
cn: 但流媒体时代却让一些意想不到的赢家发了大财：盗版者利用软件在几秒钟内从合法在线视频平台上扒下电影或电视节目，并将其放在自己运营的非法服务平台上，这些平台每年通过广告和订阅就能轻易赚取约20亿美元。

en: With no video production costs, illegal streaming sites have achieved profit margins approaching 90% according to the Motion Picture Association (MPA), a trade group representing Hollywood studios that's working to crack down on the thousands of illegal platforms that have cropped up in recent years. (44 words)
cn: 据美国电影协会称，由于无需承担视频制作成本，非法流媒体网站的利润率已接近90%。美国电影协会是代表好莱坞制片厂的行业组织，正致力于打击近年来大量涌现的数千个非法平台。

en: Initially the rise of legitimate online businesses such as Netflix actually helped curb digital piracy, which had largely been based on file uploads. (24 words)
cn: 起初，奈飞这类合法在线企业的崛起确实有助于遏制数字盗版，当时的盗版主要依靠文件上传。

en: But now piracy involving illegal streaming services as well as file-sharing costs the US economy about $30 billion in lost revenue a year and some 250,000 jobs, estimates the US Chamber of Commerce's Global Innovation Policy Center. (37 words)
cn: 但如今，据美国商会全球创新政策中心估算，涉及非法流媒体服务和文件共享的盗版行为，每年给美国经济造成约300亿美元的收入损失，并导致约25万个就业岗位流失。

en: The global impact is about $71 billion annually. (8 words)
cn: 其在全球造成的冲击每年约为710亿美元。

en: "The people who are stealing our movies and our television shows and operating piracy sites are not mom and pop operations," says Charlie Rivkin, chief executive officer of the MPA. (29 words)
cn: "那些窃取我们电影和电视节目、运营盗版网站的人，可不是家庭作坊式的小打小闹，"美国电影协会首席执行官查理·里夫金说。

en: "This is organized crime." (4 words)
cn: "这是有组织犯罪。"

en: Rivkin joined the MPA in 2017 after the organization failed five years earlier to build consensus between Hollywood and Silicon Valley to win passage of legislation in Congress aimed at stopping online piracy. (35 words)
cn: 里夫金于2017年加入美国电影协会，而在此之前五年，该组织曾试图在好莱坞和硅谷之间凝聚共识，以推动国会通过一项旨在遏制网络盗版的立法，但未能成功。

en: In 2017 the association formed the Alliance for Creativity and Entertainment (ACE), an enforcement task force of about 100 detectives circling the globe to help local authorities arrest streaming pirates. (33 words)
cn: 2017年，该协会组建了创意与娱乐联盟，这是一支由约百名探员组成的执法特遣队，奔波于全球各地，协助地方当局逮捕流媒体盗版者。

en: ACE says it's helped shrink the number of illegal streaming services in North America to 126, from more than 1,400 in 2018, aided in part by the MPA's support for a 2020 federal law that made large-scale streaming of copyright material a serious crime. (46 words)
cn: 创意与娱乐联盟称，在其推动下，北美非法流媒体服务的数量已从2018年的1400多个减少到现在的126个，这在一定程度上得益于美国电影协会支持了一项2020年通过的联邦法律，该法律将大规模流媒体播放受版权保护内容定为严重犯罪。

en: Consulting firm Parks Associates predicts that legitimate US streaming services' cumulative loss from piracy since 2022 will reach $113 billion in the next two years. (27 words)
cn: 咨询公司帕克斯联合预测，从2022年算起，美国合法流媒体服务因盗版造成的累计损失将在未来两年内达到1130亿美元。

en: "While there is some optimism that emerging countermeasures and best practices may see piracy begin to plateau by 2027, there is no consensus among stakeholders as to when it may begin to decline," says analyst Steve Hawley. (40 words)
cn: "尽管有人乐观地认为，随着新反制措施和最佳实践的涌现，盗版到2027年可能会开始趋于平稳，但利益相关方之间对于盗版究竟何时会开始下降仍无共识，"分析师史蒂夫·霍利表示。

en: Visit any antiques store and you'll encounter artifacts from the past: photographs, letters, a brochure detailing the Sinclair dinosaur exhibit from the 1964-1965 World's Fair, the ephemera of history. (31 words)
cn: 走进任何一家古董店，你都会遇到来自过去的物品：照片、信件、一本详细介绍1964至1965年世博会辛克莱恐龙展的小册子——这些历史的短暂时光之物。

en: Yet these objects aren't truly ephemeral, because they're still here, decades, even centuries later. (15 words)
cn: 然而这些物品并非真正是短暂时光之物，因为几十年、甚至几百年后，它们仍在这里。

en: Because they're tangible. (3 words)
cn: 因为它们是有形的。

en: Have you pondered the life cycle of intangible formats, digital information, given that those who produce these artifacts seldom make provision for their long-term preservation? (27 words)
cn: 你可曾深思过无形格式即数字信息的生命周期，考虑到制作这些制品的人很少为其长期保存做出安排？

en: For millennia, we've known what we've known due to artifacts that have survived, often despite their original creators' neglect. (21 words)
cn: 几千年来，我们之所以知道我们所知道的一切，是因为那些幸存下来的物品，尽管其最初的创作者往往疏于保存。

en: The thing itself is the medium that delivers the information. (11 words)
cn: 物品本身就是传递信息的媒介。

en: At the time of creation, no attempts were made at intentional preservation, yet analog materials have a chance of surviving and serving as the historical record that biographers, historians, and novelists rely on. (35 words)
cn: 在创作之时，没有人刻意去保存，然而模拟材料却有机会幸存下来，并充当传记作家、历史学家和小说家所依赖的历史记录。

en: Libraries and archives have traditionally shouldered the responsibility of organization, preservation, and access to information. (18 words)
cn: 图书馆和档案馆历来承担着整理、保存和提供信息获取的责任。

en: Thus, librarians digitize the tangible so that researchers the world over can quickly search and access their holdings. (20 words)
cn: 因此，图书馆员将实物数字化，使世界各地的研究人员能够快速检索和获取其馆藏。

en: The result is an embarrassment of historical riches, which brings its own needle-and-haystack problems. (18 words)
cn: 结果是历史资料浩如烟海，丰富得令人为难，这本身又带来了大海捞针的难题。

en: Librarians' selfless devotion can act against us when users point to universality of access by holding up a cellphone and saying, "it's all in here" as evidence that libraries are less vital for researchers today. (38 words)
cn: 当用户举起手机说"全在这里面了"，以此作为图书馆对当今研究人员已不那么重要的证据时，图书馆员的无私奉献反倒会对我们不利。

en: Yet how was that universality of access made possible and, perhaps more importantly, how is it maintained? (20 words)
cn: 然而这种获取的普遍性是如何实现的，或许更重要的是，它又是如何维持的？

en: Who curates what is preserved? (5 words)
cn: 谁在精心选择保存什么？

en: When it comes to born-digital information, the terrifying answer can be: if not librarians and archivists, then no one. (20 words)
cn: 就原生数字信息而言，可怕的答案可能是：如果不是图书馆员和档案馆员，那就没有人来做这件事。

en: Digital information requires a great deal more care than analog. (11 words)
cn: 数字信息需要比模拟信息多得多的精心照料。

en: Even when a digital object is preserved, it may only be the carrier that's saved, not the information itself. (21 words)
cn: 即使一件数字物品被保存下来，被保存的可能也只是载体，而非信息本身。

en: As technology advances and a format becomes obsolete, the object is useless. (14 words)
cn: 随着技术进步，一种格式一旦过时，该物品便毫无用处。

en: Have you ever stared helplessly at a ZIP disk, thinking: how do I get the files off this? (18 words)
cn: 你是否曾无助地盯着一张ZIP磁盘，想着：我怎么才能把文件从这里面弄出来？

en: Without constant migration of digital assets, a nightmare about the foreseeable future is what keeps historians up at night: a historical record that abruptly stops when digital replaces analog. (33 words)
cn: 如果不对数字资产进行持续迁移，一个关于可预见未来的噩梦便会让历史学家夜不能寐：当数字取代模拟时，历史记录戛然而止。

en: As a librarian whose day job revolves around special collections and digital assets, I share the night terrors of historians, and I'd be lying if I said a comprehensive preservation solution currently exists. (36 words)
cn: 作为一名日常工作围绕特色馆藏和数字资产展开的图书馆员，我与历史学家一样夜半惊惧，如果我说目前存在一套全面的保存方案，那就是在说谎。

en: Yet researchers can take some comfort in the fact that there are a multitude of librarians devoted to discovering, organizing, and preserving digital information for researchers of the present and future. (33 words)
cn: 不过，研究人员可以稍感宽慰的是，有大量图书馆员正致力于为当下和未来的研究人员发现、整理和保存数字信息。

en: Librarians are uniquely positioned to understand the ways end users seek and use information. (16 words)
cn: 图书馆员处于独特的位置，能够理解终端用户查找和使用信息的方式。

en: And so, we're indispensable in identifying, preserving and providing access to digital artifacts so that researchers of the future, while they may find it challenging to do their work in a digital realm, won't find it impossible. (42 words)
cn: 因此，我们在识别、保存数字制品和为其提供获取途径方面不可或缺，这样未来的研究人员在数字领域中开展工作时，虽可能感到困难重重，但不会觉得无从下手。

en: For thousands of years, donkeys have been critical for propelling human civilizations forward. (14 words)
cn: 数千年来，驴在推动人类文明向前发展方面一直起着至关重要的作用。

en: They've helped pull wheeled vehicles, carry travelers and move goods across the world. (15 words)
cn: 它们帮助拉动轮式车辆，驮运旅人，并在世界各地运送货物。

en: But where and when these animals first became intertwined with humans has been a mystery. (17 words)
cn: 但这些动物最初是在何时何地与人类交织在一起的，一直是个谜。

en: Now, researchers have used the genomes of over 200 donkeys to trace their domestication back to a single event around 7,000 years ago in East Africa – about 3,000 years before humans tamed horses. (38 words)
cn: 如今，研究人员利用200多头驴的基因组，将其驯化追溯至约7000年前东非的一次单一事件——比人类驯化马早了大约3000年。

en: The team published their findings in the journal Science this month. (12 words)
cn: 该团队本月在《科学》杂志上发表了他们的发现。

en: "Through their DNA, the animals are telling their history themselves," co-author Samantha Brooks, an equine researcher at the University of Florida, says in a statement. (29 words)
cn: "通过这些动物的DNA，它们正在亲自讲述自己的历史，"共同作者、佛罗里达大学马属动物研究员萨曼莎·布鲁克斯在一份声明中说。

en: "We usually only get the human's side of history through written accounts, but of course written history does not always record exactly how something happened. (27 words)
cn: "我们通常只能通过文字记载得到人类那一面的历史，但文字历史当然并不总能准确记录事情的真相。

en: Looking at these DNA sequences, we get a biological testimony to the environment these animals lived in and the experiences they survived." (24 words)
cn: 通过研究这些DNA序列，我们获得了关于这些动物所生活的环境以及它们所经历的一切的生物学证明。"

en: The researchers examined 207 genomes from modern donkeys living in 31 countries across the globe. (18 words)
cn: 研究人员检测了来自全球31个国家现代驴的207个基因组。

en: They also looked at genomes from 15 wild equids and 31 earlier donkeys that lived between about 4,000 and 100 years ago. (23 words)
cn: 他们还检测了15种野生马属动物以及31头生活在约4000年至100年前的早期驴的基因组。

en: The team reconstructed the animals' evolutionary tree and used computer models to pinpoint the domestication event, when herders in Kenya and the Horn of Africa tamed wild asses. (31 words)
cn: 该团队重建了这些动物的进化树，并利用计算机模型精确定位了驯化事件：当时肯尼亚和非洲之角的牧人驯服了野生驴。

en: They then traced how the animals spread across the rest of the continent and into Europe and Asia about 2,500 years later. (22 words)
cn: 接着，他们追踪了这些动物如何在大约2500年后扩散到非洲大陆的其他地区，并进入欧洲和亚洲。

en: Though it's still unclear why the original domestication happened, Science News' Freda Kreier reports that the event coincided with the Sahara growing larger and drier. (30 words)
cn: 尽管最初驯化发生的原因尚不清楚，但《科学新闻》的弗雷达·克赖尔报道称，这一事件恰逢撒哈拉沙漠扩张且变得更加干燥的时期。

en: "Donkeys are champions when it comes to carrying stuff and are good at going through deserts," co-author Ludovic Orlando, an evolutionary biologist at Paul Sabatier University in France, tells the publication. (33 words)
cn: "驴在驮运东西方面是佼佼者，而且擅长穿越沙漠，"共同作者、法国保罗·萨巴捷大学进化生物学家吕多维克·奥兰多告诉该刊物。

en: Prehistoric humans may have enlisted donkeys' help in navigating the expanding Sahara. (14 words)
cn: 史前人类可能曾借助驴的帮助来穿越不断扩张的撒哈拉沙漠。

en: Researchers say these findings could help put donkeys in the spotlight. (12 words)
cn: 研究人员表示，这些发现可能有助于让驴成为公众关注的焦点。

en: The animals could benefit from more research: Currently, there are no published genomes from donkeys located south of the equator in Africa. (25 words)
cn: 这些动物可以从更多的研究中获益：目前，还没有已发表的来自非洲赤道以南驴的基因组。

en: But understanding where the animals were first domesticated could guide archaeologists to a narrower region to search for insights about the original tamed donkeys. (26 words)
cn: 但了解这些动物最初是在哪里被驯化的，可以引导考古学家将搜索范围缩小到更具体的区域，以寻找关于最早被驯化驴的线索。

en: Not only does understanding the equines' genetic makeup reveal their contributions to human history, but it also might improve their management in the future, as climate change alters the planet's environment, write the authors. (36 words)
cn: 作者们写道，了解这些马属动物的基因构成不仅能揭示它们对人类历史的贡献，而且随着气候变化改变地球环境，这也可能有助于未来改善对它们的管理。

en: There's no business like show business — but in Los Angeles, it feels like there's no business at all. (20 words)
cn: 没有什么行业比得上演艺行业——但在洛杉矶，感觉就像根本无业可营。

en: If that sounds melodramatic, consider this: The Art Directors Guild, a labor union representing about 3,000 film workers, has suspended a training program and issued a statement explaining that "we cannot in good conscience encourage you to pursue our profession while so many of our members remain unemployed." (52 words)
cn: 如果这听起来有些夸张，不妨想想这个：代表约3000名电影从业者的工会——艺术总监协会，已经暂停了一个培训项目，并发表声明解释说，"在我们有如此多的会员仍处于失业状态的情况下，我们无法凭良心鼓励你们投身我们这个行业。"

en: The guild's pessimism is a reaction to Hollywood's decline, which is reaching a critical point for the industry and Southern California. (23 words)
cn: 该工会的悲观情绪是对好莱坞衰退的回应，这种衰退对该行业和南加州来说正在达到一个临界点。

en: Production has been slipping away from Hollywood since the 1950s, but the effects have never been more apparent than at present. (25 words)
cn: 自20世纪50年代以来，影片制作一直在从好莱坞流失，但其影响从未像现在这样明显。

en: Other regions in the United States, Canada and Europe have steadily increased incentives to attract TV shows and movies, leaving California in the dust. (27 words)
cn: 美国其他地区、加拿大和欧洲纷纷稳步加大激励力度以吸引电视剧和电影项目，把加利福尼亚州远远甩在了后面。

en: Georgia offers up to 30% in transferable tax credits on film and TV production costs, plus an additional 10% increase on the base tax credit if the project includes a Georgia promotional logo. (37 words)
cn: 佐治亚州对影视制作成本提供高达30%的可转让税收抵免，如果项目包含佐治亚州的宣传标识，还可在此基础上额外增加10%的税收抵免。

en: Even as California lost a huge volume of production to other locations, there was still plenty of film production taking place in Los Angeles before this year. (27 words)
cn: 即便加州将大量制作项目拱手让给了其他地方，在今年之前，洛杉矶仍有大量电影制作在进行。

en: We were kept afloat by "peak TV": the glut of content that was required by the explosion of streaming services. (22 words)
cn: 我们靠"巅峰电视"维持住了局面：流媒体服务的爆炸式增长所带来的海量内容需求。

en: But 2022 was the peak of peak TV. (9 words)
cn: 但2022年是巅峰电视的顶峰之年。

en: Back then platforms such as Netflix, Amazon and Apple TV blew billions of dollars to generate content to attract new subscribers, resulting in 633 scripted series being released that year. (33 words)
cn: 彼时，奈飞、亚马逊和苹果电视等平台投入数十亿美元制作内容以吸引新订户，结果是那一年共有633部有剧本剧集发布。

en: As the streamers' emphasis changed from subscriber growth to profitability, prices for the services went up and the number of new shows went down to 481 released in 2023, with the number expected to dip into the 300s within a few years. (45 words)
cn: 随着流媒体平台的重心从用户增长转向盈利，服务价格上涨，新剧数量在2023年降至481部，预计未来几年还将下滑至300多部。

en: If productions in Southern California dip below a critical level for too long, the industry's essential talent will drift away along with enormous sums of revenue. (28 words)
cn: 如果南加州的影视制作长期低于关键水平，该行业的核心人才将大量流失，随之带走的还有巨额收入。

en: Persuading studios to film here would become much more challenging if we couldn't offer a deep bench of local film workers, on-screen talent and local businesses that support the entertainment industry. (34 words)
cn: 如果我们无法提供一支实力雄厚的本地电影从业者、幕前人才和支持娱乐业的本地企业队伍，说服制片厂来这里拍摄的难度将大大增加。

en: That's why the California Film Commission and its Los Angeles counterpart, FilmLA, should act now, before it's too late. (22 words)
cn: 这就是为什么加州电影委员会及其洛杉矶同行FilmLA应该趁还来得及，立即采取行动。

en: Those agencies and other government bodies should dramatically improve incentives to keep our current shows and attract new productions to Los Angeles. (25 words)
cn: 这些机构和其他政府部门应大幅提高激励力度，以留住我们现有的剧集项目，并吸引新的制作来到洛杉矶。

en: Let's go on with the show … and make sure the show doesn't go on without us. (18 words)
cn: 让我们把这场秀继续下去……并确保这场秀不会在没有我们的情况下继续进行。

en: The pioneers of wireless saw it as a gift to all the people. (13 words)
cn: 无线技术的先驱们将其视为献给全体人民的礼物。

en: Sir John Reith said that it would end "isolation of the spirit" and rejoiced: "It does not matter how many thousands may be listening, there is always enough for others … the genius and the fool, the wealthy and the poor listen simultaneously." (42 words)
cn: 约翰·里思爵士说，它将终结"精神的孤立"，并欣喜地说道："无论有几千人在收听都无关紧要，留给其他人的总还是够的……天才与愚人，富人与穷人，都在同时收听。"

en: Between two great wars this technological innovation built a new kind of national consciousness. (17 words)
cn: 在两次大战之间，这项技术创新构建了一种新的国民意识。

en: Opening this week, a book and exhibition curated by Beaty Rubens at the Bodleian in Oxford records how radio changed everyday life from 1922 to 1939. (28 words)
cn: 本周开幕的、由比蒂·鲁本斯在牛津博德利图书馆策划的一本书籍与展览，记录了从1922年到1939年广播如何改变日常生活。

en: She draws on letters, diaries and fiction, and a 1939 field notebook of verbatim audience research by Winifred Gill. (22 words)
cn: 她利用了书信、日记和小说，以及威妮弗雷德·吉尔在1939年进行的一项逐字记录受众研究的田野笔记。

en: There's fun in testimonies of people enjoying the sheer newness. (11 words)
cn: 人们享受纯粹新鲜感的种种记述中充满了趣味。

en: A cartoon mocks a group failing to converse because they're all in headphones. (15 words)
cn: 一幅漫画讽刺了一群人因为都戴着耳机而无法交谈的场景。

en: People report that broadcast music made workmen whistle new tunes. (11 words)
cn: 有报道称，广播音乐让工人们吹起了新的口哨曲调。

en: A woman says there have been fewer street fights since the arrival of the wireless but also less stopping and "talking on the brush handle". (28 words)
cn: 一位女士说，自无线广播出现以来，街头斗殴减少了，但停下来"倚着扫帚柄聊天"的情形也少了。

en: By and large the wireless was welcome. (7 words)
cn: 总的来说，无线广播是受欢迎的。

en: I loved a man from the Thirties research who found that wireless suddenly offered "the lot of variety … things I thought I'd never be interested in … ice hockey perhaps". (32 words)
cn: 我喜欢三十年代研究中出现的一个男人，他发现无线广播突然带来了"应有尽有的多样性……那些我以为自己永远不会感兴趣的事情……也许是冰球"。

en: True: for more than 80 pre-digital years, linear speech broadcasting brought the gift of serendipity, random enlivenings of a car journey or dull manual task. (28 words)
cn: 确实如此：在数字时代到来前的80多年里，线性语音广播带来了意外发现的馈赠，为一次驾车旅行或枯燥的手工劳动随机注入活力。

en: In my own book about radio I recorded how, on one drive: "I caught up with the news, learnt some 17th-century history, was startlingly educated by an unpretentious programme on the history of the stethoscope." (35 words)
cn: 在我自己关于广播的书中，我记录了一次驾车途中的经历："我跟上了新闻，了解了一些17世纪的历史，被一档朴实无华的关于听诊器历史的节目大大地教育了一番。"

en: But radio's enriching serendipity is ebbing. (7 words)
cn: 但广播那种丰富心灵的意外发现正在消退。

en: With multiple networks and countless podcasts, a smartphone user selects what to hear and when. (17 words)
cn: 有了众多网络和无数的播客，智能手机用户可以自主选择听什么、何时听。

en: And while it is wonderful to take a walk with anything in your headphones, infinite choice encourages us to shrink into niche interests and sympathetic beliefs. (28 words)
cn: 尽管戴着耳机听着任何东西散步是件美妙的事，但无限的选择却促使我们退缩到小众兴趣和同质化的信念之中。

en: A hundred years on from Reith, is the art of mere listening endangered? (14 words)
cn: 距离里思提出其愿景已过去一百年，纯粹的聆听艺术是否正面临危机？

en: Some will say the audiobook boom revives it, though I suppose you can then worry about the decline of reading. (23 words)
cn: 有人会说有声读物的繁荣复兴了聆听艺术，不过我想你接着就可以担心阅读的衰落了。

en: But inventions shape all of us and it is worth noticing when techno-social habits do change, and asking whether to control them a bit or shield the youngest. (30 words)
cn: 但发明塑造着我们每一个人，值得留意的是技术社会习惯何时确实发生了变化，并追问是否该稍加约束，或者保护最年幼的群体。

en: Whatever we do, innovation will happen. (7 words)
cn: 无论我们做什么，创新都会发生。

en: Today we worry about the isolating culture of smartphone-staring and selfie-vanity, but already in 1939 there was that lady regretting how, when all her street got wirelesses, it lost the neighbourly habit of "talking on the brush handle". (40 words)
cn: 今天，我们担忧盯着智能手机和自拍虚荣的孤立文化，但早在1939年，那位女士就在惋惜，当整条街都装上了无线收音机时，邻里间"倚着扫帚柄聊天"的习惯是如何消失的。

en: It's enough to make a person put down the smartphone and go out front with a yard broom. (20 words)
cn: 这足以让人放下智能手机，拿起一把庭院扫帚走出前门。

en: When Tom Swetnam joined the U.S. Forest Service in the 1970s, his mandate was to "put everything out," he recalled. (22 words)
cn: 汤姆·斯韦特南姆回忆说，当他在20世纪70年代加入美国林务局时，他的职责就是"把所有火都扑灭"。

en: But when Swetnam enrolled in graduate school at the University of Arizona's Laboratory of Tree-Ring Research, he was surprised to find a record of repeated blazes dating back hundreds of years before European colonists arrived on the continent. (42 words)
cn: 但当他进入亚利桑那大学树木年轮研究实验室攻读研究生时，他惊讶地发现了一份可以追溯到欧洲殖民者抵达这片大陆之前数百年间反复发生的火灾记录。

en: Some of the trees he analyzed bore more than 20 fire scars spaced among their rings. (17 words)
cn: 他分析的一些树木上，年轮之间分布着超过20处火灾疤痕。

en: The fact that fires happened so often meant they couldn't have been severe enough to kill most trees. (20 words)
cn: 火灾发生得如此频繁这一事实，意味着它们不可能严重到足以杀死大多数树木。

en: Instead, a growing body of research showed that frequent, low-severity fires made many ecosystems healthier. (18 words)
cn: 相反，越来越多的研究表明，频繁的低强度火灾使许多生态系统变得更加健康。

en: They rid the forest of dead and sick trees, reducing competition and curbing the spread of disease. (18 words)
cn: 它们清除了森林中的枯死和病弱树木，减少了竞争，并遏制了疾病的传播。

en: Because flammable material couldn't build up on the landscape, blazes tended to move slowly and peter out when they reached the footprints of previous burns. (28 words)
cn: 由于可燃物无法在地表上大量堆积，火灾往往移动缓慢，并在蔓延到先前燃烧过的地方时逐渐熄灭。

en: In 2022, Swetnam and other scientists teamed up to compile a database of fire-scarred trees from across the continent. (19 words)
cn: 2022年，斯韦特南姆与其他科学家合作，汇编了来自全大陆火灾疤痕树木的数据库。

en: Their North American tree-ring fire-scar network (NAFSN) provided the basis for a study published last month. (18 words)
cn: 他们的北美树木年轮火灾疤痕网络为上个月发表的一项研究提供了基础。

en: In the study, the researchers compared the historical fire cadence with the wildfires recorded over the past few decades, and uncovered a striking shortfall. (28 words)
cn: 在该研究中，研究人员将历史上的火灾节律与过去几十年记录的野火进行了比较，发现了一个惊人的缺口。

en: The NAFSN sites experienced less than a quarter of the number of fires that would have been expected without fire suppression. (23 words)
cn: 在没有灭火措施的情况下原本预期会发生的火灾数量中，这些NAFSN站点实际经历的还不到四分之一。

en: This deficit is a testament to the effectiveness of modern firefighting, said Kelly Martin, a past president of the International Association of Wildland Fire. (27 words)
cn: 这一缺口证明了现代灭火的有效性，国际野外火灾协会前任主席凯莉·马丁说道。

en: Yet the combined consequences of suppression and climate change have eroded humanity's ability to suppress fires, particularly those that ignite under the most dangerous weather conditions. (30 words)
cn: 然而，灭火措施和气候变化的共同后果，已削弱了人类扑灭火灾的能力，尤其是在最危险的天气条件下点燃的火灾。

en: To prevent entire ecosystems from going up in smoke, Martin said, people must bring healthy fire back to places that need it. (25 words)
cn: 马丁说，为防止整个生态系统化为灰烬，人们必须将健康的火带回需要它的地方。

en: At Yosemite National Park, Martin oversaw the use of what are known as prescribed burns to make the landscape more resilient. (23 words)
cn: 在优胜美地国家公园，马丁监督实施了所谓的计划烧除，以使景观更具韧性。

en: These fires were carefully planned and intentionally ignited during periods when weather kept the blazes easy to control, and helped eliminate some of the fuel that had built up around important park facilities. (37 words)
cn: 这些火灾经过精心规划，并在天气使火势易于控制的时期有意点燃，帮助清除了在重要公园设施周围堆积的部分可燃物。

en: Research shows that these prescribed burns make subsequent wildfires less severe, even if the later fires happen under the most dangerous weather conditions. (26 words)
cn: 研究表明，即便后来的火灾发生在最危险的天气条件下，这些计划烧除也能降低后续野火的严重程度。

en: Yet even as scientists and public officials increasingly agree on the need for more fires in our forests, climate change is making this tactic more challenging, experts said. (29 words)
cn: 然而，专家表示，尽管科学家和政府官员越来越认同我们需要在森林中进行更多火烧，但气候变化正在使这一策略变得更具挑战性。

en: "It's a double-edged sword because wildfires are getting more severe and larger under climate change and we need this work even more, but then the work gets more challenging," said Susan Prichard, a fire ecologist at the University of Washington. (43 words)
cn: "这是一把双刃剑，因为在气候变化的影响下，野火正变得更加严重、规模更大，我们比以往更需要这项工作，但这项工作同时也变得更加困难，"华盛顿大学火灾生态学家苏珊·普里查德说。

en: The longest bull run in a century of art-market history ended on a dramatic note with a sale of 56 works by Damien Hirst, Beautiful Inside My Head Forever, at Sotheby’s in London on September 15th, 2008. (37 words)
cn: 2008年9月15日，在伦敦苏富比举行的“我脑海中美丽永存”拍卖会上，随着达米恩赫斯特56件作品售出，艺术品市场一个世纪以来最长的牛市戏剧性的落幕了。

en: All but two pieces sold, fetching more than ￡70m, a record for a sale by a single artist. (19 words)
cn: 除了两件，所有其他作品悉数拍出，拍卖总额超七千万美元；创造了艺术家单场个人拍卖纪录。

en: It was a last victory. (5 words)
cn: 这是最后的成功。

en: As the auctioneer called out bids, in New York one of the oldest banks on Wall Street, Lehman Brothers, filed for bankruptcy. (22 words)
cn: 拍卖师大声喊出拍卖价格，而在纽约，华尔街上一家历史最悠久的银行，雷曼兄弟，申请破产了。

en: The world art market had already been losing momentum for a while after rising bewilderingly since 2003. (17 words)
cn: 自2003年以来，经过急剧攀升之后，世界艺术品市场逐渐失势已经有一段时间了。

en: At its peak in 2007 it was worth some $65 billion, reckons Clare McAndrew, founder of Arts Economics, a research firm—double the figure five years earlier. (27 words)
cn: 克莱尔·麦克安德鲁创办了艺术经济研究院，是一家研究型公司。据他估算，在2007年鼎盛时期，世界艺术品市场价值大约为650亿美元，是五年前的两倍。

en: Since then it may have come down to $50 billion. (10 words)
cn: 从那之后，艺术品市场可能已经降至500亿美元。

en: But the market generates interest far beyond its size because it brings together great wealth, enormous egos, greed, passion and controversy in a way matched by few other industries. (29 words)
cn: 但是，艺术品市场产生的利益远远超过其本身的规模；这里汇集了巨大的财富，人人妄自尊大、贪得无厌、情绪高涨、争论不休，这一点，其它产业少有能与之匹敌的。

en: In the weeks and months that followed Mr. Hirst’s sale, spending of any sort became deeply unfashionable. (17 words)
cn: 在赫斯特作品售出之后的数周甚至数月，投资任何艺术品都非常不合时宜。

en: In the art world that meant collectors stayed away from galleries and salerooms. (13 words)
cn: 在艺术领域，这就意味着收藏家不再光顾画廊和拍卖场。

en: Sales of contemporary art fell by two-thirds, and in the most overheated sector, they were down by nearly 90% in the year to November 2008. (25 words)
cn: 当代艺术品的销售金额下降了三分之二；在最热门的领域，如当代中国艺术，截止到 2008年9月，年销售额下跌近百分之九十。

en: Within weeks the world’s two biggest auction houses, Sotheby’s and Christie’s, had to pay out nearly $200m in guarantees to clients who had placed works for sale with them. (29 words)
cn: 几周之内，世界最大的两家拍卖行，苏富比和佳士得，不得不给作品送拍委托人支付近两亿美元巨额担保费。

en: The current downturn in the art market is the worst since the Japanese stopped buying Impressionists at the end of 1989. (21 words)
cn: 当前低迷的艺术品市场是自1989年年底日本人停止购买印象派作品以来最糟糕的一次。

en: This time experts reckon that prices are about 40% down on their peak on average, though some have been far more fluctuant. (22 words)
cn: 专家估计这次艺术品价格相对其峰值平均下降了百分之四十，尽管有些艺术品的价格有很大的震荡。

en: But Edward Dolman, Christie’s chief executive, says: “I’m pretty confident we’re at the bottom.” (14 words)
cn: 但佳士得首席执行官爱德华·多尔曼说：“我深信，我们正在谷底。”

en: What makes this slump different from the last, he says, is that there are still buyers in the market. (19 words)
cn: 他说，这次萧条与上次不同之处在于，现在市场上仍有买家。

en: Almost everyone who was interviewed for this special report said that the biggest problem at the moment is not a lack of demand but a lack of good work to sell. (31 words)
cn: 几乎每一位接受这个特别报道采访的人都说，当前最大的问题并不是没有需求，而是没有好的作品出售。

en: The three Ds—death, debt and divorce—still deliver works of art to the market. (15 words)
cn: “三大”因素——死亡、债务、离婚——依然会将艺术品推向市场。

en: But anyone who does not have to sell is keeping away, waiting for confidence to return. (16 words)
cn: 但那些不一定非要出售作品的人会一直远离市场，等待信心的回归。

en: I was addressing a small gathering in a suburban Virginia living room—a women’s group that had invited men to join them. (22 words)
cn: 我正在弗吉尼亚郊区的一间会客厅里的小型聚会上讲话——这是一个女性团体的聚会，但也邀请了男士参加。

en: Throughout the evening, one man had been particularly talkative, frequently offering ideas and anecdotes, while his wife sat silently beside him on the couch. (24 words)
cn: 整个晚上，有一位男士相当健谈，时时抛出高见，还穿插着逸闻趣事；而他妻子则在他身旁，安静地坐在沙发上。

en: Toward the end of the evening, I commented that women frequently complain that their husbands don’t talk to them. (19 words)
cn: 聚会快结束时，我评论说，女人常常抱怨丈夫不跟她们说话。

en: This man quickly nodded in agreement. (6 words)
cn: 这位男士连忙点头表示赞同。

en: He gestured toward his wife and said, “She’s the talker in our family.” (13 words)
cn: 他指了指他妻子，说：“她是我们家的话匣子。”

en: The room burst into laughter; the man looked puzzled and hurt. (11 words)
cn: 屋子里哄堂大笑，而他却迷惑不解，感觉受到了伤害一样。

en: “It’s true,” he explained. (4 words)
cn: “这是真的，”他解释说。

en: “When I come home from work I have nothing to say. (11 words)
cn: 我下班回家觉得无话可说。

en: This episode crystallizes the irony that although American men tend to talk more than women in public situations, they often talk less at home. (24 words)
cn: 这段插曲形象地说明了一个具有讽刺意味的现象：在公开场合，美国男人往往比女人更为健谈，但回到家里他们常常话就少了。

en: And this pattern is wreaking havoc with marriage. (8 words)
cn: 这种生活方式对婚姻造成了严重的破坏。

en: The pattern was observed by political scientist Andrew Hacker in the late 1970s. (13 words)
cn: 政治科学家安德鲁•海克在20世纪70年代末就注意了这种生活方式。

en: Sociologist Catherine Kohler Riessman reports in her new book Divorce Talk that most of the women she interviewed—but only a few of the men—gave lack of communication as the reason for their divorces. (35 words)
cn: 社会学家凯瑟琳•科勒•里斯曼在她的新书《离婚访谈录》中指出，她采访的大多数女性都把缺乏交谈看做是离婚的原因，但是只有极少数男性这样认为。

en: Given the current divorce rate of nearly 50 percent, that amounts to millions of cases in the United States every year—a virtual epidemic of failed conversation. (27 words)
cn: 考虑到最近的离婚率达到了近50%，这就意味着在美国每年有总计数百万的离婚案例——可谓是一场沟通不良引发的流行病。

en: In my own research, complaints from women about their husbands most often focused not on tangible inequities such as having given up the chance for a career to accompany a husband to his, or doing far more than their share of daily life-support work like cleaning, cooking, and social arrangements. (50 words)
cn: 据我个人的研究，女性对丈夫不满大多不是集在一些具体不公平的事情上，比如：放弃发展自己事业的机会而陪伴丈夫追求他的事业，或者她们承担了远超自己份内要做的家务，如打扫卫生、做饭和安排社交活动。

en: Instead, they focused on communication: “He doesn’t listen to me.” (10 words)
cn: 相反，她们的不满集中在沟通交流上，比如：“他不听我说话。”

en: “He doesn’t talk to me.” (5 words)
cn: “他不跟我讲话。”

en: I found, as Hacker observed years before, that most wives want their husbands to be, first and foremost, conversational partners, but few husbands share this expectation of their wives. (29 words)
cn: 我发现，大多数妻子首先最期望的是，想让自己的丈夫成为她们能够交流的伴侣；但很少有丈夫对妻子抱有同样的期望，这点海克多年前就已经注意到了。

en: In short, the image that best represents the current crisis is the stereotypical cartoon scene of a man sitting at the breakfast table with a newspaper held up in front of his face, while a woman glares at the back of it, wanting to talk. (45 words)
cn: 简言之，有一幅经典的漫画最能形象地说明目前这场危机：一个男人坐在早餐桌前，埋头看报；一个女人生气地盯着报纸背面，想说话。

en: Over the past decade, many companies had perfected the art of creating automatic behaviors—habits—among consumers. (17 words)
cn: 过去十年来，许多公司刻意培养消费者养成无意识行为，形成习惯；它们已经把这一技巧发展到了完美的地步。

en: These habits have helped companies earn billions of dollars when customers eat snacks or wipe counters almost without thinking, often in response to a carefully designed set of daily cues. (30 words)
cn: 消费者常常会响应那些精心设计好的每日提醒，在他们不假思索地吃点心或擦柜台的时候，这些习惯已经帮助很多公司赚到了数十亿美元。

en: “There are fundamental public health problems, like dirty hands instead of a soap habit, that remain killers only because we can’t figure out how to change people’s habits,” said Dr. Curtis, the director of the Hygiene Center at the London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine. (46 words)
cn: 伦敦卫生与热带医学院卫生中心主任科提斯博士说：“现在仍存在一些基本的公共卫生问题，比如手脏了却没有养成用肥皂洗手的习惯，这些卫生问题俨然成了杀手一样夺命，就仅仅是因为我们还没有弄清如何才能改变人们的习惯”。

en: “We wanted to learn from private industry how to create new behaviors that happen automatically.” (15 words)
cn: 我们想从私营企业那里学习如何培养人们自然而然地就形成新的行为习惯。

en: The companies that Dr. Curtis turned to—Procter & Gamble, Colgate-Palmolive and Unilever—had invested hundreds of millions of dollars finding the subtle cues in consumers’ lives that corporations could use to introduce new routines. (35 words)
cn: 科提斯博士求助的公司——保洁、高露洁和联合利华——已经投入了数亿美元以寻找消费者生活中的各种蛛丝马迹，好让企业利用这些线索来引导人们养成新的生活习惯。

en: If you look hard enough, you’ll find that many of the products we use every day—chewing gums, skin moisturizers, disinfecting wipes, air fresheners, water purifiers, health snacks, teeth whiteners, fabric softeners, vitamins—are results of manufactured habits. (38 words)
cn: 如果观察够仔细，你就会发现我们每一天使用的很多产品，包括口香糖、润肤霜、消毒湿巾、空气清新剂、净水器、健康小吃、牙齿美白剂、衣物柔顺剂和维生素，结果都是制造出来的习惯。

en: A century ago, few people regularly brushed their teeth multiple times a day. (13 words)
cn: 一个世纪之前，很少有人会一天有规律地刷好几次牙。

en: Today, because of shrewd advertising and public health campaigns, many Americans habitually give their pearly whites a cavity-preventing scrub twice a day, often with Colgate, Crest or one of the other brands. (32 words)
cn: 而现在，由于精明的广告宣传加上公共卫生运动，很多美国人为了预防龋齿，已经习惯每天刷两次他们本来就珍珠般洁白的牙齿。他们使用的，常常是高露洁、佳洁士或者其他某种品牌。

en: A few decades ago, many people didn’t drink water outside of a meal. (13 words)
cn: 几十年前，很多人不会在就餐以外的时间喝水。

en: Then beverage companies started bottling the production of far-off springs, and now office workers unthinkingly sip bottled water all day long. (21 words)
cn: 后来，饮料公司开始把远方的山泉水装入瓶中；现在，办公室的职员会整天不假思索地一小口一小口喝着瓶装水。

en: Chewing gum, once bought primarily by adolescent boys, is now featured in commercials as a breath freshener and teeth cleanser for use after a meal. (25 words)
cn: 口香糖，曾经主要是处于青春期的男孩会购买；而现在，商业广告却将它定位为饭后使用的口气清新剂和牙齿清洁剂。

en: Skin moisturizers are advertised as part of morning beauty rituals, slipped in between hair brushing and putting on makeup. (19 words)
cn: 广告宣传把润肤霜说成是早上化妆的必须步骤，于是梳头之后，化妆之前，人们就会不由自主的开始使用润肤霜。

en: “Our products succeed when they become part of daily or weekly patterns,” said Carol Berning, a consumer psychologist who recently retired from Procter & Gamble, the company that sold $76 billion of Tide, Crest and other products last year. (39 words)
cn: 最近刚从宝洁公司退休下来的消费心理学家卡罗尔·柏宁“如果能成为人们每周或者每天生活习惯的一部分，那我们的产品就成功了”。去年，宝洁公司的汰渍、佳洁士和其他品牌的产品销售高达760亿美元。

en: “Creating positive habits is a huge part of improving our consumers’ lives, and it’s essential to making new products commercially viable.” (21 words)
cn: 培养良好的生活习惯是改善消费者生活的一个重要部分，它也对于新产品

en: Through experiments and observation, social scientists like Dr. Berning have learned that there is power in tying certain behaviors to habitual cues through ruthless advertising. (25 words)
cn: 通过实验和观察，柏宁博士等社会学家发现，通过大量无休止的广告，提示人们养成某些习惯，进而变成特定行为，这样做是有效的。

en: As this new science of habit has emerged, controversies have erupted when the tactics have been used to sell questionable beauty creams or unhealthy foods. (25 words)
cn: 随着这一新兴的习惯科学的兴起，有人把这些策略用来销售有问题的美容霜和不健康食品，从而爆发了广泛的争议。

en: Many Americans regard the jury system as a concrete expression of crucial democratic values, including the principles that all citizens who meet minimal qualifications of age and literacy are equally competent to serve on juries; that jurors should be selected randomly from a representative cross section of the community; that no citizen should be denied the right to serve on a jury on account of race, religion, sex, or national origin; that defendants are entitled to trial by their peers; and that verdicts should represent the conscience of the community and not just the letter of the law. (98 words)
cn: 许多美国人把陪审团制度看作是其基本民主价值观的具体体现，该制度包括以下原则：所有符合最低年龄限制和文化程度要求的公民都享有担任陪审员的平等权力；陪审员应该从代表社会各个阶层的人群中随机挑选；不得以种族、宗教、性别或民族等原因剥夺任何公民担任陪审员的权力；被告有权接受同龄人的审判；判决体现的应该是社会良知，而不仅仅是法律条文。

en: The jury is also said to be the best surviving example of direct rather than representative democracy. (17 words)
cn: 据说，陪审团制度是现存体现直接民主制度而不是代表民主制度的典范。

en: In a direct democracy, citizens take turns governing themselves, rather than electing representatives to govern for them. (17 words)
cn: 在直接民主制度中，公民可以轮流自治，而不是选举代表来替自己管理。

en: But as recently as in 1968, jury selection procedures conflicted with these democratic ideals. (14 words)
cn: 但就在最近的1968年，陪审团遴选过程仍然与这种民主理想发生了冲突。

en: In some states, for example, jury duty was limited to persons of supposedly superior intelligence, education, and moral character. (19 words)
cn: 比如，在有些州，担任陪审的职责仅仅限于那些人们认为智商更高、教育程度更高、道德品质更高的人。

en: Although the Supreme Court of the United States had prohibited intentional racial discrimination in jury selection as early as the 1880 case of Strauder v. West Virginia, the practice of selecting so-called elite or blue-ribbon juries provided a convenient way around this and other antidiscrimination laws. (46 words)
cn: 尽管美国高等法院早在1880年斯特劳德诉西弗吉尼亚州一案中就已经禁止陪审团遴选过程中出现任何蓄意的种族歧视，但是那种选拔所谓的精英或蓝带陪审团的做法非常容易地绕过了这一反歧视法及其他反歧视法案。

en: The system also failed to regularly include women on juries until the mid-20th century. (14 words)
cn: 直到20世纪中叶，该体制也未能定期吸纳女性加入陪审团中。

en: Although women first served on state juries in Utah in 1898, it was not until the 1940s that a majority of states made women eligible for jury duty. (28 words)
cn: 虽然早在1898年就有女性首次担任犹他州的陪审员，但直到20世纪4年代，大多数州才赋予女性担任陪审员的资格。

en: Even then several states automatically exempted women from jury duty unless they personally asked to have their names included on the jury list. (23 words)
cn: 即使是在那个时候还有几个州，如果女性不自己要求加入陪审团，他们会顺理成章的把女性排除在陪审团行列之外。

en: This practice was justified by the claim that women were needed at home, and it kept juries unrepresentative of women through the 1960s. (23 words)
cn: 他们这么做的理由是家庭需要女性，这使得整个60年代陪审团一直都没有女性代表。

en: In 1968, the Congress of the United States passed the Jury Selection and Service Act, ushering in a new era of democratic reforms for the jury. (26 words)
cn: 1968年，美国国会通过了《陪审团选任及任职法》，开创了陪审团制度民主改革的新时代。

en: This law abolished special educational requirements for federal jurors and required them to be selected at random from a cross section of the entire community. (25 words)
cn: 该法案废除了对联邦陪审员应具备特殊教育背景的要求，并规定陪审员选应该从社会各阶层中随机选出。

en: In the landmark 1975 decision Taylor v. Louisiana, the Supreme Court extended the requirement that juries be representative of all parts of the community to the state level. (28 words)
cn: 根据1975年泰勒诉路易斯安那州一案中具有里程碑意义的裁定，高等法院进一步要求各州也执行陪审团成员需代表社会各阶层这一规

en: The Taylor decision also declared sex discrimination in jury selection to be unconstitutional and ordered states to use the same procedures for selecting male and female jurors. (27 words)
cn: 泰勒案件的裁决也宣布陪审员遴选过程中的性别歧视违反宪法，并命令各州按照同样的程序遴选男女陪审员。

en: Ruth Simmons joined Goldman Sachs’s board as an outside director in January 2000; a year later she became president of Brown University. (22 words)
cn: 2000年1月，露丝西蒙斯加入了高盛公司的董事会，成为其外部董事；一年之后，她又成为布朗大学校长。

en: For the rest of the decade she apparently managed both roles without attracting much criticism. (15 words)
cn: 在随后的九年时间里，她似乎能兼顾两个角色，并且没有招致太多非议。

en: But by the end of 2009 Ms. Simmons was under fire for having sat on Goldman’s compensation committee; how could she have let those enormous bonus payouts pass unremarked? (29 words)
cn: 不过，到了2009年底，西蒙斯女士却因在高盛薪酬委员会的任职而受到抨击；她怎么能没有注意到那些巨额奖金的发放呢？

en: By February the next year Ms. Simmons had left the board. (11 words)
cn: 次年2月，西蒙斯离开了董事会。

en: The position was just taking up too much time, she said. (11 words)
cn: 她称只是因为这个职位太占时间。

en: Outside directors are supposed to serve as helpful, yet less biased, advisers on a firm’s board. (16 words)
cn: 外部董事应该是公司董事会中能提供帮助、却较少持有偏见的顾问。

en: Having made their wealth and their reputations elsewhere, they presumably have enough independence to disagree with the chief executive’s proposals. (20 words)
cn: 由于他们在别的地方或者了财富和名声，照理应该足够独立，可以反对首席执行官的提议。

en: If the sky, and the share price, is falling, outside directors should be able to give advice based on having weathered their own crises. (24 words)
cn: 如果公司面临灭顶之灾，股价下跌，外部董事应该能够根据自己渡过危机的经验，为公司出谋划策。

en: The researchers from Ohio University used a database that covered more than 10,000 firms and more than 64,000 different directors between 1989 and 2004. (24 words)
cn: 俄亥俄大学的研究人员利用一个数据库进行研究，该数据库涵盖了1989年到2004年间10000家公司和64000多名董事的信息。

en: Then they simply checked which directors stayed from one proxy statement to the next. (14 words)
cn: 然后，他们只需要核查一年的股东委托书和下年的委托书上，有哪些董事会一直留任。

en: The most likely reason for departing a board was age, so the researchers concentrated on those “surprise” disappearances by directors under the age of 70. (25 words)
cn: 离开董事会最大的理由可能性是年龄，因此研究人员重点关注了那些70岁以下、“突然”离职的董事。

en: They found that after a surprise departure, the probability that the company will subsequently have to restate earnings increases by nearly 20%. (22 words)
cn: 他们发现，董事突然离任后，公司随后必须重申盈利的可能性增加了近百分之二十。

en: The likelihood of being named in a federal class-action lawsuit also increases, and the stock is likely to perform worse. (20 words)
cn: 公司卷入联邦集体诉讼案的可能性也会增加，股票也可能表现更糟。

en: The effect tended to be larger for larger firms. (9 words)
cn: 公司越大，受到的影响越大。

en: Although a correlation between them leaving and subsequent bad performance at the firm is suggestive, it does not mean that such directors are always jumping off a sinking ship. (29 words)
cn: 尽管外部董事离职与公司后来表现糟糕这两者之间有一定的联系，但这并不意味着这些董事总是会跳离沉船。

en: Often they “trade up,” leaving riskier, smaller firms for larger and more stable firms. (14 words)
cn: 通常，他们会“另谋高就”，离开风险较大、规模较小的公司，去规模更大、更稳定的公司。

en: But the researchers believe that outside directors have an easier time of avoiding a blow to their reputations if they leave a firm before bad news break, even if a review of history shows they were on the board at the time any wrongdoing occurred. (45 words)
cn: 但研究者相信，如果外部董事在坏消息出现之前离开公司，他们会更容易避免自己声誉受损，即便后来回顾历史发现，在他们任职期间，公司出现过一些过错，这也不会对他们造成太坏的影响。

en: Firms who want to keep their outside directors through tough times may have to create incentives. (16 words)
cn: 如果想要外部董事帮助公司度过困难时期，也许得建立激励制度。

en: Otherwise outside directors will follow the example of Ms. Simmons, once again very popular on campus. (16 words)
cn: 否则，外部董事将会效仿西蒙斯女士，重返校园，仍受欢迎。

en: Whatever happened to the death of newspaper? (7 words)
cn: 报业要消亡，到底遇到了什么事情

en: A year ago the end seemed near. (7 words)
cn: 一年前，报纸的末日似乎就要来临。

en: The recession threatened to remove the advertising and readers that had not already fled to the Internet. (17 words)
cn: 经济衰退也会给报业带来威胁，让那些还没有投入到网络的广告和读者跑得一干二净。

en: Newspapers like the San Francisco Chronicle were chronicling their own doom. (11 words)
cn: 像《旧金山纪事报》之类的报纸当时就在记录自己的灭亡过程。

en: America’s Federal Trade Commission launched a round of talks about how to save newspapers. (14 words)
cn: 美国的联邦贸易委员发起了一轮会谈，商量如何拯救报业。

en: Should they become charitable corporations? (5 words)
cn: 报社是否应该转型为公益企业？

en: Should the state subsidize them? (5 words)
cn: 州政府是否应该给予其补贴？

en: It will hold another meeting soon. (6 words)
cn: 委员会马上就要召开下一轮会议

en: But the discussions now seem out of date. (8 words)
cn: 但现在这种讨论似乎已经不合时宜了。

en: In much of the world there is little sign of crisis. (11 words)
cn: 放眼全球，危机的征兆不明显。

en: German and Brazilian papers have shrugged off the recession. (9 words)
cn: 德国和巴西的报纸行业对危机不屑一顾。

en: Even American newspapers, which inhabit the most troubled corner of the global industry, have not only survived but often returned to profit. (22 words)
cn: 即使是全球行业中处境最艰难的美国报纸行业也挺了过来，并且基本恢复盈利。

en: Not the 20% profit margins that were routine a few years ago, but profit all the same. (17 words)
cn: 虽然不像几年前，通常都有20%的利润空间，但毕竟是有钱可赚了。

en: It has not been much fun. (6 words)
cn: 但并没有太多值得开心的事。

en: Many papers stayed afloat by pushing journalists overboard. (8 words)
cn: 多家报社得辞退记者来渡过难关。

en: The American Society of News Editors reckons that 13,500 newsroom jobs have gone since 2007. (15 words)
cn: 美国新闻编辑协会估计，2007年以来，削减了13500个新闻编辑室岗位。

en: Readers are paying more for slimmer products. (7 words)
cn: 读者付费更多，产品却在缩水。

en: Some papers even had the nerve to refuse delivery to distant suburbs. (12 words)
cn: 一些报社甚至明目张胆拒绝向偏远郊区递送报纸。

en: Yet these desperate measures have proved the right ones and, sadly for many journalists, they can be pushed further. (19 words)
cn: 不过这些孤注一掷的措施已经证明是有效的；但不幸的是，那些记者，他们可能会被推得更远。

en: Newspapers are becoming more balanced businesses, with a healthier mix of revenues from readers and advertisers. (16 words)
cn: 随着来自读者和广告商的收益比例更加健康合理，报业发展日趋平衡。

en: American papers have long been highly unusual in their reliance on ads. (12 words)
cn: 长期以来，美国报业畸形发展，过度依赖广告收益。

en: Fully 87% of their revenues came from advertising in 2008, according to the Organization for Economic Cooperation & Development (OECD). (20 words)
cn: 经济合作与发展组织称，2008年美国报业至少87%的利润来自广告。

en: In Japan the proportion is 35%. (6 words)
cn: 而日本的比例为35%。

en: Not surprisingly, Japanese newspapers are much more stable. (8 words)
cn: 理所应当，日本报业要稳定很多。

en: The whirlwind that swept through newsrooms harmed everybody, but much of the damage has been concentrated in areas where newspapers are least distinctive. (23 words)
cn: 这场旋风席卷编辑部，无人幸免；但是大部分损失集中在那些没有特色的报纸上。

en: Car and film reviewers have gone. (6 words)
cn: 汽车和电影评论员已经走了。

en: So have science and general business reporters. (7 words)
cn: 科学和大众商业记者也走了。

en: Foreign bureaus have been savagely cut off. (7 words)
cn: 驻外办事处被无情撤销。

en: Newspapers are less complete as a result. (7 words)
cn: 报纸不再包罗万象。

en: But completeness is no longer a virtue in the newspaper business. (11 words)
cn: 毕竟包罗万象不再是报纸行业的优势了。

en: We tend to think of the decades immediately following World War II as a time of prosperity and growth, with soldiers returning home by the millions, going off to college on the G.I. Bill and lining up at the marriage bureaus. (41 words)
cn: 我们往往会把二战刚结束之后的几十年看做是繁荣和成长时期：数百万计的士兵返回家园，按照军人安置法上了大学，登记结婚。

en: But when it came to their houses, it was a time of common sense and a belief that less could truly be more. (23 words)
cn: 但说到房子问题，少真的可以变多，是那个时代人们秉持的常识和信念。

en: During the Depression and the war, Americans had learned to live with less, and that restraint, in combination with the postwar confidence in the future, made small, efficient housing positively stylish. (31 words)
cn: 在大萧条和战争时期，美国人早就学会了生活节俭，这种克制，以及战后对未来生活的自信，使得小而高效利用空间的房子成为绝对的时尚。

en: Economic condition was only a stimulus for the trend toward efficient living. (12 words)
cn: 经济状况只是一个刺激因素，推动这种高效生活潮流的发展。

en: The phrase “less is more” was actually first popularized by a German, the architect Ludwig Mies van der Rohe, who like other people associated with the Bauhaus, a school of design, emigrated to the United States before World War II and took up posts at American architecture schools. (48 words)
cn: “少即是多”这种说法实际上首先是由一位德国建筑师推广开来的，他名叫路德维希·密斯·范德罗。像其他与包豪斯设计学院的追随者一样，他在二战前移民到美国，并曾在美国多个建筑学院任职。

en: These designers came to exert enormous influence on the course of American architecture, but none more so than Mies. (19 words)
cn: 这些设计师的到来，对美国的建筑业的发展产生了巨大的影响，但密斯的影响力却无人能及。

en: Mies’s signature phrase means that less decoration, properly organized, has more impact than a lot. (15 words)
cn: 密斯的口头禅的意思是，简约的装饰，安排得当，会比复杂更有冲击力。

en: Elegance, he believed, did not derive from abundance. (8 words)
cn: 他认为，优雅并非源于丰富。

en: Like other modern architects, he employed metal, glass and laminated wood—materials that we take for granted today but that in the 1940s symbolized the future. (26 words)
cn: 和其它现代建筑师一样，他当时使用了金属、玻璃和复合板等材料——这些材料在今天看来是习以为常的，但在20世纪40年代却是未来的象征。

en: Mies’s sophisticated presentation masked the fact that the spaces he designed were small and efficient, rather than big and often empty. (21 words)
cn: 密斯精心布置的展示掩盖了一个事实：他所设计的空间小而紧凑，而不是大且空旷。

en: The apartments in the elegant towers Mies built on Chicago’s Lake Shore Drive, for example, were smaller—two-bedroom units under 1,000 square feet—than those in their older neighbors along the city’s Gold Coast. (34 words)
cn: 例如，在芝加哥湖滨大道上密斯设计的优雅的塔楼公寓——不足1000平方英尺的两居室——比黄金海岸边上附近的旧公寓还要更小。

en: But they were popular because of their airy glass walls, the views they afforded and the elegance of the buildings’ details and proportions, the architectural equivalent of the abstract art so popular at the time. (35 words)
cn: 但这些公寓很受欢迎，因其通透的玻璃墙，优美的景致，以及建筑优雅的细节，和谐的比例，是当时广受欢迎的抽象艺术在建筑上的体现。

en: The trend toward “less” was not entirely foreign. (8 words)
cn: 这种“尚简”的趋势并不完全是外来的。

en: In the 1930s Frank Lloyd Wright started building more modest and efficient houses—usually around 1,200 square feet—than the spreading two-story ones he had designed in the 1890s and the early 20th century. (34 words)
cn: 20世纪30年代，弗兰克·劳埃德·赖特就开始建造更为朴实有用的房子，通常约1200平尺；这不像他早在19世纪90年代和20世纪初设计的宽敞开阔的两层建筑。

en: The “Case Study Houses” commissioned from talented modern architects by California Arts & Architecture magazine between 1945 and 1962 were yet another homegrown influence on the “less is more” trend. (30 words)
cn: 1945年到1962年间，加州《艺术与建筑》杂志委托一些杰出的现代建筑师，建造了“案例研究住宅”，这成了另一股美国本土的力量，推动“少即是多”建筑趋势的发展。

en: Aesthetic effect came from the landscape, new materials and forthright detailing. (11 words)
cn: 美学影响来自房子的景观，新材料和直观明快的细节。

en: In his Case Study House, Ralph Rapson may have mispredicted just how the mechanical revolution would impact everyday life—few American families acquired helicopters, though most eventually got clothes dryers—but his belief that self-sufficiency was both desirable and inevitable was widely shared. (43 words)
cn: 在罗夫拉普森的案例研究住宅中，他可能错误预测机械革命对日常生活的影响——极少美国家庭拥有直升机，尽管大多数家庭有衣服烘干机——但他认为自给自足的生活方式是人们的理想，势不可挡，他的这一信念得到了广泛认可。

en: Will the European Union make it? (6 words)
cn: 欧盟还能行吗？

en: The question would have sounded strange not long ago. (9 words)
cn: 要是在不久前，这个问题听来还会让人觉得奇怪。

en: Now even the project’s greatest cheerleaders talk of a continent facing a “Bermuda triangle” of debt, population decline and lower growth. (21 words)
cn: 如今，即使该项目最大的支持者也在谈论欧洲面临的百慕大三角问题：债台高筑、人口下降和经济增长放缓。

en: As well as those chronic problems, the EU faces an acute crisis in its economic core, the 16 countries that use the single currency. (24 words)
cn: 除了那些长期存在的问题外，欧盟的经济核心区，即使用单一货币的16个成员国，还面临一场亟待解决的严重危机。

en: Markets have lost faith that the euro zone’s economies, weaker or stronger, will one day converge thanks to the discipline of sharing a single currency, which denies uncompetitive members the quick fix of devaluation. (34 words)
cn: 人们曾相信，欧元区经济体，无论国家强弱，只要本着使用单一货币的原则就能有朝一日实现统一，单一货币能避免了那些缺乏竞争力的国家采取货币贬值这一权宜之计；如今市场已经丧失了这种信念。

en: Yet the debate about how to save Europe’s single currency from disintegration is stuck. (14 words)
cn: 然而，就如何挽救欧洲单一货币免于瓦解的讨论却陷入了僵局。

en: It is stuck because the euro zone’s dominant powers, France and Germany, agree on the need for greater harmonization within the euro zone, but disagree about what to harmonies. (29 words)
cn: 陷入僵局原因在于，欧元区的两大主导国家，法国和德国在欧元区内需要加强统一的观点上一致，但在统一内容上却存在分歧。

en: Germany thinks the euro must be saved by stricter rules on borrowing, spending and competitiveness, backed by quasi-automatic sanctions for governments that do not obey. (25 words)
cn: 德国认为，若想拯救欧元，就必须在借贷消费和竞争力方面实行更加严格的规定，同时还要对违规的政府进行某种程度的自动制裁。

en: These might include threats to freeze EU funds for poorer regions and EU mega-projects and even the suspension of a country’s voting rights in EU ministerial councils. (27 words)
cn: 这些制裁包括，可能会冻结欧盟对贫困地区和重大项目的投资，甚至还包括暂停一个国家在欧盟部长级会议上的投票权。

en: It insists that economic coordination should involve all 27 members of the EU club, among whom there is a small majority for free-market liberalism and economic rigour; in the inner core alone, Germany fears, a small majority favour French interference. (40 words)
cn: 德国坚持认为经济合作应该包括所有27个欧盟成员国，其中支持市场自由主义和从严经济政策的国家占微弱多数；而仅仅在欧盟核心成员国内部，德国担心，会有微弱多数的国家支持法国干预。

en: A “southern” camp headed by France wants something different: “European economic government” within an inner core of euro-zone members. (19 words)
cn: 以法国为首的“南部”阵营则另有所求：在欧元区的内部核心成员国中建立一个“欧洲经济政府”。

en: Translated, that means politicians intervening in monetary policy and a system of redistribution from richer to poorer members, via cheaper borrowing for governments through common Eurobonds or complete fiscal transfers. (30 words)
cn: 换句话说，这意味着政治家会通过欧洲共同债券和完整的财政转移来实现政府的低息借贷，从而干预货币政策，也会干预从富裕到贫穷的成员之间的收入再分配体系。

en: Finally, figures close to the France government have murmured, euro-zone members should agree to some fiscal and social harmonization: e.g., curbing competition in corporate-tax rates or labour costs. (28 words)
cn: 最后，那些亲法国政府联系的大人物私下抱怨说，欧元区成员国应该同意就财政和社会协调方案达成一致，例如抑制企业税率和劳工成本的竞争。

en: It is too soon to write off the EU. (9 words)
cn: 但解散欧盟为时尚早。

en: It remains the world’s largest trading block. (7 words)
cn: 它仍然是世界上最大的贸易组织。

en: At its best, the European project is remarkably liberal: built around a single market of 27 rich and poor countries, its internal borders are far more open to goods, capital and labour than any comparable trading area. (37 words)
cn: 在其最佳状态下，这一欧洲大工程相当开放：建立在27个贫富不同的国家组成的单一市场之上，其内部国界间商品、资本和劳动力自由流动，其开放程度超过任何类似的贸易区。

en: It is an ambitious attempt to blunt the sharpest edges of globalization, and make capitalism benign. (16 words)
cn: 欧盟工程是一次雄心壮举，它试图磨钝全球化锋利的刀刃，使资本主义发展良好。

en: Homework has never been terribly popular with students and even many parents, but in recent years it has been particularly scorned. (21 words)
cn: 家庭作业从来就不太受学生欢迎，甚至许多家长也是，但近年来却格外饱受诟病。

en: School districts across the country, most recently Los Angeles Unified, are revising their thinking on this educational ritual. (18 words)
cn: 全国的学区，如最近的洛杉矶联合学区，都在改变思路，对家庭作业这一教育惯例做出调整。

en: Unfortunately, L.A. Unified has produced an inflexible policy which mandates that with the exception of some advanced courses, homework may no longer count for more than 10% of a student’s academic grade. (32 words)
cn: 不幸的是，洛杉矶联合学区出台了一项硬性规定，规定要求：除了几门高级课程之外，家庭作业在学生成绩中所占比例不得超过10%。

en: This rule is meant to address the difficulty that students from impoverished or chaotic homes might have in completing their homework. (21 words)
cn: 此规定旨在解决贫困家庭或问题家庭的学生完成家庭作业方面的困难。

en: But the policy is unclear and contradictory. (7 words)
cn: 但该规定内容不明且自相矛盾。

en: Certainly, no homework should be assigned that students cannot complete on their own or that they cannot do without expensive equipment. (21 words)
cn: 当然，不应该给学生布置他们无法独立完成的家庭作业，也不应该给学生布置他们需要昂贵设备才能完成的家庭作业。

en: But if the district is essentially giving a pass to students who do not do their homework because of complicated family lives, it is going riskily close to the implication that standards need to be lowered for poor children. (39 words)
cn: 但如果该学区本质上是想要给那些因家庭环境复杂而不做作业的学生开绿灯，那么就会冒着风险，几乎是在暗示：对于贫困学生，标准要降低。

en: District administrators say that homework will still be a part of schooling; teachers are allowed to assign as much of it as they want. (24 words)
cn: 学区管理者表示，家庭作业仍将是学校教育的一部分：老师想布置多少家庭作业就布置多少。

en: But with homework counting for no more than 10% of their grades, students can easily skip half their homework and see very little difference on their report cards. (28 words)
cn: 但如果家庭作业在学生成绩中所占比例不到10%的话，学生就能轻易忽略一半的作业，而成绩报告单上却几乎看不出差别。

en: Some students might do well on state tests without completing their homework, but what about the students who performed well on the tests and did their homework? (27 words)
cn: 有些学生可能不做完家庭作业也会在州考中表现良好；但是那些做了家庭作业才考得好的学生呢，该怎么解释？

en: It is quite possible that the homework helped. (8 words)
cn: 家庭作业完全有可能帮助了他们。

en: Yet rather than empowering teachers to find what works best for their students, the policy imposes a flat, across-the-board rule. (20 words)
cn: 然而该政策不是授权教师去发现最适合学生的家庭作业方式，而是强制颁布了一项呆板的、一刀切的规定。

en: At the same time, the policy addresses none of the truly thorny questions about homework. (15 words)
cn: 同时，有关家庭作业方面真正棘手的问题，该政策一点都没有解决。

en: If the district finds homework to be unimportant to its students’ academic achievement, it should move to reduce or eliminate the assignments, not make them count for almost nothing. (29 words)
cn: 如果该学区发现家庭作业对于孩子的学业不重要，那就应该减少或者免除家庭作业，而不应该让家庭作业占一个无足轻重的比例。

en: Conversely, if homework matters, it should account for a significant portion of the grade. (14 words)
cn: 相反，如果家庭作业重要，那它就应该在学生成绩中占一个重要的比例。

en: Meanwhile, this policy does nothing to ensure that the homework students receive is meaningful or appropriate to their age and the subject, or that teachers are not assigning more than they are willing to review and correct. (37 words)
cn: 同时，要确保学生要做的家庭作业有意义，且适合其年龄和课程；还要确保教师会布置的作业不多，他们自己愿意检查批改；对于上述两点，该政策毫无建树。

en: The homework rules should be put on hold while the school board, which is responsible for setting educational policy, looks into the matter and conducts public hearings. (27 words)
cn: 有关家庭作业的规定应该暂缓，而负责制定教育政策的学校董事会应该深入调查此事，并举行公众听证会。

en: It is not too late for L.A. Unified to do homework right. (12 words)
cn: 洛杉矶联合学区要做好家庭作业还为时未晚。

en: Pretty in pink: adult women do not remember being so obsessed with the colour, yet it is pervasive in our young girls’ lives. (23 words)
cn: 穿粉色好看：成年女性已经记不起自己曾如此迷恋这个颜色；然而年轻女孩的生命却处处是粉色。

en: It is not that pink is intrinsically bad, but it is such a tiny slice of the rainbow and, though it may celebrate girlhood in one way, it also repeatedly and firmly fuses girls’ identity to appearance. (37 words)
cn: 不是说粉红色本质上不好，它只不过是彩虹上微小的一抹，尽管从某个程度上来说它歌颂少女情怀，但它也会反复不断地把少女特征清楚地表现到外表上。

en: Then it presents that connection, even among two-year-olds, between girls as not only innocent but as evidence of innocence. (19 words)
cn: 此外，粉色还表现出女孩之间，甚至两岁女孩之间都具有的那种特征：单纯，粉色还能证明自己单纯。

en: Looking around, I despaired at the singular lack of imagination about girls’ lives and interests. (15 words)
cn: 四周看看，我很失望地看到，人们对女孩生活和兴趣的想象是如此的贫乏。

en: Girls’ attraction to pink may seem unavoidable, somehow encoded in their DNA, but according to Jo Paoletti, an associate professor of American Studies, it is not. (26 words)
cn: 女孩可能会不可避免地受到粉色的吸引，似乎以某种方式编码进入了他们的基因一样；但是，根据美国研究学院的副教授乔帕雷提的观点，情况并非如此。

en: Children were not colour-coded at all until the early 20th century: in the era before domestic washing machines all babies wore white as a practical matter, since the only way of getting clothes clean was to boil them. (38 words)
cn: 在20世纪初期以前，儿童的基因里根本没有颜色编码：在家用洗衣机问世之前，出于实用，所有婴儿都穿白色，因为衣服只能靠煮才能弄干净。

en: What’s more, both boys and girls wore what were thought of as gender-neutral dresses. (14 words)
cn: 而且，那时的男孩女孩都穿中性的衣服。

en: When nursery colours were introduced, pink was actually considered the more masculine colour, a pastel version of red, which was associated with strength. (23 words)
cn: 当育婴颜色引入的时候，人们认为粉色是更有男性特征的颜色，是一种柔和的红颜色，代表着力量。

en: Blue, with its intimations of the Virgin Mary, constancy and faithfulness, symbolised femininity. (13 words)
cn: 蓝色象征着，令人联想到圣母玛利亚，代表着坚贞与忠诚。

en: It was not until the mid-1980s, when amplifying age and sex differences became a dominant children’s marketing strategy, that pink fully came into its own, when it began to seem inherently attractive to girls, part of what defined them as female, at least for the first few critical years. (49 words)
cn: 20世纪80年代中期，放大年龄和性别差异成为儿童用品市场的主要营销策略，粉色似乎天然吸引了女孩子的喜欢；到此时，它才完全成为定义女性特征的一种颜色，至少在起初的那几个关键的几年是如此。

en: I had not realised how profoundly marketing trends dictated our perception of what is natural to kids, including our core beliefs about their psychological development. (25 words)
cn: 我之前一直没有意识到，市场营销趋势会深刻地影响我们对孩子天性的看法，包括我们对儿童心理发展的核心信念。

en: Take the toddler. (3 words)
cn: 以学步时期儿童为例。

en: I assumed that phase was something experts developed after years of research into children’s behaviour: wrong. (16 words)
cn: 我曾以为这个阶段是专家经过数年对儿童行为研究提出的概念：错。

en: Turns out, according to Daniel Cook, a historian of childhood consumerism, it was popularised as a marketing trick by clothing manufacturers in the 1930s. (24 words)
cn: 丹尼尔·库克是一位研究儿童消费主义的历史学家，根据他的观点，这其实是20世纪30年代服装制造商的一种营销手段，从而普及开来。

en: Trade publications counselled department stores that, in order to increase sales, they should create a “third stepping stone” between infant wear and older kids’ clothes. (25 words)
cn: 贸易出版物给百货商店出谋划策：要增加销量，就应该在婴儿服装和年龄稍大点孩子的服装之间创建“第三跳板”。

en: It was only after “toddler” became a common shoppers’ term that it evolved into a broadly accepted developmental stage. (19 words)
cn: 只有在“学步儿童”成为顾客常用的说法之后，它才逐渐成为人们广泛接受的儿童发展阶段。

en: Splitting kids, or adults, into ever-tinier categories has proved a sure-fire way to boost profits. (15 words)
cn: 把儿童或者成人群体不断细分为更小的群体，的确是增加收益万无一失的手段。

en: And one of the easiest ways to segment a market is to magnify gender differences—or invent them where they did not previously exist. (24 words)
cn: 细分市场最容易的一种方法就是放大性别差异——或者创造出一些之前不存在的差异。

en: In 2010, a federal judge shook America’s biotech industry to its core. (12 words)
cn: 2010年，一位联邦法官彻底震撼了美国生物技术行业。

en: Companies had won patents for isolated DNA for decades—by 2005 some 20% of human genes were patented. (18 words)
cn: 几十年来，各个公司一直都享有分离DNA（脱氧核糖核酸）的专利，到2005年，已有大约 20%的人类基因注册了专利。

en: But in March 2010 a judge ruled that genes were unpatentable. (11 words)
cn: 但2012年3月，有一位法官裁决：基因不能申请专利。

en: Executives were violently agitated. (4 words)
cn: 公司的管理者对此感到非常不安。

en: The Biotechnology Industry Organisation (BIO), a trade group, assured members that this was just a “preliminary step” in a longer battle. (21 words)
cn: 生物技术工业组织（BIO）是一家贸易团体，它向其会员组织保证，这只是长期斗争的“初步阶段”。

en: On July 29th they were relieved, at least temporarily. (9 words)
cn: 7月29日，他们终于如释重负，起码暂时如此。

en: A federal appeals court overturned the prior decision, ruling that Myriad Genetics could indeed hold patents to two genes that help forecast a woman’s risk of breast cancer. (28 words)
cn: 联邦上诉法院推翻了先前的判决，现在裁定万基遗传公司（Myriad Genetics）可以实际上拥有最多两项可以帮助预测女性乳腺癌风险的基因专利。

en: The chief executive of Myriad, a company in Utah, said the ruling was a blessing to firms and patients alike. (20 words)
cn: 万基，一家犹他州的公司，其执行总裁说，此项裁决对于公司和病人而言，都是福音。

en: But as companies continue their attempts at personalised medicine, the courts will remain rather busy. (15 words)
cn: 但是随着公司继续尝试医疗个性化服务，法院依然会相当忙碌。

en: The Myriad case itself is probably not over. (8 words)
cn: 万基公司一案本身或许还没有完结。

en: Critics make three main arguments against gene patents: a gene is a product of nature, so it may not be patented; gene patents suppress innovation rather than reward it; and patents’ monopolies restrict access to genetic tests such as Myriad’s. (40 words)
cn: 批评者摆出三点理由反对基因专利：基因是自然产物，不可申请专利；基因专利会压制创新，而非奖励创新；基因垄断限制了对基因测试的使用，比如万基公司的基因测试技术。

en: A growing number seem to agree. (6 words)
cn: 越来越多的人似乎对此表示赞同。

en: Last year a federal task-force urged reform for patents related to genetic tests. (13 words)
cn: 去年，一个联邦专案组就曾敦促要求改革有关基因测试的专利。

en: In October the Department of Justice filed a brief in the Myriad case, arguing that an isolated DNA molecule “is no less a product of nature...than are cotton fibers that have been separated from cotton seeds.”(36 words)
cn: 十月，司法部对万基一案提出的诉讼摘要中认为，单个的脱氧核糖核酸（DNA）分子“只不过是自然的产物......与棉花籽中分离出来的棉花纤维无异”。

en: Despite the appeals court’s decision, big questions remain unanswered. (9 words)
cn: 尽管上诉法院作出了裁决，但还有重大问题悬而未决。

en: For example, it is unclear whether the sequencing of a whole genome violates the patents of individual genes within it. (20 words)
cn: 例如，对整个基因组排序是否会侵犯该基因组内某个基因的所拥有的专利，这一问题尚不明确。

en: The case may yet reach the Supreme Court. (8 words)
cn: 该案件可能会上诉至最高法院。

en: As the industry advances, however, other suits may have an even greater impact. (13 words)
cn: 然而，随着这个行业的发展，其他诉讼案件可能产生甚至更大的影响。

en: Companies are unlikely to file many more patents for human DNA molecules—most are already patented or in the public domain. (21 words)
cn: 公司已经不太可能就人类DNA分子来提出更多的专利申请，大多数已经注册，或者是属于公共领域。

en: Firms are now studying how genes interact, looking for correlations that might be used to determine the causes of disease or predict a drug’s efficacy. (25 words)
cn: 现在各个公司都在研究基因之间如何相互作用，找出关联，用以查明引发疾病的诱因，或者预测药物疗效。

en: Companies are eager to win patents for “connecting the dots,” explains Hans Sauer, a lawyer for the BIO. (18 words)
cn: 各公司都着急能够获得“连点”专利，汉斯·索尔这样解释说，他是生物技术工业组织的一位律师。

en: Their success may be determined by a suit related to this issue, brought by the Mayo Clinic, which the Supreme Court will hear in its next term. (27 words)
cn: 他们能否成功或许得由一场与此问题相关的诉讼来决定；这场诉讼是妙佑诊所提出的，最高法院将在下一庭审期进行听证。

en: The BIO recently held a convention which included sessions to coach lawyers on the shifting landscape for patents. (18 words)
cn: 生物技术工业组织最近召开了一次大会，其中包括培训律师的议程，以使他们能够应对专利方面的局势变化。

en: Each meeting was packed. (4 words)
cn: 每场会议场场爆满。

en: The great recession may be over, but this era of high joblessness is probably beginning. (15 words)
cn: 大衰退或许已经结束了，但高失业率时代可能才刚刚开始。

en: Before it ends, it will likely change the life course and character of a generation of young adults. (18 words)
cn: 结束之前，它很可能会改变一代年轻人的生命历程，也会改变他们的性格。

en: And ultimately, it is likely to reshape our politics, our culture, and the character of our society for years. (19 words)
cn: 最终，它可能在未来多年会重塑我们的政策、文化，及社会特征。

en: No one tries harder than the jobless to find silver linings in this national economic disaster. (16 words)
cn: 在这场全国经济灾难中，失业的人，比其他任何人都要努力，想从困境中找到一线希望。

en: Many said that unemployment, while extremely painful, had improved them in some ways: they had become less materialistic and more financially prudent; they were more aware of the struggles of others. (31 words)
cn: 很多人说，失业，尽管极度痛苦，但从某些方面讲使他们进步：少了些物欲，多了些节俭；对他人的艰辛，更能感同身受。

en: In limited respects, perhaps the recession will leave society better off. (11 words)
cn: 从少数几个方面看来，大衰退或许会使社会变得更好。

en: At the very least, it has awoken us from our national fever dream of easy riches and bigger houses, and put a necessary end to an era of reckless personal spending. (31 words)
cn: 至少，它把我们从暴富或者豪宅的民族热梦中唤醒，及时地结束了那种挥霍无度个人消费时代。

en: But for the most part, these benefits seem thin, uncertain, and far off. (13 words)
cn: 但很大程度上，这些好处似乎显得微不足道、难以确定，且遥不可及。

en: In The Moral Consequences of Economic Growth, the economic historian Benjamin Friedman argues that both inside and outside the U.S., lengthy periods of economic stagnation or decline have almost always left society more mean-spirited and less inclusive, and have usually stopped or reversed the advance of rights and freedoms. (49 words)
cn: 本杰明·弗里德曼是经济历史学家，在其《经济增长的道德意义》一书中，他指出，无论是在美国国内还是国外，长期的经济停滞或者衰退几乎总是使社会变得更加狭隘，缺乏包容；并且通常还会使自由和权利的发展停滞，甚至倒退。

en: Anti-immigrant sentiment typically increases, as does conflict between races and classes. (11 words)
cn: 反移民情绪通常会增加，正如不同种族和不同阶层之间的冲突一样。

en: Income inequality usually falls during a recession, but it has not shrunk in this one. (15 words)
cn: 收入不公平现象通常会在经济衰退期减少；但这一次并没有。

en: Indeed, this period of economic weakness may reinforce class divides, and decrease opportunities to cross them—especially for young people. (20 words)
cn: 的确，这一轮经济疲软可能会加深阶级对立，并使消除阶级对立的机会减少，尤其是对于年轻人而言。

en: The research of Till Von Wachter, the economist in Columbia University, suggests that not all people graduating into a recession see their life chances dimmed: those with degrees from elite universities catch up fairly quickly to where they otherwise would have been if they had graduated in better times; it is the masses beneath them that are left behind. (59 words)
cn: 蒂尔·冯·瓦赫特是哥伦比亚大学的经济学家，他的研究表明，不是所有陷入经济衰退的人都觉得人生机会渺茫：那些毕业于名牌大学的人就能较快赶上，达到他们要是在毕业时正逢盛世所该有的水平；恰好是普罗大众，不如他们，落后了。

en: In the Internet age, it is particularly easy to see the resentment that has always been hidden within American society. (20 words)
cn: 在互联网时代，很容易就看出一直掩藏于美国社会的那种愤世嫉俗情绪。

en: More difficult, in the moment, is discerning precisely how these lean times are affecting society’s character. (16 words)
cn: 更大的困难，在目前，是要准确弄清楚经济这样惨淡的世道对社会性格有何影响。

en: In many respects, the U.S. was more socially tolerant entering this recession than at any time in its history, and a variety of national polls on social conflict since then have shown mixed results. (34 words)
cn: 进入到经济衰退时期，美国社会在很多方面极其宽容，历史上前所未见。此后，对社会冲突做了各种民调，结果千差万别。

en: We will have to wait and see exactly how these hard times will reshape our social fabric. (17 words)
cn: 这些艰难岁月将如何重塑社会结构，且让我们拭目以待。

en: But they certainly will reshape it, and all the more so the longer they extend. (15 words)
cn: 但肯定会重塑，并且时间越久，改变越大。

en: In an essay entitled “Making It in America,” the author Adam Davidson relates a joke from cotton country about just how much a modern textile mill has been automated: The average mill has only two employees today, “a man and a dog. (42 words)
cn: 在一篇题为《在美国制造》的文章中，作者亚当·戴维森提到了一个笑话，来自棉花产地，是关于现代纺织厂自动化程度的：现在一般的工厂只有两个员工，“一人一狗。

en: The man is there to feed the dog, and the dog is there to keep the man away from the machines.”(21 words)
cn: 人在那里是养狗；狗在那里是防人，怕他们破坏机器。

en: Davidson’s article is one of a number of pieces that have recently appeared making the point that the reason we have such stubbornly high unemployment and declining middle-class incomes today is largely because of the big drop in demand because of the Great Recession, but it is also because of the advances in both globalization and the information technology revolution, which are more rapidly than ever replacing labor with machines or foreign workers. (73 words)
cn: 戴维森的文章是最近涌现的诸多文章中的一篇，这些文章都认为，之所以当前失业率居高不下，中产阶级收入下滑，主要是因为大萧条带来的需求急剧下降，但同时也因为全球化和信息技术革命这两者所带来的进步，这些进步发展迅猛，前所未见，迅速地使用用机器替代了人力，或者使用外来工人。

en: In the past, workers with average skills, doing an average job, could earn an average lifestyle. (16 words)
cn: 在过去，平庸的工人，有平庸的技能，干一份平庸的工作，也能过上平庸的生活。

en: But, today, average is officially over. (6 words)
cn: 但现在，我们已经正式告别平庸了。

en: Being average just won’t earn you what it used to. (10 words)
cn: 平平庸庸根本不会让你过得跟过去一样。

en: It can’t when so many more employers have so much more access to so much more above average cheap foreign labor, cheap robotics, cheap software, cheap automation and cheap genius. (30 words)
cn: 它不能，是因为如此多得多的雇主，有如此多得多的途径，得到如此多得多的超越平庸的廉价外劳、廉价机器人、廉价软件、廉价自动装置、和廉价天才。

en: Therefore, everyone needs to find their extra—their unique value contribution that makes them stand out in whatever is their field of employment. (23 words)
cn: 因此，每个人都需要发现自己的附加值——其特有的价值贡献，可以使他们在自己受雇的任何领域都能脱颖而出。

en: Yes, new technology has been eating jobs forever, and always will. (11 words)
cn: 是的，新技术永远都在吞噬工作岗位，将来也一直会。

en: But there’s been an acceleration. (5 words)
cn: 并且，速度一直在加快。

en: As Davidson notes, “In the 10 years ending in 2009, [U.S.] factories shed workers so fast that they erased almost all the gains of the previous 70 years; roughly one out of every three manufacturing jobs—about 6 million in total—disappeared.” (42 words)
cn: 正如戴维森指出，“在2009年以前的十年间，美国工厂裁员之快，几乎抹去了70年来所有新增人员的总数；大概每三个制造业岗位中就有一个，总共大约是600万个岗位消失了。”

en: There will always be change—new jobs, new products, new services. (11 words)
cn: 变化总是会有的——新岗位、新产品、新服务。

en: But the one thing we know for sure is that with each advance in globalization and the I.T. revolution, the best jobs will require workers to have more and better education to make themselves above average. (36 words)
cn: 但有一件事情我们确信无疑，全球化和信息技术革命的每一次发展过程中，最好的岗位都会要求员工有更高更好的教育，使他们能够超越平庸。

en: In a world where average is officially over, there are many things we need to do to support employment, but nothing would be more important than passing some kind of G.I. Bill for the 21st century that ensures that every American has access to post-high school education. (47 words)
cn: 在这样一个已经正式告别平庸的世界，扶持就业，我们需要做的事情有很多，但重中之重是要为21世纪出台类似于《美国退伍军人权利法案》的某种法案，确保每一个美国人高中毕业后还有机会获得教育。

en: A century ago, the immigrants from across the Atlantic included settlers and sojourners. (13 words)
cn: 一个世纪前，跨越大西洋而来的移民中，有长期定居的，也有暂时旅居的。

en: Along with the many folks looking to make a permanent home in the United States came those who had no intention to stay, and who would make some money and then go home. (33 words)
cn: 很多人想在美国永居；但同时来的，还有人无意久留，只想赚钱，然后回家。

en: Between 1908 and 1915, about 7 million people arrived while about 2 million departed. (14 words)
cn: 在1908到1915年间，来的有700万人，离开的有200万人。

en: About a quarter of all Italian immigrants, for example, eventually returned to Italy for good. (15 words)
cn: 例如，所有意大利移民中，大约有四分之一的最后都永远回到了意大利。

en: They even had an affectionate nickname, “uccelli di passaggio,” birds of passage. (12 words)
cn: 他们甚至还得一爱称，“uccelli di passaggio”，候鸟。

en: Today, we are much more rigid about immigrants. (8 words)
cn: 现在，我们对移民严格了太多。

en: We divide newcomers into two categories: legal or illegal, good or bad. (12 words)
cn: 我们把新来的分成两类：合法的，非法的；好的，坏的。

en: We hail them as Americans in the making, or brand them as aliens to be kicked out. (17 words)
cn: 我们赞誉他们为“准美国人”，或者诽谤他们是“得赶走的异类”。

en: That framework has contributed mightily to our broken immigration system and the long political paralysis over how to fix it. (20 words)
cn: 这样的体制在很大程度上就造成了我们移民系统支离破碎，也造成了长期的政治瘫痪，不知如何修复这一系统。

en: We don’t need more categories, but we need to change the way we think about categories. (16 words)
cn: 我们不需要更多的分类，但是我们需要改变自己对分类的看法。

en: We need to look beyond strict definitions of legal and illegal. (11 words)
cn: 合法与非法有严格的定义，但我们需要突破这些定义来看问题。

en: To start, we can recognize the new birds of passage, those living and thriving in the gray areas. (18 words)
cn: 首先，我们得认可这些“新来的候鸟”，这些在灰色地带生活和发展的人。

en: We might then begin to solve our immigration challenges. (9 words)
cn: 然后，我们就可以开始解决移民所带来的挑战。

en: Crop pickers, violinists, construction workers, entrepreneurs, engineers, home health-care aides and physicists are among today’s birds of passage. (18 words)
cn: 现在的候鸟人群中，有从事作物采摘的、拉小提琴的，还有建筑工人、家庭护工、企业家、工程师、和物理学家。

en: They are energetic participants in a global economy driven by the flow of work, money and ideas. (17 words)
cn: 受到工作、金钱和思想流动的驱使，他们成了全球经济的积极参与者。

en: They prefer to come and go as opportunity calls them. (10 words)
cn: 他们喜欢来去自如，伺机而动。

en: They can manage to have a job in one place and a family in another. (15 words)
cn: 工作在一个地方，家在另一个地方，他们也能设法应对。

en: With or without permission, they straddle laws, jurisdictions and identities with ease. (12 words)
cn: 不管有无许可，他们都能轻松越过法律、辖区和身份。

en: We need them to imagine the United States as a place where they can be productive for a while without committing themselves to staying forever. (25 words)
cn: 我们需要他们把美国想成是这样一个地方：能够暂时成就自我，且不必承诺久留。

en: We need them to feel that home can be both here and there and that they can belong to two nations honorably. (22 words)
cn: 我们需要他们感到，家既可以在这里又可以在那里，他们可以体面地属于两个国家。

en: Accommodating this new world of people in motion will require new attitudes on both sides of the immigration battle. (19 words)
cn: 认可这群流动人员的新世界，需要这场移民之争的双方都要有新的态度。

en: Looking beyond the culture war logic of right or wrong means opening up the middle ground and understanding that managing immigration today requires multiple paths and multiple outcomes, including some that are not easy to accomplish legally in the existing system. (41 words)
cn: 如果要超越文化战争非此即彼的逻辑来看问题，就意味着我们要打开中间地带，也意味着我们要理解，现在的移民管理需要多措并举，多出成果，包括在现有系统下通过法律难以达成的措施和成果。

en: Scientists have found that although we are prone to snap overreactions, if we take a moment and think about how we are likely to react, we can reduce or even eliminate the negative effects of our quick, hard-wired responses. (39 words)
cn: 科学家已经发现，即使我们很容易产生仓促的过激反应，但如果我们稍微缓缓，并且想想自己应该怎么做，那我们就可以减少，甚至彻底根除自己快速做出本能反应带来的负面影响。

en: Snap decisions can be important defense mechanisms; if we are judging whether someone is dangerous, our brains and bodies are hard-wired to react very quickly, within milliseconds. (27 words)
cn: 仓促做决定，是一种重要的保护机制；如果我们在判断一个人是否危险，我们的大脑和身体就会本能地快速反应，毫秒之间。

en: But we need more time to assess other factors. (9 words)
cn: 但我们需要更多时间来评估其他因素。

en: To accurately tell whether someone is sociable, studies show, we need at least a minute, preferably five. (17 words)
cn: 研究表明，要准确弄清楚一个人是否喜欢交际，我们需要至少一分钟，最好五分钟。

en: It takes a while to judge complex aspects of personality, like neuroticism or open-mindedness. (14 words)
cn: 要评价复杂的个性特征，比如神经质程度或者性格开放程度，得花点时间。

en: But snap decisions in reaction to rapid stimuli aren’t exclusive to the interpersonal realm. (14 words)
cn: 但应对瞬间突如其来的刺激做出的仓促决定，并不只是人际交往范畴才有的。

en: Psychologists at the University of Toronto found that viewing a fast-food logo for just a few milliseconds primes us to read 20 percent faster, even though reading has little to do with eating. (33 words)
cn: 多伦多大学的心理学家发现，看快餐标识，只看几秒，就能使我们阅读加快20%，及时阅读和吃饭没有关系。

en: We unconsciously associate fast food with speed and impatience and carry those impulses into whatever else we’re doing. (18 words)
cn: 我们不知不觉地会把快速和急躁与跟快餐联系在一起，并且无论我们做别的啥事，都会掺杂这些念头。

en: Subjects exposed to fast-food flashes also tend to think a musical piece lasts too long. (15 words)
cn: 有些实验对象，闪了一眼快餐标识，也往往会认为一曲音乐持续时间太长。

en: Yet we can reverse such influences. (6 words)
cn: 但我们却能够逆转这些影响。

en: If we know we will overreact to consumer products or housing options when we see a happy face (one reason good sales representatives and real estate agents are always smiling), we can take a moment before buying. (37 words)
cn: 如果我们知道，自己在选择消费品或者房子的时候看到笑脸会产生强烈的反应（这解释了为什么优秀的销售代表或者房产中介总是面带笑容），那么我们在购买之前就能缓缓再说。

en: If we know female job screeners are more likely to reject attractive female applicants, we can help screeners understand their biases—or hire outside screeners. (25 words)
cn: 如果我们知道，女性招聘人员可能会拒绝有魅力的女性求职者，那么我们就可以帮助招聘人员看到自己的偏见，或者聘请外部招聘人员。

en: John Gottman, the marriage expert, explains that we quickly “thin slice” information reliably only after we ground such snap reactions in “thick sliced” long-term study. (25 words)
cn: 约翰·戈特曼是一位婚恋专家，他解释说，只有在我们把这些快速反应基于对“厚片”的长期研究之后，我们才能快速可靠地理解“薄片”信息。

en: When Dr. Gottman really wants to assess whether a couple will stay together, he invites them to his island retreat for a much longer evaluation: two days, not two seconds. (30 words)
cn: 如果戈特曼博士真的想评估一双情侣是否能融洽生活，他会邀请他们去他的私人避世小岛，做更长时间评估，两天，不是两秒。

en: Our ability to mute our hard-wired reactions by pausing is what differentiates us from animals: doges can think about the future only intermittently or for a few minutes. (28 words)
cn: 我们能够缓缓，控制自己的本能反应，这是我们与动物的不同之处：狗狗能够思考未来，只不过是间歇性地，或者持续几分钟。

en: But historically we have spent about 12 percent of our days contemplating the longer term. (15 words)
cn: 但从历史上看来，我们会用自己12%的时间来思考更长远的事情。

en: Although technology might change the way we react, it hasn’t changed our nature. (13 words)
cn: 尽管科技可能会改变我们做出反应的方式，但并没有改变我们的天性。

en: We still have the imaginative capacity to rise above temptation and reverse the high-speed trend. (15 words)
cn: 我们仍具有丰富的想象力，超越诱惑，控制自己快速反应的本能驱使。

en: Europe is not a gender-equality heaven. (6 words)
cn: 欧洲并非性别平等的天堂。

en: In particular, the corporate workplace will never be completely family-friendly until women are part of senior management decisions, and Europe’s top corporate-governance positions remain overwhelmingly male. (26 words)
cn: 具体说来，如果女性不能参与高管决策，其公司职场决不能真正如家庭般的友好，且欧洲公司管理的顶层绝大多数依然是男性。

en: Indeed, women hold only 14 per cent of positions on European corporate boards. (13 words)
cn: 实际上，在欧洲，女性在公司董事会席位中只占14%。
