en: The European Union is now considering legislation to compel corporate boards to maintain a certain proportion of women—up to 60 per cent. (23 words)
cn: 现在欧盟正考虑立法，以强迫公司董事会的女性要保持一定的比例，可达60%。

en: This proposed mandate was born of frustration. (7 words)
cn: 这项授权令的提出，诞生于一次挫折。

en: Last year, European Commission Vice President Viviane Reding issued a call to voluntary action. (14 words)
cn: 去年，欧盟委员会副主席薇薇安·雷丁发起了一项呼吁，请求大家自愿行动起来。

en: Reding invited corporations to sign up for gender balance goals of 40 per cent female board membership. (17 words)
cn: 雷丁提出了一个性别平衡目标，即女性要在董事会成员中达到40%，并邀请各企业加入，达成这一目标。

en: But her appeal was considered a failure: only 24 companies took it up. (13 words)
cn: 但她的呼吁被认为是失败了：只有24个公司买账。

en: Do we need quotas to ensure that women can continue to climb the corporate ladder fairly as they balance work and family? (22 words)
cn: 女性要平衡工作和家庭，又要在公司晋升层级上继续公平地攀登，我们真的需要一个确定的数额来确保做到这样吗？

en: “Personally, I don’t like quotas,” Reding said recently. (8 words)
cn: “在我个人看来，我不喜欢定额，”雷丁最近这样说道。

en: Quotas get action: they “open the way to equality and they break through the glass ceiling,” according to Reding, a result seen in France and other countries with legally binding provisions on placing women in top business positions. (38 words)
cn: 确定数额能激发行动：定额，根据雷丁的观点，“打开了通往平等的道路，打破了玻璃天花板”，法国就可以看到这样的效果，还有其他一些国家，法律上明文规定公司管理高层要有女性。

en: I understand Reding’s reluctance—and her frustration. (7 words)
cn: 我理解雷丁的犹豫，还有她的沮丧。

en: I don’t like quotas either; they run counter to my belief in meritocracy, governance by the capable. (17 words)
cn: 我也不喜欢定额，因为它们有悖于我们信奉的贤能管理制度，即能者居之。

en: But, when one considers the obstacles to achieving the meritocratic ideal, it does look as if a fairer world must be temporarily ordered. (23 words)
cn: 但如果我们考虑到在实现贤能管理这一理想过程中的诸多障碍，确实看起来是必须得进行暂时的强制管理才能让世界更公平。

en: After all, four decades of evidence has now shown that corporations in Europe as well as the US are evading the meritocratic hiring and promotion of women to top position—no matter how much “soft pressure” is put upon them. (40 words)
cn: 毕竟，四十年来的证据都表明，欧美的企业都在避免雇用贤能管理人才，也在避免提拔女性到顶层管理岗位——无论给这些公司施加多少“软压力”。

en: When women do break through to the summit of corporate power—as, for example, Sheryl Sandberg recently did at Facebook—they attract massive attention precisely because they remain the exception to the rule. (33 words)
cn: 一旦女性真有所突破，进入公司权力巅峰，比如，像雪莉·桑德伯格最近在脸书的作为，她们就会备受关注，正好因为她们之于规则是个例外。

en: If appropriate public policies were in place to help all women—whether CEOs or their children’s caregivers—and all families, Sandberg would be no more newsworthy than any other highly capable person living in a more just society. (38 words)
cn: 如果能有恰当的公共政策来帮助所有女性（不管是首席执行官还是其孩子的保姆）和所有家庭，桑德博格就不会有任何值得报道的价值，生活在更公正社会上的其他卓越人士也一样。

en: What would you do with $590m? (6 words)
cn: 要有5亿9000万美元，你会用来做啥？

en: This is now a question for Gloria Mackenzie, an 84-year-old widow who recently emerged from her small, tin-roofed house in Florida to collect the biggest undivided lottery jackpot in history. (30 words)
cn: 现在，这对于格洛丽亚·麦肯齐来说就是个问题，她84岁，寡妇，住在佛罗里达一个狭小的铁皮屋里，最近她独揽史上最大的彩票头奖，出来领奖才为人所知。

en: If she hopes her new-found fortune will yield lasting feelings of fulfillment, she could do worse than read Happy Money by Elizabeth Dunn and Michael Norton. (26 words)
cn: 如果她想自己这笔意外之财能够带来更长久的满足感，她倒不如去读一读伊丽莎白·邓恩和迈克·诺顿写的《快乐金钱》。

en: These two academics use an array of behavioral research to show that the most rewarding ways to spend money can be counterintuitive. (22 words)
cn: 这两位学者采用一系列的行为研究表明，花钱最值得的方式，不能靠直觉。

en: Fantasies of great wealth often involve visions of fancy cars and extravagant homes. (13 words)
cn: 幻想拥有巨额财富，通常就会想到香车豪宅。

en: Yet satisfaction with these material purchases wears off fairly quickly. (10 words)
cn: 但买东西带来的满足感消失得很快。

en: What was once exciting and new becomes old-hat; regret creeps in. (11 words)
cn: 曾经心动的新东西变旧了，懊悔之情也涌了上来。

en: It is far better to spend money on experiences, say Ms. Dunn and Mr. Norton, like interesting trips, unique meals or even going to the cinema. (26 words)
cn: 邓恩女士和诺顿先生认为，最好是把钱花在体验上，比如来几次有趣的旅行，吃几顿独特的美食，甚至去看几场电影。

en: These purchases often become more valuable with time—as stories or memories—particularly if they involve feeling more connected to others. (21 words)
cn: 这样的消费体验通常会随着时间而更加弥足珍贵——有故事或者有记忆——尤其是，如果这些消费体验让你感觉自己能更加融洽地和他人相处。

en: It seems most people would be better off if they could shorten their commutes to work, spend more time with friends and family and less of it watching television (something the average American spends a whopping two months a year doing, and is hardly jollier for it). (47 words)
cn: 大多数人若能缩短通勤时间，多花时间陪陪家人和朋友，少花时间看电视（这种事情，美国人平均每年竟然要花多达两个月的时间去做，且很难为之感到更快乐），似乎会过得更好。

en: Buying gifts or giving to charity is often more pleasurable than purchasing things for oneself, and luxuries are most enjoyable when they are consumed sparingly. (25 words)
cn: 购买礼物或者捐给慈善通常都比给自己买东西要更快乐；并且，有节制地购买奢侈品才会最开心。

en: This is apparently the reason MacDonald’s restricts the availability of its popular McRib—a marketing trick that has turned the pork sandwich into an object of obsession. (27 words)
cn: 这也明显解释了为什么麦当劳会限量供应广受欢迎的烤汁猪排堡，这种营销策略已经把一个猪肉三明治变成人们迷恋之物。

en: Readers of Happy Money are clearly a privileged lot, anxious about fulfillment, not hunger. (14 words)
cn: 《快乐金钱》的读者明显是很幸运的一群人，他们忧虑的是自己的成就，而非饥饿。

en: Money may not quite buy happiness, but people in wealthier countries are generally happier than those in poor ones. (19 words)
cn: 钱或许不一定能够买到快乐，但是富裕一点的国家的人总体上要比贫穷国家的人要快乐一点。

en: Yet the link between feeling good and spending money on others can be seen among rich and poor people around the world, and scarcity enhances the pleasure of most things for most people. (33 words)
cn: 然而，花钱帮助他人，感觉很好，我们可以发现这两者之间有联系，全世界的人，无论贫富都如此，并且对于大多数人而言，大多数东西，若是罕见，就能带来更多的快乐。

en: Not everyone will agree with the authors’ policy ideas, which range from mandating more holiday time to reducing tax incentives for American homebuyers. (23 words)
cn: 作者提出的一些政策理念，包括批准增加休假时间，还有对美国购房者的减税激励计划等，并非每个人都认同作者的这些理念。

en: But most people will come away from this book believing it was money well spent. (15 words)
cn: 但大多数人释卷之后，都会认为此书物有所值。

en: An article in Scientific American has pointed out that empirical research says that, actually, you think you’re more beautiful than you are. (22 words)
cn: 《科学美国人》的一篇文章指出，实证研究标明，事实上，人们会觉得自己比实际长相要好看点。

en: We have a deep-seated need to feel good about ourselves and we naturally employ a number of self-enhancing strategies to achieve this. (22 words)
cn: 自我感觉良好，是我们发自内心的需求；并且为了让自己感觉良好，我们自然而然地就会用各种方式来抬高自己。

en: Social psychologists have amassed oceans of research into what they call the “above average effect”, or “illusory superiority”, and shown that, for example, 70% of us rate ourselves as above average in leadership, 93% in driving and 85% at getting on well with others—all obviously statistical impossibilities. (48 words)
cn: 对于所谓的“优于平均效应”和“虚幻的优越感”，社会心理学家搜集了海量的研究成果，表明，举例说吧，在领导力方面，我们有70%的人会认为自己优于常人；在驾驶上，有93%；在与人相处方面，有85%，所有的这些明显在统计学上是不可能的事情。

en: We rose-tint our memories and put ourselves into self-affirming situations. (10 words)
cn: 我们给自己的记忆涂上梦幻色彩，处于一种自我肯定的状态。

en: We become defensive when criticized, and apply negative stereotypes to others to boost our own esteem. (16 words)
cn: 受到批评，我们会心存戒备；并且为了提高自尊，会采用消极的方式对待他人。

en: We stalk around thinking we’re hot stuff. (7 words)
cn: 我们总是趾高气昂，自命不凡。

en: Psychologist and behavioral scientist Nicholas Epley oversaw a key study into self-enhancement and attractiveness. (14 words)
cn: 尼古拉斯·埃普利，心理学家兼行为科学家，他督导了一项对自我提升和吸引力的重大研究。

en: Rather than have people simply rate their beauty compared with others, he asked them to identify an original photograph of themselves from a lineup including versions that had been altered to appear more and less attractive. (36 words)
cn: 他没有让人们只是简单地比较他人来评价自己好看与否，而是把他们的一些照片修过，要么变得更美，要么更丑，然后再让他们从这些照片中选出自己最原始的那张。

en: Visual recognition, reads the study, is “an automatic psychological process, occurring rapidly and intuitively with little or no apparent conscious deliberation”. (21 words)
cn: 视觉识别，该研究指出，是“一自动的心理过程，凭直觉快速发生，显然是几乎没有或者根本没有去做刻意地考虑”。

en: If the subjects quickly chose a falsely flattering image—which most did—they genuinely believed it was really how they looked. (21 words)
cn: 如果实验对象迅速选出一张显得更好看的假照片——大多数人这样做了——他们真的觉得自己看起来的确是长这个样子。

en: Epley found no significant gender difference in responses. (8 words)
cn: 埃普利在上述反应中完全没有发现明显的性别差异。

en: Nor was there any evidence that those who self-enhanced the most (that is, the participants who thought the most positively doctored pictures were real) were doing so to make up for profound insecurities. (33 words)
cn: 也没有证据表明，那些抬高自己最多的人（即，参与者中认为修得最好看的照片是真实形象的人）这样做，是为了弥补强烈的不安全感。

en: In fact, those who thought that the images higher up the attractiveness scale were real directly corresponded with those who showed other markers for having higher self-esteem. (27 words)
cn: 事实上，那些认为吸引力爆表的照片是真实形象的人，恰好就是那些表现出具有更强烈自尊心的人。

en: “I don’t think the findings that we have are any evidence of personal delusion,” says Epley. (16 words)
cn: “我认为，我们得出的这些发现完全不能证明有人是在自欺欺人”， 埃普利这样说道。

en: “It’s a reflection simply of people generally thinking well of themselves.”  (11 words)
cn: “它只不过是表明了人们一般都会自我感觉良好。”

en: If you are depressed, you won’t be self-enhancing. (8 words)
cn: 如果你心情沮丧，就不会抬高自己。

en: Knowing the results of Epley’s study, it makes sense that many people hate photographs of themselves viscerally—on one level, they don’t even recognize the person in the picture as themselves. (31 words)
cn: 我们知道了埃普利研究结果，就自然明白为什么人们会发自内心地不喜欢自己的照片——从某种角度上看，他们甚至都不承认到照片上的人就是自己。

en: Facebook, therefore, is a self-enhancer’s paradise, where people can share only the most flattering photos, the cream of their wit, style, beauty, intellect and lifestyles. (25 words)
cn: 因此，脸书就是那些自我抬高的人的天堂，他们可以分享最光彩亮丽的照片、横生的妙趣、优雅的格调、绝妙的美好、过人的智力，还有精彩的生活。

en: It’s not that people’s profiles are dishonest, says Catalina Toma of Wisconsin-Madison University, “but they portray an idealized version of themselves.” (21 words)
cn: “不是人们照片上的形象具有欺骗性”，威斯康星大学麦迪逊分销的卡特尼拉·托马说，“而是大家描绘了一个自己理想的样子”。

en: The concept of man versus machine is at least as old as the industrial revolution, but this phenomenon tends to be most acutely felt during economic downturns and fragile recoveries. (30 words)
cn: 人机矛盾的概念由来已久，至少可以追溯到工业革命时期，但在经济下滑且复苏乏力的形势下，这一矛盾现象往往会让人感受最为强烈。

en: And yet, it would be a mistake to think we are right now simply experiencing the painful side of a boom and bust cycle. (24 words)
cn: 然而，如果认为我们现在恰好在经历的只不过是经济繁荣与萧条交替循环的艰难时期，那就大错特错了。

en: Certain jobs have gone away for good, outmoded by machines. (10 words)
cn: 某些工作因机器取代而过时，已经彻底消失。

en: Since technology has such an insatiable appetite for eating up human jobs, this phenomenon will continue to restructure our economy in ways we cannot immediately foresee. (26 words)
cn: 因为技术吞噬人类工作，胃口之大已无法满足，这一现象还将继续以无法及时预测的方式重组我们的经济。

en: When there is rapid improvement in the price and performance of technology, jobs that were once thought to be immune from automation suddenly become threatened. (25 words)
cn: 当技术的价格和性能迅速改善，那些曾经认为不会受到自动化影响的工作，也会突然受到威胁。

en: This argument has attracted a lot of attention, via the success of the book Race Against the Machine, by Erik Brynjolfsson and Andrew McAfee, who both hail from MIT’s Center for Digital Business. (33 words)
cn: 这一观点因《与机器赛跑》一书的成功而广受关注，该书的两位作者埃里克·布伦乔尔森和安德鲁·迈克菲，都在麻省理工数字化商业中心工作。

en: This is a powerful argument, and a scary one. (9 words)
cn: 这一观点非常有力，不过有点骇人听闻。

en: And yet, John Hagel, author of The Power of Pull and other books, says Brynjolfsson and McAfee miss the reason why these jobs are so vulnerable to technology in the first place. (32 words)
cn: 约翰·哈格尔著有《拉动力》及其他书籍，然而他却认为，布伦乔尔森和迈克菲两位都忽视了究竟是为什么这些工作在技术面前不堪一击。

en: Hagel says we have designed jobs in the U.S. that tend to be “tightly scripted” and “highly standardized” ones that leave no room for “individual initiative or creativity”. (28 words)
cn: 哈格尔说，在美国，我们的工作都是设计过的，往往是“高度模式化、高度标准化”，没有给“个人主动性和创造力”留下任何空间。

en: In short, these are the types of jobs that machines can perform much better at than human beings. (18 words)
cn: 简言之，这样的工作，机器比人干得好。

en: That is how we have put a giant target sign on the backs of American workers, Hagel says. (18 words)
cn: 我们就是这样把一个巨大的靶子放在美国工人的背上，哈格尔说。

en: It’s time to reinvent the formula for how work is conducted, since we are still relying on a very 20th century notion of work, Hagel says. (26 words)
cn: 是时候彻底改造我们的工作模式了，因为我们对待工作的认识仍然还停留在 20 纪，哈格尔指出。

en: In our rapidly changing economy, we more than ever need people in the workplace who can take initiative and exercise their imagination “to respond to unexpected events.”  (27 words)
cn: 经济发展迅猛，我们比以往任何时候都更需要那种在工作中能积极主动，发挥想象去“应对突发事件”的人才。

en: That is not something machines are good at. (8 words)
cn: 机器不善长这个。

en: They are designed to perform very predictable activities. (8 words)
cn: 他们是为做那种非常容易预测的事情而设计的。

en: As Hagel notes, Brynjolfsson and McAfee indeed touched on this point in their book. (14 words)
cn: 正如哈格尔指出，布伦乔尔森和迈克菲的确在书中提到了这一点。

en: We need to reframe race against the machine as race with the machine. (13 words)
cn: 我们需要重新把“与机器赛跑”这一说法，看成为“与机器齐跑”。

en: In other words, we need to look at the ways in which machines can augment human labor rather than replace it. (21 words)
cn: 换言之，我们需要看到机器是如何加强了人类劳动，而不是取代了人类劳动。

en: So then the problem is not really about technology, but rather, “how do we innovate our institutions and our work practices?” (21 words)
cn: 所以，这样看来，问题真的不在于技术，而在于“我们应该如何创改革体制，如何改变

en: When the government talks about infrastructure contributing to the economy the focus is usually on roads, railways, broadband and energy. (20 words)
cn: 政府在谈及基础设施有助于经济发展的时候，其关注的通常是公路、铁路、宽带和能源等。

en: Housing is seldom mentioned. (4 words)
cn: 住房问题，则很少提及。

en: Why is that? (3 words)
cn: 缘何如此？

en: To some extent the housing sector must shoulder the blame. (10 words)
cn: 从某种程度上看，房产部门难辞其咎。

en: We have not been good at communicating the real value that housing can contribute to economic growth. (17 words)
cn: 住房对经济发展贡献出的真实价值，我们一直都不太善于传达出来。

en: Then there is the scale of the typical housing project. (10 words)
cn: 然后就是典型住房项目的规模。

en: It is hard to shove for attention among multibillion-pound infrastructure projects, so it is inevitable that the attention is focused elsewhere. (21 words)
cn: 在数十亿英镑的基建项目中，要抢眼球很难；因此，关注的焦点必然就不在于此。

en: But perhaps the most significant reason is that the issue has always been so politically charged. (16 words)
cn: 但或许最重要的原因在于，这个问题一直以来总是太政治化了。

en: Nevertheless, the affordable housing situation is desperate. (7 words)
cn: 然而，经济适用房的形势还是相当严峻的。

en: Waiting lists increase all the time and we are simply not building enough new homes. (15 words)
cn: 等着买房的人一直在增加，并且新房，我们确实建得不够多。

en: The comprehensive spending review offers an opportunity for the government to help rectify this. (14 words)
cn: 进行综合支出评估，使政府有机会去纠正这一问题。

en: It needs to put historical prejudices to one side and take some steps to address our urgent housing need. (19 words)
cn: 政府需要放下历史偏见，采取措施解决紧迫的住房需要。

en: There are some indications that it is preparing to do just that. (12 words)
cn: 有一些迹象表明，政府正在准备那么做。

en: The communities minister, Don Foster, has hinted that George Osborne, Chancellor of the Exchequer, may introduce more flexibility to the current cap on the amount that local authorities can borrow against their housing stock debt. (35 words)
cn: 社区事务部部长唐·福斯特曾暗示说，财政大臣乔治·奥斯本可能会对地方政府住房存量债务目前的借贷上限采取更灵活的政策。

en: Evidence shows that 60,000 extra new homes could be built over the next five years if the cap were lifted, increasing GDP by 0.6%. (24 words)
cn: 证据表明，如果限额提高，将会在未来五年新建60000套住房，国内生产总值增加0.6%。

en: Ministers should also look at creating greater certainty in the rental environment, which would have a significant impact on the ability of registered providers to fund new developments from revenues. (30 words)
cn: 各位部长大臣也应该考虑提高租赁环境的稳定性，这将会对注册房产机构靠收入来投资新建住房的能力产生重大的影响。

en: But it is not just down to the government. (9 words)
cn: 但这事情不能只是落在政府的头上。

en: While these measures would be welcome in the short term, we must face up to the fact that the existing £4.5bn programme of grants to fund new affordable housing, set to expire in 2015, is unlikely to be extended beyond then. (41 words)
cn: 尽管这些措施会在短期内会受到欢迎，但我们必须面对这样一个事实：目前用于投资新建经济适用房的45亿拨款将在2015年到期，且不太可能延期。

en: The Labour party has recently announced that it will retain a large part of the coalition’s spending plans if it returns to power. (23 words)
cn: 工党最近宣布，如果重新执政，会保留大部分联合政府的支出计划。

en: The housing sector needs to accept that we are very unlikely to ever return to the era of large-scale public grants. (21 words)
cn: 我们的确不太可能再回到那种大规模公共拨款的时代了，房产部门得接受这个事实。

en: We need to adjust to this changing climate. (8 words)
cn: 我们需要做出调整，应对这一形势变化。

en: While the government’s commitment to long-term funding may have changed, the very pressing need for more affordable housing is real and is not going away. (25 words)
cn: 尽管政府要长期投资的承诺或许已经改变，但我们迫切需要修建更多的经济适用房，这种需求真实存在，且不会消失。

en: A new study suggests that contrary to most surveys, people are actually more stressed at home than at work. (19 words)
cn: 一项新的研究表明，人们在家比上班的压力更大，这与大多数调查结果不同。

en: Researchers measured people’s cortisol, which is a stress marker, while they were at work and while they were at home and found it higher at what is supposed to be a place of refuge. (34 words)
cn: 研究人员测量了人们在家和上班时候的皮质醇（一种压力标志物），发现在本应该是避风港的地方，皮质醇更高。

en: “Further contradicting conventional wisdom, we found that women as well as men have lower levels of stress at work than at home”, writes one of the researchers, Sarah Damaske. (29 words)
cn: “与大多数人的看法更加矛盾的是，我们发现，女性和男性一样，上班比在家压力更小”， 其中一位研究人员莎拉·达玛斯克这样写道。

en: In fact women even say they feel better at work, she notes. (12 words)
cn: 她指出，事实上，女性甚至说自己上班感觉更好。

en: Another surprise is that the findings hold true for both those with children and without, but more so for nonparents. (20 words)
cn: 还有一个令人吃惊的是，这些发现成果对于有无子女的人来说都一样真实有效，尤其是对于没有子女的人，更是如此。

en: This is why people who work outside the home have better health. (12 words)
cn: 这就解释了为什么那些出门在外工作的人身体更健康。

en: What the study doesn’t measure is whether people are still doing work when they’re at home, whether it is household work or work brought home from the office. (28 words)
cn: 这项研究没有考量的问题是，人们在家的时候是否还在工作，他们所做的工作是家务还是从办公室带回家的工作。

en: For many men, the end of the workday is a time to kick back. (14 words)
cn: 对许多男性来说，工作日结束，就是放松的时间。

en: For women who stay home, they never get to leave the office. (12 words)
cn: 对于家庭主妇而言，他们从来没有离开办公室的机会；

en: And for women who work outside the home, they often are playing catch-up-with-household tasks. (14 words)
cn: 对于在外工作的女性来说，他们通常总是在赶着工作做家务。

en: With the blurring of roles, and the fact that the home front lags well behind the workplace in making adjustments for working women, it’s not surprising that women are more stressed at home. (33 words)
cn: 由于角色模糊，再加之职业女性在角色调整中，家庭大后方远落后于职场，所以女性在家压力更大就不足为奇了。

en: But it’s not just a gender thing. (7 words)
cn: 但这并不仅仅是性别问题。

en: At work, people pretty much know what they’re supposed to be doing: working, making money, doing the tasks they have to do in order to draw an income. (28 words)
cn: 上班的话，人们差不多知道自己应该做的事情：工作、挣钱、完成任务获取收入。

en: The bargain is very pure: Employee puts in hours of physical or mental labor and employee draws out life-sustaining moola. (20 words)
cn: 交易非常明确：员工干几个小时的体力或脑力，赚几个维持生计的子。

en: On the home front, however, people have no such clarity. (10 words)
cn: 然而，在大后方，人们却没有那么明确。

en: Rare is the household in which the division of labor is so clinically and methodically laid out. (17 words)
cn: 鲜有这样的家庭：家务劳动分工安排得不多不少，一清二楚。

en: There are a lot of tasks to be done, there are inadequate rewards for most of them. (17 words)
cn: 家务活，有太多事情要做；大多数人，回报又少得可怜。

en: Your home colleagues—your family—have no clear rewards for their labor; they need to be talked into it, or if they’re teenagers, threatened with complete removal of all electronic devices. (31 words)
cn: 你的家庭同事——你的家庭成员——自己的劳动没有明确回报；他们需要有人劝，要是青少年，还得扬言要彻底拿走所有电子设备来威胁他们。

en: Plus, they’re your family. (4 words)
cn: 并且，他们是你家人。

en: You cannot fire your family. (5 words)
cn: 你不能解雇自己的家人。

en: You never really get to go home from home. (9 words)
cn: 你永不可能离开家务回家。

en: So it’s not surprising that people are more stressed at home. (11 words)
cn: 所以人们在家感觉压力更大，就不足为奇了。

en: Not only are the tasks apparently infinite, the co-workers are much harder to motivate. (14 words)
cn: 家务活似乎没完没了，一起干活的人的积极性还更难激发。

en: For years, studies have found that first-generation college students—those who do not have a parent with a college degree—lag other students on a range of education achievement factors. (30 words)
cn: 长期以来，研究发现，第一代大学生，就是那些父母都没有读过大学的，在学业成就的很多方面都会落后于其他学生。

en: Their grades are lower and their dropout rates are higher. (10 words)
cn: 分数更低；辍学率更高。

en: But since such students are most likely to advance economically if they succeed in higher education, colleges and universities have pushed for decades to recruit more of them. (28 words)
cn: 但这一类学生，一旦成功完成高等教育，他们最有可能在经济方面大展宏图，所以各大院校几十年来一直都在力争多招一些。

en: This has created “a paradox” in that recruiting first-generation students, but then watching many of them fail, means that higher education has “continued to reproduce and widen, rather than close” an achievement gap based on social class, according to the depressing beginning of a paper forthcoming in the journal Psychological Science. (51 words)
cn: 这就产生了“一个悖论”：把第一代大学生招进来，然后眼睁睁地看着大部分人失败，这就意味着高等教育“持续产生并扩大了”社会阶层带来的成就差距，“而非弥合”，即将发表在《心理科学》杂志上的一篇文章，开头部分就这么令人沮丧。

en: But the article is actually quite optimistic, as it outlines a potential solution to this problem, suggesting that an approach (which involves a one-hour, next-to-no-cost program) can close 63 percent of the achievement gap (measured by such factors as grades) between first-generation and other students. (45 words)
cn: 但这篇文章实际上相当乐观，它概述了可能会解决这一问题的方案，认为有一种途径（包括一个零成本的一小时项目）可以缩小第一代大学生和其他学生之间63%的成就差异（以成绩等因素来衡量）。

en: The authors of the paper are from different universities, and their findings are based on a study involving 147 students (who completed the project) at an unnamed private university. (29 words)
cn: 该论文的作者来自不同的大学，他们的发现成果是基于对一所匿名的私立大学中147名学生进行的调研而成（这些学生都完成了该研究项目）。

en: First generation was defined as not having a parent with a four-year college degree. (14 words)
cn: 第一代大学生，就是其父母都没有四年制大学学历的学生。

en: Most of the first-generation students (59.1 percent) were recipients of Pell Grants, a federal grant for undergraduates with financial need, while this was true only for 8.6 percent of the students with at least one parent with a four-year degree. (40 words)
cn: 佩尔助学金是一项联邦拨款，用于支助经济困难的本科生，大多数第一代大学生（59.1%）都获得过佩尔助学金，而那些父母至少一个人读过本科的学生，只有8.6%获得过佩尔助学金。

en: Their thesis—that a relatively modest intervention could have a big impact—was based on the view that first-generation students may be most lacking not in potential but in practical knowledge about how to deal with the issues that face most college students. (43 words)
cn: 他们的论题是较小的干预能产生巨大的影响，该论题基于立足的观点是：第一代大学生或许最缺乏的不是潜力，而是在处理大多数大学生都会碰到的各种问题上缺乏实践知识。

en: They cite past research by several authors to show that this is the gap that must be narrowed to close the achievement gap. (23 words)
cn: 他们引用了几位前人的研究以说明，要弥合成就差距，这就是必须缩小的缺口。

en: Many first-generation students “struggle to navigate the middle-class culture of higher education, learn the ‘rules of the game,’ and take advantage of college resources,” they write. (26 words)
cn: 他们在文章中写道，很多第一代大学生都在“尽力去适应高等教育的中产阶级文化，掌握‘游戏规则’，利用大学资源”。

en: And this becomes more of a problem when colleges don’t talk about the class advantages and disadvantages of different groups of students. (22 words)
cn: 如果大学生对不同学生群体的阶层优势和劣势避而不谈的话，这就更是个问题了。

en: “Because US colleges and universities seldom acknowledge how social class can affect students’ educational experiences, many first-generation students lack insight about why they are struggling and do not understand how students ‘like them’ can improve.” (35 words)
cn: “美国高校很少承认社会阶层能够影响学生的教育体验，所以很多第一代大学生并不明

en: Even in traditional offices, “the lingua franca of corporate America has gotten much more emotional and much more right-brained than it was 20 years ago,” said Harvard Business School professor Nancy Koehn. (32 words)
cn: 即使是在传统的办公室，“美国企业的通用语与20年前相比更多的靠右脑控制，更感性，”哈佛大学商学院南希·科恩这样说道。

en: She started spinning off examples. (5 words)
cn: 她旋即就抛出了几个例子。

en: “If you and I parachuted back to Fortune 500 companies in 1990, we would see much less frequent use of terms like journey, mission, passion. (25 words)
cn: “如果你我穿越回1990年的世界500强，我们会发现很少有像‘旅程、使命、激情’这样的说法。”

en: There were goals, there were strategies, there were objectives, but we didn’t talk about energy; we didn’t talk about passion.” (20 words)
cn: 我们会说“目标”，“目的”，“战略”，但我们不谈“能源”；不谈“激情”。

en: Koehn pointed out that this new era of corporate vocabulary is very “team”-oriented—and not by coincidence. (17 words)
cn: 科恩指出，新时代下的企业用词具有明显的“团队”导向——这并非偶然。

en: “Let’s not forget sports—in male-dominated corporate America, it’s still a big deal. (13 words)
cn: 别忘了体育运动——在男性主导的美国企业，它仍然是桩大事。

en: It’s not explicitly conscious; it’s the idea that I’m a coach, and you’re my team, and we’re in this together. (20 words)
cn: 它并非明明白白的有意行为，它是一种观念：我是教练，你是队员，我们在一条船上。

en: There are lots and lots of CEOs in very different companies, but most think of themselves as coaches and this is their team and they want to win.” (28 words)
cn: 公司各不相同，老总不计其数；不过，大家都觉得自己是教练，公司是自己团队，得赢。

en: These terms are also intended to infuse work with meaning—and, as Rakesh Khurana, another professor, points out, increase allegiance to the firm. (23 words)
cn: 这些说法也旨在让工作变得有意义——并且，正如另一个教授拉凯什·库拉纳指出，提高对公司的忠诚度。

en: “You have the importation of terminology that historically used to be associated with non-profit organizations and religious organizations: terms like vision, values, passion, and purpose,” said Khurana. (27 words)
cn: 库什纳说：“我们引入的术语在历史上曾经是与非盈利组织和宗教团体相联系的：比如，愿景、价值、激情、旨意等”。

en: This new focus on personal fulfillment can help keep employees motivated amid increasingly loud debates over work-life balance. (18 words)
cn: 关于“如何平衡工作生活”的争论越来越激烈，着眼于个人成就，这种全所未有的关怀有助于激励员工。

en: The “mommy wars” of the 1990s are still going on today, prompting arguments about why women still can’t have it all and books like Sheryl Sandberg’s Lean In, whose title has become a buzzword in its own right. (38 words)
cn: 二十世纪九十年代的“妈咪大战”如今仍在继续，激起了“女性为什么不能兼顾”的讨论，还催生了像谢丽尔·桑德伯格《向前一步》之类书籍的出版，这书名本身就是个流行词。

en: Terms like unplug, offline, life-hack, bandwidth, and capacity are all about setting boundaries between the office and the home. (19 words)
cn: “不插电、下线、生活黑客、带宽、能力”等术语，都是在设定办公与家庭之间的边界。

en: But if your work is your “passion,” you’ll be more likely to devote yourself to it, even if that means going home for dinner and then working long after the kids are in bed. (34 words)
cn: 但如果工作就是你的“激情”，你就会更愿意投身其中，即使这意味着回家吃完饭然后等孩子睡了还要工作很久。

en: But this seems to be the irony of office speak: Everyone makes fun of it, but managers love it, companies depend on it, and regular people willingly absorb it. (29 words)
cn: 但这似乎就使得办公室用语有点搞笑：每个人都拿来调侃，经理喜欢，公司靠它，普通员工乐意去理解它。

en: As a linguist once said, “You can get people to think it’s nonsense at the same time that you buy into it.”  (22 words)
cn: 正如一位语言学家曾说：“你能让人觉得它是无稽之谈，同时你自己竟然对此也买账。”

en: In a workplace that’s fundamentally indifferent to your life and its meaning, office speak can help you figure out how you relate to your work—and how your work defines who you are. (33 words)
cn: 在一个毫不在乎你的生活及其意义的职场，办公室用语能够帮助你弄清自己与工作的联系——还有工作是如何定义了你自己。

en: Many people talked of the 288,000 new jobs the Labor Department reported for June, along with the drop in the unemployment rate to 6.1 percent, as good news. (28 words)
cn: 劳工部6月份公布工作岗位新增28.8万，失业率降至6.1%，很多人都认为这是好消息。

en: And they were right. (4 words)
cn: 并且他们是对的。

en: For now it appears the economy is creating jobs at a decent pace. (13 words)
cn: 目前看来，经济创造就业，似乎速度还很不错。

en: We still have a long way to go to get back to full employment, but at least we are now finally moving forward at a faster pace. (27 words)
cn: 重回充分就业，前路仍然漫长；但至少我们现在终于能以较快的速度前进了。

en: However, there is another important part of the jobs picture that was largely overlooked. (14 words)
cn: 然而，就业形式还有很重要的一面，人们基本上都忽略了。

en: There was a big jump in the number of people who report voluntarily working part-time. (15 words)
cn: 自愿申报兼职工作的人数猛增。

en: This figure is now 830,000 (4.4 percent) above its year ago level. (12 words)
cn: 现在这个数字超过上一年的水平已达83万（增长4.4%）。

en: Before explaining the connection to the Obamacare, it is worth making an important distinction. (14 words)
cn: 在解释其与奥巴马医改的关联之前，值得做出一个重要的区别。

en: Many people who work part-time jobs actually want full-time jobs. (10 words)
cn: 很兼职工作的人，实际上想要全职工作。

en: They take part-time work because this is all they can get. (11 words)
cn: 他们兼职，是因为他们只能如此。

en: An increase in involuntary part-time work is evidence of weakness in the labor market and it means that many people will be having a very hard time making ends meet. (30 words)
cn: 非自愿的兼职工作增加，证明劳动力市场疲软，还意味着有很多人要勉强维持生计都会很难。

en: There was an increase in involuntary part-time in June, but the general direction has been down. (16 words)
cn: 六月份非自愿兼职增加，但总体趋势在下降。

en: Involuntary part-time employment is still far higher than before the recession, but it is down by 640,000(7.9percent) from its year ago level. (22 words)
cn: 非志愿兼职工作远远要比经济衰退前高，但与一年前的水平相比，下降了64万（即7.9%）。

en: We know the difference between voluntary and involuntary part-time employment because people tell us. (14 words)
cn: 我们之所以能区分自愿兼职和非志愿兼职，是因为大家会告诉我们。

en: The survey used by the Labor Department asks people if they worked less than 35 hours in the reference week. (20 words)
cn: 劳工部所做的调查会询问人们在参考周内所工作的时间是否会少于35小时。

en: If the answer is “yes”, they are classified as working part-time. (11 words)
cn: 如果“是”，就认为他们是在做兼职工作。

en: The survey then asks whether they worked less than 35 hours in that week because they wanted to work less than full time or because they had no choice. (29 words)
cn: 接下来会进一步调查他们在那周工作少于35个小时，是因为希望工作时间少于全职还是因为别无选择。

en: They are only classified as voluntary part-time workers if they tell the survey taker they chose to work less than 35 hours a week. (24 words)
cn: 只有他们告诉做调查的人，说自己选择每周工作少于35小时，他们才会归类成是自愿兼职工作者。

en: The issue of voluntary part-time relates to Obamacare because one of the main purposes was to allow people to get insurance outside of employment. (24 words)
cn: 自愿兼职工作的问题之所以与奥巴马医改相关，是因为其主要目的之一是使人们即使没有工作也能得到保险。

en: For many people, especially those with serious health conditions or family members with serious health conditions, before Obamacare the only way to get insurance was through a job that provided health insurance. (32 words)
cn: 对于很多人，尤其是那些自己或者家人有严重健康问题的人来说，奥巴马医改之前，他们获得保险的唯一途径就是找一份有医保的工作。

en: However, Obamacare has allowed more than 12 million people to either get insurance through Medicaid or the exchanges. (18 words)
cn: 然而，奥巴马医改使超过1200万人通过医补或者保险交易市场获得保险。

en: These are people who may previously have felt the need to get a full-time job that provided insurance in order to cover themselves and their families. (26 words)
cn: 这些人他们以前可能觉得需要找一份全职工作，可以为自己或者家人提供保险。

en: With Obamacare there is no longer a link between employment and insurance. (12 words)
cn: 如果有奥巴马医改，就业和保险就不再有任何关联。

en: It’s true that high-school coding classes aren’t essential for learning computer science in college. (14 words)
cn: 事实上，高中编程课对于大学计算机课并非是必要的。

en: Students without experience can catch up after a few introductory courses, said Tom Cortina, the assistant dean at Carnegie Mellon’s School of Computer Science. (24 words)
cn: 没有经验的学生在几节基础课后就可以赶上，卡内基梅隆大学计算机科学学院的助理院长汤姆·科蒂娜这样说道。

en: However, Cortina said, early exposure is beneficial. (7 words)
cn: 不过，柯蒂娜却说，早点接触编程还是有好处的。

en: When younger kids learn computer science, they learn that it’s not just a confusing, endless string of letters and numbers—but a tool to build apps, or create artwork, or test hypotheses. (32 words)
cn: 年龄较小的孩子学计算机的时候，他们能认识到，计算机科学不只是字母和数字组成的无穷无尽的字符串，而是一门工具，可以编写应用程序（apps），创造艺术作品，或者检验假设。

en: It’s not as hard for them to transform their thought processes as it is for older students. (17 words)
cn: 对于他们而言，要转变思维过程，就不像年龄较大的学生那么难。

en: Breaking down problems into bite-sized chunks and using code to solve them becomes normal. (14 words)
cn: 把问题化整为零，再用编程来解决就成了常态。

en: Giving more children this training could increase the number of people interested in the field and help fill the jobs gap, Cortina said. (23 words)
cn: 让更多孩子接受这样的训练可以使更多的人对此领域感兴趣，且有助于填补职位空缺，柯蒂娜说道。

en: Students also benefit from learning something about coding before they get to college, where introductory computer-science classes are packed to the brim, which can drive the less-experienced or -determined students away. (31 words)
cn: 学生在读大学前学一点编程，也是有好处的；因为大学里计算机入门课程排得满满当当，会把经验不足或者意志不坚的学生给吓跑。

en: The Flatiron School, where people pay to learn programming, started as one of the many coding bootcamps that’s become popular for adults looking for a career change. (27 words)
cn: 熨斗学校是一所编程培训机构，创办之初是众多编程训练营中的一个，如今在那些寻求职业转型的成年人中广受欢迎。

en: The high-schoolers get the same curriculum, but “we try to gear lessons toward things they’re interested in,” said Victoria Friedman, an instructor. (22 words)
cn: 高中生的教学大纲也一样，但“我们在尽力根据他们的兴趣来调整课程”， 维多利亚·弗里德曼老师这样说道。

en: For instance, one of the apps the students are developing suggests movies based on your mood. (16 words)
cn: 例如，学生在开发的一款应用程序能根据你心情来推荐电影给你。

en: The students in the Flatiron class probably won’t drop out of high school and build the next Facebook. (18 words)
cn: 熨斗学校的学生可能不会从高中辍学再去创办下一个脸书（Facebook）。

en: Programming languages have a quick turnover, so the “Ruby on Rails” language they learned may not even be relevant by the time they enter the job market. (27 words)
cn: 编程语言更替很快，他们学到的Ruby on Rails语言，等找工作的时候甚至可能已经不合时宜了。

en: But the skills they learn—how to think logically through a problem and organize the results—apply to any coding language, said Deborah Seehorn, an education consultant for the state of North Carolina. (33 words)
cn: 但他们所学的技能——如何有逻辑地想明白一个问题并整理结果——适用于任何编程语言，南卡罗莱纳州的教育顾问德博拉·希霍恩这样说道。

en: Indeed, the Flatiron students might not go into IT at all. (11 words)
cn: 诚然，熨斗学校的学生可能根本就不会进到信息技术行业。

en: But creating a future army of coders is not the sole purpose of the classes. (15 words)
cn: 但创造一支未来的程序员队伍并非是这些课堂的唯一目的。

en: These kids are going to be surrounded by computers—in their pockets, in their offices, in their homes—for the rest of their lives. (24 words)
cn: 这些孩子未来的生活会被电脑包围，口袋里、办公室、家里都是。

en: The younger they learn how computers think, how to coax the machine into producing what they want—the earlier they learn that they have the power to do that—the better. (31 words)
cn: 他们越早学到计算机的思维方式，学到耐着性子驾驭这些机器去做出自己想要的东西，越早知道自己有这样的能力，越好。

en: Biologists estimate that as many as 2 million lesser prairie chickens—a kind of bird living on stretching grasslands—once lent red to the often gray landscape of the midwestern and southwestern United States. (34 words)
cn: 生物学家预测，会有多达200万的小草原松鸡（一种鸟，栖息在绵延的草场上），它们曾给美国中西部和西南部常年灰暗的风景增添了一抹红色。

en: But just some 22,000 birds remain today, occupying about 16% of the species’ historic range. (15 words)
cn: 但现在仅存约22000只，栖息在该物种历史范围的16%的地方。

en: The crash was a major reason the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service (USFWS) decided to formally list the bird as threatened. (21 words)
cn: 主要因为鸟类在数量上急剧减少，美国鱼类及野生动物管理局决定正式将其列为受到威胁的物种。

en: “The lesser prairie chicken is in a desperate situation,” said USFWS Director Daniel Ashe. (14 words)
cn: “小草原松鸡的处境非常艰难”，美国鱼类及野生动物管理局局长丹尼尔·阿什这样说道。

en: Some environmentalists, however, were disappointed. (5 words)
cn: 然而，一些环保主义者却深感失望。

en: They had pushed the agency to designate the bird as “endangered,” a status that gives federal officials greater regulatory power to crack down on threats. (25 words)
cn: 他们曾施压管理局，要把这一鸟类命名为“濒危物种”，这一地位可以让联邦官员有更大的监管权力打击威胁。

en: But Ashe and others argued that the “threatened” tag gave the federal government flexibility to try out new, potentially less confrontational conservations approaches. (23 words)
cn: 但阿什等人认为，“受到威胁的物种”这一标签可以让联邦政府更灵活地去尝试新的，可能不那么具有对抗性的保护措施。

en: In particular, they called for forging closer collaborations with western state governments, which are often uneasy with federal actions and with the private landowners who control an estimated 95% of the prairie chicken’s habitat. (34 words)
cn: 他们特别呼吁不仅要加强与西部各州政府更紧密的合作，尽管这些州政府通常和联邦政府的行动格格不入；还要加强与私人业主更紧密的合作，草原松鸡约95%的栖息地都是他们的。

en: Under the plan, for example, the agency said it would not prosecute landowners or businesses that unintentionally kill, harm, or disturb the bird, as long as they had signed a range-wide management plan to restore prairie chicken habitat. (38 words)
cn: 比如，据此计划，管理局表示，将不予起诉那些非故意惊扰、伤害、或者杀死该鸟类的业主或公司，前提是他们已经签订了恢复草原松鸡栖息地的范围管理计划。

en: Negotiated by USFWS and the states, the plan requires individuals and businesses that damage habitat as part of their operations to pay into a fund to replace every acre destroyed with 2 new acres of suitable habitat. (37 words)
cn: 该计划经过管理局和各州政府协商之后，要求那些在其经营过程中破坏栖息地的个人和企业需向一项基金支付款项，破坏一英亩地，就得赔上两英亩适宜的新栖息地。

en: The fund will also be used to compensate landowners who set aside habitat. (13 words)
cn: 该基金也将用来补偿那些留出栖息地的土地拥有者。

en: USFWS also set an interim goal of restoring prairie chicken populations to an annual average of 67,000 birds over the next 10 years. (23 words)
cn: 美国鱼类及野生动物管理局也设定了一个短期目标，未来10年要把草原松鸡的数量恢复到年平均6.7万只。

en: And it gives the Western Association of Fish and Wildlife Agencies (WAFWA), a coalition of state agencies, the job of monitoring progress. (22 words)
cn: 并且管理局把过程监管的工作交给了一个州级机构联盟——鱼类及野生动物机构西部联合会。

en: Overall, the idea is to let “states remain in the driver’s seat for managing the species,” Ashe said. (18 words)
cn: 总之，想的是要让“各州政府负责管理好这一物种”，阿什说。

en: Not everyone buys the win-win rhetoric. (6 words)
cn: 说是双赢，并非每个人都吃这一套。

en: Some Congress members are trying to block the plan, and at least a dozen industry groups, four states, and three environmental groups are challenging it in federal court. (28 words)
cn: 一些国会议员就在试图阻止这一计划，并且至少有十几个行业团体，四个州，三个环保组织会在联邦法庭上对此提起质疑。

en: Not surprisingly, industry groups and states generally argue it goes too far; environmentalists say it doesn’t go far enough. (19 words)
cn: 不出意外，行业团体和各州政府普遍认为它做得太过；而环保主义者则说它做得不够。

en: “The federal government is giving responsibility for managing the bird to the same industries that are pushing it to extinction,” says biologist Jay Lininger. (24 words)
cn: “联邦政府把这一鸟类的管理责任给到的，恰恰是推动其走向灭亡的行业”，生物学家杰伊·林宁格说。

en: That everyone’s too busy these days is a cliché. (9 words)
cn: 现在大家都太忙，可谓是老生常谈。

en: But one specific complaint is made especially mournfully: There’s never any time to read. (14 words)
cn: 但这样的抱怨中有一点具体的尤其值得悲叹：永远没有时间阅读。

en: What makes the problem thornier is that the usual time-management techniques don’t seem sufficient. (14 words)
cn: 让这一问题变得更加棘手的是，常规的时间管理技巧似乎并不奏效。

en: The web’s full of articles offering tips on making time to read: “Give up TV” or “Carry a book with you at all times.”  (24 words)
cn: 网上充斥着各种文章，指点我们找时间阅读的各种门道：“放弃电视”或者“随身带本书”。

en: But in my experience, using such methods to free up the odd 30 minutes doesn’t work. (16 words)
cn: 但就我的经验而言，用这种办法挤出个零碎的30分钟，并没有什么作用。

en: Sit down to read and the flywheel of work-related thoughts keeps spinning—or else you’re so exhausted that a challenging book’s the last thing you need. (26 words)
cn: 坐下，读书，大脑还转个不停，思考工作有关的问题——否则你会精疲力竭，再挑战读一本书就成了你最不想做的事情。

en: The modern mind, Tim Parks, a novelist and critic, writes, “is overwhelmingly inclined toward communication… (15 words)
cn: 现代人的大脑，小说家兼批评家蒂姆·帕克斯写道，“非常愿意交流……

en: It is not simply that one is interrupted; it is that one is actually inclined to interruption.” (17 words)
cn: 根本不是人们被打断了；而是人们真的愿意打断。”

en: Deep reading requires not just time, but a special kind of time which can’t be obtained merely by becoming more efficient. (21 words)
cn: 深度阅读需要的不只是时间，而是一段特殊的时间，只靠提高效率是得不到的。

en: In fact, “becoming more efficient” is part of the problem. (10 words)
cn: 事实上，“提高效率”就是部分问题所在。

en: Thinking of time as a resource to be maximised means you approach it instrumentally, judging any given moment as well spent only in so far as it advances progress toward some goal. (32 words)
cn: 把时间看成一种资源，可以最大限度利用，这就意味着你对待时间太功利，认为只要每分每秒都能催你奋斗，朝某个目标前进，这个时间就花得很值。

en: Immersive reading, by contrast, depends on being willing to risk inefficiency, goallessness, even time-wasting. (14 words)
cn: 相比之下，沉浸式阅读需要我们自愿承担风险，可能收效甚微，漫无目的，甚至是浪费时间。

en: Try to slot it in as a to-do list item and you’ll manage only goal-focused reading—useful, sometimes, but not the most fulfilling kind. (24 words)
cn: 把这种阅读方式插进自己的任务清单，你只能勉强做到有目的的阅读——有时候有

en: “The future comes at us like empty bottles along an unstoppable and nearly infinite conveyor belt,” writes Gary Eberle in his book Sacred Time, and “we feel a pressure to fill these different-sized bottles (days, hours, minutes) as they pass, for if they get by without being filled, we will have wasted them.”  (53 words)
cn: “未来向我们走来，如一个个空瓶，在几乎没有尽头永不停歇的传送带上，”盖利·埃博拉在其《神圣时间》中这样写道，“时间流过，我们要把这些不同形状的各种瓶子（日子、小时、分钟）填满，倍感压力，因为如果过去了却没有填满，我们就觉得自己浪费了时间”。

en: No mind-set could be worse for losing yourself in a book. (11 words)
cn: 对沉浸式阅读，没有什么观念比这还要更糟糕的了。

en: So what does work? (4 words)
cn: 那到底什么能管用？

en: Perhaps surprisingly, scheduling regular times for reading. (7 words)
cn: 或许有点让人吃惊，安排固定的阅读时间才管用。

en: You’d think this might fuel the efficiency mind-set, but in fact, Eberle notes, such ritualistic behaviour helps us “step outside time’s flow” into “soul time.”  (25 words)
cn: 你或许认为，这或许会助长那种追求效率的观念，但事实上，埃博拉指出，这种具有仪式感的行为能帮助尾门“从流逝的时间中走出来”，进入“内心时间”。

en: You could limit distractions by reading only physical books, or on single-purpose e-readers. (13 words)
cn: 要避免分心，我们可以阅读纸质书籍，或者那种只有单一功能的电子阅读器来。

en: “Carry a book with you at all times” can actually work, too—providing you dip in often enough, so that reading becomes the default state from which you temporarily surface to take care of business, before dropping back down. (39 words)
cn: “总是随身带本书”实际上也真的管用——只要你沉下来翻书够勤，阅读就会成为默认状态，你可以从中暂时浮出来处理事情，然后再沉浸其中。

en: On a really good day, it no longer feels as if you’re “making time to read,” but just reading, and making time for everything else. (25 words)
cn: 在某个非常好的日子，我们似乎感觉再没有必要“抽空阅读”，只是阅读，然后抽空做点别的事情。

en: Against a backdrop of drastic changes in economy and population structure, younger Americans are drawing a new 21st-century road map to success, a latest poll has found. (27 words)
cn: 最近的民意调查发现，在经济和人口结构急剧变化的情况下，美国的年轻人正在描绘一副新的21世纪的成功路线图。

en: Across generational lines, Americans continue to prize many of the same traditional milestones of a successful life, including getting married, having children, owning a home, and retiring in their sixties. (30 words)
cn: 跨越代际线，美国人依然珍视成功人生中很多相同的传统的里程碑事件，包括结婚、生子、买房、六十岁退休。

en: But while young and old mostly agree on what constitutes the finish line of a fulfilling life, they offer strikingly different paths for reaching it. (25 words)
cn: 但什么才算是成功人生的终点线，尽管年轻一辈和老一辈人对此看法几乎一致，如何抵达，他们要走的道路却大相径庭。

en: Young people who are still getting started in life were more likely than older adults to prioritize personal fulfillment in their work, to believe they will advance their careers most by regularly changing jobs, to favor communities with more public services and a faster pace of life, to agree that couples should be financially secure before getting married or having children, and to maintain that children are best served by two parents working outside the home, the survey found. (79 words)
cn: 调查发现：和稍年长的成年人相比，年轻人处于人生起步阶段，他们更可能会在工作中优先考虑个人成就感；更可能会认为，频繁换工作最能促进职业发展；更可能会青睐那种公共服务更多且生活节奏更快的社区；更可能会认同夫妻在结婚生子之前应该有经济保障；更可能会坚持认为，父母双方都有工作才能最好地养育孩子。

en: From career to community and family, these contrasts suggest that in the aftermath of the searing Great Recession, those just starting out in life are defining priorities and expectations that will increasingly spread through virtually all aspects of American life, from consumer preferences to housing patterns to politics. (48 words)
cn: 从事业到家庭和社区，这些差异表明，在大萧条的阵痛之后，人生才刚刚起步的那些人，他们正在定义大家所看重的和期望的东西，这些东西越来越体现在几乎是美国生活的方方面面，从消费者偏爱到住宅模式，再到政治，无所不在。

en: Young and old converge on one key point: Overwhelming majorities of both groups said they believe it is harder for young people today to get started in life than it was for earlier generations. (34 words)
cn: 新老两辈人都认同一个关键点：两个群体的压倒性多数都认为，现在的年轻人比早几代人要更难起步。

en: While younger people are somewhat more optimistic than their elders about the prospects for those starting out today, big majorities in both groups believe those “just getting started in life” face a tougher climb than earlier generations in reaching such signpost achievements as securing a good-paying job, starting a family, managing debt, and finding affordable housing. (56 words)
cn: 对于现在刚起步的人前途的看法，年轻的要比年长的人要多少更乐观一点，但两组人

en: Pete Schneider considers the climb tougher today. (7 words)
cn: 皮特·施耐德觉得现在要攀登上去更难。

en: Schneider, a 27-year-old auto technician from the Chicago suburbs, says he struggled to find a job after graduating from college. (20 words)
cn: 施耐德27岁，是芝加哥郊区的汽车技术员，他说自己大学毕业后很费力才找到了工作。

en: Even now that he is working steadily, he said, “I can’t afford to pay my monthly mortgage payments on my own, so I have to rent rooms out to people to make that happen.” (34 words)
cn: 即使他工作稳定，他说，“我靠自己挣的钱也付不起按揭贷款的月供，我得把房间租出去才能”。

en: Looking back, he is struck that his parents could provide a comfortable life for their children even though neither had completed college when he was young. (26 words)
cn: 回首过去，他深感震撼的是，在他小时候父母虽然都没念完大学，却仍能为孩子们提供舒适的生活。

en: “I still grew up in an upper middle-class home with parents who didn’t have college degrees,” Schneider said. (18 words)
cn: "我仍然是在一个上层中产阶级家庭长大的，而我的父母并没有大学文凭，"施耐德说。

en: “I don’t think people are capable of that anymore.” (9 words)
cn: "我觉得人们再也做不到那样了。"

en: Every Saturday morning, at 9 am, more than 50,000 runners set off to run 5km around their local park. (19 words)
cn: 每周六早上九点，超过五万名跑者便会在各自当地的公园里起跑，跑完五公里。

en: The Parkrun phenomenon began with a dozen friends and has inspired 400 events in the UK and more abroad. (19 words)
cn: 公园跑这一现象始于十几个朋友，如今已在英国催生出四百场活动，在国外则更多。

en: Events are free, staffed by thousands of volunteers. (8 words)
cn: 这些活动免费参加，由成千上万名志愿者提供服务。

en: Runners range from four years old to grandparents; their times range from Andrew Baddeley's world record 13 minutes 48 seconds up to an hour. (24 words)
cn: 跑者的年龄跨度从四岁的孩童到祖父母辈都有；他们完成的时间从安德鲁·巴德利创下的13分48秒世界纪录，到将近一小时不等。

en: Parkrun is succeeding where London's Olympic "legacy" is failing. (9 words)
cn: 公园跑正做得风生水起，而伦敦奥运的"遗产"却在节节败退。

en: Ten years ago on Monday, it was announced that the Games of the 30th Olympiad would be in London. (19 words)
cn: 十年前的星期一，第三十届奥林匹克运动会宣布将在伦敦举行。

en: Planning documents pledged that the great legacy of the Games would be to lever a nation of sport lovers away from their couches. (23 words)
cn: 规划文件郑重承诺，奥运会留下的伟大遗产将是撬动一个热爱体育的民族离开他们的沙发。

en: The population would be fitter, healthier and produce more winners. (10 words)
cn: 国民将更加健壮、更加健康，并产生更多的胜利者。

en: It has not happened. (4 words)
cn: 这一切并未发生。

en: The number of adults doing weekly sport did rise, by nearly 2 million in the run-up to 2012—but the general population was growing faster. (25 words)
cn: 每周进行体育锻炼的成年人数量确实上升了，在2012年奥运会前夕增加了近两百万——但总人口的增长速度更快。

en: Worse, the numbers are now falling at an accelerating rate. (10 words)
cn: 更糟的是，如今这些数字正以越来越快的速度下降。

en: The opposition claims primary school pupils doing at least two hours of sport a week have nearly halved. (18 words)
cn: 反对党声称，每周至少运动两小时的小学生人数几乎减半。

en: Obesity has risen among adults and children. (7 words)
cn: 成年人和儿童中的肥胖率都在上升。

en: Official retrospections continue as to why London 2012 failed to "inspire a generation." (13 words)
cn: 官方的反思仍在继续，追问2012年伦敦奥运会为何没能"激励一代人"。

en: The success of Parkrun offers answers. (6 words)
cn: 公园跑的成功提供了答案。

en: Parkrun is not a race but a time trial: Your only competitor is the clock. (15 words)
cn: 公园跑不是一场竞赛，而是一场计时赛：你唯一的对手就是时钟。

en: The ethos welcomes anybody. (4 words)
cn: 其理念欢迎任何人。

en: There is as much joy over a puffed-out first-timer being clapped over the line as there is about top talent shining. (21 words)
cn: 一个气喘吁吁的新手在掌声中冲过终点线，与顶尖高手光芒四射的表现，所获得的喜悦别无二致。

en: The Olympic bidders, by contrast, wanted to get more people doing sports and to produce more elite athletes. (18 words)
cn: 相形之下，奥运申办方既想让更多人参与体育运动，又想要培养出更多的精英运动员。

en: The dual aim was mixed up: The stress on success over taking part was intimidating for newcomers. (17 words)
cn: 这两重目标混为一谈：对成功的强调压倒了对参与的重视，令新手望而生畏。

en: Indeed, there is something a little absurd in the state getting involved in the planning of such a fundamentally "grassroots" concept as community sports associations. (25 words)
cn: 的确，国家参与规划像社区体育协会这种本质上属于"草根"的概念，本身就是一件有些荒唐的事。

en: If there is a role for government, it should really be getting involved in providing common goods—making sure there is space for playing fields and the money to pave tennis and netball courts, and encouraging the provision of all these activities in schools. (44 words)
cn: 如果说政府能扮演什么角色，那其实应该是在提供公共产品上有所介入——确保有足够的场地作为运动场，有资金来铺设网球场和篮网球球场，并鼓励在学校里开展所有这些体育活动。

en: But successive governments have presided over selling green spaces, squeezing money from local authorities and declining attention on sport in education. (21 words)
cn: 然而，历届政府却坐视绿地被出售、挤压地方政府的资金，并对体育在教育中的地位日渐淡漠。

en: Instead of wordy, worthy strategies, future governments need to do more to provide the conditions for sport to thrive. (19 words)
cn: 未来的政府不应再空谈那些冗长而冠冕堂皇的战略，而是要实实在在地多做些事，为体育的蓬勃发展创造条件。

en: Or at least not make them worse. (7 words)
cn: 或者至少，不要雪上加霜。

en: With so much focus on children's use of screens, it's easy for parents to forget about their own screen use. (20 words)
cn: 人们如此关注孩子使用电子屏幕的问题，父母便很容易忘记自己使用屏幕这件事。

en: "Tech is designed to really suck you in," says Jenny Radesky in her study of digital play, "and digital products are there to promote maximal engagement. (26 words)
cn: "科技本就是设计来把你牢牢吸住的，"珍妮·拉德斯基在其关于数字娱乐的研究中说道，"数字产品存在的目的就是尽可能让人沉浸其中。

en: It makes it hard to disengage, and leads to a lot of bleed-over into the family routine." (17 words)
cn: 这让人很难抽身，并导致大量的溢出效应渗入家庭日常生活。"

en: Radesky has studied the use of mobile phones and tablets at mealtimes by giving mother-child pairs a food-testing exercise. (19 words)
cn: 拉德斯基通过让多对母子完成一项食物品尝任务，对进餐时段手机和平板电脑的使用情况进行了研究。

en: She found that mothers who used devices during the exercise started 20 per cent fewer verbal and 39 per cent fewer nonverbal interactions with their children. (26 words)
cn: 她发现，在任务过程中使用设备的母亲与孩子的言语交流减少了百分之二十，非言语交流减少了百分之三十九。

en: During a separate observation, she saw that phones became a source of tension in the family. (16 words)
cn: 在一次单独的观察中，她看到手机成了家庭矛盾的一个源头

en: Parents would be looking at their emails while the children would be making excited bids for their attention. (18 words)
cn: 父母往往在查看邮件，而孩子则在兴奋地试图争取他们的注意力。

en: Infants are wired to look at parents' faces to try to understand their world, and if those faces are blank and unresponsive—as they often are when absorbed in a device—it can be extremely disconcerting for the children. (39 words)
cn: 婴幼儿天生就会去看父母的脸，试图理解自己的世界，而如果那些面孔毫无表情、毫无反应——就像沉浸在电子设备中时常常出现的那样——这会让孩子感到极为困惑不安。

en: Radesky cites the "still face experiment" devised by developmental psychologist Ed Tronick in the 1970s. (15 words)
cn: 拉德斯基引用了发展心理学家埃德·特罗尼克在20世纪70年代设计的"无表情面孔实验"。

en: In it, a mother is asked to interact with her child in a normal way before putting on a blank expression and not giving them any visual social feedback: The child becomes increasingly distressed as she tries to capture her mother's attention. (42 words)
cn: 在实验中，母亲先是以正常方式与孩子互动，随后被要求摆出毫无表情的面孔，不再给孩子任何视觉上的社交反馈：当孩子试图抓住母亲的注意力时，会变得越来越痛苦。

en: "Parents don't have to be exquisitely present at all times, but there needs to be a balance and parents need to be responsive and sensitive to a child's verbal or nonverbal expressions of an emotional need," says Radesky. (38 words)
cn: "父母不必在所有时刻都细致入微地陪伴在旁，但需要有一个平衡，父母需要对孩子在情感需求上的言语或非言语表达做出回应，保持敏感，"拉德斯基说。

en: On the other hand, Tronick himself is concerned that the worries about kids' use of screens are born out of an "oppressive ideology that demands that parents should always be interacting" with their children: "It's based on a somewhat fantasized, very white, very upper-middle-class ideology that says if you're failing to expose your child to 30,000 words you are neglecting them." (61 words)
cn: 另一方面，特罗尼克本人则担心，对孩子使用电子屏幕的种种忧虑源自一种"压迫性的意识形态，这种观念要求父母必须时刻与孩子互动"："它建立在一种近乎臆想、非常白人化、非常上层中产阶级的意识形态之上，认为如果你没能让孩子接触到三万个词汇，你就是忽视了他们。"

en: Tronick believes that just because a child isn't learning from the screen doesn't mean there's no value to it—particularly if it gives parents time to have a shower, do housework or simply have a break from their child. (39 words)
cn: 特罗尼克认为，仅仅因为孩子没有从屏幕中学到东西，并不意味着屏幕就没有价值——尤其是当它能让父母有时间去冲个澡、做点家务，或者只是从孩子身边抽身片刻的时候。

en: Parents, he says, can get a lot out of using their devices to speak to a friend or get some work out of the way. (25 words)
cn: 他说，父母能通过使用电子设备和朋友聊聊天，或者处理掉一些工作，从中获益良多。

en: This can make them feel happier, which lets them be more available to their child the rest of the time. (20 words)
cn: 这能让他们感到更快乐，从而在其余时间里能更好地陪伴孩子。

en: Today, widespread social pressure to immediately go to college in conjunction with increasingly high expectations in a fast-moving world often causes students to completely overlook the possibility of taking a gap year. (32 words)
cn: 如今，高中一毕业就要立刻上大学的普遍社会压力，加之这个快节奏世界里日益高涨的期望值，常常导致学生们完全忽视了度过间隔年的可能性。

en: After all, if everyone you know is going to college in the fall, it seems silly to stay back a year, doesn't it? (23 words)
cn: 毕竟，如果你认识的每一个人秋天都要去上大学，自己却晚一年才去，那看起来不是件很傻的事吗？

en: And after going to school for 12 years, it doesn't feel natural to spend a year doing something that isn't academic. (21 words)
cn: 况且读了12年书之后，要花一年时间去做与学业无关的事，感觉也很不自然。

en: But while this may be true, it's not a good enough reason to condemn gap years. (16 words)
cn: 然而，即便这些感受不无道理，也不足以成为一个充分理由来否定间隔年。

en: There's always a constant fear of falling behind everyone else on the socially perpetuated "race to the finish line," whether that be toward graduate school, medical school or a lucrative career. (31 words)
cn: 人们总是有一种挥之不去的恐惧，害怕在这场社会上长期存在的"冲向终点线"的竞赛中落后于他人，无论这终点是研究生院、医学院还是一份高薪职业。

en: But despite common misconceptions, a gap year does not hinder the success of academic pursuits—in fact, it probably enhances it. (21 words)
cn: 然而，尽管人们普遍存在误解，间隔年并不会阻碍学业上的成功——事实上，它很可能还会促进学业进步。

en: Studies from the United States and Australia show that students who take a gap year are generally better prepared for and perform better in college than those who do not. (30 words)
cn: 美国和澳大利亚的研究都表明，有过间隔年经历的学生在大学里通常比没有间隔年的学生准备得更充分，表现也更好。

en: Rather than pulling students back, a gap year pushes them ahead by preparing them for independence, new responsibilities and environmental changes—all things that first-year students often struggle with the most. (31 words)
cn: 间隔年非但没有拖学生的后腿，反而通过让他们为独立生活、新的责任和环境变化做好准备而推动他们向前——所有这些恰恰都是大一新生最难以适应的。

en: Gap year experiences can lessen the blow when it comes to adjusting to college and being thrown into a brand new environment, making it easier to focus on academics and activities rather than acclimation blunders. (35 words)
cn: 间隔年的经历可以缓解适应大学生活、被抛入一个全新环境时所受的冲击，让学生更容易专注于学业和活动，而不必为适应过程中的种种磕绊所困扰。

en: If you're not convinced of the inherent value in taking a year off to explore interests, then consider its financial impact on future academic choices. (25 words)
cn: 如果你还不信服花一年时间去探索兴趣本身就有其价值，那就想想它在经济层面对未来学业选择的影响吧。

en: According to the National Center for Education Statistics, nearly 80 percent of college students end up changing their majors at least once. (22 words)
cn: 根据国家教育统计中心的数据，近百分之八十的大学生在毕业前至少转过一次专业。

en: This isn't surprising, considering the basic mandatory high school curriculum leaves students with a poor understanding of the vast academic possibilities that await them in college. (26 words)
cn: 这并不奇怪，因为高中基础的必修课程让学生对大学里等待他们的广阔学术可能性知之甚少。

en: Many students find themselves listing one major on their college applications, but switching to another after taking college classes. (19 words)
cn: 许多学生在大学申请时填报的是一个专业，但上了一些大学课程后，却发现自己又转到了另一个专业。

en: It's not necessarily a bad thing, but depending on the school, it can be costly to make up credits after switching too late in the game. (26 words)
cn: 这不一定是件坏事，但取决于不同学校，如果转专业的时间太晚，补修学分可能要付出高昂的代价。

en: At Boston College, for example, you would have to complete an extra year were you to switch to the nursing school from another department. (24 words)
cn: 以波士顿学院为例，如果你从其他院系转入护理学院，就得多上一年才能完成学业。

en: Taking a gap year to figure things out initially can help prevent stress and save money later on. (18 words)
cn: 用间隔年一开始就把事情理清楚，可以帮助避免压力，也能在日后省下不少钱。

en: Though often viewed as a problem for western states, the growing frequency of wildfires is a national concern because of its impact on federal tax dollars, says Professor Max Moritz, a specialist in fire ecology and management. (37 words)
cn: 尽管日益频发的野火常被视为西部各州的问题，但由于其对联邦税收的影响，这已成为一个全国性的关切，火灾生态与管理专家马克斯·莫里茨教授表示。

en: In 2015, the US Forest Service for the first time spent more than half of its $5.5 billion annual budget fighting fires—nearly double the percentage it spent on such efforts 20 years ago. (34 words)
cn: 2015年，美国林务局首次将其55亿美元年度预算的一半以上用于扑救火灾——这一比例几乎是20年前的两倍。

en: In effect, fewer federal funds today are going towards the agency's other work—such as forest conservation, watershed and cultural resources management, and infrastructure upkeep—that affect the lives of all Americans. (32 words)
cn: 其后果是，如今用于该机构其他工作的联邦资金变少了——比如森林保护、流域与文化资源管理以及基础设施维护——而这些工作影响着所有美国人的生活。

en: Another nationwide concern is whether public funds from other agencies are going into construction in fire-prone districts. (17 words)
cn: 另一个全国性的担忧是，其他机构的公共资金是否正在流入火灾易发地区的建设工程。

en: As Moritz puts it, how often are federal dollars building homes that are likely to be lost to a wildfire? (20 words)
cn: 正如莫里茨所言，联邦资金有多少次是在建造那些很可能被野火吞噬的房屋？

en: "It's already a huge problem from a public expenditure perspective for the whole country," he says. (16 words)
cn: "从公共支出的角度看，这对整个国家已经是一个巨大的问题，"他说。

en: "We need to take a magnifying glass to that. (9 words)
cn: "我们需要用放大镜来审视这件事。

en: Like, 'Wait a minute, is this OK?' (7 words)
cn: 比方说，'等等，这样真的没问题吗？'

en: Do we want instead to redirect those funds to concentrate on lower-hazard parts of the landscape?" (16 words)
cn: 我们是否转而想把那些资金重新导向，集中投到这片大地上风险较低的区域？"

en: Such a view would require a corresponding shift in the way US society today views fire, researchers say. (18 words)
cn: 研究人员表示，这样的观点需要当今美国社会看待火灾的方式也相应地转变。

en: For one thing, conversations about wildfires need to be more inclusive. (11 words)
cn: 首先，关于野火的讨论需要更具包容性。

en: Over the past decade, the focus has been on climate change—how the warming of the Earth from greenhouse gases is leading to conditions that worsen fires. (27 words)
cn: 过去十年间，焦点一直放在气候变化上——即温室气体导致的地球变暖如何引发各种加剧火灾的状况。

en: While climate is a key element, Moritz says, it shouldn't come at the expense of the rest of the equation. (20 words)
cn: 莫里茨说，虽然气候是一个关键因素，但不应该以牺牲等式中的其他部分为代价。

en: "The human systems and the landscapes we live on are linked, and the interactions go both ways," he says. (19 words)
cn: "人类系统与我们赖以生存的地貌是相互关联的，而且这种作用是双向的，"他说。

en: Failing to recognize that, he notes, leads to "an overly simplified view of what the solutions might be. (18 words)
cn: 他指出，认识不到这一点，就会导致"对可能的解决方案持有一种过度简化的看法。

en: Our perception of the problem and of what the solution is becomes very limited." (14 words)
cn: 我们对问题的认知以及对解决方案的认识就会变得非常局限。"

en: At the same time, people continue to treat fire as an event that needs to be wholly controlled and unleashed only out of necessity, says Professor Balch at the University of Colorado. (32 words)
cn: 与此同时，人们继续把火灾视为需要完全控制、只在万不得已时才能任其燃烧的事件，科罗拉多大学的巴尔奇教授表示。

en: But acknowledging fire's inevitable presence in human life is an attitude crucial to developing the laws, policies, and practices that make it as safe as possible, she says. (28 words)
cn: 但她说，承认火灾在人类生活中必然存在，这种态度对于制定能尽可能确保安全的法律、政策和实践至关重要。

en: "We've disconnected ourselves from living with fire," Balch says. (9 words)
cn: "我们已经把自己从与火共存中割裂开了，"巴尔奇说。

en: "It is really important to understand and try and tease out what is the human connection with fire today." (19 words)
cn: "理解并努力梳理出当今人类与火的联结究竟是什么，这真的非常重要。"

en: It is curious that Stephen Koziatek feels almost as though he has to justify his efforts to give his students a better future. (23 words)
cn: 说来奇怪，斯蒂芬·科齐亚克几乎觉得，他必须为自己努力给学生一个更好未来的做法辩护。

en: Mr. Koziatek is part of something pioneering. (7 words)
cn: 科齐亚克先生正在参与一项开创性的事业。

en: He is a teacher at a New Hampshire high school where learning is not something of books and tests and mechanical memorization, but practical. (24 words)
cn: 他是新罕布什尔州一所高中的老师，在那里，学习不是书本、考试和机械记忆那回事，而是实践性的。

en: When did it become accepted wisdom that students should be able to name the 13th president of the United States but be utterly overwhelmed by a broken bike chain? (29 words)
cn: 学生应该能说出美国第13任总统的名字，却在一条断掉的自行车链条面前完全束手无策——这种观念何时成了公认的常理？

en: As Koziatek know, there is learning in just about everything. (10 words)
cn: 正如科齐亚克所知，几乎任何事情中都有值得学习的东西。

en: Nothing is necessarily gained by forcing students to learn geometry at a graffitied desk stuck with generations of discarded chewing gum. (21 words)
cn: 让学生坐在一张涂满涂鸦、粘着一代又一代被人丢弃的口香糖的课桌前学习几何，未必有什么收获。

en: They can also learn geometry by assembling a bicycle. (9 words)
cn: 他们也可以通过组装一辆自行车来学习几何。

en: But he's also found a kind of insidious prejudice. (9 words)
cn: 但他也发现了一种暗中潜伏的偏见。

en: Working with your hands is seen as almost a mark of inferiority. (12 words)
cn: 靠双手劳动几乎被看作一种低人一等的标志。

en: School in the family of vocational education "have that stereotype...that it's for kids who can't make it academically," he says. (20 words)
cn: 他说，职业教育类的学校"身上贴着那种刻板标签……觉得那是给学业上成不了器的孩子准备的"。

en: On one hand, that viewpoint is a logical product of America's evolution. (12 words)
cn: 一方面，这种观点是美国发展演变的合乎逻辑的产物。

en: Manufacturing is not the economic engine that it once was. (10 words)
cn: 制造业已不再是过去那样的经济引擎。

en: The job security that the US economy once offered to high school graduates has largely evaporated. (16 words)
cn: 美国经济曾经能为高中毕业生提供的就业保障，如今已基本消失殆尽。

en: More education is the new principle. (6 words)
cn: 接受更多的教育成了新的准则。

en: We want more for our kids, and rightfully so. (9 words)
cn: 我们想为自己的孩子谋求更多，这也理所当然。

en: But the headlong push into bachelor's degrees for all—and the subtle devaluing of anything less—misses an important point: That's not the only thing the American economy needs. (29 words)
cn: 但是，贸然推动人人都去拿学士学位——并且暗中贬低任何低于学士学位的东西——忽略了一个重要之处：那并非美国经济唯一需要的东西。

en: Yes, a bachelor's degree opens more doors. (7 words)
cn: 没错，学士学位会打开更多扇门。

en: But even now, 54 percent of the jobs in the country are middle-skill jobs, such as construction and high-skill manufacturing. (20 words)
cn: 然而即便是现在，这个国家仍有54%的工作属于中等技能岗位，比如建筑和高技能制造业。

en: But only 44 percent of workers are adequately trained. (9 words)
cn: 但只有44%的劳动者获得了充分的培训。

en: In other words, at a time when the working class has turned the country on its political head, frustrated that the opportunity that once defined America is vanishing, one obvious solution is staring us in the face. (37 words)
cn: 换言之，在这样一个时代——工人阶级已经在政治上彻底颠覆了这个国家，他们因曾经定义美国的那种机会正在消逝而感到愤怒——一个显而易见的解决方案正摆在我们眼前。

en: There is a gap in working-class jobs, but the workers who need those jobs most aren't equipped to do them. (20 words)
cn: 工人阶级岗位存在着缺口，但最需要这些工作的劳动者却不具备从事这些岗位的能力。

en: Koziatek's Manchester School of Technology High School is trying to fill that gap. (13 words)
cn: 科齐亚克的曼彻斯特技术高中正在努力填补这一缺口。

en: Koziatek's school is a wake-up call. (6 words)
cn: 科齐亚克的学校是一记警钟。

en: When education becomes one-size-fits-all, it risks overlooking a nation's diversity of gifts. (12 words)
cn: 当教育变成一刀切的模式时，它就有忽视一个国家天赋多样性的危险。

en: While fossil fuels—coal, oil, gas—still generate roughly 85 percent of the world's energy supply, it's clearer than ever that the future belongs to renewable sources such as wind and solar. (32 words)
cn: 尽管化石燃料——煤、石油、天然气——仍提供着全球约85%的能源供应，但未来属于风能和太阳能等可再生能源这一点已经比以往任何时候都更加清晰。

en: The move to renewables is picking up momentum around the world: They now account for more than half of new power sources going on line. (25 words)
cn: 全球范围内，向可再生能源的转变正在加速：如今，新上线的电力来源中，可再生能源已占到一半以上。

en: Some growth stems from a commitment by governments and farsighted businesses to fund cleaner energy sources. (16 words)
cn: 部分增长源于各国政府和高瞻远瞩的企业对资助更清洁能源的承诺。

en: But increasingly the story is about the plummeting prices of renewables, especially wind and solar. (15 words)
cn: 但越来越重要的原因是可再生能源价格的急剧下跌，尤其是风能和太阳能。

en: The cost of solar panels has dropped by 80 percent and the cost of wind turbines by close to one-third in the past eight years. (25 words)
cn: 过去八年间，太阳能电池板的成本下降了80%，风力涡轮机的成本下降了近三分之一。

en: In many parts of the world renewable energy is already a principal energy source. (14 words)
cn: 在世界许多地方，可再生能源已经成为一种主要的能源来源。

en: In Scotland, for example, wind turbines provide enough electricity to power 95 percent of homes. (15 words)
cn: 例如在苏格兰，风力涡轮机提供的电力足以满足95%家庭的用电需求。

en: While the rest of the world takes the lead, notably China and Europe, the United States is also seeing a remarkable shift. (22 words)
cn: 在世界其他地方——尤其是中国和欧洲——率先引领的同时，美国也正在经历一场显著的转变。

en: In March, for the first time, wind and solar power accounted for more than 10 percent of the power generated in the US, reported the US Energy Information Administration. (29 words)
cn: 据美国能源信息署报告，今年三月，风能和太阳能的发电量首次占到美国总发电量的10%以上。

en: President Trump has underlined fossil fuels—especially coal—as the path to economic growth. (14 words)
cn: 特朗普总统一直强调化石燃料——尤其是煤炭——是通向经济增长的路径。

en: In a recent speech in Iowa, he dismissed wind power as an unreliable energy source. (15 words)
cn: 最近在艾奥瓦州的一次演讲中，他对风能不屑一顾，称之为不可靠的能源来源。

en: But that message did not play well with many in Iowa, where wind turbines dot the fields and provide 36 percent of the state's electricity generation—and where tech giants like Microsoft are being attracted by the availability of clean energy to power their data centers. (46 words)
cn: 但这一论调在艾奥瓦州并未得到许多人的认可，那里风力涡轮机遍布田野，提供了该州36%的发电量——而且像微软这样的科技巨头也正被那里的清洁能源供应所吸引，用以驱动其数据中心。

en: The question "what happens when the wind doesn't blow or the sun doesn't shine?" has provided a quick put-down for skeptics. (21 words)
cn: "风不吹、太阳不照的时候该怎么办？"这个问题成了怀疑者随口就来的诘难。

en: But a boost in the storage capacity of batteries is making their ability to keep power flowing around the clock more likely. (22 words)
cn: 但电池储能容量的大幅提升，正使昼夜不间断地保持电力输送变得越来越有可能。

en: The advance is driven in part by vehicle manufacturers, who are placing big bets on battery-powered electric vehicles. (18 words)
cn: 这一进步部分是由汽车制造商推动的，他们正对电池驱动的电动汽车押下重注。

en: Although electric cars are still a rarity on roads now, this massive investment could change the picture rapidly in coming years. (21 words)
cn: 尽管电动汽车如今在道路上仍属罕见，但这一巨额投资可能会在未来几年迅速改变局面。

en: While there's a long way to go, the trend lines for renewables are spiking. (14 words)
cn: 尽管还有很长的路要走，但可再生能源的发展曲线正急剧上扬。

en: The pace of change in energy sources appears to be speeding up—perhaps just in time to have a meaningful effect in slowing climate change. (25 words)
cn: 能源来源的变革步伐似乎正在加快——也许恰好能及时对减缓气候变化产生实质性的影响。

en: What Washington does—or doesn't do—to promote alternative energy may mean less and less at a time of a global shift in thought. (24 words)
cn: 在一个全球思维发生转变的时代，华盛顿为推动替代能源所做或所不做的事，其重要性可能越来越小。

en: The power and ambition of the giants of the digital economy is astonishing—Amazon has just announced the purchase of the upmarket grocery chain Whole Foods for $13.5bn, but two years ago Facebook paid even more than that to acquire the WhatsApp messaging service, which doesn't have any physical product at all. (52 words)
cn: 数字经济巨头的势力与野心令人震惊——亚马逊刚刚宣布以135亿美元收购高端连锁超市全食，而两年前，脸书为收购即时通讯服务WhatsApp支付了比这还要高的价格，而WhatsApp根本没有任何实体产品。

en: What WhatsApp offered Facebook was an intricate and finely detailed web of its users' friendships and social lives. (18 words)
cn: WhatsApp带给脸书的，是一张错综复杂、细致入微的用户友谊和社交生活之网。

en: Facebook promised the European commission then that it would not link phone numbers to Facebook identities, but it broke the promise almost as soon as the deal went through. (29 words)
cn: 脸书当时向欧盟委员会承诺，不会将手机号码与脸书身份关联，但几乎在交易一完成，它就违背了诺言。

en: Even without knowing what was in the messages, the knowledge of who sent them and to whom was enormously revealing and still could be. (24 words)
cn: 即便不知道信息里说了什么，知道是谁、发送给了谁，就已经极具信息量，而且以后依然可能如此。

en: What political journalist, what party whip, would not want to know the makeup of the WhatsApp groups in which Theresa May's enemies are currently plotting? (25 words)
cn: 哪个政治记者，哪个政党党鞭，会不想知道特蕾莎·梅的政敌们此刻正在哪些WhatsApp群里密谋策划呢？

en: It may be that the value of Whole Foods to Amazon is not so much the 460 shops it owns, but the records of which customers have purchased what. (29 words)
cn: 或许对亚马逊而言，全食超市的价值不在于它拥有的460家门店，而在于哪些顾客购买过哪些东西的消费记录。

en: Competition law appears to be the only way to address these imbalances of power. (14 words)
cn: 竞争法似乎是解决这些权力失衡的唯一途径。

en: But it is clumsy. (4 words)
cn: 但它过于笨拙。

en: For one thing, it is very slow compared to the pace of change within the digital economy. (17 words)
cn: 首先，与数字经济变化的速度相比，它太过迟缓。

en: By the time a problem has been addressed and remedied it may have vanished in the marketplace, to be replaced by new abuses of power. (25 words)
cn: 等到一个问题被正视并得到纠正时，它可能早已从市场上消失，取而代之的是新的权力滥用行为。

en: But there is a deeper conceptual problem, too. (8 words)
cn: 但还有一个更深层的概念问题。

en: Competition law as presently interpreted deals with financial disadvantage to consumers and this is not obvious when the users of these services don't pay for them. (26 words)
cn: 按现有解读，竞争法处理的是对消费者造成的经济损失，而当这些服务的用户并不为其付费时，这种损失便难以显现。

en: The users of their services are not their customers. (9 words)
cn: 它们服务的使用者，并不是它们的客户。

en: That would be the people who buy advertising from them—and Facebook and Google, the two virtual giants, dominate digital advertising to the disadvantage of all other media and entertainment companies. (31 words)
cn: 那应该是向它们购买广告的人——而脸书和谷歌这两家虚拟巨头，正主导着数字广告市场，让所有其他媒体和娱乐公司都处于劣势。

en: The product they're selling is data, and we, the users, convert our lives to data for the benefit of the digital giants. (22 words)
cn: 它们出售的产品是数据，而我们，作为用户，则把自己的生活转化为数据，为数字巨头牟利。

en: Just as some ants farm the bugs called aphids for the honeydew they produce when they feed, so Google farms us for the data that our digital lives yield. (29 words)
cn: 就像有些蚂蚁会饲养一种名为蚜虫的小虫子，以获取它们进食时分泌出的蜜露一样，谷歌也在饲养我们，以获取我们数字生活所产生的数据。

en: Ants keep predatory insects away from where their aphids feed; Gmail keeps the spammers out of our inboxes. (18 words)
cn: 蚂蚁会赶走蚜虫进食处的捕食性昆虫；谷歌邮箱则会把垃圾邮件发送者挡在我们的收件箱之外。

en: It doesn't feel like a human or democratic relationship, even if both sides benefit. (14 words)
cn: 即便双方都从中获益，这种感觉也不像是一种人性化的、或者民主对等的关系。

en: To combat the trap of putting a premium on being busy, Cal Newport, author of Deep Work: Rules for Focused Success in a Distracted World, recommends building a habit of "deep work"—the ability to focus without distraction. (38 words)
cn: 为了对抗给忙碌赋予过高价值这一陷阱，《深度工作：在分心世界中专注成功的规则》一书的作者卡尔·纽波特建议培养一种"深度工作"的习惯——即那种能够专注而不受干扰的能力。

en: There are a number of approaches to mastering the art of deep work—be it lengthy retreats dedicated to a specific task; developing a daily ritual; or taking a "journalistic" approach to seizing moments of deep work when you can throughout the day. (43 words)
cn: 掌握深度工作这门艺术有多种方法——可以是专门为某项特定任务而进行的长时间静修，可以是培养一种每日例行习惯，也可以是采取一种"记者式"的方式，在一天中随时抓住可以深度工作的时刻。

en: Whichever approach, the key is to determine your length of focus time and stick to it. (16 words)
cn: 无论采用哪种方法，关键是确定你的专注时长，然后坚持恪守。

en: Newport also recommends "deep scheduling" to combat constant interruptions and get more done in less time. (16 words)
cn: 纽波特还建议进行"深度日程安排"，以对抗源源不断的干扰，并在更短的时间内完成更多的事。

en: "At any given point, I should have deep work scheduled for roughly the next month. (15 words)
cn: "在任何一个时间点，我大概都应该把接下来一个月里深度工作的时段安排妥当。

en: Once on the calendar, I protect this time like I would a doctor's appointment or important meeting", he writes. (19 words)
cn: 一旦写到了日历上，我就会像保护医生的预约或重要会议一样来保护这段时间，"他写道。

en: Another approach to getting more done in less time is to rethink how you prioritize your day—in particular how we craft our to-do lists. (25 words)
cn: 另一个在更短时间内完成更多事的方法是重新思考你如何为一天中各项事务安排优先次序——尤其是我们如何制定待办清单。

en: Tim Harford, author of Messy: The Power of Disorder to Transform Our Lives, points to a study in the early 1980s that divided undergraduates into two groups: some were advised to set out monthly goals and study activities; others were told to plan activities and goals in much more detail, day by day. (53 words)
cn: 《混乱：无序改变生活的力量》一书的作者蒂姆·哈福德引用了20世纪80年代初的一项研究，该研究把本科生分成了两组：一组被建议制定月度目标和学习活动，另一组则被要求更详细地逐日规划活动和目标。

en: While the researchers assumed that the well-structured daily plans would be most effective when it came to the execution of tasks, they were wrong: the detailed daily plans demotivated students. (30 words)
cn: 研究者本以为条理分明的每日计划在执行任务时会最为有效，但他们错了：过于详细的每日计划反而让学生失去了动力。

en: Harford argues that inevitable distractions often render the daily to-do list ineffective, while leaving room for improvisation in such a list can reap the best results. (26 words)
cn: 哈福德认为，不可避免的干扰常常让每日待办清单变得无效，而在这样的清单中留出临场发挥的余地，反而能收获最好的效果。

en: In order to make the most of our focus and energy, we also need to embrace downtime, or as Newport suggests, "be lazy." (12 words)
cn: 为了最大限度利用我们的专注力和精力，我们还需要接纳休息时间，或者如纽波特所说的，要"懒一点"。

en: "Idleness is not just a vacation, an indulgence or a vice; it is as indispensable to the brain as Vitamin D is to the body...[idleness] is, paradoxically, necessary to getting any work done," he argues. (35 words)
cn: "无所事事并不仅仅是一次休假、一种放纵或一个恶习；它对大脑来说不可或缺，就像维生素D对身体一样……而矛盾的是，无所事事恰恰是完成任何工作所必需的条件，"他论证道。

en: Srini Pillay, an assistant professor of psychiatry at Harvard Medical School, believes this counterintuitive link between downtime and productivity may be due to the way our brains operate. (28 words)
cn: 哈佛医学院精神病学助理教授斯瑞尼·皮莱认为，休息时间与生产率之间这种反直觉的关联，可能源于我们大脑的运作方式。

en: When our brains switch between being focused and unfocused on a task, they tend to be more efficient. (18 words)
cn: 当大脑在一项任务上于专注与不专注两种状态间切换时，往往效率更高。

en: "What people don't realise is that in order to complete these tasks they need to use both the focus and unfocus circuits in their brain", says Pillay. (27 words)
cn: "人们没有意识到的是，为了完成这些任务，他们需要用到大脑中负责专注和不专注的两种回路，"皮莱说道。

en: Unlike so-called basic emotions such as sadness, fear, and anger, guilt emerges a little later, in conjunction with a child's growing grasp of social and moral norms. (27 words)
cn: 与悲伤、恐惧和愤怒这些所谓的基本情绪不同，内疚感出现得稍晚一些，伴随着儿童对社会规范和道德准则日益加深的掌握。

en: Children aren't born knowing how to say "I'm sorry"; rather, they learn over time that such statements appease parents and friends—and their own consciences. (25 words)
cn: 孩子并非天生就知道如何说"对不起"；而是随着时间的推移，他们学到这样的表述能够安抚父母和朋友——也能安抚自己的良心。

en: This is why researchers generally regard so-called moral guilt, in the right amount, to be a good thing. (18 words)
cn: 这就是为什么研究人员通常将适量的所谓道德内疚视为一件好事。

en: In the popular imagination, of course, guilt still gets a bad rap. (12 words)
cn: 当然，在大众的普遍认知中，内疚依然背负着恶名。

en: It is deeply uncomfortable—it's the emotional equivalent of wearing a jacket weighted with stones. (15 words)
cn: 它让人深感不适——在情绪上，就相当于穿着一件坠满石头的夹克。

en: Yet this understanding is outdated. (5 words)
cn: 然而这种理解已经过时了。

en: "There has been a kind of revival or a rethinking about what guilt is and what role guilt can serve," says Amrisha Vaish, a psychology researcher at the University of Virginia, adding that this revival is part of a larger recognition that emotions aren't binary—feelings that may be advantageous in one context may be harmful in another. (58 words)
cn: "对内疚是什么以及内疚能发挥什么作用，已经有了一种复兴或者说重新思考，"弗吉尼亚大学心理学研究员阿姆里沙·瓦伊什说道，并补充说，这种复兴是一个更广泛认识的一部分，即情绪并非二元对立的——在一种情境下可能有益的感受，在另一种情境下也许是有害的。

en: Jealousy and anger, for example, may have evolved to alert us to important inequalities. (14 words)
cn: 例如，嫉妒和愤怒在进化中也许是为了提醒我们注意那些重要的不平等。

en: Too much happiness can be destructive. (6 words)
cn: 过多的快乐也可能是有害的。

en: And guilt, by prompting us to think more deeply about our goodness, can encourage humans to make up for errors and fix relationships. (23 words)
cn: 而内疚，通过促使我们更深刻地思考自己的善，能够鼓励人们弥补过失、修复关系。

en: Guilt, in other words, can help hold a cooperative species together. (11 words)
cn: 换句话说，内疚可以帮助将一种合作型物种凝聚在一起。

en: It is a kind of social glue. (7 words)
cn: 它是一种社会黏合剂。

en: Viewed in this light, guilt is an opportunity. (8 words)
cn: 从这个角度来看，内疚是一种机缘。

en: Work by Tina Malti, a psychology professor at the University of Toronto, suggests that guilt may compensate for an emotional deficiency. (21 words)
cn: 多伦多大学心理学教授蒂娜·马尔蒂的研究表明，内疚或许可以弥补某种情感上的缺陷。

en: In a number of studies, Malti and others have shown that guilt and sympathy may represent different pathways to cooperation and sharing. (22 words)
cn: 在一系列研究中，马尔蒂和其他研究者已经表明，内疚和同情可能分别代表了通往合作与分享的不同路径。

en: Some kids who are low in sympathy may make up for that shortfall by experiencing more guilt, which can rein in their nastier impulses. (24 words)
cn: 一些缺乏同情心的孩子可以通过更多地体验内疚来弥补这一不足，因为内疚能够抑制他们那些更恶劣的冲动。

en: And vice versa: High sympathy can substitute for low guilt. (10 words)
cn: 反之亦然：高同情心可以弥补低内疚感。

en: In a 2014 study, for example, Malti looked at 244 children. (11 words)
cn: 例如，在2014年的一项研究中，马尔蒂观察了244名儿童。

en: Using caregiver assessments and the children's self-observations, she rated each child's overall sympathy level and his or her tendency to feel negative emotions after moral transgressions. (26 words)
cn: 通过看护者的评估和孩子们的自我观察，她对每个孩子的整体同情心水平以及他们在道德越轨后产生负面情绪的倾向进行了评级。

en: Then the kids were handed chocolate coins, and given a chance to share them with an anonymous child. (18 words)
cn: 然后，孩子们拿到了巧克力硬币，并有机会与一位匿名的孩子分享。

en: For the low-sympathy kids, how much they shared appeared to turn on how inclined they were to feel guilty. (19 words)
cn: 对于那些低同情心的孩子来说，他们分享的多少似乎取决于他们有多容易感到内疚。

en: The guilt-prone ones shared more, even though they hadn't magically become more sympathetic to the other child's deprivation. (18 words)
cn: 容易产生内疚的孩子分享了更多，尽管他们并没有神奇地对另一孩子的缺失变得更富同情心。

en: "That's good news," Malti says. (5 words)
cn: "这是个好消息，"马尔蒂说道。

en: "We can be prosocial because we caused harm and we feel regret." (12 words)
cn: "我们之所以能做出亲社会行为，是因为我们曾造成了伤害，我们心感悔意。"

en: Forests give us shade, quiet and one of the harder challenges in the fight against climate change. (17 words)
cn: 森林给予我们阴凉与宁静，也带给我们应对气候变化过程中一个颇为棘手的挑战。

en: Even as we humans count on forests to soak up a good share of the carbon dioxide we produce, we are threatening their ability to do so. (27 words)
cn: 正当我们人类指望森林吸收掉自己排出的大部分二氧化碳时，我们却在威胁森林这样做的能力。

en: The climate change we are hastening could one day leave us with forests that emit more carbon than they absorb. (20 words)
cn: 我们所加速造成的气候变化也许终有一天会让森林碳的排放量超过吸收量。

en: Thankfully, there is a way out of this trap—but it involves striking a subtle balance. (16 words)
cn: 好在仍有走出这一困境的出路——但这需要达成某种微妙的平衡。

en: Helping forests flourish as valuable "carbon sinks" long into the future may require reducing their capacity to absorb carbon now. (20 words)
cn: 要帮助森林在长远的未来作为宝贵的"碳汇"生生不息，或许现在就需要降低它们吸收碳的能力。

en: California is leading the way, as it does on so many climate efforts, in figuring out the details. (18 words)
cn: 加利福尼亚州正在率先摸索具体的做法，正如它在诸多气候行动中所做的那样。

en: The state's proposed Forest Carbon Plan aims to double efforts to thin out young trees and clear brush in parts of the forest. (23 words)
cn: 该州拟定的《森林碳计划》旨在加倍推进森林局部地段的幼树间伐和灌丛清理工作。

en: This temporarily lowers carbon-carrying capacity. (5 words)
cn: 这暂时降低了碳承载能力。

en: But the remaining trees draw a greater share of the available moisture, so they grow and thrive, restoring the forest's capacity to pull carbon from the air. (27 words)
cn: 但留下来的树木能汲取到更多可用的水分，从而茁壮成长，恢复森林从空气中吸收碳的能力。

en: Healthy trees are also better able to fend off insects. (10 words)
cn: 健康的树木也更能抵御昆虫的侵害。

en: The landscape is rendered less easily burnable. (7 words)
cn: 这片土地也就变得不那么容易燃烧了。

en: Even in the event of a fire, fewer trees are consumed. (11 words)
cn: 即使发生了火灾，被烧毁的树木也会更少。

en: The need for such planning is increasingly urgent. (8 words)
cn: 推行这种规划的必要性正日益紧迫。

en: Already, since 2010, drought and insects have killed over 100 million trees in California, most of them in 2016 alone, and wildfires have burned hundreds of thousands of acres. (29 words)
