cn: 自2010年以来，干旱和昆虫已经使加利福尼亚州超过一亿棵树死亡，其中大部分就发生在2016年，而野火已烧毁了几十万英亩的林地。

en: California plans to treat 35,000 acres of forest a year by 2020, and 60,000 by 2030—financed from the proceeds of the state's emissions-permit auctions. (25 words)
cn: 加州计划到2020年每年治理3.5万英亩森林，到2030年增至每年6万英亩——资金来自该州排放许可拍卖的收益。

en: That's only a small share of the total acreage that could benefit, about half a million acres in all, so it will be vital to prioritize areas at greatest risk of fire or drought. (34 words)
cn: 这仅占可受益总林地面积的一小部分，总计约有50万英亩，因此必须优先治理火灾或干旱风险最大的区域。

en: The strategy also aims to ensure that carbon in woody material removed from the forests is locked away in the form of solid lumber or burned as biofuel in vehicles that would otherwise run on fossil fuels. (37 words)
cn: 该战略的另一目标是确保从森林中移除的木本材料中所含的碳被固定为实木木材，或作为生物燃料供车辆燃烧，而这些车辆原本是靠化石燃料驱动的。

en: New research on transportation biofuels is already under way. (9 words)
cn: 有关交通用生物燃料的新研究已经在进行中。

en: State governments are well accustomed to managing forests, but traditionally they've focused on wildlife, watersheds and opportunities for recreation. (19 words)
cn: 州政府早已习惯于管理森林，但传统上它们侧重于野生动物、流域以及休闲娱乐的场所。

en: Only recently have they come to see the vital part forests will have to play in storing carbon. (18 words)
cn: 直到最近，他们才开始认识到森林在储存碳方面将要发挥的关键作用。

en: California's plan, which is expected to be finalized by the governor next year, should serve as a model. (18 words)
cn: 加州的这项计划预计于明年由州长最终签署敲定，它应当会成为一个范本。

en: American farmers have been complaining of labor shortages for several years. (11 words)
cn: 美国农民多年来一直在抱怨劳动力短缺的问题。

en: The complaints are unlikely to stop without an overhaul of immigration rules for farm works. (15 words)
cn: 如果不彻底改革针对农场工人的移民规则，这些抱怨不太可能停止。

en: Congress has obstructed efforts to create a more straightforward visa for agricultural workers that would let foreign workers stay longer in the U.S. and change jobs within the industry. (29 words)
cn: 国会一直阻挠为农业工人设立一种更简便签证的努力，这种签证本可以让外国工人在美国停留更长时间，并在行业内更换工作。

en: If this doesn't change, American businesses, communities, and consumers will be the losers. (13 words)
cn: 如果这一点不改变，美国的企业、社区和消费者都将成为输家。

en: Perhaps half of U.S. farm laborers are undocumented immigrants. (9 words)
cn: 大约一半的美国农场工人都是无证移民。

en: As fewer such workers enter the country, the characteristics of the agricultural workforce are changing. (15 words)
cn: 随着这类工人入境人数减少，农业劳动力的构成特征正在发生变化。

en: Today's farm laborers, while still predominantly born in Mexico, are more likely to be settled rather than migrating and more likely to be married than single. (26 words)
cn: 如今的农场工人虽然仍主要在墨西哥出生，但他们更倾向于定居而非流动，而且已婚的比例高于未婚。

en: They're also aging. (3 words)
cn: 他们也在逐渐老去。

en: At the start of this century, about one-third of crop workers were over the age of 35. (17 words)
cn: 本世纪初，约有三分之一的农作物工人年龄超过35岁。

en: Now more than half are. (5 words)
cn: 如今已超过半数。

en: And picking crops is hard on older bodies. (8 words)
cn: 而采摘农作物对年迈的身体来说是件苦差事。

en: One oft-debated cure for this labor shortage remains as implausible as it's been all along: Native U.S. workers won't be returning to the farm. (24 words)
cn: 一个常被讨论的解决劳动力短缺的办法，一如既往地不切实际：美国本土工人不会回到农场工作。

en: Mechanization isn't the answer, either—not yet, at least. (9 words)
cn: 机械化也不是答案——至少目前还不是。

en: Production of corn, cotton, rice, soybeans, and wheat has been largely mechanized, but many high-value, labor-intensive crops, such as strawberries, need labor. (22 words)
cn: 玉米、棉花、水稻、大豆和小麦的生产已基本实现机械化，但许多高价值、劳动密集型的作物，如草莓，仍然需要人工。

en: Even dairy farms, where robots do a small share of milking, have a long way to go before they're automated. (20 words)
cn: 即便是机器人已经承担了一小部分挤奶工作的奶牛场，离实现自动化也还有很长的路要走。

en: As a result, farms have grown increasingly reliant on temporary guest workers using the H-2A visa to fill the gaps in the workforce. (23 words)
cn: 因此，农场越来越依赖持有H-2A签证的临时客籍工人来填补劳动力缺口。

en: Starting around 2012, requests for the visas rose sharply; from 2011 to 2016 the number of visas issued more than doubled. (21 words)
cn: 大约从2012年开始，这类签证的申请量急剧上升；从2011年到2016年，签发的签证数量翻了一倍多。

en: The H-2A visa has no numerical cap, unlike the H-2B visa for nonagricultural work, which is limited to 66,000 a year. (21 words)
cn: H-2A签证没有数量上限，这不同于非农工作的H-2B签证，后者每年限定为六万六千个。

en: Even so, employers complain they aren't given all the workers they need. (12 words)
cn: 即便如此，雇主们仍抱怨他们没能得到所需的全部工人。

en: The process is cumbersome, expensive, and unreliable. (7 words)
cn: 申请流程繁琐、费用高昂，且极不可靠。

en: One survey found that bureaucratic delays led the average H-2A worker to arrive on the job 22 days late. (19 words)
cn: 一项调查发现，由于官僚程序的延误，H-2A工人平均到岗时间晚22天。

en: The shortage is compounded by federal immigration raids, which remove some workers and drive others underground. (16 words)
cn: 联邦移民突击检查使一些工人被带走，另一些工人则转入地下，这让劳动力短缺问题雪上加霜。

en: In a 2012 survey, 71 percent of tree-fruit growers and nearly 80 percent of raisin and berry growers said they were short of labor. (24 words)
cn: 在2012年的一项调查中，71%的果树种植者和近80%的葡萄干和浆果种植者表示他们缺少劳动力。

en: Some western farmers have responded by moving operations to Mexico. (10 words)
cn: 一些西部的农场主对此的回应是将经营业务迁往墨西哥。

en: From 1998 to 2000, 14.5 percent of the fruit Americans consumed was imported. (13 words)
cn: 从1998年到2000年，美国人消费的水果有14.5%来自进口。

en: Little more than a decade later, the share of imports was 25.8 percent. (13 words)
cn: 仅仅十多年后，这一进口比例就达到了25.8%。

en: In effect, the U.S. can import food or it can import the workers who pick it. (16 words)
cn: 实际上，美国要么进口食品，要么就得进口采摘食品的工人。

en: Arnold Schwarzenegger, Dia Mirza and Adrian Grenier have a message for you: It's easy to beat plastic. (17 words)
cn: 阿诺德·施瓦辛格、迪娅·米尔扎和阿德里安·格雷尼尔要向你传达一个信息：战胜塑料很容易。

en: They're part of a bunch of celebrities starring in a new video for World Environment Day—encouraging you, the consumer, to swap out your single-use plastic staples to combat the plastics crisis. (32 words)
cn: 他们是世界环境日一部新视频中的一众名人，鼓励作为消费者的你换掉一次性塑料日常用品，以应对塑料危机。

en: The key messages that have been put together for World Environment Day do include a call for governments to enact legislation to curb single-use plastics. (25 words)
cn: 为世界环境日汇集的关键信息中，确实包含了呼吁政府立法以遏制一次性塑料制品的内容。

en: But the overarching message is directed at individuals. (8 words)
cn: 但其首要的讯息却是针对个人的。

en: My concern with leaving it up to the individual, however, is our limited sense of what needs to be achieved. (20 words)
cn: 然而，我对于把这件事实交到个体手中的担忧在于，我们对于需要达成什么样的目标认知非常有限。

en: On their own, taking our own bags to the grocery store or quitting plastic straws, for example, will accomplish little and require very little of us. (26 words)
cn: 单靠自己，比如自备购物袋去杂货店，或者不再用塑料吸管，成效微乎其微，也几乎不需要我们付出什么。

en: They could even be harmful, satisfying a need to have "done our bit" without ever progressing onto bigger, bolder, more effective actions—a kind of "moral licensing" that eases our concerns and stops us doing more and asking more of those in charge. (43 words)
cn: 它们甚至可能是有害的，满足了那种"已尽本分"的需要，却永远不会推进到更宏大、更果敢、更有效的行动——这是一种"道德许可"，它缓解了我们的忧虑，却阻止了我们做得更多，也要求问责当权者做得更多。

en: While the conversation around our environment and our responsibility toward it remains centered on shopping bags and straws, we're ignoring the balance of power that implies that as "consumers" we must shop sustainably, rather than as "citizens" hold our governments and industries to account to push for real systemic change. (50 words)
cn: 当围绕环境和我们对其责任的讨论仍集中在购物袋和吸管上时，我们便忽视了其中隐含的权力格局：我们以"消费者"的身份进行可持续消费，而非以"公民"的身份要求政府和业界承担责任去推动真正的系统性变革。

en: It's important to acknowledge that the environment isn't everyone's priority—or even most people's. (14 words)
cn: 重要的是要承认，环境并非每个人的首要任务——甚至不算大多数人优先考虑的。

en: We shouldn't expect it to be. (6 words)
cn: 我们也不该期待如此。

en: In her latest book, Why Good People Do Bad Environmental Things, Wellesley College professor Elizabeth R. DeSombre argues that the best way to collectively change the behavior of large numbers of people is for the change to be structural. (39 words)
cn: 韦尔斯利学院教授伊丽莎白·R·德松布尔在其新书《为什么好人会做对环境有害的事》中指出，要集体改变大量人群的行为，最好的方式是从结构层面入手。

en: This might mean implementing policy such as a plastic tax that adds a cost to environmentally problematic action, or banning single-use plastics altogether. (23 words)
cn: 这可能意味着实施诸如塑料税这类政策，为有损环境的行为增加一项成本，或者完全禁止一次性塑料制品。

en: India has just announced it will "eliminate all single-use plastic in the country by 2022." (15 words)
cn: 印度刚刚宣布，将在2022年前"在全国范围内消除所有一次性塑料"。

en: There are also incentive-based ways of making better environmental choices easier, such as ensuring recycling is at least as easy as trash disposal. (23 words)
cn: 还有一些基于激励的方法能让更好的环保选择变得更容易，比如确保回收利用至少像扔垃圾一样便捷。

en: DeSombre isn't saying people should stop caring about the environment. (10 words)
cn: 德松布尔并不是在说人们应该不再在乎环境。

en: It's just that individual actions are too slow, she says, for that to be the only, or even primary, approach to changing widespread behavior. (24 words)
cn: 她说的只是，个人行动太过迟缓，无法将其作为改变普遍行为的唯一、甚至是主要的途径。

en: None of this is about writing off the individual. (9 words)
cn: 这一切都不是要否定个体的作用。

en: It's just about putting things into perspective. (7 words)
cn: 只是要理性看待问题。

en: We don't have time to wait. (6 words)
cn: 我们没有时间等待。

en: We need progressive policies that shape collective action, alongside engaged citizens pushing for change. (14 words)
cn: 我们需要能够塑造集体行动的进步性政策，同时需要有积极参与的公民推动变革。

en: Rats and other animals need to be highly attuned to social signals from others so they can identify friends to cooperate with and enemies to avoid. (26 words)
cn: 老鼠和其他动物需要对来自其他个体的社交信号高度敏感，以便辨别哪些是可以合作的朋友，哪些是应当避开的敌人。

en: To find out if this extends to non-living beings, Laleh Quinn at the University of California, San Diego, and her colleagues tested whether rats can detect social signals from robotic rats. (31 words)
cn: 为了探究这是否也延伸至无生命体，加利福尼亚大学圣迭戈分校的拉莱·奎因及其同事测试了老鼠能否察觉到来自机器鼠的社交信号。

en: They housed eight adult rats with two types of robotic rat—one social and one asocial—for four days. (19 words)
cn: 他们将八只成年老鼠与两种类型的机器鼠——一只是社交型的，一只则不善社交——一起饲养了四天。

en: The robot rats were quite minimalist, resembling a chunkier version of a computer mouse with wheels to move around and colorful markings. (22 words)
cn: 这两只机器鼠非常简约，外型像加大版的电脑鼠标，装有轮子可以四处走动，上面还有彩色的标记。

en: During the experiment, the social robot rat followed the living rats around, played with the same toys, and opened cage doors to let trapped rats escape. (26 words)
cn: 在实验过程中，那只会社交的机器鼠跟随着活老鼠四处走，玩相同的玩具，还会打开笼门，让被困的老鼠逃走。

en: Meanwhile, the asocial robot simply moved forwards and backwards and side to side. (13 words)
cn: 与此同时，那只不善社交的机器鼠只会简单地前后移动和左右移动。

en: Next, the researchers trapped the robots in cages and gave the rats the opportunity to release them by pressing a lever. (21 words)
cn: 接下来，研究人员将两只机器鼠分别关在笼子里，然后给老鼠们通过按压杠杆来释放它们的机会。

en: Across 18 trials each, the living rats were 52 percent more likely on average to set the social robot free than the asocial one. (24 words)
cn: 在各自进行的18次试验中，这些活老鼠平均释放那只会社交的机器鼠的可能性比释放那只不善社交的高出52%。

en: This suggests that the rats perceived the social robot as a genuine social being. (14 words)
cn: 这表明，老鼠将那只会社交的机器鼠视为一个真正的社会性存在。

en: They may have bonded more with the social robot because it displayed behaviours like communal exploring and playing. (18 words)
cn: 它们之所以与那只会社交的机器鼠建立了更多的联结，很可能是因为它表现出了共同探索和玩耍这类行为

en: This could lead to the rats better remembering having freed it earlier, and wanting the robot to return the favour when they get trapped, says Quinn. (26 words)
cn: 奎因表示，这可能让老鼠更清楚地记得自己先前释放过它，并且当自己被困时，希望那只机器鼠也能回报这份恩惠。

en: "Rats have been shown to engage in multiple forms of reciprocal help and cooperation, including what is referred to as direct reciprocity—where a rat will help another rat that has previously helped them," says Quinn. (36 words)
cn: "已有研究表明，老鼠会参与多种形式的互助与合作，其中包括所谓的直接互惠——即一只老鼠会去帮助先前曾经帮过自己的另一只老鼠，"奎因说。

en: The readiness of the rats to befriend the social robot was surprising given its minimal design. (16 words)
cn: 考虑到那只会社交的机器鼠设计极为简陋，老鼠愿意与之结交的积极性是令人惊讶的。

en: The robot was the same size as a regular rat but resembled a simple plastic box on wheels. (18 words)
cn: 这只机器鼠和普通老鼠大小相同，但看上去就像一个带轮子的简易塑料盒子。

en: "We'd assumed we'd have to give it a moving head and tail, facial features, and put a scent on it to make it smell like a real rat, but that wasn't necessary," says Janet Wiles at the University of Queensland in Australia, who helped with the research. (47 words)
cn: "我们原本以为必须给它装上会动的头和尾巴，做出面部特征，还要在上面放些气味让它闻起来像一只真老鼠，但结果这些都不需要，"协助此项研究的澳大利亚昆士兰大学的珍妮特·怀尔斯说。

en: The finding shows how sensitive rats are to social cues, even when they come from basic robots. (17 words)
cn: 这一发现表明，老鼠对社会线索是多么敏感，即使这些线索来自极为简陋的机器人。

en: Similarly, children tend to treat robots as if they are fellow beings, even when they display only simple social signals. (20 words)
cn: 类似地，儿童也倾向于将机器人当作同类来对待，即便它们只表现出简单的社交信号。

en: "We humans seem to be fascinated by robots, and it turns out other animals are too," says Wiles. (18 words)
cn: "我们人类似乎对机器人为之着迷，而事实证明，其他动物也是如此，"怀尔斯说。

en: It is true that CEO pay has gone up—top ones may make 300 times the pay of typical workers on average, and since the mid-1970s, CEO pay for large publicly traded American corporations has, by varying estimates, gone up by about 500%. (43 words)
cn: 诚然，首席执行官的薪酬确实上涨了——顶尖首席执行官的平均收入可能是普通工人的300倍，而且自20世纪70年代中期以来，美国大型上市公司的首席执行官薪酬据不同估算已上涨了约500%。

en: The typical CEO of a top American corporation now makes about $18.9 million a year. (15 words)
cn: 如今，一家顶级美国公司的典型首席执行官年收入约为1890万美元。

en: The best model for understanding the growth of CEO pay is that of limited CEO talent in a world where business opportunities for the top firms are growing rapidly. (29 words)
cn: 理解首席执行官薪酬增长的最佳模型是：在一个顶尖公司商业机会迅速扩张的世界里，首席执行官人才十分有限。

en: The efforts of America's highest-earning 1% have been one of the more dynamic elements of the global economy. (18 words)
cn: 美国收入最高的1%人群的努力，一直是全球经济中更具活力的元素之一。

en: It's not popular to say, but one reason their pay has gone up so much is that CEOs really have upped their game relative to many other workers in the U.S. economy. (32 words)
cn: 这话并不受欢迎，但他们薪酬涨得如此之高的一个原因是，相对于美国经济中许多其他劳动者，首席执行官们确实拿出了更出色的表现。

en: Today's CEO, at least for major American firms, must have many more skills than simply being able to "run the company". (21 words)
cn: 如今的首席执行官，至少对美国大公司而言，所需具备的技能远远不止"能管理公司"这么简单。

en: CEOs must have a good sense of financial markets and maybe even how the company should trade in them. (19 words)
cn: 首席执行官必须对金融市场有良好的感觉，甚至可能需要知道公司在其中应当如何交易。

en: They also need better public relations skills than their predecessors, as the costs of even a minor slipup can be significant. (21 words)
cn: 他们还需要比前辈更出色的公关技巧，因为哪怕一个小小的失误都可能代价惨重。

en: Then there's the fact that large American companies are much more globalized than ever before, with supply chains spread across a larger number of countries. (25 words)
cn: 此外还有一个事实，美国大公司比以往任何时候都更加全球化了，供应链遍布更多的国家。

en: To lead in that system requires knowledge that is fairly mind-boggling. (11 words)
cn: 要在这样的体系中担纲领导，所需的知识量相当令人难以想象。

en: Plus, virtually all major American companies are becoming tech companies, often with their own research and development. (17 words)
cn: 另外，几乎所有美国主要公司都在变成科技公司，往往拥有自己的研发部门。

en: And beyond this, major CEOs still have to do all the day-to-day work they have always done. (17 words)
cn: 除此之外，大公司的首席执行官们仍然要处理他们一直在做的所有日常工作。

en: The common idea that high CEO pay is mainly about ripping people off doesn't explain history very well. (18 words)
cn: 那种认为首席执行官高薪主要是为了盘剥他人的普遍看法，并不能很好地解释历史。

en: By most measures, corporate governance has become a lot tighter and more rigorous since the 1970s. (16 words)
cn: 从大多数衡量标准来看，自20世纪70年代以来，公司治理已经变得严格和缜密了许多。

en: Yet it is principally during this period of stronger governance that CEO pay has been high and rising. (18 words)
cn: 然而，恰恰是在这一治理更加有力的时期，首席执行官的薪酬居高不下且持续攀升。

en: That suggests it is in the broader corporate interest to recruit top candidates for increasingly tough jobs. (17 words)
cn: 这表明，为日益艰巨的工作招募顶尖人才，是符合公司更广泛利益的。

en: Furthermore, the highest CEO salaries are paid to outside candidates, not to the cozy insider picks, another sign that high CEO pay is not some kind of depredation at the expense of the rest of the company. (37 words)
cn: 再者，最高的首席执行官薪酬是付给外部候选人的，而非那些舒舒服服的内部钦定人选，这同样说明首席执行官高薪不是那种以公司其他人为代价的掠夺行为。

en: And the stock market reacts positively when companies tie CEO pay to, say, stock prices, a sign that those practices build up corporate value not just for the CEO. (29 words)
cn: 而且，当公司将首席执行官薪酬与股价等因素挂钩时，股市反应积极，这表明这些做法不只是为首席执行官创造价值，也在提升公司整体价值。

en: Madrid was hailed as a public health guiding light last November when it rolled out ambitious restrictions on the most polluting cars. (22 words)
cn: 去年十一月，当马德里对污染最严重的车辆推出雄心勃勃的限制措施时，它被誉为公共卫生领域的一座灯塔。

en: Seven months and one election day later, a new conservative city council suspended enforcement of the clean air zone, a first step toward its possible termination. (26 words)
cn: 七个月零一个选举日之后，新上台的保守派市议会暂停了空气净化区的执法工作，这是朝着可能废止该区迈出的第一步。

en: Mayor José Luis Martínez-Almeida made opposition to the zone a centrepiece of his election campaign, despite its success in improving air quality. (22 words)
cn: 市长何塞·路易斯·马丁内斯-阿尔梅达将反对该区域作为他竞选活动的核心议题，尽管它在改善空气质量方面取得了成功。

en: A judge has now overruled the city's decision to stop levying fines, ordering them restored. (15 words)
cn: 如今，一位法官已驳回该市停止罚款的决定，命令恢复罚款。

en: But with legal battles ahead, the zone's future looks uncertain at best. (12 words)
cn: 但由于法律斗争还在前头，这一区域的前景往好里说也是不确定的。

en: Madrid's back and forth on clean air is a pointed reminder of the limits to the patchwork, city-by-city approach that characterises efforts on air pollution across Europe, Britain very much included. (31 words)
cn: 马德里在清洁空气问题上的反复，尖锐地提醒人们，那种逐城拼凑的做法存在诸多局限，而这正是整个欧洲——英国当属其中——在治理空气污染方面的典型特征。

en: Among other weaknesses, the measures cities must employ when left to tackle dirty air on their own are politically controversial, and therefore vulnerable. (23 words)
cn: 除了其他弱点之外，各城市被留给自行解决空气污染问题时不得不采取的措施，在政治上存在争议，因此非常脆弱。

en: That's because they inevitably put the costs of cleaning the air on to individual drivers—who must pay fees or buy better vehicles—rather than on to the car manufacturers whose cheating is the real cause of our toxic pollution. (40 words)
cn: 这是因为它们不可避免地将清洁空气的成本转嫁到了个体车主身上——他们必须缴费或购买更好的车辆——而非转嫁给汽车制造商，而后者的作弊行为才是我们有毒污染的真正根源。

en: It's not hard to imagine a similar reversal happening in London. (11 words)
cn: 不难想象，类似的逆转也会在伦敦发生。

en: The new ultra-low emission zone (Ulez) is likely to be a big issue in next year's mayoral election. (18 words)
cn: 新的超低排放区很可能会成为明年市长选举中的一大议题。

en: And if Sadiq Khan wins and extends it to the North and South Circular roads in 2021 as he intends, it is sure to spark intense opposition from the far larger number of motorists who will then be affected. (39 words)
cn: 而如果萨迪克·汗获胜并如愿在2021年将其扩展至南北环线道路，这必定会引发来自届时受影响的大得多的驾车者群体的强烈反对。

en: It's not that measures such as London's Ulez are useless. (10 words)
cn: 这并不是说伦敦超低排放区等措施毫无用处。

en: Far from it. (3 words)
cn: 远非如此。

en: Local officials are using the levers that are available to them to safeguard residents' health in the face of a serious threat. (22 words)
cn: 面对严重威胁，地方官员正在利用他们所能运用的手段来保护居民的健康。

en: The zones do deliver some improvements to air quality, and the science tells us that means real health benefits. (19 words)
cn: 这些区域确实使空气质量有所改善，而科学告诉我们，这意味着实实在在的健康收益。

en: But mayors and councilors can only do so much about a problem that is far bigger than any one city or town. (22 words)
cn: 但对于一个远比任何一座城镇都更庞大的问题，市长和议员们只能尽力而为。

en: They are acting because national governments—Britain's and others across Europe—have failed to do so. (16 words)
cn: 他们之所以采取行动，是因为各国家政府——英国以及欧洲其他国家的政府——未能做到这一点。

en: Restrictions that keep highly polluting cars out of certain areas—city centres, "school streets", even individual roads—are a response to the absence of a larger effort to properly enforce existing regulations and require auto companies to bring their vehicles into compliance. (42 words)
cn: 将高污染车辆挡在某些区域之外——市中心、"学校街道"，甚至某一条道路——这些限制措施，都是对缺乏更广泛努力的一种回应，而这种努力本应致力于恰当执行既有法规，并要求汽车公司使其车辆达标。

en: Wales has introduced special low speed limits to minimise pollution. (10 words)
cn: 威尔士已引入特别低速限制，以尽量减少污染。

en: We're doing everything but insist that manufacturers clean up their cars. (11 words)
cn: 我们什么都做了，唯独没有坚持要求制造商清理他们自家的车辆。

en: Now that members of Generation Z are graduating college this spring—the most commonly-accepted definition says this generation was born after 1995, give or take a year—the attention has been rising steadily in recent weeks. (36 words)
cn: 随着Z世代成员今年春天大学毕业——最为人普遍接受的定义称这一代人出生于1995年后，前后误差一年左右——近几周来，对他们的关注度一直在稳步上升。

en: Gen Zs are about to hit the streets looking for work in a labor market that's tighter than it's been in decades. (22 words)
cn: Z世代即将走上街头，在一个比过去几十年都更紧张的劳动力市场中寻找工作。

en: And employers are planning on hiring about 17 percent more new graduates for jobs in the U.S. this year than last, according to a survey conducted by the National Association of Colleges and Employers. (34 words)
cn: 而根据美国大学与雇主协会的一项调查，雇主们今年计划在美国招聘的应届毕业生数量比去年多出约17%。

en: Everybody wants to know how the people who will soon inhabit those empty office cubicles will differ from those who came before them. (23 words)
cn: 人人都想知道，那些即将坐进这些空荡荡办公格子间的人，与他们的前辈会有何不同。

en: If "entitled" is the most common adjective, fairly or not, applied to millennials (those born between 1981 and 1995), the catchwords for Generation Z are practical and cautious. (28 words)
cn: 如果"被惯坏"是用于描述千禧一代（出生于1981年至1995年间的人）最常见的形容词，无论公平与否，那么Z世代的关键词就是务实和审慎。

en: According to the career counselors and experts who study them, Generation Zs are clear-eyed, economic pragmatists. (16 words)
cn: 据研究他们的职业顾问和专家称，Z世代是头脑清醒的经济实用主义者。

en: Despite graduating into the best economy in the past 50 years, Gen Zs know what an economic train wreck looks like. (21 words)
cn: 尽管毕业时面对的是过去50年来最好的经济形势，Z世代却知道经济火车失事是什么样子。

en: They were impressionable kids during the crash of 2008, when many of their parents lost their jobs or their life savings or both. (23 words)
cn: 在2008年那场大崩溃中，他们正是容易受到影响的孩童，那时很多人的父母失去了工作，或毕生积蓄，或者两者皆失。

en: They aren't interested in taking any chances. (7 words)
cn: 他们对冒险毫无兴趣。

en: The booming economy seems to have done little to assuage this underlying generational sense of anxious urgency, especially for those who have college debt. (24 words)
cn: 蓬勃发展的经济似乎并未怎么缓解这一代人骨子里的焦虑紧迫感，尤其是对那些身负大学债务的人。

en: College loan balances in the U.S. now stand at a record $1.5 trillion, according to the Federal Reserve. (18 words)
cn: 根据美联储的数据，美国的大学贷款余额目前达到了创纪录的1.5万亿美元。

en: One survey from Accenture found that 88 percent of graduating seniors this year chose their major with a job in mind. (21 words)
cn: 埃森哲的一项调查发现，今年88%的应届毕业生在选择专业时心中已有一份工作。

en: In a 2019 survey of University of Georgia students, meanwhile, the career office found the most desirable trait in a future employer was the ability to offer secure employment (followed by professional development and training, and then inspiring purpose). (39 words)
cn: 与此同时，在2019年对佐治亚大学学生的一项调查中，就业指导中心发现，未来雇主最令人向往的特质是能够提供安稳的就业（其次依次是职业发展与培训，以及鼓舞人心的使命感）。

en: Job security or stability was the second most important career goal (work-life balance was number one), followed by a sense of being dedicated to a cause or to feel good about serving the greater good. (35 words)
cn: 工作保障或稳定性是排在第二位的职业目标（工作与生活的平衡位列第一），紧随其后的是投身于某项事业的感觉，或者是为服务更广大利益而感到自己做得不错。

en: That's a big change from the previous generation. (8 words)
cn: 这与上一代人相比是个巨大的变化。

en: "Millennials wanted more flexibility in their lives," notes Tanya Michelson, Associate Director of YouthSight, a UK-based brand manager that conducts regular 60-day surveys of British youth, in findings that might just as well apply to American youth. (37 words)
cn: "千禧一代希望在生活中有更多灵活性，"青年视野副总监塔尼娅·米切尔森指出，该机构是一家定期对英国青年进行为期60天调查的英国品牌管理公司，其研究发现可能同样适用于美国青年。

en: "Generation Zs are looking for more certainty and stability, because of the rise of the gig economy. (17 words)
cn: "Z世代寻求的是更多的确定性和稳定性，因为零工经济的兴起。

en: They have trouble seeing a financial future and they are quite risk averse." (13 words)
cn: 他们很难看清财务上的未来，而且相当厌恶风险。"

en: "Reskilling" is something that sounds like a buzzword but is actually a requirement if we plan to have a future where a lot of would-be workers do not get left behind. (31 words)
cn: "重新技能培训"听起来像是个流行词，但如果我们想拥有一个不让大量潜在劳动力掉队的未来，它实际上是一种必需。

en: We know we are moving into a period where the jobs in demand will change rapidly, as will the requirements of the jobs that remain. (25 words)
cn: 我们知道，我们正进入一个需求岗位将迅速变化的时期，而那些保留下来的岗位本身的要求也会同样快速变化。

en: Research by the World Economic Forum finds that on average 42 per cent of the "core skills" within job roles will change by 2022. (24 words)
cn: 世界经济论坛的研究发现，到2022年，各个岗位中平均42%的"核心技能"将会改变。

en: That is a very short timeline. (6 words)
cn: 这是一个非常短的时间线。

en: The question of who should pay for reskilling is a thorny one. (12 words)
cn: 谁该为重新技能培训买单，是一个棘手的问题。

en: For individual companies, the temptation is always to let go of workers whose skills are no longer in demand and replace them with those whose skills are. (27 words)
cn: 对个别公司来说，诱惑总是放掉那些技能已不再需要的工人，换成拥有所需技能的人。

en: That does not always happen. (5 words)
cn: 这种情况并不总是发生。

en: AT&T is often given as the gold standard of a company who decided to do a massive reskilling program rather than go with a fire-and-hire strategy. (26 words)
cn: AT&T常被视为一家公司的典范，因为它决定推行大规模的重新技能培训项目，而不是采取先裁后招的策略。

en: Other companies had also pledged to create their own plans. (10 words)
cn: 其他公司也承诺要制定自己的计划。

en: When the skills mismatch is in the broader economy, though, the focus usually turns to government to handle. (18 words)
cn: 然而，当技能错配出现在更广泛的经济层面时，焦点通常转向由政府来加以处理。

en: Efforts in Canada and elsewhere have been arguably languid at best, and have given us a situation where we frequently hear of employers begging for workers, even at times and in regions where unemployment is high. (36 words)
cn: 加拿大及其他地方的努力充其量也只能说是迟缓无力的，这就导致了一种局面：我们频繁听到雇主求着要工人，即便是在失业率很高的时期和地区。

en: With the pandemic, unemployment is very high indeed. (8 words)
cn: 伴随疫情到来，失业率真的很严重。

en: In February, at 3.5 per cent and 5.5 per cent respectively, unemployment rates in Canada and the United States were at generational lows and worker shortages were everywhere. (28 words)
cn: 二月份，加拿大和美国的失业率分别处于3.5%和5.5%，为一代人以来的低点，工人短缺的问题无处不在。

en: As of May, those rates had spiked up to 13.3 per cent and 13.7 per cent, and although many worker shortages had disappeared, not all had done so. (28 words)
cn: 到了五月，这两个数字已飙升至13.3%和13.7%，尽管许多工人短缺的现象已消失，但并非全部如此。

en: In the medical field, to take an obvious example, the pandemic meant that there were still clear shortages of doctors, nurses and other medical personnel. (25 words)
cn: 举一个明显的例子，在医疗领域，疫情意味着医生、护士和其他医务人员的短缺依然十分明显。

en: Of course, it is not like you can take an unemployed waiter and train him to be a doctor in a few weeks, no matter who pays for it. (29 words)
cn: 当然，你不可能把一名失业的侍者用几周就培训成一名医生，不管由谁掏钱。

en: But even if you cannot close that gap, maybe you can close others, and doing so would be to the benefit of all concerned. (24 words)
cn: 但即便你无法弥合那道鸿沟，也许可以弥合其他缺口，这样做对各方都有利。

en: That seems to be the case in Sweden: When forced to furlough 90 per cent of their cabin staff, Scandinavian Airlines decided to start up a short retraining program that reskilled the laid-off workers to support hospital staff. (38 words)
cn: 瑞典似乎就是这种情况：斯堪的纳维亚航空公司在被迫让90%的客舱员工休无薪假时，决定启动一个短期再培训项目，让那些被临时裁员的工人获得新技能来支援医院人员。

en: The effort was a collective one and involved other companies as well as a Swedish university. (16 words)
cn: 这一努力是集体性的，还有其他公司以及一所瑞典大学共同参与。

en: With the global population predicted to hit close to 10 billion by 2050, and forecasts that agricultural production in some regions will need to nearly double to keep pace, food security is increasingly making headlines. (35 words)
cn: 随着全球人口预计到2050年将逼近百亿，且据预测有些地区的农业产量需要将近翻番才能跟上需求，粮食安全正越来越多地成为头条新闻。

en: In the UK, it has become a big talking point recently too, for a rather particular reason: Brexit. (18 words)
cn: 在英国，由于一个相当特殊的原因——脱欧，近来它也成了一大热议话题。

en: Brexit is seen by some as an opportunity to reverse a recent trend towards the UK importing food. (18 words)
cn: 脱欧被一些人视为一个契机，可以逆转英国近年来依赖食品进口的趋势。

en: The country produces only about 60 per cent of the food it eats, down from almost three-quarters in the late 1980s. (21 words)
cn: 这个国家仅生产其消费食物的60%左右，低于20世纪80年代末的近四分之三。

en: A move back to self-sufficiency, the argument goes, would boost the farming industry, political sovereignty and even the nation's health. (20 words)
cn: 这种观点认为，回归自给自足将促进农业发展，强化政治主权，甚至提升国民健康。

en: Sounds great—but how feasible is this vision? (8 words)
cn: 听起来不错——但这一愿景有多大的可行性呢？

en: According to a report on UK food production from the University of Leeds, UK, 85 per cent of the country's total land area is associated with meat and dairy production. (30 words)
cn: 根据利兹大学一份关于英国粮食生产的报告，该国85%的土地面积与肉类和奶制品生产有关。

en: That supplies 80 per cent of what is consumed, so even covering the whole country in livestock farms wouldn't allow us to cover all our meat and dairy needs. (29 words)
cn: 这部分土地供给了所消费肉奶产品的80%，所以就算把整个国家都变成畜牧场，也无法完全满足我们对肉类和奶制品的全部需求。

en: There are many caveats to those figures, but they are still grave. (12 words)
cn: 这些数字有许多需要加以说明的地方，但形势仍然严峻。

en: To become much more self-sufficient, the UK would need to drastically reduce its consumption of animal foods, and probably also farm more intensively—meaning fewer green fields, and more factory-style production. (31 words)
cn: 要提高自给自足程度，英国就需要大幅减少对动物性食品的消费，并且很可能还要进行更集约化的养殖——这意味着更少的绿色田野，更多的工厂式生产。

en: But switching to a mainly plant-based diet wouldn't help. (9 words)
cn: 但转向以植物性膳食为主的饮食结构也无济于事。

en: There is a good reason why the UK is dominated by animal husbandry: most of its terrain doesn't have the right soil or climate to grow crops on a commercial basis. (31 words)
cn: 英国之所以以畜牧业为主，有一个很现实的理由：其大部分地区的土壤或气候都不适合经济作物的商业种植。

en: Just 25 per cent of the country's land is suitable for crop-growing, most of which is already occupied by arable fields. (21 words)
cn: 这个国家只有25%的土地适合种植作物，而其中绝大部分已被现有的耕地所占用。

en: Even if we converted all the suitable land to fields of fruit and veg—which would involve taking out all the nature reserves and removing thousands of people from their homes—we would achieve only a 30 per cent boost in crop production. (43 words)
cn: 即便我们将所有适宜土地都改造成水果和蔬菜田——这将涉及取消所有自然保护区，让成千上万的人迁离家园——我们也只能实现农作物产量30%的增长。

en: Just 23 per cent of the fruit and vegetables consumed in the UK are currently home-grown, so even with the most extreme measures we could meet only 30 per cent of our fresh produce needs. (35 words)
cn: 目前英国消费的水果和蔬菜只有23%是本土种植的，所以即便采取最极端的措施，我们也只能满足30%的新鲜农产品需求。

en: That is before we look for the space to grow the grains, sugars, seeds and oils that provide us with the vast bulk of our current calorie intake. (28 words)
cn: 而这还尚未考虑到，我们需要另寻空间来种植为我们提供绝大部分热量摄入的谷物、糖类、籽实和油料。

en: When Microsoft bought task management app Wunderlist and mobile calendar Sunrise in 2015, it picked two newcomers that were attracting considerable buzz in Silicon Valley. (25 words)
cn: 当微软在2015年收购任务管理应用奇妙清单和移动日历应用日出时，它挑选的是在硅谷引发了不少轰动的两个新来者。

en: Microsoft's own Office dominates the market for "productivity" software, but the start-ups represented a new wave of technology designed from the ground up for the smartphone world. (27 words)
cn: 微软自家的Office在"生产力"软件市场占据主导地位，但这俩初创公司代表着从零开始为智能手机世界打造的一波新技术浪潮。

en: Both apps, however, were later scrapped after Microsoft said it had used their best features in its own products. (19 words)
cn: 然而，这两款应用后来都被停掉了，微软称其已将二者最好的功能用在了自家的产品中。

en: Their teams of engineers stayed on, making them two of the many "acqui-hires" that the biggest companies have used to feed their great hunger for tech talent. (27 words)
cn: 它们的工程师团队留了下来，成为众多"人才收购"中的两例，那些大公司正是通过这种方式来满足自身对科技人才的巨大渴求。

en: To Microsoft's critics, the fates of Wunderlist and Sunrise are examples of a remorseless drive by Big Tech to chew up any innovative companies that lie in their path. (29 words)
cn: 在微软的批评者看来，奇妙清单和日出的命运正是大型科技公司无情吞噬任何挡路创新型企业的例证。

en: "They bought the seedlings and closed them down," complained Paul Arnold, a partner at San Francisco-based Switch Ventures, putting an end to businesses that might one day turn into competitors. (30 words)
cn: "他们买下了幼苗，又把它们关停，"总部位于旧金山的Switch Ventures的合伙人保罗·阿诺德抱怨道，这使得那些将来可能成长为竞争者的企业就此终结。

en: Microsoft declined to comment. (4 words)
cn: 微软拒绝置评。

en: Like other start-up investors, Mr. Arnold's own business often depends on selling start-ups to larger tech companies, though he admits to mixed feelings about the result: "I think these things are good for me, if I put my selfish hat on. (41 words)
cn: 像其他初创公司投资者一样，阿诺德自身的业务常常就要靠把这些初创公司卖给更大的科技公司，尽管他承认对这样的结果心情复杂："如果我自私一点讲，我认为这些事对我个人而言是好事。

en: But are they good for the American economy? (8 words)
cn: 但这对美国经济好吗？

en: I don't know." (3 words)
cn: 我不知道。"

en: The U.S. Federal Trade Commission says it wants to find the answer to that question. (15 words)
cn: 美国联邦贸易委员会表示，它想找到这个问题的答案。

en: This week, it asked the five most valuable U.S. tech companies for information about their many small acquisitions over the past decade. (22 words)
cn: 本周，它要求美国五家市值最高的科技公司提供关于它们近十年来许多小规模收购的信息。

en: Although only a research project at this stage, the request has raised the prospect of regulators wading into early-stage tech markets that until now have been beyond their reach. (29 words)
cn: 虽然现阶段这只是一个研究项目，但这一请求已经让人看到监管机构介入早期科技市场的前景，而这些市场迄今为止一直是它们触碰不到的。

en: Given their combined market value of more than $5.5 trillion, rifling through such small deals—many of them much less prominent than Wunderlist and Sunrise—might seem beside the point. (30 words)
cn: 鉴于这五家公司的总市值超过5.5万亿美元，细翻这些小型交易——其中许多远不如奇妙清单和日出那样引人注目——看起来可能有些不着重点。

en: Between them, the five biggest tech companies have spent an average of only 3.4billion a year on sub−1 billion acquisitions over the past five years—a drop in the ocean compared with their massive financial reserves, and the more than $130 billion of venture capital that was invested in the U.S. last year. (54 words)
cn: 过去五年间，这五家最大的科技公司每年花在十亿美元以下规模收购上的钱，平均加在一起也才34亿美元——与它们庞大的财务储备相比，与去年投资在美国市场上超过1300亿美元的风险资本相比，这都不过是沧海一粟。

en: However, critics say that the big companies use such deals to buy their most threatening potential competitors before their businesses have a chance to gain momentum, in some cases as part of a "buy and kill" tactic to simply close them down. (42 words)
cn: 然而，批评者指出，大公司利用这类交易在这些最具威胁的潜在竞争对手有机会形成势头之前就将它们买下，在某些情况下，这不过是"买来即杀"策略的一部分，目的就是干脆把它们关停。

en: We're fairly good at judging people based on first impressions, thin slices of experience ranging from a glimpse of a photo to a five-minute interaction, and deliberation can be not only extraneous but intrusive. (34 words)
cn: 我们相当擅长根据第一印象来判断他人，这些"薄片式"的经验片段可以短至瞥一眼照片，长至五分钟的互动，而仔细思量不仅多余，有时甚至反成干扰。

en: In one study of the ability she called "thin slicing," the late psychologist Nalini Ambady asked participants to watch silent 10-second video clips of professors and to rate the instructor's overall effectiveness. (32 words)
cn: 在一项关于她称之为"薄片化处理"能力的研究中，已故心理学家纳利尼·安巴迪让参与者观看教授们的10秒无声视频片段，并对授课教师的整体效果进行评分。

en: Their ratings correlated strongly with students' end-of-semester ratings. (8 words)
cn: 他们的评分与学生期末给出的评分高度相关。

en: Another set of participants had to count backward from 1,000 by nines as they watched the clips, occupying their conscious working memory. (22 words)
cn: 另一组参与者在观看视频片段时还得从1000开始以9为单位往回倒数，以此占用他们有意识的工作记忆。

en: Their ratings were just as accurate, demonstrating the intuitive nature of the social processing. (14 words)
cn: 他们的评分同样准确，这表明了社交信息处理的直觉本质。

en: Critically, another group was asked to spend a minute writing down reasons for their judgment, before giving the rating. (19 words)
cn: 关键的是，还有一组被要求在给出评分前花一分钟写下他们做出判断的理由

en: Accuracy dropped dramatically. (3 words)
cn: 准确率急剧下降。

en: Ambady suspected that deliberation focused them on vivid but misleading cues, such as certain gestures or utterances, rather than letting the complex interplay of subtle signals form a holistic impression. (30 words)
cn: 安巴迪推测，仔细思量让他们的注意力集中在了某些生动却会误导的线索上，比如某些手势或言辞，而不是让诸多细微信号复杂的交互作用形成一个整体印象。

en: She found similar interference when participants watched 15-second clips of pairs of people and judged whether they were strangers, friends, or dating partners. (23 words)
cn: 当参与者观看一对对人之间的15秒视频片段，并判断他们是陌生人、朋友还是约会对象时，她发现了类似的干扰。

en: Other research shows we're better at detecting deception from thin slices when we rely on intuition instead of reflection. (19 words)
cn: 别的研究表明，当我们依赖直觉而非反思时，从薄片式信息中辨别谎言的能力更强。

en: "It's as if you're driving a stick shift," says Judith Hall, a psychologist at Northeastern University, "and if you start thinking about it too much, you can't remember what you're doing. (31 words)
cn: "这就像你开着手动挡车，"东北大学心理学家朱迪丝·霍尔说，"如果你开始想得太多，反倒记不得自己在做什么。

en: But if you go on automatic pilot, you're fine. (9 words)
cn: 但如果你靠本能自动去操作，就没事。

en: Much of our social life is like that." (8 words)
cn: 我们大部分的社会生活也是如此。"

en: Thinking too much can also harm our ability to form preferences. (11 words)
cn: 想得太多还会损害我们形成偏好的能力。

en: College students' ratings of strawberry jams and college courses aligned better with experts' opinions when the students weren't asked to analyze their rationale. (23 words)
cn: 当不被要求分析自己的理由时，大学生对草莓酱的评判和对大学课程的评价，都与专家意见吻合得更好。

en: And people made car-buying decisions that were both objectively better and more personally satisfying when asked to focus on their feelings rather than on details, but only if the decision was complex—when they had a lot of information to process. (41 words)
cn: 人们在被要求关注自己的感受而非细节时，做出的购车决定既在客观上更优，也让个人更满意，但这只有在决策足够复杂——即他们需要处理大量信息时——才成立。

en: Intuition's special powers are unleashed only in certain circumstances. (9 words)
cn: 直觉的特殊力量只有在某些特定情形下才会被释放出来。

en: In one study, participants completed a battery of eight tasks, including four that tapped reflective thinking (discerning rules, comprehending vocabulary) and four that tapped intuition and creativity (generating new products or figures of speech). (34 words)
cn: 在一项研究中，参与者完成了一组共八项任务，包括四项利用反思性思维的任务（辨别规则、理解词汇）和四项利用直觉与创造力的任务（生成新产品或修辞手法）。

en: Then they rated the degree to which they had used intuition ("gut feelings," "hunches," "my heart"). (16 words)
cn: 然后，他们对自己运用直觉的程度进行了评分，（使用的是诸如"直觉感受""预感""我的内心"这类描述）。

en: Use of their gut hurt their performance on the first four tasks, as expected, and helped them on the rest. (20 words)
cn: 正如所料，凭直觉行事有损于他们在前四项任务中的表现，却有助于完成其余各项。

en: Sometimes the heart is smarter than the head. (8 words)
cn: 有时候，心灵比头脑更聪明。

en: On a recent sunny day, 13,000 chickens roam over Larry Brown's 40 windswept acres in Shiner, Texas. (17 words)
cn: 在近期一个风和日丽的日子里，一万三千只鸡在拉里·布朗位于得克萨斯州夏纳镇那40英亩迎风起伏的土地上漫步。

en: Some rest in the shade of a parked car. (9 words)
cn: 有的在一辆停着的车的荫凉下休息。

en: Others drink water with the cows. (6 words)
cn: 还有的和牛一起饮水。

en: This all seems random, but it's by design, part of what the $6.1 billion U.S. egg industry bets will be its next big thing: climate-friendly eggs. (26 words)
cn: 这一切看似随意，却是有意为之，是美国价值61亿美元的鸡蛋产业押注的下一件大事的一部分：对气候友好的鸡蛋。

en: These eggs, which are making their debut now on shelves for as much as $8 a dozen, are still labeled organic and animal-friendly, but they're also from birds that live on farms using regenerative agriculture—special techniques to cultivate rich soils that can trap greenhouse gases. (46 words)
cn: 这些鸡蛋如今正以高达8美元一打的价格初次上架销售，仍然贴着"有机"和"动物友好"的标签，但它们还产自采用再生农业的农场——这种农业运用特殊技术来培育能够捕获温室气体的肥沃土壤。

en: Such eggs could be marketed as helping to fight climate change. (11 words)
cn: 这类鸡蛋可以打着有助于抗击气候变化的旗号来销售。

en: "I'm excited about our progress," says Brown, who harvests eggs for Denver-based NestFresh Eggs and is adding more cover crops that draw worms and crickets for the chickens to eat. (30 words)
cn: "对我们取得的进展我感到兴奋，"布朗说，他为总部位于丹佛的NestFresh鸡蛋公司收蛋，眼下正在种植更多的覆盖作物，这些作物会吸引蠕虫和蟋蟀来给鸡吃。

en: The birds' waste then fertilizes fields. (6 words)
cn: 鸡的排泄物随后又会给田地施肥。

en: Such improvements "allow our hens to forage for higher-quality natural feed that will be good for the land, the hens, and the eggs that we supply to our customers." (29 words)
cn: 这样的改良"让我们的母鸡能觅食到品质更高的天然饲料，这对土地、对母鸡、对我们供应给顾客的鸡蛋都有好处。"

en: The egg industry's push is the first major test of whether animal products from regenerative farms can become the next premium offering. (22 words)
cn: 鸡蛋产业的此番发力，是对来自再生农场的动物产品能否成为下一个高端品类首次重大检验。

en: In barely more than a decade, organic eggs went from being dismissed as a niche product in natural foods stores to being sold at Walmart. (25 words)
cn: 在仅仅十余年的时间里，有机鸡蛋就从被不屑一顾地放在天然食品商店当小众产品，走到了在沃尔玛销售的阶段。

en: More recently there were similar doubts about probiotics and plant- based meats, but both have exploded into major supermarket categories. (19 words)
cn: 更近一段时期，益生菌和植物基肉类也遭遇过类似的质疑，但两者都已爆炸式地成长为超市的主要品类。

en: If the sustainable-egg rollout is successful, it could open the floodgates for regenerative beef, broccoli, and beyond. (17 words)
cn: 如果这种可持续鸡蛋的推广获得成功，就可能为再生方式生产的牛肉、西兰花以及更多品类打开闸门。

en: Regenerative products could be a hard sell, because the concept is tough to define quickly, says Julie Stanton, associate professor of agricultural economics at Pennsylvania State University Brandywine. (28 words)
cn: 宾夕法尼亚州立大学布兰迪万校区农业经济学副教授朱莉·斯坦顿说，再生类产品可能很难推销，因为这一概念很难用三言两语就说清楚。

en: Such farming also brings minimal, if any, improvement to the food products (though some producers say their eggs have more protein). (21 words)
cn: 这类农业生产对食品本身带来的改良，就算有的话也是微乎其微的（尽管一些生产者称他们的鸡蛋蛋白质含量更高）。

en: The industry is betting that the same consumers paying more for premium attributes such as free-range, non-GMO, and pasture- raised eggs will embrace sustainability. (23 words)
cn: 业界在押注的是，那些肯为散养、非转基因和草场饲养鸡蛋这些高端属性付出更高价格的消费者，会同样拥抱可持续性。

en: Surveys show that younger generations are more concerned about climate change, and some of the success of plant-based meat can be chalked up to shoppers wanting to signal their desire to protect the environment. (34 words)
cn: 调查显示，年轻一代对气候变化更加忧虑，植物基肉类取得的成功一定程度上可以归因于顾客想要表达自身保护环境意愿的这种需求。

en: Young adults "really care about the planet," says John Brunnquell, president of Egg Innovations. (14 words)
cn: 年轻人"真的很在乎这个星球，"鸡蛋创新公司总裁约翰·布伦奎尔说。

en: "They are absolutely altering the food chain beyond what I think even they understand what they're doing." (17 words)
cn: "他们绝对在改变食物链，其程度甚至我觉得连他们自己都不完全明白。"

en: More Americans are opting to work well into retirement, a growing trend that threatens to upend the old workforce model. (20 words)
cn: 越来越多的美国人选择在退休后仍长期工作，这一日益增长的趋势有可能颠覆旧的劳动力模式。

en: One in three Americans who are at least 40 have or plan to have a job in retirement to prepare for a longer life, according to a survey conducted by Harris Poll for TD Ameritrade. (35 words)
cn: 根据哈里斯民意调查为TD Ameritrade进行的一项调查，年满40岁的美国人中每三个就有个已在或计划在退休后仍拥有一份工作，以便为更长的寿命做准备。

en: Even more surprising is that more than half of "unretirees"—those who plan to work in retirement or went back to work after retiring – said they would be employed in their later years even if they had enough money to settle down, the survey showed. (45 words)
cn: 更令人惊讶的是，调查显示，超过一半的"未退休者"——那些计划在退休后继续工作，或已退休后又重返岗位的人——表示，就算有足够的钱安稳下来，他们在晚年也仍会去工作。

en: Financial needs aren't the only culprit for the "unretirement" trend. (10 words)
cn: 经济需求并非"不退休"趋势的唯一原因。

en: Other reasons, according to the study, include personal fulfillment such as staying mentally fit, preventing boredom or avoiding depression. (19 words)
cn: 根据这项研究，其他原因还包括个人成就感，比如保持心理健康、防止无聊或避免抑郁。

en: "The concept of retirement is evolving," said Christine Russell, senior manager of retirement at TD Ameritrade. (16 words)
cn: "退休的概念正在演变，"德美利证券（TD Ameritrade）的退休事务高级经理克里斯蒂娜·拉塞尔说。

en: "It's not just about finances. (5 words)
cn: "这不仅仅是钱财的问题。

en: The value of work is also driving folks to continue working past retirement." (13 words)
cn: 工作的价值也在驱使着人们在过了退休年龄后继续工作。"

en: One reason for the change in retirement patterns: Americans are living longer. (12 words)
cn: 退休模式发生这种变化的一个原因是：美国人的寿命越来越长。

en: The share of the population 65 and older was 16% in 2018, up 3.2% from the prior year, according to the U.S. Census Bureau. (24 words)
cn: 根据美国人口普查局的数据，2018年65岁及以上人口占比为16%，较前一年上升了3.2%。

en: That's also up 30.2% since 2010. (6 words)
cn: 自2010年以来，这一占比也上升了30.2%。

en: Because of longer life spans, Americans are also boosting their savings to preserve their nest eggs, the TD Ameritrade study showed, which surveyed 2,000 adults between 40 to 79. (29 words)
cn: 德美利证券公司的这项研究对2000名40至79岁的成年人进行了调查，结果显示，由于寿命更长，美国人也在增加储蓄以保全自己的养老积蓄。

en: Six in 10 "unretirees" are increasing their savings in anticipation of a longer life, according to the survey. (18 words)
cn: 根据调查，十分之六的"未退休者"正因预期寿命更长而增加储蓄。

en: Among the most popular ways they are doing this, the company said, is by reducing their overall expenses, securing life insurance or maximizing their contributions to retirement accounts. (28 words)
cn: 该公司称，他们这样做最普遍的方式是削减总开支、购买人寿保险，或者向退休账户缴入最大额度的资金。

en: Unfortunately, many people who are opting to work in retirement are preparing to do so because they are worried about making ends meet in their later years, said Brent Weiss, a co-founder at Baltimore-based financial-planning firm Facet Wealth. (38 words)
cn: 不幸的是，总部位于巴尔的摩的理财规划公司法塞特财富管理公司（Facet Wealth）的联合创始人布伦特·韦斯指出，许多选择在退休后工作的人之所以做此准备，是因为他们担心晚年生计难以为继。

en: He suggested that preretirees should speak with a financial adviser to set long-term financial goals. (15 words)
cn: 他建议，尚未退休的人应当和理财顾问聊聊，设定长期的财务目标。

en: "The most challenging moments in life are getting married, starting a family and ultimately retiring," Weiss said. (17 words)
cn: "人生中最具挑战性的几个时刻是结婚、组建家庭，以及最后退休，"韦斯说。

en: "It's not just a financial decision, but an emotional one. (10 words)
cn: "这不仅仅是一个财务决定，也是一个情感上的决定。

en: Many people believe they can't retire." (6 words)
cn: 很多人相信他们没法退休。"

en: We have all encountered them, in both our personal and professional lives. (12 words)
cn: 无论是在个人生活还是职场生涯中，我们都碰到过它们。

en: Think about the times you felt tricked or frustrated by a membership or subscription that had a seamless signup process but was later difficult to cancel. (26 words)
cn: 想想那些你感到被捉弄或恼火的时刻：某个会员或订阅服务的注册过程毫无障碍，可后来却很难取消。

en: Something that should be simple and transparent can be complicated, intentionally or unintentionally, in ways that impair consumer choice. (19 words)
cn: 本该简单透明的事情，却可能以或有意或无意的方式变得复杂起来，从而损害消费者的选择权。

en: These are examples of dark patterns. (6 words)
cn: 这些都是暗黑模式的例证。

en: First coined in 2010 by user experience expert Harry Brignull, "dark patterns" is a catch-all term for practices that manipulate user interfaces to influence the decision-making ability of users. (29 words)
cn: "暗黑模式"一词由用户体验专家哈里·布里格努尔于2010年首次提出，是一个统称术语，指那些操纵用户界面以影响用户决策能力的做法。

en: On darkpatterns.org, Brignull identifies 12 types of common dark patterns, ranging from misdirection and hidden costs to "roach motel," where a user experience seems easy and intuitive at the start, but turns difficult when the user tries to get out. (41 words)
cn: 在darkpatterns.org网站上，布里格努尔列出了12种常见的暗黑模式，从误导、隐藏成本，到"蟑螂旅馆"——即用户体验一开始似乎简单直观，但当用户想退出时却变得困难重重。

en: In a 2019 study of 53,000 product pages and 11,000 websites, researchers found that about one in 10 employs these design practices. (22 words)
cn: 在2019年一项针对5.3万个产品页面和1.1万个网站的研究中，研究者发现大约十分之一都采用了这类设计做法。

en: Though widely prevalent, the concept of dark patterns is still not well understood. (13 words)
cn: 尽管暗黑模式十分普遍，但这一概念至今仍未被充分理解。

en: Business and nonprofit leaders should be aware of dark patterns and try to avoid the gray areas they engender. (19 words)
cn: 企业和非营利机构的领导者应当对暗黑模式有所认识，并尽量避免其所引发的那些灰色地带。

en: Where is the line between ethical, persuasive design and dark patterns? (11 words)
cn: 合乎道德的劝说性设计与暗黑模式之间的界限在哪里？

en: Businesses should engage in conversations with IT, compliance, risk, and legal teams to review their privacy policy, and include in the discussion the customer/user experience designers and coders responsible for the company's user interface, as well as the marketers and advertisers responsible for sign-ups, checkout baskets, pricing, and promotions. (49 words)
cn: 企业应当与信息技术、合规、风控和法务团队展开对话，去审视其隐私政策，并在讨论中纳入负责公司用户界面的客户/用户体验设计师与程序编写人员，以及那些负责用户注册、购物车、定价和促销活动的市场营销和广告人员。

en: Any or all these teams can play a role in creating or avoiding "digital deception." (15 words)
cn: 这些团队中的任何一个或全部，都可能在制造或避免"数字欺骗"中发挥作用。

en: Lawmakers and regulators are slowly starting to address the ambiguity around dark patterns, most recently at the state level. (19 words)
cn: 立法者和监管者正缓慢地开始着手应对围绕暗黑模式的这种模糊性，最近已推进到了州一级层面。

en: In March, the California Attorney General announced the approval of additional regulations under the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) that "ensure that consumers will not be confused or misled when seeking to exercise their data privacy rights." (37 words)
cn: 三月份，加利福尼亚州总检察长宣布，已批准《加州消费者隐私法案》下的补充规定，以"确保消费者在试图行使其数据隐私权利时不会感到困惑或受到误导"。

en: The regulations aim to ban dark patterns — this means prohibiting companies from using "confusing language or unnecessary steps such as forcing them to click through multiple screens or listen to reasons why they shouldn't opt out." (36 words)
cn: 这些规定旨在禁止暗黑模式——这意味着禁止公司使用"令人费解的措辞或不必要的步骤，比如强迫他们点击多个屏幕，或听那些解释为什么他们不该选择退出的理由"。

en: As more states consider promulgating additional regulations, there is a need for greater accountability from within the business community. (19 words)
cn: 随着更多州考虑出台补充性的法规，企业界内部需要有更强的责任担当。

en: Dark patterns also can be addressed on a self-regulatory basis, but only if organizations hold themselves accountable, not just to legal requirements but also to industry best practices and standards. (30 words)
cn: 暗黑模式也可以在自我监管的基础上加以解决，但这只有在组织不仅对法律要求负责，还对行业最佳实践与标准负责时才能实现。

en: Although ethics classes are common around the world, scientists are unsure if their lessons can actually change behavior; evidence either way is weak, relying on contrived laboratory tests or sometimes unreliable self-reports. (32 words)
cn: 尽管伦理课在世界各地都很常见，但科学家尚不确定这些课程是否真能改变行为；支持正反两方的证据都很薄弱，依赖的是人为设置的实验室测试，或者是有时并不可靠的自我报告。

en: But a new study published in Cognition found that, in at least one real-world situation, a single ethics lesson may have had lasting effects. (24 words)
cn: 但发表在《认知》期刊上的一项新研究发现，至少在一种真实情境中，单单一次伦理课或许就能产生持久的效果。

en: The researchers investigated one class session's impact on eating meat. (10 words)
cn: 研究者调查了一节课的内容对食用肉类行为的影响。

en: They chose this particular behavior for three reasons, according to study co-author Eric Schwitzgebel, a philosopher at the University of California, Riverside: students' attitudes on the topic are variable and unstable, behavior is easily measurable, and ethics literature largely agrees that eating less meat is good because it reduces environmental harm and animal suffering. (54 words)
cn: 据该研究的合著者、加利福尼亚大学河滨分校的哲学家埃里克·施维茨格贝尔称，他们选择这一特定行为有三点原因：学生对这一话题的态度多变且不稳定，该行为很容易测量，而且伦理文献很大程度上一致认为少吃肉是件好事，因为它能减少环境危害和动物的苦难。

en: Half of the students in four large philosophy classes read an article on the ethics of factory-farmed meat, optionally watched an 11-minute video on the topic and joined a 50-minute discussion. (31 words)
cn: 在四个大型哲学课堂中，有一半学生阅读了一篇关于工厂化养殖肉类伦理问题的文章，可选观看了一段11分钟的相关视频，并参加了一场50分钟的讨论。

en: The other half focused on charitable giving instead. (8 words)
cn: 另一半学生则将重点放在慈善捐赠上。

en: Then, unbeknownst to the students, the researchers studied their anonymized meal-card purchases for that semester—nearly 14,000 receipts for almost 500 students. (22 words)
cn: 随后，在学生不知情的情况下，研究人员查看了他们那一学期匿名化的餐卡消费记录——涉及近500名学生的约1.4万张收据。

en: "It's an awesome data set," says Nina Strohminger, a psychologist who teaches business ethics at the University of Pennsylvania and was not involved in the study. (26 words)
cn: "这是一份非常棒的数据集，"宾夕法尼亚大学讲授商业伦理的心理学家尼娜·施特罗明格说，她并未参与此项研究。

en: Schwitzgebel predicted the intervention would have no effect; he had previously found that ethics professors do not differ from other professors on a range of behaviors, including voting rates, blood donation and returning library books. (35 words)
cn: 施维茨格贝尔原本预测这项干预不会产生任何效果；他此前曾发现，伦理教授在一系列行为上——包括投票率、献血和归还图书馆藏书——与其他教授并无区别。

en: But among student subjects who discussed meat ethics, meal purchases containing meat decreased from 52 to 45 percent—and this effect held steady for the study's duration of several weeks. (30 words)
cn: 但在讨论过肉类伦理的学生受试者中，含肉类的餐食购买比例从52%下降到了45%——而且该效果在持续数周的研究期间一直保持稳定。

en: Purchases from the other group remained at 52 percent. (9 words)
cn: 另一组的购买比例则保持在52%。

en: "That's actually a pretty large effect for a pretty small intervention," Schwitzgebel says. (13 words)
cn: "就一次如此小规模的干预来说

en: In the quest for the perfect lawn, homeowners across the country are taking a shortcut—and it is the environment that is paying the price. (25 words)
cn: 在追求完美草坪的过程中，全国各地的房主都在走捷径——而付出代价的，正是环境。

en: About eight million square metres of plastic grass is sold each year but opposition has now spread to the highest gardening circles. (22 words)
cn: 每年约有八百万平方米的塑料草皮被售出，但反对之声现已蔓延至园艺界最高层。

en: The Chelsea Flower Show has banned fake grass from this year's event, declaring it to be not part of its ethos. (21 words)
cn: 切尔西花展已禁止人造草皮参加今年的展会，宣称它不符合其精神理念。

en: The Royal Horticultural Society (RHS), which runs the annual show in west London, says it has introduced the ban because of the damage plastic grass does to the environment and biodiversity. (31 words)
cn: 在伦敦西部举办这一年度盛会的皇家园艺学会表示，之所以推出这项禁令，是因为塑料草皮对环境和生物多样性造成了破坏。

en: Ed Horne, of the RHS, said: "We launched our sustainability strategy last year and fake grass is just not in line with our ethos and views on plastic. (28 words)
cn: 皇家园艺学会的埃德·霍恩说："我们去年推出了可持续发展战略，而假草与我们的精神理念和对塑料的看法完全不符。

en: We recommend using real grass because of its environmental benefits, which include supporting wildlife, alleviating flooding and cooling the environment." (20 words)
cn: 我们推荐使用真草，因为它具有诸多环境益处，包括供养野生动植物、缓解洪涝以及为环境降温。"

en: The RHS's decision comes as campaigners try to raise awareness of the problems fake grass causes. (16 words)
cn: 皇家园艺学会做出这一决定之际，活动人士正试图提高人们对假草所造成问题的认识。

en: A Twitter account, which claims to "cut through the greenwash" of artificial grass, already has more than 20,000 followers. (19 words)
cn: 一个声称要"揭穿人造草皮绿色伪装"的推特账号，已经拥有超过两万名关注者。

en: It is trying to encourage people to sign two petitions, one calling for a ban on the sale of plastic grass and another calling for an "ecological damage" tax on such lawns. (32 words)
cn: 它正试图鼓励人们签署两份请愿书，一份呼吁禁止销售塑料草皮，另一份则呼吁对铺此类草皮的草坪征收"生态损害"税。

en: They have gathered 7,276 and 11,282 signatures. (7 words)
cn: 它们已分别收集到了7276个和11282个签名。

en: However, supporters of fake grass point out that there is also an environmental impact with natural lawns, which need mowing and therefore usually consume electricity or petrol. (27 words)
cn: 然而，假草的支持者指出，天然草坪同样会对环境产生影响，因为其需要修剪，因而通常会消耗电力或汽油。

en: The industry also points out that real grass requires considerable amounts of water, weed killer or other treatments and that people who lay fake grass tend to use their garden more. (31 words)
cn: 该行业还指出，真草需要大量的水、除草剂或其他处理，而铺设假草的人往往更多地使用他们的花园。

en: The industry also claims that people who lay fake grass spend an average of £500 on trees or shrubs for their garden, which provides habitat for insects. (27 words)
cn: 该行业还声称，铺设假草的人平均花费500英镑为其花园添置树木或灌木，这为昆虫提供了栖息地。

en: In response to another petition last year about banning fake lawns, which gathered 30,000 signatures, the government responded that it has "no plans to ban the use of artificial grass". (30 words)
cn: 在回应去年另一份获得三万签名的关于禁止假草坪的请愿时，政府答复称，"没有计划禁止使用人造草皮"。

en: It added: "We prefer to help people and organisations make the right choice rather than legislating on such matters. (19 words)
cn: 它补充说："我们更倾向于帮助个人和组织做出正确的选择，而不是就此类事项立法。

en: However, the use of artificial grass must comply with the legal and policy safeguards in place to protect biodiversity and ensure sustainable drainage, while measures such as the strengthened biodiversity duty should serve to encourage public authorities to consider sustainable alternatives." (41 words)
cn: 然而，人造草皮的使用必须遵守为保护生物多样性和确保可持续排水而制定的现行法律和政策保障，而诸如强化版生物多样性责任等措施，则应起到鼓励公共机构考虑可持续替代方案的作用。"

en: It's easy to dismiss as absurd the federal government's ideas for plugging the chronic funding gap of our national parks. (20 words)
cn: 对于联邦政府为填补国家公园长期资金缺口所提出的那些构想，人们很容易嗤之以鼻，视之为荒谬。

en: Can anyone really think it's a good idea to allow Amazon deliveries to your tent in Yosemite or food trucks to line up under the redwood trees at Sequoia National Park? (31 words)
cn: 难道真有人会认为，允许亚马逊把快递送到优胜美地你的帐篷边，或者让餐车在红杉国家公园的红杉树下排成一排，是个好主意吗？

en: But the government is right about one thing: U.S. national parks are in crisis. (14 words)
cn: 但政府在一件事上是正确的：美国国家公园正处于危机之中。

en: Collectively, they have a maintenance backlog of more than $12 billion. (11 words)
cn: 它们加在一起，积压的维护工作所需资金超过120亿美元。

en: Roads, trails, restrooms, visitor centers and other infrastructure are crumbling. (10 words)
cn: 道路、步道、卫生间、游客中心及其他基础设施日渐残破。

en: But privatizing and commercializing the campgrounds would not be a cure-all. (11 words)
cn: 但是将露营地进行私有化和商业化，也并非能解决一切问题的万能良药。

en: Campgrounds are a tiny portion of the overall infrastructure backlog, and businesses in the parks hand over, on average, only about 5% of their revenues to the National Park Service. (30 words)
cn: 露营地仅占整体积压基础设施维护工作的一小部分，而公园内的企业平均只将它们约5%的收入上交给国家公园管理局。

en: Moreover, increased privatization would certainly undercut one of the major reasons why 300 million visitors come to the parks each year: to enjoy nature and get a break from the commercial drumbeat that overwhelms daily life. (36 words)
cn: 此外，加强私有化必将会削弱每年三亿访客来到公园的一个主要原因：享受自然，并从日常生活铺天盖地的商业喧嚣中得到片刻喘息。

en: The real problem is that the parks have been chronically starved of funding. (13 words)
cn: 真正的问题在于，这些公园长期遭受资金严重不足之苦。

en: An economic survey of 700 U.S. taxpayers found that people would be willing to pay a significant amount of money to make sure the parks and their programs are kept intact. (31 words)
cn: 一项针对700名美国纳税人的经济调查发现，人们愿意付出一笔数额可观的钱，以确保这些公园及其各项项目得以完好保留。

en: Some 81% of respondents said they would be willing to pay additional taxes for the next 10 years to avoid any cuts to the national parks. (26 words)
cn: 约81%的受访者表示，他们愿意在今后十年缴纳额外的税收，以避免国家公园遭受任何削减。

en: The national parks provide great value to U.S. residents both as places to escape and as symbols of nature. (19 words)
cn: 国家公园为美国居民提供了巨大的价值，它们既是休憩逃避的去处，也是大自然的象征。

en: On top of this, they produce value from their extensive educational programs, their positive impact on the climate through carbon sequestration, their contribution to our cultural and artistic life, and of course through tourism. (34 words)
cn: 除此之外，它们还通过广泛的教育项目、固碳对气候产生的积极影响、对文化和艺术生活的贡献，当然还有通过旅游，创造着价值。

en: The parks also help keep America's past alive, working with thousands of local jurisdictions around the country to protect historical sites and to bring the stories of these places to life. (31 words)
cn: 这些公园还与全国各地成千上万的地方管辖机构合作，保护历史遗址，让这些地方的故事活起来，从而帮助延续美国的过往历史。

en: The parks do all this on a shoestring. (8 words)
cn: 公园做着这一切，却只有微不足道的预算。

en: Congress allocates only $3 billion a year to the national park system—an amount that has been flat since 2001 (in inflation-adjusted dollars) with the exception of a onetime boost in 2009. (32 words)
cn: 国会每年只向国家公园体系拨款30亿美元——扣除通胀因素，这一金额自2001年以来一直保持不变，仅2009年有一次性的增加。

en: Meanwhile, the number of annual visitors has increased by more than 50% since 1980, and now stands at 330 million visitors per year. (23 words)
cn: 与此同时，年游客数量自1980年以来已增长超过50%，目前已达每年3.3亿人次。

en: The Internet may be changing merely what we remember, not our capacity to do so, suggests Columbia University psychology professor Betsy Sparrow. (22 words)
cn: 哥伦比亚大学心理学教授贝琪·斯帕罗提出，互联网或许只改变我们记忆的内容，而非我们记忆的能力。

en: In 2011, Sparrow led a study in which participants were asked to record 40 factoids in a computer ("an ostrich's eye is bigger than its brain," for example). (28 words)
cn: 2011年，斯帕罗领导了一项研究，参与者被要求往电脑里录入40条小知识（例如"鸵鸟的眼睛比它的脑子大"）。

en: Half of the participants were told the information would be erased, while the other half were told it would be saved. (21 words)
cn: 一半的参与者被告知这些信息将被删除，而另一半则被告知信息将被保存。

en: Guess what? (2 words)
cn: 猜猜结果如何？

en: The latter group made no effort to recall the information when quizzed on it later, because they knew they could find it on their computers. (25 words)
cn: 后一组在后来接受测试时，根本不去努力回忆那些信息，因为他们知道在自己的电脑上就能找到。

en: In the same study, a group was asked to remember both the information and the folders it was stored in. (20 words)
cn: 在同一项研究中，有一组人被要求既记住信息，也记住信息存储在哪些文件夹里。

en: They didn't remember the information, but they remembered how to find the folders. (13 words)
cn: 他们并没有记住信息，却记住了如何找到那些文件夹。

en: In other words, human memory is not deteriorating but "adapting to new communications technology," Sparrow says. (16 words)
cn: 换句话说，人类的记忆并没有退化，而是在"适应新的通讯技术"，斯帕罗说。

en: In a very practical way, the Internet is becoming an external hard drive for our memories, a process known as "cognitive offloading." (22 words)
cn: 以一种非常实际的方式，互联网正成为我们记忆的外置硬盘，这一过程被称为"认知卸载"。

en: Traditionally, this role was fulfilled by data banks, libraries, and other humans. (12 words)
cn: 传统上，这一角色是由数据库、图书馆以及其他人类来扮演的。

en: Your father may never remember birthdays because your mother does, for instance. (12 words)
cn: 例如，你父亲可能永远记不住生日，因为你母亲替他記住了。

en: Some worry that this is having a destructive effect on society, but Sparrow sees an upside. (16 words)
cn: 有人担心这正在对社会产生破坏性的影响，但斯帕罗却看到积极的一面。

en: Perhaps, she suggests, the trend will change our approach to learning from a focus on individual facts and memorization to an emphasis on more conceptual thinking—something that is not available on the Internet. (34 words)
cn: 她提出，这一趋势或许会把我们的学习方法从聚焦单个事实和记忆，转变为强调更加概念化的思维——而这是互联网上所没有的东西。

en: "I personally have never seen all that much intellectual value in memorizing things," Sparrow says, adding that we haven't lost our ability to do it. (25 words)
cn: "我个人从来没有在记忆东西这件事上看到过有多大智力上的价值，"斯帕罗说，并补充道我们并未丧失那样做的能力。

en: Still other experts say it's too soon to understand how the Internet affects our brains. (15 words)
cn: 还有别的专家则表示，要理解互联网如何影响我们的大脑，还为时过早。

en: There is no experimental evidence showing that it interferes with our ability to focus, for instance, wrote psychologists Christopher Chabris and Daniel J. Simons. (24 words)
cn: 例如，并没有实验证据表明它会干扰我们集中注意力的能力，心理学家克里斯托弗·查布里斯和丹尼尔·J·西蒙斯写道。

en: And surfing the web exercised the brain more than reading did among computer-savvy older adults in a 2008 study involving 24 participants at the Semel Institute for Neuroscience and Human Behavior at the University of California, Los Angeles. (38 words)
cn: 而在2008年一项于加州大学洛杉矶分校西梅尔神经科学与人类行为研究所进行的、有24名参与者参加的研究中，在上网的懂电脑老年人中，网上冲浪比阅读更能锻炼大脑。

en: "There may be costs associated with our increased reliance on the Internet, but I'd have to imagine that overall the benefits are going to outweigh those costs," observes psychology professor Benjamin Storm. (32 words)
cn: "我们日益依赖互联网可能会伴随一些代价，但我不得不说，总体而言收益将会大于代价，"心理学教授本杰明·斯托姆评述道。

en: "It seems pretty clear that memory is changing, but is it changing for the better? (15 words)
cn: "似乎挺清楚的一点是，记忆在发生变化，但这是在往好的方向变吗？

en: At this point, we don't know." (6 words)
cn: 在目前，我们还不知道。"

en: Teenagers are paradoxical. (3 words)
cn: 青少年是充满矛盾的。

en: That's a mild and detached way of saying something that parents often express with considerably stronger language. (17 words)
cn: 这是一种温和而客观的说法，用来表达父母们通常用强烈得多的言辞所说的事情。

en: But the paradox is scientific as well as personal. (9 words)
cn: 但这种矛盾既是个人层面的，也是科学层面的。

en: In adolescence, helpless and dependent children who have relied on grown-ups for just about everything become independent people who can take care of themselves and help each other. (28 words)
cn: 在青春期，那些几乎事事依赖大人的无助而依附的儿童，变成了能够照顾自己、相互帮助的独立个体。

en: At the same time, once cheerful and compliant children become rebellious teenage risk-takers. (13 words)
cn: 与此同时，曾经欢乐顺从的孩子变成了叛逆、爱冒险的青少年。

en: A new study published in the journal Child Development, by Eveline Crone of the University of Leiden and colleagues, suggests that the positive and negative sides of teenagers go hand in hand. (32 words)
cn: 莱顿大学的伊芙琳·克罗内及其同事发表在《儿童发展》期刊上的一项新研究表明，青少年的正面与负面特质是同时并存的。

en: The study is part of a new wave of thinking about adolescence. (12 words)
cn: 这项研究是关于青春期新一轮思考的一部分。

en: For a long time, scientists and policy makers concentrated on the idea that teenagers were a problem that needed to be solved. (22 words)
cn: 很长一段时间里，科学家和政策制定者都聚焦于这样一种观念，即青少年是需要被解决的问题。

en: The new work emphasizes that adolescence is a time of opportunity as well as risk. (15 words)
cn: 这项新研究则强调，青春期既是机遇期，也是风险期。

en: The researchers studied "prosocial" and rebellious traits in more than 200 children and young adults, ranging from 11 to 28 years old. (22 words)
cn: 研究者对200多名年龄从11岁到28岁的儿童和年轻成年人的"亲社会"特质与叛逆特质进行了研究。

en: The participants filled out questionnaires about how often they did things that were altruistic and positive, like sacrificing their own interests to help a friend, or rebellious and negative, like getting drunk or staying out late. (36 words)
cn: 参与者填写了问卷，回答他们做利他而积极的事——如牺牲自身利益去帮助朋友——以及做叛逆而消极的事——如喝醉酒或在外面待到很晚——的频率。

en: Other studies have shown that rebellious behavior increases as you become a teenager and then fades away as you grow older. (21 words)
cn: 其他研究已表明，叛逆行为会随着你成为青少年而增多，又随着你年龄渐长而消退。

en: But the new study shows that, interestingly, the same pattern holds for prosocial behavior. (14 words)
cn: 但这项新研究显示，有趣的是，亲社会行为也遵循同一模式。

en: Teenagers were more likely than younger children or adults to report that they did things like unselfishly help a friend. (20 words)
cn: 相比更小的儿童或成人，青少年更可能报告自己做过像无私帮助朋友这样的事。

en: Most significantly, there was a positive correlation between prosociality and rebelliousness. (11 words)
cn: 最为重要的是，亲社会倾向与叛逆性之间呈正相关关系。

en: The teenagers who were more rebellious were also more likely to help others. (13 words)
cn: 越叛逆的青少年，也越有可能去帮助他人。

en: The good and bad sides of adolescence seem to develop together. (11 words)
cn: 青春期好的一面与坏的一面似乎一同发展。

en: Is there some common factor that underlies these apparently contradictory developments? (11 words)
cn: 是否存在某种共同因素，构成了这些明显矛盾之发展变化的基础？

en: One idea is that teenage behavior is related to what researchers call "reward sensitivity." (14 words)
cn: 一种观点认为，青少年行为与研究者所称的"奖赏敏感性"有关。

en: Decision-making always involves balancing rewards and risks, benefits and costs. (10 words)
cn: 决策总是涉及在奖赏与风险、收益与代价之间进行权衡。

en: "Reward sensitivity" measures how much reward it takes to outweigh risk. (11 words)
cn: "奖赏敏感性"衡量的是需要多大奖赏才能压倒风险。

en: Teenagers are particularly sensitive to social rewards—winning the game, impressing a new friend, getting that boy to notice you. (20 words)
cn: 青少年对社交奖赏尤其敏感——赢得比赛、给新朋友留下印象、让那男孩注意到你。

en: Reward sensitivity, like prosocial behavior and risk-taking, seems to go up in adolescence and then down again as we age. (20 words)
cn: 奖赏敏感性，就像亲社会行为和冒险行为一样，似乎在青春期上升，而后随着年龄增长再度下降。

en: Somehow, when you hit 30, the chance that something exciting and new will happen at that party just doesn't seem to outweigh the effort of getting up off the couch. (30 words)
cn: 不知怎么，等到了三十岁，那场派对上可能会发生某种激动人心的新鲜事的几率，似乎就是抵不过从沙发上起身所费的力气了。

en: Anger over AI's role in exacerbating inequality could endanger the technology's future. (12 words)
cn: 人们对人工智能加剧不平等的愤怒，可能危及这项技术的未来。

en: In her new book Cogs and Monsters: What Economics Is, and What It Should Be, Diane Coyle, an economist at Cambridge University, argues that the digital economy requires new ways of thinking about progress. (34 words)
cn: 剑桥大学经济学家黛安·科伊尔在其新书《齿轮与怪物：经济学是什么，以及它应该是什么》中指出，数字经济需要关于进步的新思考方式。

en: "Whatever we mean by the economy growing, by things getting better, the gains will have to be more evenly shared than in the recent past," she writes. (29 words)
cn: "无论我们所说的经济增长、情况变好指的是什么，这些成果都必须比最近这阶段更公平地分享，"她写道。

en: "An economy of tech millionaires or billionaires and gig workers, with middle-income jobs undercut by automation, will not be politically sustainable." (23 words)
cn: "一个由科技百万富翁或亿万富翁和零工工人组成、中等收入岗位被自动化侵蚀的经济，在政治上是不可持续的。"

en: Improving living standards and increasing prosperity for more people will require greater use of digital technologies to boost productivity in various sectors, including health care and construction, says Coyle. (29 words)
cn: 科伊尔说，要提高更多人的生活水平并增进繁荣，就需要更多地使用数字技术来提升包括医疗保健和建筑在内的各个部门的生产率

en: But people can't be expected to embrace the changes if they're not seeing the benefits—if they're just seeing good jobs being destroyed. (23 words)
cn: 但如果人们看不到其中的收益——如果他们看到的只是好的工作岗位被摧毁——就不能指望他们会拥抱这些变革。

en: In a recent interview with MIT Technology Review, Coyle said she fears that tech's inequality problem could be a roadblock to deploying AI. (23 words)
cn: 在最近接受《麻省理工科技评论》采访时，科伊尔说她担心技术带来的不平等问题可能成为部署人工智能的路障。

en: "We're talking about disruption," she says. (8 words)
cn: "我们谈论的是颠覆，"她说。

en: "These are transformative technologies that change the ways we spend our time every day, that change business models that succeed." (22 words)
cn: "这些是变革性技术，会改变我们每天度过时间的方式，会改变那些成功的商业模式。"

en: To make such "tremendous changes," she adds, you need social buy-in. (13 words)
cn: 要推动如此"巨大的变革"，她补充道，你需要社会的认可。

en: Instead, says Coyle, resentment is simmering among many as the benefits are perceived to go to elites in a handful of prosperous cities. (23 words)
cn: 科伊尔说，相反，由于被视作好处都落入了少数几个繁荣城市的精英手中，许多人心中正暗暗滋生着怨恨。

en: According to the Brookings Institution, a short list of eight American cities that included San Francisco, San Jose, Boston, and Seattle had roughly 38% of all tech jobs by 2019. (30 words)
cn: 据布鲁金斯学会统计，到2019年，包括旧金山、圣何塞、波士顿和西雅图在内的一份仅八个美国城市的短名单，占据了全美约38%的科技岗位。

en: New AI technologies are particularly concentrated: Brookings's Mark Muro and Sifan Liu estimate that just 15 cities account for two-thirds of the AI assets and capabilities in the United States (San Francisco and San Jose alone account for about one-quarter). (40 words)
cn: 新的人工智能技术尤为集中：布鲁金斯学会的马克·穆罗和刘思凡估计，仅15个城市就占据了美国人工智能资产和能力的三分之二（单单旧金山和圣何塞就占了约四分之一）。

en: The dominance of a few cities in the invention and commercialization of AI means that geographical disparities in wealth will continue to soar. (23 words)
cn: 少数几个城市在人工智能的发明和商业化中占据主导地位，这意味着财富的地理差距将继续急剧扩大。

en: Not only will this foster political and social unrest, but it could, as Coyle suggests, hold back the sorts of AI technologies needed for regional economies to grow. (28 words)
cn: 这不仅会助长政治和社会动荡，而且正如科伊尔所提出的，可能还会阻碍地区经济增长所需的那种人工智能技术的发展。

en: Part of the solution could lie in somehow loosening the stranglehold that Big Tech has on defining the AI agenda. (20 words)
cn: 解决方案的一部分，可能在于以某种方式松开大型科技公司对于定义人工智能议程的钳制。

en: That will likely take increased federal funding for research independent of the tech giants. (14 words)
cn: 这很可能需要增加联邦资金，以支持独立于科技巨头之外的研究。

en: Muro and others have suggested hefty federal funding to help create US regional innovation centers, for example. (17 words)
cn: 例如，穆罗和其他人曾建议投入大量联邦资金，帮助创建美国区域创新中心。

en: A more immediate response is to broaden our digital imaginations to conceive of AI technologies that don't simply replace jobs but expand opportunities in the sectors that different parts of the country care most about, like health care, education, and manufacturing. (41 words)
cn: 一个更直接的回应是拓宽我们的数字想象力，去构想那些不是简单地取代工作，而是在全国各地均最为关心的部门中——如医疗保健、教育和制造业——拓宽机会的人工智能技术。2402

en: The UK is facing a future construction crisis because of a failure to plant trees to produce wood, Confor has warned. (21 words)
cn: 英国林业与木材行业机构Confor已发出警告，由于长期未能植树造林以生产木材，英国正面临着一场未来的建筑危机。

en: The forestry and wood trade body has called for urgent action to reduce the country's reliance on timber imports and provide a stable supply of wood for future generations. (29 words)
cn: 这一林业和木材贸易机构呼吁采取紧急行动，以减少国家对木材进口的依赖，并为未来几代人提供稳定的木材供应。

en: Currently only 20 percent of the UK's wood requirement is home-grown while it remains the second-largest net importer of timber in the world. (23 words)
cn: 目前，英国的木材需求中只有20%为本土生产，而它仍然是世界上第二大木材净进口国。

en: Coming at a time of fresh incentives from the UK government for landowners to grow more trees, the trade body says these don't go far enough and fail to promote the benefits of planting them to boost timber supplies. (39 words)
cn: 这一呼吁正值英国政府出台新激励措施鼓励土地所有者种更多树之际，但该贸易机构表示，这些措施力度不够，且未能宣传植树对增加木材供应的好处。

en: "Not only are we facing a carbon crisis now, but we will also be facing a future construction crisis because of failure to plant trees to produce wood," said Stuart Goodall, chief executive of Confor. (28 words)
cn: "我们现在不仅面临着碳危机，而且由于未能植树产材，未来还将面临建筑危机，"Confor首席执行官斯图尔特·古道尔说。

en: "For decades we have not taken responsibility for investing in our domestic wood supply, leaving us exposed to fluctuating prices and fighting for future supplies of wood as global demand rises and our own supplies fall." (36 words)
cn: "几十年来，我们一直没有承担起投资于国内木材供应的责任，这让我们暴露在波动的价格之下，并且随着全球需求上升和本国供应下降，我们还不得不在争夺未来的木材供应中拼抢。"

en: The UK has ideal conditions for growing wood to build low-carbon homes and is a global leader in certifying that its forests are sustainably managed, Confor says. (27 words)
cn: Confor称，英国拥有为建造低碳住宅而种植木材的理想条件，并且在森林可持续管理认证方面处于全球领先地位。

en: While around three quarters of Scottish homes are built from Scottish timber, the use of home-grown wood in England is only around 25 percent. (24 words)
cn: 尽管大约四分之三的苏格兰住宅是用苏格兰木材建成的，但英格兰对本土木材的使用率仅为25%左右。

en: While productive tree planting can deliver real financial benefits to rural economies and contribute to the UK's net-zero strategy, the focus of government support continues to be on food production and the rewilding and planting of native woodland solely for biodiversity. (41 words)
cn: 尽管有产出的植树造林能为乡村经济带来实实在在的经济效益，并为英国的净零排放战略做出贡献，然而政府支持的重点仍放在粮食生产，以及单纯为生物多样性而进行的野化归真和本土林地种植上。

en: Goodall added: "While food production and biodiversity are clearly of critical importance, we need our land to also provide secure supplies of wood for construction, manufacturing and contribute to net zero." (33 words)
cn: 古道尔补充道："尽管粮食生产和生物多样性显然至关重要，但我们也需要自己的土地能为建筑、制造业提供可靠的木材供应，并为净零排放做贡献。"

en: "While the UK government has stated its ambition for more tree planting, there has been little action on the ground." (20 words)
cn: "虽然英国政府已表明了增加树木种植的雄心，但实地行动却微乎其微。"

en: Confor is now calling for much greater impetus behind those aspirations to ensure we have enough wood to meet increasing demand. (21 words)
cn: Confor现在呼吁，在这些抱负背后要有更强大的推动力，以确保我们有足够的木材来满足日益增长的需求。

en: One big challenge in keeping unsafe aging drivers off the road is convincing them that it is time to turn over the key. (23 words)
cn: 让不安全的老龄驾驶员不再开车的一大挑战，是说服他们交钥匙的时候到了。

en: It is a complete life-changer when someone stops—or is forced to stop—driving, said former risk manager Anne M. Menke. (21 words)
cn: 当一个人停止——或被迫停止——驾驶时，这彻底改变生命，前风险管理师安妮·M·门克说。

en: The American Medical Association advises physicians that in a situation where clear evidence of substantial driving impairment implies a strong threat to patient and public safety, and where the physician's advice to discontinue driving privileges is ignored, it is desirable and ethical to notify the Department of Motor Vehicles, Menke wrote. (50 words)
cn: 门克写道，美国医学协会建议医生，在明显证据表明存在严重驾驶能力障碍，对患者和公众安全构成重大威胁，且医生关于停止驾驶权利的建议被忽视的情况下，通知车辆管理局是可取且合乎伦理的。

en: "Some states require physicians to report, others allow but do not mandate reports, while a few consider a report breach of confidentiality. (22 words)
cn: "有些州要求医生报告，另一些州允许但不强制要求报告，而少数州则将报告视为违反保密规定。

en: There could be liability and penalties if a physician does not act in accordance with state laws on reporting and confidentiality," she counseled. (24 words)
cn: 如果医生不按照州法律关于报告和保密的规定行事，可能会面临责任和处罚，"她劝诫道。

en: Part of the problem in keeping older drivers safe is that the difficulties are addressed piecemeal by different professions with different focuses, including gerontologists, highway administration officials, automotive engineers and others, said gerontologist Elizabeth Dugan. (35 words)
cn: 老年学家伊丽莎白·杜根说，保障老龄驾驶员安全的一个问题是，这些困难是由不同关注点的不同专业人士零敲碎打地处理的，包括老年学家、公路管理官员、汽车工程师等等。

en: "There's not a National Institute of Older Driver Studies," she said. (11 words)
cn: "并不存在一个国家老年驾驶员研究所，"她说。

en: "We need better evidence on what makes drivers unsafe" and what can help, said Dugan. (15 words)
cn: "我们需要更好的证据，了解是什么让驾驶员变得不安全，"以及什么能有所帮助，杜根说。

en: One thing that does seem to work is requiring drivers to report in person for license renewal. (17 words)
cn: 一件似乎确实有效的事，是要求驾驶员亲自到场换领驾照。

en: Mandatory in-person renewal was associated with a 31 percent reduction in fatal crashes involving drivers 85 or older, according to one study. (22 words)
cn: 根据一项研究，强制性亲自到场换证与85岁及以上驾驶员涉及的致命撞车事故减少31%相关联。

en: Passing vision tests also produced a similar decline in fatal crashes for those drivers, although there appeared to be no benefit from combining the two. (25 words)
cn: 通过视力测试同样使这些驾驶员的致命撞车事故出现了相似的下降，尽管将两者结合起来似乎并无额外益处。

en: Many old drivers don't see eye doctors or can't afford to. (11 words)
cn: 许多老龄驾驶员不去看眼科医生，或者负担不起。

en: Primary care providers have their hands full and may not be able to follow through with patients who have trouble driving because they can't turn their heads or remember where they are going—or have gotten shorter and haven't changed their seat settings sufficiently to reach car pedals easily. (49 words)
cn: 初级保健提供者们已经忙得不可开交，可能无暇对那些驾驶有困难的患者进行后续跟进，因为他们无法转头，或记不住要去哪里——抑或因为身高缩水却未充分调整座椅设置，以致难以轻松够到汽车踏板。

en: As long as there are other cars on the roads, self-driving cars won't solve the problems of crashes, said Dugan. (20 words)
cn: 杜根说，只要路上还有别的车，自动驾驶汽车就解决不了撞车问题。

en: Avoiding dangers posed by all those human drivers would require too many algorithms, she said. (15 words)
cn: 她说，要避免所有那些人类驾驶员造成的危险，将需要太多太多的算法。

en: But we need to do more to improve safety, said Dugan. (11 words)
cn: 但我们需要做得更多来提升安全，杜根说。

en: "If we're going to have 100-year lives, we need cars that a 90-year-old can drive comfortably." (16 words)
cn: "如果我们要活到一百岁，我们就需要能让九十岁老人舒适驾驶的车。"

en: If you look at the apps on your phone, chances are you have at least one related to your health—and probably several. (23 words)
cn: 如果你看看自己手机上的应用，很可能至少有一款与你的健康有关——而且很可能有好几款。

en: Whether it is a mental health app, a fitness tracker, a connected health device or something else, many of us are taking advantage of this technology to keep better track of our health in some shape or form. (38 words)
cn: 无论是心理健康应用、健身追踪器、互联健康设备还是其他什么，我们许多人都在利用这项技术，以某种形式更好地追踪自己的健康状况。

en: Recent research from the Organization for the Review of Care and Health Applications found that 350,000 health apps were available on the market, 90,000 of which launched in 2020 alone. (30 words)
cn: 护理与健康应用审查组织最近的研究发现，市场上有35万款健康应用可供使用，其中仅2020年就推出了9万款。

en: While these apps have a great deal to offer, it is not always clear how the personal information we input is collected, safeguarded and shared online. (26 words)
cn: 尽管这些应用能提供很多东西，但我们输入的个人信息在网上如何被收集、保护以及分享，却往往并不清楚。

en: Existing health privacy law, such as the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act, is primarily focused on the way hospitals, doctors' offices, clinics and insurance companies store health records online. (30 words)
cn: 现有的健康隐私法律，如《健康保险可携性与责任法案》，主要聚焦于医院、医生诊室、诊所和保险公司在线存储健康记录的方式。

en: The health information these apps and health data tracking wearables are collecting typically does not receive the same legal protections. (20 words)
cn: 这些应用以及健康数据追踪可穿戴设备所收集的健康信息，通常并不受同等法律保护。

en: Without additional protections in place, companies may share (and potentially monetize) personal health information in a way consumers may not have authorized or anticipated. (24 words)
cn: 在没有额外保护措施的情形下，公司可能会以消费者未必授权或未曾预料的方式分享（并可能借此盈利）个人健康信息。

en: In 2021, Flo Health faced a Federal Trade Commission (FTC) investigation. (13 words)
cn: 2021年，弗洛健康（Flo Health）面临美国联邦贸易委员会的调查。

en: The FTC alleged in a complaint that "despite express privacy claims, the company took control of users' sensitive fertility data and shared it with third parties." (26 words)
cn: 联邦贸易委员会在一份投诉中指称，"尽管有明确的隐私声明，该公司却控制了用户敏感的生育数据，并将其分享给了第三方。"

en: Flo Health and the FTC settled the matter with a Consent Order requiring the company to get app users' express affirmative consent before sharing their health information as well as to instruct the third parties to delete the data they had obtained. (42 words)
cn: 弗洛健康与联邦贸易委员会以一项同意令了结了此事，该同意令要求该公司在分享应用用户的健康信息之前必须征得他们的明确肯定同意，同时要求其指示第三方删除他们已经获取的数据。

en: Section 5 of the FTC Act empowers the FTC to initiate enforcement action against unfair or deceptive acts, meaning the FTC can only act after the fact if a company's privacy practices are misleading or cause unjustified consumer harm. (39 words)
cn: 《联邦贸易委员会法案》第5条授权联邦贸易委员会对不公平或欺骗性行为发起执法行动，这意味着联邦贸易委员会只能在事后采取行动，前提是该公司的隐私做法存在误导性或对消费者造成了不当损害。
