en: While the FTC is doing what it can to ensure apps are keeping their promises to consumers around the handling of their sensitive health information, the rate at which these health apps are hitting the market demonstrates just how immense of a challenge this is. (45 words)
cn: 尽管联邦贸易委员会正在尽其所能确保应用遵守就处理消费者敏感健康数据向消费者做出的承诺，但此类健康应用涌入市场的速度恰恰表明，这是一个多么巨大的挑战。

en: As to the prospects for federal legislation, commentators suggest that comprehensive federal privacy legislation seems unlikely in the short term. (20 words)
cn: 至于联邦立法的前景，评论人士表示，全面的联邦隐私立法短期内似乎不太可能出现。

en: States have begun implementing their own solutions to shore up protections for consumer-generated health data. (15 words)
cn: 各州已开始实施自己的方案，以加强对消费者产生的健康数据的保护。

en: California has been at the forefront of state privacy efforts with the California Consumer Privacy Act of 2018. (18 words)
cn: 加利福尼亚州一直走在各州隐私保护努力的前列，于2018年通过了《加州消费者隐私法案》。

en: Virginia, Colorado and Utah have also recently passed state consumer data privacy legislation. (14 words)
cn: 弗吉尼亚州、科罗拉多州和犹他州最近也通过了州级消费者数据隐私立法。

en: U.S. customers historically tipped the people they assumed were earning most of their income via tips, such as restaurant servers earning less than the minimum wage. (26 words)
cn: 过去，美国顾客通常会给那些他们认为主要靠小费赚取收入的人付小费，比如收入低于最低工资标准的餐厅服务员。

en: In the early 2010s, a wide range of businesses began processing purchases with iPads and other digital payment systems. (21 words)
cn: 在21世纪10年代初，各种各样的商家开始用iPad和其他数字支付系统来处理交易。

en: These systems often prompted customers to tip for services that were not previously tipped. (15 words)
cn: 这类系统往往会提示顾客为从前无需付小费的服务留下小费。

en: Today's tip requests are often disconnected from the salary and service norms that used to determine when and how people tip. (22 words)
cn: 如今的小费请求常常与过去决定人们何时付小费、怎样付小费的薪资和服务规范脱了节。

en: Customers in the past nearly always paid the tip after receiving a service, such as at the conclusion of a restaurant meal, after getting a haircut or once a pizza was delivered. (33 words)
cn: 以前的顾客几乎总是在接受完服务之后才付小费，比如在餐厅用完餐时、理完发后，或者披萨送到之后。

en: That timing could reward high-quality service and give workers an incentive to provide it. (16 words)
cn: 那种时机选择可以奖励高质量的服务，也给劳动者提供了提供优质服务的动力。

en: It's becoming more common for tips to be requested beforehand. (10 words)
cn: 如今，提前索要小费正变得越来越普遍。

en: And new tipping technology may even automatically add tips. (9 words)
cn: 而新的小费支付技术甚至可能自动加收小费。

en: The prevalence of digital payment devices has made it easier to ask customers for a tip. (17 words)
cn: 数字支付设备的普及使得向顾客索要小费变得更容易了。

en: That helps explain why tip requests are creeping into new kinds of services. (13 words)
cn: 这有助于解释为何小费请求正悄然蔓延至各种新型服务中。

en: Customers now routinely see menus of suggested default options—often well above 20% of what they owe. (18 words)
cn: 顾客如今会习惯性地看到建议默认选项的菜单——这些选项往往远超他们应付金额的20%。

en: The amounts have risen from 10% or less in the 1950s to 15% around the year 2000 to 20% or higher today. (23 words)
cn: 这一比例已从20世纪50年代的10%甚至更低，上升到2000年前后的15%，再到如今的20%或更高。

en: This increase is sometimes called tippflation—the expectation of ever-higher tip amounts. (13 words)
cn: 这种增长有时被称为"小费通胀"——即对小费金额不断攀升的预期。

en: Tipping has always been a vital source of income for workers in historically tipped services, like restaurants, where the tipped minimum wage can be as low as US $2.13 an hour. (33 words)
cn: 对于长期以来收取小费的行业中的劳动者，比如餐厅服务员，小费一直是他们至关重要的收入来源，这些行业的小费最低工资可以低至每小时2.13美元。

en: Tip creep and tippflation are now further supplementing the income of many low-wage service workers. (16 words)
cn: 小费蔓延和小费通胀如今正在进一步贴补许多低薪服务行业劳动者的收入。

en: Notably, tipping primarily benefits some of these workers such as cooks and dishwashers, so ensure that all employees were paid fair wages. (22 words)
cn: 值得注意的是，小费主要让其中一部分劳动者受益，如厨师和洗碗工，因此要确保所有员工都得到公平的工资。

en: Some restaurants banned tipping and increased prices, but this movement towards no-tipping services has largely fizzled out. (19 words)
cn: 一些餐厅曾禁止小费并提高菜价，但这场走向无小费服务的运动在很大程度上已偃旗息鼓。

en: So to increase employee wages without raising prices, more employers are succumbing to the temptations of tip creep and tippflation. (21 words)
cn: 因此，为了在不涨价的情况下提高员工工资，越来越多的雇主正屈服于小费蔓延和小费通胀的诱惑。

en: However, many customers are frustrated because they feel they are being asked for too high of a tip too often. (22 words)
cn: 然而，许多顾客却感到沮丧，因为他们觉得自己被索要小费太频繁，金额也太高。

en: And, as our research emphasizes, tipping now seems to be more coercive, less generous, and often completely disassociated from service quality. (21 words)
cn: 而且，正如我们的研究所强调的，如今的小费似乎更具强制性，少了慷慨之意，且常常与服务质量完全脱节。

en: When it was established, the National Health Service (NHS) was visionary: offering high-quality, timely care to meet the dominant needs of the population it served. (26 words)
cn: 英国国家医疗服务体系创立之初是富有远见的：提供高质量、及时的医疗护理，以满足其所服务人群的主要需求。

en: Nearly 75 years on, with the UK facing very different health challenges, it is clear that the model is out of date. (22 words)
cn: 近75年过去了，随着英国面临着截然不同的健康挑战，这一模式显然已经过时。

en: From the life expectancy to cancer and infant mortality rates, we are lagging behind many of our peers. (20 words)
cn: 从预期寿命到癌症及婴儿死亡率，我们正落后于许多同等国家。

en: With more than 6.8 million on waitlists, healthcare is becoming increasingly inaccessible for those who cannot opt to pay for private treatment; and the cost of providing healthcare is increasingly squeezing our investment in other public services. (39 words)
cn: 候诊名单上有超过680万人，对那些无法选择自费接受私人医疗的人来说，医疗保健正变得越来越难以获取；而提供医疗保健的成本正日益挤压我们对其他公共服务的投入。

en: As demand for health care continues to grow, pressures on the workforce — which is already near breaking point — will only become more acute. (25 words)
cn: 随着医疗保健需求持续增长，对本来就已濒临崩溃的医务劳动力的压力只会变得更加严峻。

en: Many of the answers to the crisis in the health and care are well rehearsed. (16 words)
cn: 应对这场健康和医疗危机的许多对策早已是老生常谈。

en: We need to be much better at reducing and diverting demand on health services, rather than simply managing it. (21 words)
cn: 我们需要做得更出色的是减少并分流对医疗服务的需求，而非仅仅加以管理。

en: Much more needs to be invested in communities and primary care to reduce our reliance on hospitals. (18 words)
cn: 需要大幅增加对社区和初级医疗的投入，以减少我们对医院的依赖。

en: And capacity in social care needs to be greater, to support the growing number of people living with long-term conditions. (20 words)
cn: 社会护理的能力也需要进一步增强，以支持越来越多患有长期慢性疾病的人群。

en: Yet despite two decades of strategies and a number of major health reforms, we have failed to make meaningful progress on any of these aims. (26 words)
cn: 然而，尽管经过了二十年的各种战略和多次重大医疗改革，我们在这些目标上却都未能取得有实际意义的进展。

en: That is why the Reform think tank is launching a new programme of work entitled "Reimagining health", supported by ten former health ministers. (25 words)
cn: 正因如此，改革智库正在推出一项名为"重新构想健康"的新工作方案，该方案得到了十位前卫生部长的支持。

en: Together, we are calling for a much more open and honest conversation about the future of health in the UK, and an "urgent rethink" of the hospital-centric model we retain. (31 words)
cn: 我们共同呼吁，就英国医疗的未来展开一场更加开诚布公的对话，并呼吁对我们仍沿用的以医院为中心的模式进行"紧急反思"。

en: This must begin with the question of how we maximise the health of the nation, rather than "fix" the NHS. (22 words)
cn: 这必须从我们如何最大限度地提升国民健康这一问题入手，而不是一味想要"修补"国家医疗服务体系。

en: It is estimated, for example, that healthcare accounts for only about 20% of health outcomes. (17 words)
cn: 例如，据估计，医疗保健对健康结果的影响只占约20%。

en: Much more important are the places we live, work and socialise – yet there is no clear cross-government strategy for improving these social determinants of health. (27 words)
cn: 更为重要的是我们生活、工作和社交的场所——然而却没有一项明确的跨政府战略来改善这些影响健康的社会决定因素。

en: Worse, when policies like the national obesity strategy are scrapped, taxpayers are left with the hefty price tag of treating the illnesses, like diabetes, that result. (27 words)
cn: 更糟的是，当国家肥胖战略这类政策被废弃时，纳税者就要为治疗由此引发的疾病——比如糖尿病——承担高昂的代价。

en: Reform wants to ask how power and resources should be distributed in our health system. (17 words)
cn: 改革智库想要探究的是，权力和资源在我们的医疗体系中应当如何分配。

en: What health functions should remain at the centre, and what should be given to local leaders, often responsible for services that create health, and with a much better understanding of the needs of their populations? (34 words)
cn: 哪些健康职能应该留在中央，哪些又该交给地方领导者——他们往往负责创造健康的各项服务，并且对当地人口的需求要了解得多？

en: Heat action plans, or HAPs, have been proliferating in India in the past few years. (14 words)
cn: 过去几年里，高温行动计划在印度迅速增多。

en: In general, an HAP spells out when and how officials should issue heat warnings and alert hospitals and other institutions. (20 words)
cn: 一般来说，一份高温行动计划会明确规定官员应于何时、以何种方式发布高温预警，并通知医院及其他机构。

en: Nagpur's plan, for instance, calls for hospitals to set aside "cold wards" in the summer for treating heatstroke patients, and advises builders to give construction laborers a break from work on very hot days. (35 words)
cn: 例如，那格浦尔的计划要求医院在夏季预留"冷病房"以收治中暑病人，并建议建筑商在酷热天气里让建筑工人暂停工作休息。

en: But implementation of existing HAPs has been uneven, according to a report from the Center for Policy Research. (18 words)
cn: 但根据政策研究中心的一份报告，现有高温行动计划的执行情况参差不齐。

en: Many lack adequate funding, it found. (7 words)
cn: 报告发现，许多计划缺少充足的资金。

en: And their triggering thresholds often are not customized to the local climate. (12 words)
cn: 而且它们的触发阈值往往没有根据当地气候进行调整。

en: In some areas, high daytime temperatures alone might serve as an adequate trigger for alerts. (16 words)
cn: 在有些地区，仅凭白天的高温就足以成为发布预警的触发条件。

en: But in other places, nighttime temperatures or humidity might be as important a gauge of risk as daytime highs. (19 words)
cn: 但在别的地方，夜间温度或湿度可能和白天的最高气温同等重要，是衡量风险的标尺。

en: Mumbai's April heat stroke deaths highlighted the need for more nuanced and localized warnings, researchers say. (17 words)
cn: 研究人员表示，孟买四月份的中暑死亡事件凸显了更精细、更本地化的预警的必要性。

en: That day's high temperature of roughly 36°C was 1°C shy of the heat wave alert threshold for coastal cities set by national meteorological authorities. (25 words)
cn: 当天约36摄氏度的最高气温，比国家气象部门为沿海城市设定的热浪预警阈值低了1摄氏度。

en: But the effects of the heat were amplified by humidity—an often neglected factor in heat alert systems—and the lack of shade at the late-morning outdoor ceremony. (28 words)
cn: 但高温的效应被湿度——高温预警系统中一个常被忽视的因素——以及上午晚些时候户外仪式中缺少遮荫这两点放大了。

en: To help improve HAPs, urban planner Kotharkar's team is working on a model plan that outlines best practices and could be adapted to local conditions. (26 words)
cn: 为帮助改进高温行动计划，城市规划师科塔卡尔的团队正在制定一份示范性计划，该计划列出了最佳做法，并能根据当地情况加以调整。

en: Among other things, she says, all cities should create a vulnerability map to help focus responses on the populations most at risk. (22 words)
cn: 她说，除了其他措施之外，所有城市都应绘制脆弱性地图，以帮助将应对措施聚焦于风险最高的人群。

en: Such mapping doesn't need to be complex, Kotharkar says. (9 words)
cn: 科塔卡尔说，这种地图绘制并不需要很复杂。

en: "A useful map can be created by looking at even a few key parameters." (14 words)
cn: "哪怕只考察几个关键参数，也能制作出一幅有用的地图。"

en: For example, neighborhoods with a large elderly population or informal dwellings that cope poorly with heat could get special warnings or be bolstered with cooling centers. (26 words)
cn: 例如，居住着大量老年人口或房屋为非正规建造、应对高温能力差的社区，可以获得特别预警，或通过设置降温中心来增强应对能力。

en: The Nagpur project has already created a risk and vulnerability map, which enabled Kotharkar to tell officials which neighborhoods to focus on in the event of a heat wave this summer. (31 words)
cn: 那格浦尔的项目已经绘制了一份风险与脆弱性地图，这让科塔卡尔得以告诉官员们，今年夏天若发生热浪，应重点关注哪些社区。

en: HAPs shouldn't just include short-term emergency responses, researchers say, but also recommend medium- to long-term measures that could make communities cooler. (22 words)
cn: 研究人员说，高温行动计划不应只包含短期的应急响应，还应提出能够让社区变得更加凉爽的中长期措施。

en: In Nagpur, for example, Kotharkar's team has been able to advise city officials about where to plant trees to provide shade. (21 words)
cn: 例如，在那格浦尔，科塔卡尔的团队已能够就何处植树以提供荫凉向市官员提出建议。

en: HAPs could also guide efforts to retrofit homes or modify building regulations. (12 words)
cn: 高温行动计划还可以指导房屋的翻新改造工作或建筑规范的修改。

en: "Reducing deaths in an emergency is a good target to have, but it's the lowest target," says climate researcher Chandni Singh. (23 words)
cn: "在紧急情况下减少死亡人数是一个好的目标，但那只是最低的目标，"气候研究员钱德尼·辛格说道。

en: Navigating beyond the organised pavements and parts of our urban spaces, desire paths are the unofficial footprints of a community, revealing the unspoken preferences, shared shortcuts and collective choices of humans. (33 words)
cn: 在规整的人行道和城市空间那些井然有序的区域之外，渴望路径是一个社区的非官方足迹，揭示着人们未曾言明的偏好、共同的捷径和集体的选择。

en: Often appearing as trodden dirt tracks through otherwise neat green spaces, these routes of collective disobedience cut corners, bisect lawns and cross hills, representing the natural capability of people, and animals, to go from point A to point B most effectively. (40 words)
cn: 这些集体违逆的路线常常呈现为踩踏而成的土径，穿过原本整齐的绿地，它们抄近道、横穿草坪、翻越小山，代表着人和动物以最高效的方式从一处到达另一处的天然能力。

en: Urban planners interpret desire paths as more than just convenient shortcuts; they offer valuable insights into the dynamics between planning and behaviour. (24 words)
cn: 城市规划者将渴望路径解读为不仅仅是便捷的捷径；它们为洞察规划与行为之间的动态关系提供了宝贵的线索。

en: Ohio State University allowed its students to navigate the Oval, a lawn in the centre of campus, freely, then proceeded to pave the desire paths, creating a web of effective routes students had established. (35 words)
cn: 俄亥俄州立大学先是让学生们自由穿行校园中央名为椭圆广场的草坪，随后便将那些渴望路径铺装起来，形成了一张由学生们开辟的高效路线网。

en: Yet, reluctance persists among other planners to integrate desire paths into formal plans, citing concerns about safety, environmental impact, or primarily, aesthetics. (24 words)
cn: 然而，其他一些规划者仍不愿将渴望路径纳入正式规划，其理由涉及安全、环境影响，或者主要是出于美观方面的考虑。

en: A Reddit webpage devoted to the phenomenon, boasting nearly 50,000 members, showcases images of local desire paths adorned with signs instructing pedestrians to adhere to designated walkways, underscoring the rebellious nature inherent in these human-made tracks. (36 words)
cn: 一个专门关注这一现象的Reddit页面拥有近五万名成员，上面展示着各地渴望路径的图片，那些路径旁竖着指示行人必须走指定步道的标牌，凸显了这些人造小径与生俱来的叛逆本质。

en: This clash highlights an ongoing struggle between the organic, user-driven evolution of public spaces and the desire for a visually curated and controlled urban environment. (26 words)
cn: 这一冲突突显了公共空间在由使用者驱动的有机演变与追求视觉上精心设计和控制的城市环境之间持续不断的角力。

en: The Wickquasgeck Trail is an example of a historical desire path, created by Native Americans to cross the forests of Manhattan and move between settlements quickly. (27 words)
cn: 威克瓦斯格克小径便是一条历史渴望路径的例子，它由美洲原住民开辟，用以穿越曼哈顿的森林，在聚居地之间快速往来。

en: This trail, when Dutch colonists arrived, was widened and made into one of the main trade roads across the island, known at the time as de Heere Straat, or Gentlemen's Street. (32 words)
cn: 当荷兰殖民者到来时，这条小径被拓宽，变成了横穿该岛的主要贸易道路之一，当时被称为绅士街。

en: Following the British assumption of control in New York, the street was renamed Broadway. (14 words)
cn: 英国取得纽约的控制权后，这条街被更名为百老汇。

en: Notably, Broadway stands out as one of the few areas in NYC that defies the grid-based system applied to the rest of the city, cutting a diagonal across parts of the city. (32 words)
cn: 值得注意的是，百老汇是纽约市少数几处打破了该市其余地方所采用的网格系统的区域之一，斜向贯穿了城中部分地区。

en: In online spaces, desire paths have sparked a fascination that can approach obsession, with the Reddit page serving as a hub. (22 words)
cn: 在网络空间里，渴望路径引发了一种近乎痴迷的着迷，那个Reddit页面则充当着交流中心。

en: Contributors offer a wide array of stories, from little-known new shortcuts to long-established alternate routes. (16 words)
cn: 贡献者们分享着形形色色的故事，从鲜为人知的新捷径，到早已有之的替代路线。

en: Animal desire paths, such as ducks forging trails through frozen ponds or dogs carving direct routes in gardens, highlight the adaptability of these trails in both human and animal experiences. (33 words)
cn: 动物的渴望路径，比如鸭子在结冰的池塘上踏出小径，或者狗在花园里闯出直来直往的路线，都凸显了这类路径在人类和动物经验中共同的适应性。

en: As desire paths criss-cross through both physical and virtual landscapes, they stand as a proof of the collective insistence on forging unconventional routes and embracing the spirit of communal choice. (31 words)
cn: 当渴望路径纵横交错地穿行于现实与虚拟的图景之间时，它们便成为一种证明，见证了集体的那份坚持——坚持开辟非常规的路径，拥抱那种共同选择的精神。

en: Ask people about public libraries and a certain image springs to mind: dusty, old-fashioned, the sort of place you enjoyed as a child but, rather like a British seaside town, would you go there now? (36 words)
cn: 向人们问起公共图书馆，脑海中便会浮现出某种形象：布满灰尘、老派过时，那种你小时候喜欢去的地方，但是，有点像英国的滨海小镇，你现在还会去那里吗？

en: And anyway — aren't they all closing? (7 words)
cn: 而且话说回来——它们不是都在关闭吗？

en: The reality is startlingly different, as I discovered when the culture department commissioned me to conduct an independent review of English public libraries, published yesterday. (28 words)
cn: 现实情况却惊人地不同，正如文化部门委托我对英格兰公共图书馆进行一项独立审查时所发现的那样，该审查报告于昨日发布。

en: As I visited libraries up and down the country, I was surprised to learn there are more than twice the number of libraries (2,892) as there are branches of McDonald's. (30 words)
cn: 当我走访全国上下各地的图书馆时，我惊讶地发现，图书馆的数量（2892家）是麦当劳门店数量的两倍多。

en: Enter any one of them and you will find a hive of activity. (15 words)
cn: 走进其中任何一家，你都会发现里面一片忙碌景象。

en: While books are, and should always be, at the heart of any library, a multitude of other services are offered: employment advice, language classes or digital access and support. (30 words)
cn: 虽然图书处于、也应当始终处于任何图书馆的核心位置，但图书馆还提供众多其他服务：就业咨询、语言课程或数字接入与支持。

en: There are libraries with business and intellectual property centres, which can help business owners and entrepreneurs. (18 words)
cn: 有些图书馆设有商业和知识产权中心，能够帮助企业主和企业家。

en: Many have nurses on site to carry out basic health checks, with a link to the GP's surgery. (19 words)
cn: 许多图书馆有护士在现场进行基本健康检查，并与全科医生诊所有关联。

en: There are libraries where young people can borrow a Fifa-standard football free. (14 words)
cn: 有些图书馆年轻人可以免费借到符合国际足联标准的足球。

en: In return for all of this, you'll be asked for precisely nothing. (13 words)
cn: 对于所有这一切，你会被要求付出分毫。

en: There will be no charge and you will never be asked to justify or explain yourself; you will simply be welcomed in, offered help if you need it and left alone if you do not. (34 words)
cn: 不会有任何收费，你永远不会被要求证明自己或做出解释；你只会被欢迎入内，需要时有人提供帮助，不需要时则可自在独处。

en: There is no other institution, public or private, that can say the same. (14 words)
cn: 没有其他任何机构，无论是公共的还是私人的，可以说出同样的话。

en: Yet still our libraries are often overlooked and underappreciated. (9 words)
cn: 然而，我们的图书馆仍然常常被忽视，得不到应有的重视。

en: There is an overall decline in visits and many are struggling as local authorities come under continued financial pressure. (21 words)
cn: 访客量总体下降，许多图书馆由于地方政府持续面临财政压力而苦苦挣扎。

en: The number of libraries that have closed since 2010 is disputed. (12 words)
cn: 自2010年以来关闭的图书馆数量存在争议。

en: An annual survey by the Chartered Institute of Public Finance and Accountancy puts it at nearly 800 across the UK; official statistics by the Arts Council record 230 in England. (31 words)
cn: 特许公共财政与会计学会的一项年度调查认为全英有近800家图书馆关闭；而艺术委员会的官方统计则记录英格兰有230家。

en: It is, of course, 230 libraries too many. (8 words)
cn: 当然，230家也已经太多了。

en: So if we are to protect our libraries for future generations, we must raise awareness of them and the work they do. (23 words)
cn: 因此，如果我们想为子孙后代保护我们的图书馆，就必须提高对图书馆及其所从事工作的认识。

en: The review recommends a national branding campaign to give libraries a stronger physical presence, the reintroduction of a scheme to enable members to use their card in any library in the country, and automatic memberships for children. (38 words)
cn: 该审查建议开展一项全国品牌宣传活动，以赋予图书馆更强的实体存在感，重新推行一项使会员能在全国任何一家图书馆使用其借书卡的方案，并让儿童自动获得会员资格。

en: If we don't use them, we will end up losing them. (12 words)
cn: 如果我们不使用它们，我们最终就会失去它们。

en: And for those who haven't been to a library for some time, they may be surprised by what they find. (22 words)
cn: 而对于那些有些时日没去过图书馆的人来说，他们或许会为自己发现的东西感到惊讶。

en: According to our research, around one in five workers in the UK talk to AI like a friend, looking for guidance on personal and professional problems. (28 words)
cn: 根据我们的研究，英国约有五分之一的劳动者像与朋友交谈一样与人工智能对话，寻求关于个人和职业问题的指导。

en: Our data shows that engaging with AI like this can leave us feeling heard and less isolated. (18 words)
cn: 我们的数据显示，以这种方式与人工智能互动可以让我们感到被倾听，减少孤立感。

en: But, with this newfound connection, many of us share sensitive, sometimes highly confidential information, even though over a third of people don't realise that AI platforms may not be very good at keeping our secrets ... secret. (36 words)
cn: 但是，伴随着这种新建立的联系，我们许多人会分享敏感的、有时是高度机密的信息，尽管超过三分之一的人并没有意识到，人工智能平台可能并不太擅长替我们的秘密……保密。

en: For businesses, the implications are worrying. (7 words)
cn: 对企业来说，其影响令人担忧。

en: Consider Microsoft Copilot for example. (5 words)
cn: 以微软Copilot为例。

en: It grants Microsoft broad rights to the data inputted or outputted by any user – rights to use this data in any way it sees fit, it can even share it with third parties. (33 words)
cn: 它授予微软对任何用户输入或输出数据的广泛权利——可以以其认为合适的任何方式使用这些数据的权利，甚至可以将其分享给第三方。

en: This means that any sensitive information could potentially be exposed to the world. (15 words)
cn: 这意味着任何敏感信息都可能被曝光于世。

en: Employers are taking note and taking action. (8 words)
cn: 雇主们正在意识到这一点并采取行动。

en: According to our research, 25% have decided to either outright ban AI or regulate its use within their organisations. (19 words)
cn: 根据我们的研究，百分之二十五的雇主已决定要么直接禁止人工智能，要么在其所在机构内对其使用加以管控。

en: But even with these policies in place, some employees choose to break the rules. (16 words)
cn: 但即使有了这些政策，一些员工仍然选择违反规定。

en: They have their reasons – around 63% of them report that using AI increases their productivity, and some even feel AI offers more help than their human colleagues. (29 words)
cn: 他们有他们的理由——其中约百分之六十三的人表示使用人工智能提高了他们的生产率，有些人甚至觉得人工智能比他们的人类同事提供了更多帮助。

en: The situation presents a delicate balance between leveraging AI for its productivity gains and risking confidential data exposure. (20 words)
cn: 这种情形呈现了一个微妙的平衡：一边是利用人工智能来提高生产率，一边是冒着机密数据泄露的风险。

en: Employers need to manage AI tools with the same level of care as any other form of data sharing or storage. (22 words)
cn: 雇主需要以对待任何其他形式的数据共享或存储的同等谨慎程度来管理人工智能工具。

en: There's also a knowledge gap that needs to be addressed – 40% of individuals surveyed are unsure about who retains ownership over the content produced by AI. (28 words)
cn: 还有一个需要弥合的知识鸿沟——百分之四十的受访者不确定由人工智能产生的内容归谁所有。

en: By instilling best practices in AI engagement and creating policies that evolve with the technology, businesses can positively shift the AI landscape. (24 words)
cn: 通过逐步灌输人工智能使用中的最佳实践，并制定随技术演进而不断发展的政策，企业可以朝着积极的方向改变人工智能的格局。

en: With AI's potential to simplify our professional lives, do the admin and enhance the work experience, it's up to us to navigate its usage cautiously so it supports us without compromising our privacy. (34 words)
cn: 人工智能有潜力简化我们的职业生活、完成行政事务并提升工作体验，因此应当由我们来审慎驾驭其使用，使之在不损害我们隐私的前提下支持我们。

en: There's no need to back away from progress, as long as we're equipped with the knowledge and tools to make sure AI remains a friend. (25 words)
cn: 只要我们用知识和工具武装起来，确保人工智能始终是一个朋友，就没有必要从进步面前退缩。

en: And this is where the challenge lies. (8 words)
cn: 而这正是挑战所在。

en: There are so many products out there, being promoted with huge advertising and marketing budgets, that it's easy to fall victim. (21 words)
cn: 市面上有太多产品，被投入巨额广告和营销预算来推广，使人很容易就沦为受害者。

en: But by building a culture of digital responsibility within our businesses, we can create a future where AI can help us without spilling the beans. (26 words)
cn: 但通过在企业内部建立一种数字责任文化，我们可以创造一个未来，让人工智能既能帮助我们，又不会泄露我们的秘密。

en: Since the 2008 launch of the high-speed rail network between Rome and Milan, trains have become the preferred means of travel across Italy for locals and tourists alike. (30 words)
cn: 自2008年罗马至米兰高铁网络开通以来，火车已成为当地人和游客在意大利境内出行的首选交通方式。

en: Fast trains can cover the 500km between the two cities in three hours. (15 words)
cn: 快速列车可在三小时内驶完两座城市之间500公里的路程。

en: The network also connects Naples, Bologna, Florence and Turin. (10 words)
cn: 该网络还连接着那不勒斯、博洛尼亚、佛罗伦萨和都灵。

en: In many cases, it is the best option — in terms of travel time and cost — for both leisure and business travellers. (22 words)
cn: 在许多情况下，无论是休闲旅客还是商务旅客，就出行时间和费用而言，这都是最佳选择。

en: This is why the recent severe delays caused by numerous maintenance works have thrown the rail industry into chaos at a time when most people head on holiday, which matters for business in a country where tourism accounts for 10 per cent of GDP. (46 words)
cn: 正因如此，最近由于大量维护工程造成的严重延误，在大多数人外出度假之际将铁路行业抛入混乱之中，而在这个旅游业占国内生产总值百分之十的国家，这对商业来说至关重要。

en: Disruptions have become increasingly frequent with train delays now a fixture in Italian media coverage. (16 words)
cn: 中断现象已变得越来越频繁，列车晚点如今已成为意大利媒体报道中的固定内容。

en: Ferrovie dello Stato Italiano, the publicly controlled group that owns train operator Trenitalia and the national train network RFI, said 23 per cent of high-speed trains it operated were late in 2023. (33 words)
cn: 意大利国家铁路集团，这个控股列车运营商意大利铁路公司和全国铁路公司（RFI）的国有集团，表示其运营的高速列车2023年有百分之二十三出现晚点。

en: The huge improvements in Italy's obsolete infrastructure partly explained the disruption, said Ferrovie. (15 words)
cn: 意大利国家铁路集团表示，对意大利陈旧基础设施的大规模改造在一定程度上解释了这些中断。

en: RFI, the network operator, is the single largest European recovery fund beneficiary with planned investments of €24bn by 2026. (20 words)
cn: 网络运营商RFI是欧洲复苏基金最大的单一受益方，计划到2026年投资240亿欧元。

en: Ferrovie will invest a total of €124bn in infrastructure over the next 10 years. (15 words)
cn: 意大利国家铁路集团将在未来十年内总计投资1240亿欧元用于基础设施建设。

en: But lack of capacity is another problem. (7 words)
cn: 但运力不足是另一个问题。

en: Transport economist Andrea Giuricin said the planned investments would bring improvements but disruptions in the meantime were inevitable. (18 words)
cn: 交通经济学家安德烈亚·朱里钦表示，已计划好的投资会带来改善，但在此期间中断是不可避免的。

en: "We have a mixed system, there is no spare capacity and as soon as there's a hiccup on the line, the situation becomes highly complex," he said. (28 words)
cn: "我们有一个混合系统，没有任何多余运力，一旦线路上出现小故障，情况就会变得高度复杂，"他说。

en: A mixed system means that high-speed trains must travel on the regular tracks on certain routes or when passing through large cities. (24 words)
cn: 混合系统意味着高速列车在某些线路或穿过大城市时，必须在普通轨道上运行。

en: If there's congestion or, for example, a local train breaks down, the entire high-speed network is affected. (18 words)
cn: 如果出现拥堵，或者比如一列本地列车坏了，整个高速网络都会受到影响。

en: One of the most important changes the upgrades will bring is the segregation of the high-speed line from the regular one in certain urban centres through the construction of underground rail links. (33 words)
cn: 这些升级将带来的一项最重要改变是，通过修建地下铁路连接线，在某些市中心将高铁线路与普通线路分离开来。

en: Improvements will also come from the high-density technology and satellite signalling that RFI has been investing in for years, say experts. (21 words)
cn: 专家们表示，改进还将来自意大利铁路公司多年来一直在投资的高密度技术和卫星信号系统。

en: It will allow for a reduction of the distance between high-speed trains travelling on the same line at any given time, which should mean a significant increase in capacity and traffic fluidity, according to Giuricin. (35 words)
cn: 据朱里钦称，这将能缩短同一线路上任意时刻运行的高速列车之间的间距，这应该意味着运力和交通流畅度的大幅提升。

en: In 2023, Chicago lost one of its most beloved street festivals. (11 words)
cn: 2023年，芝加哥失去了其最受喜爱的街头节庆之一。

en: The Silver Room Block Party, staged by Hyde Park community leader Eric Williams, announced it would not return in 2024. (22 words)
cn: 由海德公园社区领袖埃里克·威廉姆斯操办的银厅街区派对宣布，它不会在2024年回归。

en: What began as a small neighborhood gathering blossomed into a massive cultural event, welcoming tens of thousands of people each year over nearly two decades before abruptly shutting down. (30 words)
cn: 这场起初只是小型邻里聚会的活动，已发展为一场大规模文化盛事，近二十年来每年都迎来数万人参与，如今却骤然停办。

en: Williams pointed to rising production costs and declining attendee donations as primary reasons the Silver Room Block Party could not continue, highlighting a reality that all street festival organizers face right now. (33 words)
cn: 威廉姆斯指出，制作成本上升和参与者捐款减少是银厅街区派对无法继续举办的主要原因，这凸显了所有街头节庆组织者眼下都面临的现实。

en: The cost of producing a street festival in Chicago has skyrocketed. (12 words)
cn: 在芝加哥，举办一场街头节庆的成本已急剧飙升。

en: Security, entertainment, portable restrooms, insurance and even basics such as fencing and staffing have all become significantly more expensive. (20 words)
cn: 安保、娱乐表演、移动厕所、保险，甚至围栏和人员配置这类基本项目，都变得昂贵得多。

en: At the same time, donations at festival gates have dropped dramatically. (12 words)
cn: 与此同时，节庆入口处的捐款却大幅下降。

en: Chicago's summer festivals are about more than just entertainment; they are economic engines that directly benefit the neighborhoods they're in and the city of Chicago as a whole. (30 words)
cn: 芝加哥的夏季节庆不仅仅是娱乐；它们是直接惠及所在社区以及整个芝加哥市的经济引擎。

en: Street festivals drive foot traffic to local businesses and foster the kind of cultural vibrancy that makes our city special. (20 words)
cn: 街头节庆将步行客流引向本地商家，并培育出让这座城市别具一格的文化活力。

en: We often hear people ask why we solicit donations at our entry points, especially when the city's largest festivals like the Chicago Jazz Festival do not request donations. (30 words)
cn: 我们常听到人们问，为什么我们要在入口处募捐，尤其是当芝加哥爵士音乐节这类全市最盛大的节庆都不要求捐款的时候。

en: The fact is, unlike those large, city-produced music festivals, your neighborhood street festivals receive no city funding and rely on a combination of sponsorships, vendor fees and gate donations to cover their costs. (34 words)
cn: 事实是，与那些由市政府出品的大型音乐节不同，你们身边的社区街头节庆没有市政府的资助，只能靠赞助、摊位费和入口捐款三者的结合来支付其开销。

en: Wicker Park Fest has long been one of Chicago's most anticipated summer festivals, drawing upward of 70,000 attendees for a full weekend of live indie music, local art, small business vendors and, most importantly, community connection. (37 words)
cn: 柳条公园节长期以来一直是芝加哥最受期待的夏季节庆之一，吸引着超过七万人前来，享受一整个周末的独立音乐现场、本地艺术、小商户摊位，以及最重要的——社区联结。

en: In 2024, Wicker Park Fest saw record-breaking attendance. (8 words)
cn: 2024年，柳条公园节的参与人数创下了历史新高。

en: Despite the turnout, gate donations reached their lowest point in our history. (14 words)
cn: 尽管人数众多，入口捐款却降到了我们历史上的最低点。

en: This year, we've been forced to scale back the footprint of the fest. (15 words)
cn: 今年，我们被迫缩减了节庆的场地规模。

en: We are eliminating a stage, booking fewer performers and making additional cuts to reduce our costs, all while striving to keep the festival as vibrant as ever, as supportive of local artists and businesses, and as true to Wicker Park's unique spirit and reputation as festgoers have come to expect. (50 words)
cn: 我们正取消一个舞台，预约更少的表演者，并进一步削减开支以降低成本，同时努力让节庆一如既往地充满活力，一如既往地支持本地艺术家和商家，一如节庆参与者们所期待的那样，忠于柳条公园独特的精神和声誉。

en: This summer, as you enjoy your favorite neighborhood street festival, I hope you'll remember that they exist because of community support. (24 words)
cn: 今年夏天，当你们享受自己最喜爱的社区街头节庆时，我希望你们会记得，它们之所以存在，是因为有社区的支持。

en: A thriving summer festival season doesn't happen by accident; it happens when we all chip in. (18 words)
cn: 一个繁荣的夏季节庆季并非凭空得来；它发生在我们每个人都出一份力的时候。
